display_text: Anti-Mage is a Melee Agility carry with 21 base Strength, 24 base Agility, and 12 base Intelligence. His attack rate is 1.4 seconds per hit. This gives him high starting armor and damage, but low health and mana, typical for a hard-farming carry. He is vulnerable to early magic burst and has no inherent AoE.
search_text: Anti-Mage base stats melee agility carry 21 str 24 agi 12 int 1.4 attack rate high armor low hp mana

display_text: Anti-Mage has two Facets. Magebane's Mirror enhances Counterspell: when a targeted spell is blocked, it burns a percentage of the mana cost from the enemy caster and deals damage based on that burned mana. This is aggressive, punishing spellcasters who target you. Mana Thirst adds a scaling damage bonus against enemies with low mana, making you more lethal in prolonged fights and against heroes who run out of mana.
search_text: Anti-Mage Facets Magebane's Mirror Counterspell mana burn damage Mana Thirst low mana bonus damage

display_text: In lane, prioritize last hits with your high base damage. Use your low health pool carefully against magic damage. Start with 1 point in Mana Break to harass intelligence heroes. Max Blink first to increase farming speed and escape. Use Counterspell situationally if facing heavy targeted magic damage. The goal is to survive, get early Battle Fury components, and rotate to the jungle as soon as possible.
search_text: Anti-Mage laning last hits Mana Break harass Blink max farm Counterspell magic survive jungle

display_text: Key power spikes: Level 6 (Mana Void provides kill threat). Level 12 with maxed Blink and Mana Break for fast farming. Level 18 with all skills maxed. Item timings: Battle Fury (enables flash farming), Manta Style (dispel, illusions benefit from Mana Break), and Skull Basher/Abyssal Blade (lockdown). Level 20 and 25 talents significantly increase damage and utility.
search_text: Anti-Mage power spikes level 6 Mana Void level 12 Blink Battle Fury Manta Style Basher Abyssal Blade talent 20 25

display_text: Anti-Mage is hard countered by Break mechanics (from Silver Edge or Hoodwink), which disable his Counterspell passive magic resistance and Persecutor slow. Instant disables like Hex prevent Blink escape. High physical burst damage (e.g., TA, PA) can kill him through his low health pool. Heroes with high mana pools or manaless spells (e.g., Bristleback) reduce the effectiveness of Mana Break and Mana Void.
search_text: Anti-Mage counters Break Silver Edge disables Counterspell magic resist Persecutor Hex stop Blink physical burst high mana Bristleback

display_text: Core build is Battle Fury and Manta Style. Battle Fury accelerates farm and provides AoE mana burn. Manta Style dispels debuffs, creates illusions that apply Mana Break at 50% effectiveness, and increases farming/split-push speed. These two items are non-negotiable for a standard Anti-Mage game.
search_text: Anti-Mage core items Battle Fury Manta Style farm mana burn illusion split push dispel

display_text: In teamfights, Anti-Mage's role is to Blink on the backline spellcasters, burn their mana with attacks, and kill them with Mana Void. He should avoid initiating and instead clean up after initial spells are used. Use Blink to reposition and dodge projectiles. He excels at creating split-push pressure and then joining fights with a numbers advantage.
search_text: Anti-Mage teamfight role Blink backline mana burn Mana Void kill clean up split push reposition

display_text: Mana Break is a passive ability. Each attack burns 25/30/35/40 mana from the target and deals 50% of the mana burnt as bonus damage. Illusions apply Mana Break at 50% effectiveness. This ability gives Anti-Mage strong harass and kill threat against intelligence heroes and is crucial for his farming speed with Battle Fury.
search_text: Anti-Mage ability Q Mana Break passive burn 25 30 35 40 mana damage 50% burned illusion 50% effect

display_text: Blink is a targeted point ability with a 750/900/1050/1200 cast range and a 12/10/8/6 second cooldown. It has a minimum blink range of 200. It allows Anti-Mage to teleport a short distance, enabling chase, escape, and farming efficiency. It can be used to dodge incoming projectiles. It does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Anti-Mage ability W Blink teleport range 750 900 1050 1200 cooldown 12 10 8 6 dodge projectile

display_text: Counterspell is a passive/active ability. Passively grants 16%/24%/32%/40% Magic Resistance. When activated, it creates a shell for 1.2 seconds that reflects targeted spells back at the caster. Break disables the passive magic resistance. The reflected spell amp is 0. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Anti-Mage ability E Counterspell active passive magic resist 16 24 32 40 reflect spell 1.2s Break disables

display_text: Persecutor is a passive ability. Attacks slow enemies based on missing mana, from 12.5%/15%/17.5%/20% at 50% mana down to 25%/30%/35%/40% at 0% mana. The slow lasts 0.75 seconds. No effect if the enemy is above 50% mana. Illusions apply the full effect.
search_text: Anti-Mage passive Persecutor slow missing mana 12.5 15 17.5 20 min 25 30 35 40 max 0.75s duration illusion full effect

display_text: Mana Void is a point-target AoE ultimate. Deals 0.8/0.95/1.1 damage per point of mana missing from the primary target to all enemies in a 500 radius. The main target is mini-stunned for 0.3 seconds. Cooldown is 70 seconds. Damage calculation is based on the primary target's missing mana. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Anti-Mage ultimate R Mana Void damage 0.8 0.95 1.1 per missing mana AoE 500 radius mini-stun 0.3s cooldown 70

display_text: Against heroes with high mana pools (e.g., Medusa, Storm Spirit), Mana Break and Mana Void are extremely effective. Focus on burning their mana first. Magebane's Mirror Facet is powerful here, as blocking their spells also burns their large mana pool.
search_text: Anti-Mage scenario high mana pool Medusa Storm Spirit Mana Break Mana Void effective Magebane's Mirror

display_text: When playing from behind, focus on split-pushing with Blink and Manta illusions. Avoid fights. Use Blink to escape ganks. Farm the enemy jungle if your own is unsafe. Your goal is to reach critical items (Manta, Basher) to become relevant.
search_text: Anti-Mage scenario behind losing split push Blink Manta illusion farm avoid fight escape

display_text: Against heavy magical burst lineups, prioritize leveling Counterspell early. The Magebane's Mirror Facet can turn the tables by punishing enemies for casting on you. Consider an early Hood of Defiance before or after Battle Fury.
search_text: Anti-Mage scenario magic burst lineup level Counterspell Magebane's Mirror Hood of Defiance Battle Fury

display_text: At Level 10, choose between +3 Health Regen or +200 Mana Void Radius. +3 HP Regen improves lane sustainability and jungle efficiency when taking damage. +200 Mana Void Radius is situational, better for teamfight impact if you are fighting early.
search_text: Anti-Mage talent level 10 left +3 Health Regen sustain right +200 Mana Void radius teamfight

display_text: At Level 15, choose between +1% Max Mana Mana Burn or +9%/18% Persecutor Min/Max Movement Slow. +1% Max Mana Burn significantly increases damage against high-mana heroes like Medusa. The Persecutor slow improves chase and lockdown, especially against elusive heroes.
search_text: Anti-Mage talent level 15 left +1 Max Mana Burn damage high mana right Persecutor slow 9 18 chase lockdown

display_text: At Level 20, choose between +0.2 Mana Void Damage Multiplier or +0.8s Mana Void Stun. The damage multiplier increases your burst potential against mana-dependent cores. The longer stun provides more control, which can be crucial for locking down key targets.
search_text: Anti-Mage talent level 20 left +0.2 Mana Void damage multiplier burst right +0.8s Mana Void stun duration control

display_text: At Level 25, choose between +200 Blink Cast Range or -50s Mana Void Cooldown. Increased Blink range dramatically improves positioning, chase, and escape safety. The reduced Mana Void cooldown (to 20s) allows you to use it in every engagement, even for pick-offs.
search_text: Anti-Mage talent level 25 left +200 Blink cast range positioning escape right -50s Mana Void cooldown 20s frequent use

display_text: Axe is a melee strength hero with 25 base strength, 20 agility, and 18 intelligence. His base attack time is 1.7, which is average for melee cores. This stat line provides him with high starting health and decent armor, making him naturally tanky for the laning stage.
search_text: Axe stats melee strength hero 25 strength 20 agility 18 intelligence 1.7 attack rate base armor health

display_text: Axe has two Facets. 'One Man Army' is the default Facet. The 'Call Out' Facet modifies Berserker's Call to grant attacking enemies more attack speed while giving Axe more armor for the duration. This changes the dynamic of the spell, making enemies hit him harder but also rewarding him with greater survivability.
search_text: Axe facets One Man Army default Call Out Berserker's Call gives enemies attack speed gives Axe more armor

display_text: In the laning phase, Axe excels in the offlane. Use Battle Hunger (12/18/24/30 DPS) to harass and secure last hits from a distance. Max Counter Helix first (100/120/140/160 pure damage) to trade aggressively when enemies attack you, as it procs every 7/6/5/4 attacks. Your high base strength makes you difficult to kill. Start with a point in Counter Helix for early trades.
search_text: Axe laning offlane Battle Hunger 12 18 24 30 damage per second harass Counter Helix 100 120 140 160 pure damage procs every 7 6 5 4 attacks trade

display_text: Axe's major power spikes are at level 6 (Culling Blade kill potential and reset), level 12 (maxed Counter Helix and level 2 ultimate), level 18 (level 3 ultimate), and level 20/25 talents. Key item spikes are Blink Dagger for initiation, Blade Mail for damage return during Berserker's Call, and Vanguard/Hood for survivability.
search_text: Axe power spikes level 6 Culling Blade reset level 12 max Counter Helix level 18 ultimate talents 20 25 Blink Dagger Blade Mail Vanguard Hood

display_text: Axe is hard countered by heroes with Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Hoodwink) which disables Counter Helix and his armor talents. High magic burst damage can kill him before he can cast Berserker's Call. Heroes with illusions or summons can trigger Counter Helix without putting their main hero at risk, wasting its cooldown. Debuff dispels can remove Battle Hunger.
search_text: Axe counters break Silver Edge disables Counter Helix magic burst damage illusions summons trigger helix waste cooldown dispel Battle Hunger

display_text: Axe's core build is Blink Dagger and Blade Mail. Blink Dagger is non-negotiable for initiating with Berserker's Call. Blade Mail's damage return synergizes perfectly with the forced attacks from Berserker's Call, often securing kills. These two items are the absolute minimum for Axe to function in mid-game fights.
search_text: Axe core items Blink Dagger initiation Blade Mail damage return synergy Berserker's Call forced attacks

display_text: In teamfights, Axe's role is to initiate with Blink Dagger + Berserker's Call, taunting multiple enemies in a 315 radius for 2.1/2.4/2.7/3.0 seconds while gaining 12/13/14/15 bonus armor. Activate Blade Mail immediately after. He then uses Culling Blade (275/375/475 pure damage) to execute low-health heroes, resetting its cooldown and granting allies 20/25/30 bonus move speed and 10/15/20 bonus armor for 6 seconds.
search_text: Axe teamfight initiator Blink Dagger Berserker's Call 315 radius taunt 3 seconds 15 armor Blade Mail Culling Blade 475 pure damage execute reset speed armor buff

display_text: Berserker's Call (Q) taunts all nearby enemy units within 315 radius for 2.1/2.4/2.7/3.0 seconds, forcing them to attack Axe. For the duration, Axe gains 12/13/14/15 bonus armor. This ability pierces spell immunity (BKB).
search_text: Axe Q Berserker's Call taunt 315 radius 3.0 seconds duration 15 bonus armor pierces BKB

display_text: Battle Hunger (W) enrages a single enemy, dealing 12/18/24/30 damage per second for 12 seconds and applying a 18/22/26/30% slow as long as they are facing away from Axe. The debuff is removed if the affected unit kills another unit (including buildings). It does NOT pierce spell immunity (BKB).
search_text: Axe W Battle Hunger damage per second 12 18 24 30 12 second duration 30% slow facing away dispel on kill building does not pierce BKB

display_text: Counter Helix (E) is a passive ability. After Axe is attacked 7/6/5/4 times, he performs a helix counter attack, dealing 100/120/140/160 pure damage to all enemies within a 275 radius. The counter attack has a 0.3 second cooldown between procs. This ability does NOT pierce spell immunity (BKB).
search_text: Axe E Counter Helix passive 7 6 5 4 attack trigger 160 pure damage 275 radius 0.3 sec cooldown does not pierce BKB

display_text: Culling Blade (R) is an ultimate ability that deals 275/375/475 pure damage to a single enemy. If this damage kills an enemy hero, the cooldown is reset, and all nearby allied units gain 20/25/30 bonus movement speed and 10/15/20 bonus armor for 6 seconds. The ability has a 900 travel speed for its AoE buff. It pierces spell immunity (BKB).
search_text: Axe R ultimate Culling Blade 475 pure damage execute kill reset cooldown buff allies 30 speed 20 armor 6 seconds pierces BKB

display_text: Axe's Coat of Blood (D) is an innate ability. Each time Axe kills an enemy Hero, he permanently gains 0.2/0.3/0.4/0.5 bonus armor per stack. Killing a hero with Culling Blade grants 3x the stack amount (0.6/0.9/1.2/1.5 armor). This provides Axe with significant late-game scaling armor.
search_text: Axe D innate Coat of Blood 0.2 0.3 0.4 0.5 armor per hero kill Culling Blade kill gives 3x 1.5 armor scaling

display_text: When playing against illusion or summon-heavy lineups (e.g., Chaos Knight, Nature's Prophet), Counter Helix will trigger constantly on the illusions, often wasting its cooldown and reducing your effective damage output in fights. Prioritize items like Crimson Guard or Shiva's Guard for AoE mitigation, and focus on using Culling Blade to delete the real hero quickly.
search_text: Axe vs illusions summons Chaos Knight Nature's Prophet Counter Helix wasted cooldown build Crimson Guard Shiva's Guard focus Culling Blade real hero

display_text: When playing from behind without a Blink Dagger, Axe's impact plummets. In this scenario, focus on farming safely with Battle Hunger and Counter Helix stacks from jungle camps. Build cheaper survival items like Vanguard and Hood of Defiance to become a frontline tank. Use Berserker's Call defensively to protect your core if enemies dive your tower.
search_text: Axe behind no Blink Dagger farm jungle Battle Hunger Counter Helix Vanguard Hood of Defiance tank defensive Berserker's Call

display_text: Against high physical damage lineups, Axe's natural tankiness from armor and Berserker's Call shines. Prioritize items that amplify this, such as Assault Cuirass and Shiva's Guard. The 'Call Out' Facet becomes particularly strong here, as the extra armor from the modified Berserker's Call further negates the enemy's physical attack speed bonus.
search_text: Axe vs high physical damage armor Berserker's Call Assault Cuirass Shiva's Guard Call Out Facet extra armor negates enemy attack speed

display_text: Against heavy magic damage and disables, Axe can be burst down before he can act. In these games, a fast Hood of Defiance into Pipe of Insight is critical. Consider a Black King Bar (BKB) after Blink Dagger to ensure you can get your Berserker's Call off without being interrupted by stuns or silences.
search_text: Axe vs magic burst disables Hood of Defiance Pipe of Insight Black King Bar BKB ensure Berserker's Call initiation

display_text: When your team lacks lockdown, Axe's Berserker's Call becomes the primary initiation tool. Maximize its effectiveness by getting a Blink Dagger as fast as possible and consider an Aghanim's Scepter later to apply Battle Hunger in an AoE after a Call, providing additional soft control and damage.
search_text: Axe team lacks lockdown Berserker's Call primary initiation fast Blink Dagger Aghanim's Scepter AoE Battle Hunger control

display_text: At Level 10, choose between +3s Culling Blade Kill Buff Bonus Duration and +8% Movement Speed per active Battle Hunger. The duration talent makes the post-execute speed and armor buff last 9 seconds, great for prolonged chases. The movement speed talent provides up to 40% extra speed with 5 Battle Hungers, enhancing mobility and chasing power in fights where you can't get a quick kill.
search_text: Axe talent level 10 left +3s Culling Blade buff duration 9 seconds right +8% Movement Speed per Battle Hunger 40% speed chase

display_text: At Level 15, choose between +10 Battle Hunger Damage Per Second and +10 Berserker's Call Armor. The Battle Hunger talent increases its DPS to 40 at max level, improving your harassment and wave clear. The Berserker's Call armor talent gives you a total of 25 bonus armor during the taunt, making you incredibly durable against physical damage during your initiation. Pick based on whether you need more damage/mobility or survivability.
search_text: Axe talent level 15 left +10 Battle Hunger DPS 40 damage right +10 Berserker's Call Armor 25 total armor survivability

display_text: At Level 20, choose between +15 Strength and +40 Counter Helix Damage. The Strength talent provides 285 health and some damage, adding to overall tankiness. The Counter Helix talent increases its pure damage to 200, significantly boosting your AoE damage output in fights, especially against spell immune targets. This is a classic choice between survivability and damage.
search_text: Axe talent level 20 left +15 Strength 285 health right +40 Counter Helix Damage 200 pure damage AoE

display_text: At Level 25, choose between +85 Berserker's Call AoE and +150 Culling Blade Damage. The AoE talent increases Berserker's Call radius from 315 to 400, allowing you to taunt significantly more enemies in a fight. The damage talent increases Culling Blade's pure damage to 625, making it a much more reliable execution tool against tanky heroes and increasing your kill threshold dramatically.
search_text: Axe talent level 25 left +85 Berserker's Call AoE 400 radius right +150 Culling Blade Damage 625 pure damage execute

display_text: Bane is a ranged intelligence hero. He has balanced attribute gains of 23 each. His attack time of 1.7 is relatively slow. His attack type is Ranged.
search_text: Bane base stats ranged int hero attributes strength 23 agility 23 intelligence 23 attack rate 1.7

display_text: Bane's Facet 5 'Dream Stalker' gives him increased attack speed when attacking Nightmared units, enhancing his solo kill potential. Facet 6 'Sleepwalk' makes Nightmared units walk in Bane's chosen direction, enabling powerful repositioning for setting up combos or saving allies by moving enemies away. Dream Stalker suits a core/mid Bane for solo pick-offs, while Sleepwalk is powerful for a support Bane to control teamfight positioning.
search_text: Bane Facet choice Dream Stalker Sleepwalk Nightmare attack speed sleepwalk direction

display_text: In lane, Bane's high base damage from even stats and Brain Sap's 90/160/230/300 pure damage/heal make him a strong harasser and sustain hero. Use Enfeeble (30 cast reduction, -55/60/65/70 damage) on the enemy carry to ruin their last hitting. Nightmare is a versatile tool for securing kills by setting up for allies, or for saving yourself or your lane partner from ganks due to its 1.0s invulnerability period. Prioritize Brain Sap for sustain and kill threat.
search_text: Bane laning phase Brain Sap heal damage Enfeeble attack damage reduction Nightmare setup save invulnerability 1s

display_text: Power Spike 1 (Level 6): Fiend's Grip provides a long, piercing disable (4.75/5.25/5.75s channel) for solo kills or key target lockdown. Power Spike 2 (Level 12): Talent choice (+13 Enfeeble DPS or +20% Enfeeble Cast Range Reduction) enhances his control. Power Spike 3 (Level 18): Fiend's Grip reaches max level. Power Spike 4 (Level 20): +275 Brain Sap Damage/Heal talent massively boosts his burst and survivability. Power Spike 5 (Level 25): +3s Fiend's Grip Duration talent makes his ultimate a near-guaranteed kill on any single target.
search_text: Bane power spikes level 6 Fiend's Grip level 12 talents level 18 ult level 20 Brain Sap talent level 25 Fiend's Grip talent

display_text: Bane is countered by heroes who can save his target (Abaddon, Oracle, Dazzle), heroes with AoE damage that can accidentally break his Nightmare setup, heroes with Lotus Orb to reflect his single-target spells, and heroes with strong dispels (Legion Commander Press the Attack). His channeled ultimate, Fiend's Grip, is interrupted by any stun or silence. High mobility heroes can avoid his short-range spells. Bane's abilities are all single-target, making him weak against illusion heroes like Chaos Knight or Phantom Lancer.
search_text: Counters Bane Lotus Orb dispel save AOE damage break Nightmare channel interrupt stun silence illusion heroes

display_text: Bane's core build consists of: 1) Tranquil Boots for mobility and sustain as he doesn't need mana boots. 2) Aether Lens to increase the cast range of all his crucial spells (Brain Sap, Nightmare, Fiend's Grip). 3) Force Staff or Glimmer Cape, depending on the enemy lineup, for positioning, saving allies, or saving himself while channeling Fiend's Grip.
search_text: Bane core items Tranquil Boots Aether Lens Force Staff Glimmer Cape

display_text: In teamfights, Bane's primary role is to instantly disable the most dangerous enemy core or initiator. Use Fiend's Grip to take a key hero out of the fight entirely. Use Nightmare to disable another enemy or to save an ally. Use Enfeeble on the enemy's physical damage dealer to reduce their impact. Position at the edge of the fight to avoid being interrupted during Fiend's Grip. He is a backline controller and single-target shutdown specialist.
search_text: Bane teamfight role disable key target Fiend's Grip channel Nightmare save Enfeeble damage reduction backline controller

display_text: ABILITY: Enfeeble. Deals damage every second and lowers the enemy's total attack damage and cast range. Deals 12/18/24/30 damage per second for 9 seconds. Reduces the target's total attack damage by 55/60/65/70. Reduces the target's cast range by 30. This spell is not dispellable.
search_text: Enfeeble Bane ability Q damage per second 12 18 24 30 duration 9 attack damage reduction 55 60 65 70 cast range reduction 30

display_text: ABILITY: Brain Sap. Feasts on the vital energies of an enemy unit, healing Bane and dealing damage. Deals 90/160/230/300 pure damage to the target and heals Bane for the same amount. Has a cast range of 625. Cooldown is 17/15/13/11 seconds.
search_text: Brain Sap Bane ability W pure damage heal 90 160 230 300 cast range 625 cooldown 17 15 13 11

display_text: ABILITY: Nightmare. Puts the target enemy or friendly Hero to sleep. Sleeping units are awakened when damaged. If the target was directly attacked, the Nightmare passes to the attacking unit. Bane can attack and damage Nightmared targets freely. Cooldown is 25/22/19/16 seconds. The target is invulnerable for the first 1.0 second. Bane can wake himself from Nightmare. On cast, it is replaced with Nightmare End: Ends all ongoing Nightmares on any affected units.
search_text: Nightmare Bane ability E sleep disable invulnerable 1s transfer on attack cooldown 25 22 19 16 Nightmare End

display_text: ABILITY: Fiend's Grip. CHANNELED - Grips an enemy unit, disabling it and causing heavy damage over time, while stealing mana every 0.5 seconds based on the unit's maximum mana. Deals 70/110/150 damage per tick every 0.5 seconds. Drains 5% of the target's max mana per tick (every 0.5s). Channels for 4.75/5.25/5.75 seconds. Cooldown is 120/110/100 seconds. This ability pierces spell immunity (BKB).
search_text: Fiend's Grip Bane ultimate R channel disable damage 70 110 150 per 0.5s mana drain 5% max mana per tick duration 4.75 5.25 5.75 cooldown 120 110 100 pierces BKB

display_text: SCENARIO: Versus a Heavy Physical Damage Carry. Use Enfeeble early and often on heroes like Phantom Assassin or Troll Warlord to reduce their attack damage by 55/60/65/70 for 9 seconds. This drastically lowers their teamfight damage output. In a duel, open with Enfeeble before using your other disables to maximize the damage reduction time.
search_text: Bane vs physical damage carry Enfeeble attack damage reduction 55 60 65 70 PA Troll Warlord

display_text: SCENARIO: Playing from Behind. As a support Bane, your impact diminishes. Focus on defensive Nightmare saves for your farming cores to protect them from ganks. Build cheap utility items like Force Staff and Glimmer Cape to save allies. Use Fiend's Grip defensively to stop an enemy diver on your most important hero, even if you die for it.
search_text: Bane playing from behind support defensive Nightmare save Force Staff Glimmer Cape Fiend's Grip defensive

display_text: SCENARIO: Securing Solo Kills (Core Bane). With the Dream Stalker facet, your combo is: Nightmare (self-damage to wake, granting you attack speed), immediately attack, then Brain Sap, then Enfeeble, and continue attacking. This can kill many heroes from full health once you have a few levels. Always carry a TP scroll to punish overextended lanes.
search_text: Bane solo kill combo Dream Stalker facet Nightmare attack speed Brain Sap Enfeeble

display_text: SCENARIO: Against Illusion Heroes (PL, Chaos Knight). Your single-target spells are weak here. Prioritize Aghanim's Shard to make Brain Sap heal secondary targets. Use Enfeeble on the real hero if you can identify it. Your main value is using Fiend's Grip on the real hero during a fight to lock them down, but be aware illusions can break your Nightmare setup easily.
search_text: Bane vs illusion heroes Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight single target weak Aghanim Shard Brain Sap AoE heal

display_text: LEVEL 10 TALENTS. Left: +125 Brain Sap Cast Range. Right: +20% Enfeeble Cast Range Reduction. Choose +125 Brain Sap Range for safer harassment and initiation without getting as close. This is generally the safer, more reliable choice for support Bane. Choose +20% Enfeeble Range Reduction (making it -36 cast range reduction) if you are against a specific spellcasting core (like a Puck or Storm Spirit) where further reducing their effective range is crucial.
search_text: Bane level 10 talent left +125 Brain Sap cast range right +20% Enfeeble cast range reduction 36

display_text: LEVEL 15 TALENTS. Left: +13 Enfeeble Damage Per Second. Right: -3s Nightmare Cooldown. Choose +13 Enfeeble DPS to increase your overall damage output and lane pressure, making the spell deal 25/31/37/43 DPS. This is good for a core Bane or when you need more damage. Choose -3s Nightmare Cooldown to have more frequent disables and saves (cooldown becomes 13/10/7/4). This is excellent for a support Bane focused on control and saving allies.
search_text: Bane level 15 talent left +13 Enfeeble DPS 25 31 37 43 right -3s Nightmare cooldown 13 10 7 4

display_text: LEVEL 20 TALENTS. Left: +5% Fiend's Grip Max Mana Drain. Right: +30 Movement Speed. Choose +5% Fiend's Grip Mana Drain to increase the mana drain to 10% of max mana per tick, which is situationally powerful against mana-dependent heroes like Medusa or Wraith King. Choose +30 Movement Speed for overall better positioning, chasing, escaping, and getting your spells off. The movespeed is almost always the better, more reliable choice.
search_text: Bane level 20 talent left +5% Fiend's Grip mana drain 10% right +30 movement speed

display_text: LEVEL 25 TALENTS. Left: +275 Brain Sap Damage/Heal. Right: +3s Fiend's Grip Duration. Choose +275 Brain Sap Damage/Heal to make Brain Sap deal and heal for 365/435/505/575. This turns Brain Sap into a massive nuke and self-heal on a low cooldown, significantly boosting your survivability and burst. Choose +3s Fiend's Grip Duration to increase the channel to 7.75/8.25/8.75s, making it an almost guaranteed kill on any single target and an incredibly long BKB-piercing disable. Both are extremely powerful; choose based on whether you need more personal power (Brain Sap) or more control (Grip).
search_text: Bane level 25 talent left +275 Brain Sap damage heal 365 435 505 575 right +3s Fiend's Grip duration 7.75 8.25 8.75

display_text: Bloodseeker is a melee hero with average starting stats: 24 Strength, 24 Agility, and 17 Intelligence. His base attack time is 1.7, which is standard. Being melee, he requires careful positioning to farm and harass in lane. His Agility gain provides natural armor and attack speed scaling.
search_text: Bloodseeker melee hero stats agility 24 strength 24 int 17 BAT 1.7

display_text: Arterial Spray: Ruptured units are pushed when attacked by Bloodseeker. This enhances chase and repositioning during his ultimate, synergizing with the movement damage mechanic. Bloodrush: Bloodseeker can supercharge the movement speed from Thirst temporarily. This is excellent for high-tempo ganking and initiation. Old Blood: Bloodrage silences the target and can be applied to enemies. Bloodrage increases base attack damage instead of spell damage. This fundamentally changes his role to a physical damage dealer and anti-caster, losing the spell damage amplification for allies.
search_text: Bloodseeker facets arterial spray bloodrush old blood rupture push supercharge thirst silence enemy base attack damage

display_text: In lane, leverage Sanguivore to sustain by last-hitting. It heals for 30 health plus 1.5% of the creep's max health. Use Blood Rite carefully due to its 2.6 second delay; it's best for zoning, securing ranged creeps, or setting up after a slow. Early levels in Thirst provide up to 40 bonus movement speed to chase low-health enemies. Rush boots to capitalize on Thirst and maintain lane presence with Sanguivore.
search_text: Bloodseeker laning sanguivore heal 30 + 1.5% max hp blood rite silence zoning thirst movement speed chase

display_text: Level 6 is a major ganking spike with Rupture, dealing initial damage based on current health (10%) and 35/45/55% damage per 100 units moved. Level 12 with maxed Bloodrage provides 30% spell damage amp. Level 20 talent (+20 Agility) significantly boosts physical damage. Level 25 talent (+18% Max Thirst MS) makes him incredibly fast. Key item spikes include Maelstrom for farm, Blade Mail for survivability, and Black King Bar for uninterrupted spellcasting.

display_text: Crystal Maiden is a Ranged intelligence hero with a slow base attack time of 1.7 seconds (approximately 0.59 attacks per second). display_text: Frozen Expanse: Freezing Field passively increases Crystal Maiden's cast range and area of effect. Choose this for a more traditional, positioning-focused build that enhances the threat and safety of her ultimate. Cold Comfort: Crystal Maiden restores her allies' Mana when casting spells and increases her innate Mana Regeneration Amplification. This facet supports a heavy caster lineup. Glacial Guard: Crystal Nova has increased radius and grants allied heroes a physical damage barrier, which is increased for Crystal Maiden. This is a defensive, teamfight-oriented choice against heavy physical damage. Arcane Overflow: Activating Arcane Aura temporarily increases Crystal Maiden's spell amplification and mana costs, and during the duration, she restores her and her allies' Mana when casting spells. This facet suits an aggressive, spell-spamming playstyle where you can manage the increased mana cost for burst.

display_text: Earthshaker is a Melee Strength hero with 22 base Strength, 12 base Agility, and 18 base Intelligence. His base attack time is 1.7 seconds, which is average for a melee caster. His low base Agility and average starting armor make him relatively fragile in the early laning stage before he can secure levels and items.
search_text: Earthshaker stats melee hero base stats strength agility intelligence attack rate 1.7 armor hp mana

display_text: Earthshaker has three Facets: Tectonic Buildup, Slugger, and Resonating Ridge. Tectonic Buildup increases the Aftershock radius per level of Echo Slam, enhancing his area control and chain-stun potential. Slugger sends killed enemies flying as projectiles, adding wave clear and teamfight poke. Resonating Ridge causes Fissure-stunned heroes to emit an echo, significantly boosting burst damage in fights. Slugger is for farming and sustained pressure, while Resonating Ridge is for aggressive, pick-off oriented playstyles. Tectonic Buildup is the default, reliable choice for teamfight disruption.
search_text: Earthshaker facets choice Tectonic Buildup Aftershock radius increase per Echo Slam level Slugger Enchant Totem kill projectiles Resonating Ridge Fissure stun echo damage playstyle teamfight pickoff

display_text: In the laning phase, Earthshaker is a weak laner due to low armor and damage. Prioritize securing range creep last hits with Fissure. Use Fissure defensively to block gank paths or offensively to set up kills with its 0.8/1.0/1.2/1.4 second stun and 100/160/220/280 damage. Max Fissure first for nuke damage and stun duration. Enchant Totem provides a situational last-hit securing tool with its 100/200/300/400% bonus damage on the next attack. Play safe and focus on reaching level 6 for your first major power spike.
search_text: Earthshaker laning early game weak laner Fissure last hit stun 0.8 1.0 1.2 1.4 damage 100 160 220 280 Enchant Totem secure creep play safe level 6

display_text: Earthshaker's key power spikes are at level 6 (Echo Slam), level 12 (level 2 Echo Slam and maxed Fissure), level 18 (level 3 Echo Slam), and with key item timings like Blink Dagger. Talent levels 10, 15, 20, and 25 offer significant boosts. Aghanim's Scepter (providing Enchant Totem leap) is a major mid-game spike for initiation and mobility.
search_text: Earthshaker power spikes level 6 Echo Slam level 12 level 18 Blink Dagger Aghanims Scepter talent levels 10 15 20 25

display_text: Earthshaker is hard countered by heroes with high mobility (e.g., Queen of Pain, Anti-Mage) that can dodge his Fissure and Echo Slam initiation. Instant disables like Hex prevent him from casting spells after Blinking in. Spell immunity (Black King Bar) negates most of his damage and control, though Fissure's stun pierces BKB. Heroes that can drain mana (e.g., Anti-Mage, Lion) severely limit his ability to combo.
search_text: Earthshaker counters high mobility blink escape dodge Fissure Echo Slam instant hex disable spell immunity BKB mana burn drain

display_text: Earthshaker's core build is Blink Dagger, followed by Aghanim's Scepter. Blink Dagger is non-negotiable for reliable initiation with Fissure or Echo Slam. Aghanim's Scepter upgrades Enchant Totem into a 0.8 second leap, providing unparalleled mobility, initiation range, and escape. A third essential item is often Boots of Travel or Arcane Boots for mana sustain and global presence.
search_text: Earthshaker core items build Blink Dagger Aghanims Scepter Enchant Totem leap initiation mobility Boots of Travel Arcane Boots mana

display_text: In teamfights, Earthshaker's role is to initiate with a multi-hero Fissure or Blink + Echo Slam, dealing massive AOE damage (100/140/180 initial + 70/90/110 per echo) and stunning. Follow up with Enchant Totem attacks and Aftershock procs (65/90/115/140 damage and stun) to chain-disable key targets. Use his abilities to zone and control chokepoints, protecting his backline or trapping enemies.
search_text: Earthshaker teamfight role initiator Blink Echo Slam AOE damage 100 140 180 echo 70 90 110 Fissure stun control Enchant Totem Aftershock chain disable

display_text: Fissure (Q). Slams the ground, creating an impassable ridge of stone. Deals 100/160/220/280 Magic damage and stuns for 0.8/1.0/1.2/1.4 seconds in a 225 radius line. The ridge lasts 6.5/7/7.5/8.0 seconds. Cast range is 1600. Cooldown is 18/17/16/15 seconds. The stun pierces spell immunity (BKB). Creeps will not path around it.
search_text: Fissure Q ability Earthshaker stun 0.8 1.0 1.2 1.4 seconds damage 100 160 220 280 magic impassable terrain wall duration 6.5 7 7.5 8.0 cast range 1600 cooldown 18 17 16 15 pierces BKB

display_text: Enchant Totem (W). Empowers Earthshaker's next attack. Grants 100 bonus attack range and causes the attack to deal 100/200/300/400% of Earthshaker's base damage plus damage from primary attribute as bonus damage. Cooldown is 5.0 seconds at all levels. With Aghanim's Scepter, this ability becomes a leap.
search_text: Enchant Totem W ability Earthshaker next attack bonus damage 100 200 300 400% base damage plus attribute attack range +100 cooldown 5.0 seconds Aghanims Scepter leap

display_text: Aftershock (E). Passive. Whenever Earthshaker casts a spell, he triggers an aftershock. Deals 65/90/115/140 Magic damage and stuns for 0.6/0.9/1.2/1.5 seconds in a 300 radius around him. Does not trigger from item usage.
search_text: Aftershock E passive ability Earthshaker on spell cast damage 65 90 115 140 magic stun 0.6 0.9 1.2 1.5 seconds radius 300 no item trigger

display_text: Echo Slam (R). Ultimate. Shockwaves travel through the ground, damaging enemy units in a 700 radius. Deals 100/140/180 initial Magic damage. Each enemy unit hit causes an echo to damage nearby units in a 700 radius for 70/90/110 Magic damage. Real heroes hit cause two echoes. Cooldown is 130/120/110 seconds.
search_text: Echo Slam R ultimate Earthshaker AOE damage radius 700 initial damage 100 140 180 magic echo damage 70 90 110 per unit hero causes two echoes cooldown 130 120 110 seconds

display_text: Against multiple illusion or summon-based heroes (e.g., Chaos Knight, Phantom Lancer), Echo Slam is devastating as each unit creates echoes. Prioritize Blink and positioning to hit the real hero amidst the army for maximum damage output.
search_text: Earthshaker scenario vs illusions summons Chaos Knight Phantom Lancer Echo Slam multiple units echoes damage Blink Dagger positioning real hero

display_text: When playing from behind without Blink Dagger, use Fissure defensively to cut off chase paths and protect towers. Farm jungle camps by stacking and using Fissure or Enchant Totem with the Slugger facet. Focus on saving for your core Blink Dagger to re-enter the game with a successful initiation.
search_text: Earthshaker scenario playing from behind no Blink Dagger Fissure defensive cut path protect tower farm jungle Slugger facet save gold

display_text: When your team lacks initiation, Earthshaker must be the primary engager. Secure Blink Dagger as fast as possible. Communicate with your team to follow up on your Fissure or Echo Slam initiation. Consider a Force Staff or Aether Lens if you need extra cast range to land key spells from a safer distance.
search_text: Earthshaker scenario primary initiator team lacks engage Blink Dagger fast communication follow up Fissure Echo Slam Force Staff Aether Lens cast range

display_text: Against a BKB-heavy lineup, Fissure remains your most valuable tool as its stun pierces spell immunity. Time your engagements for when key enemy BKBs are on cooldown. Your role shifts to zoning and counter-initiating with Fissure to separate BKB'd cores from their team.
search_text: Earthshaker scenario vs BKB heavy lineup Fissure pierces spell immunity stun timing engagement cooldown zoning counter initiation separate cores

display_text: With Aghanim's Scepter, your playstyle becomes highly mobile. Use the Enchant Totem leap to initiate from fog, escape over cliffs, or reposition for a better Fissure. This allows for more aggressive warding and pick-offs. The leap applies a cleaving attack on landing.
search_text: Earthshaker scenario with Aghanims Scepter Enchant Totem leap mobility initiation fog escape reposition Fissure warding pickoff cleave attack

display_text: At Level 10, choose between +25 Base Damage or +75 Fissure Damage. +25 Base Damage improves last-hitting, Enchant Totem hits, and right-click damage in prolonged fights. +75 Fissure Damage is better if you are struggling to secure kills with your combo or need more burst from a distance. The Base Damage talent synergizes with Enchant Totem.
search_text: Earthshaker talent level 10 left +25 Base Damage right +75 Fissure Damage last hit Enchant Totem synergy burst damage combo kill secure

display_text: At Level 15, choose between +200 Fissure Range or +50 Aftershock Damage. +200 Fissure Range (increasing cast range from 1600 to 1800) is invaluable for safer initiation, catching elusive heroes, and better zoning. +50 Aftershock Damage increases your overall magic damage output in extended fights. Fissure Range is the generally superior and more reliable choice.
search_text: Earthshaker talent level 15 left +200 Fissure Range right +50 Aftershock Damage cast range initiation catch elusive heroes zoning magic damage output

display_text: At Level 20, choose between +50 Echo Damage or +65% Enchant Totem Damage. +50 Echo Damage increases the echo damage per unit from 70/90/110 to 120/140/160, a massive boost to Echo Slam's total output, especially against groups. +65% Enchant Totem Damage significantly increases your single-target burst (effectively making it a 165/265/365/465% damage multiplier). Pick Echo Damage for teamfight dominance; pick Enchant Totem Damage for pick-offs and melting single targets.
search_text: Earthshaker talent level 20 left +50 Echo Damage right +65% Enchant Totem Damage Echo Slam AOE teamfight output single target burst pickoff multiplier

display_text: At Level 25, choose between -50% Echo Slam Cooldown or -1.5s Enchant Totem Cooldown. -50% Echo Slam Cooldown reduces it to 65/60/55 seconds, allowing you to use your ultimate in almost every engagement and for wave clear. -1.5s Enchant Totem Cooldown reduces it to 3.5 seconds, enabling incredible sustained damage and stun lockdown with Aftershock procs. The Echo Slam cooldown talent is typically better for its fight-winning potential and map pressure.
search_text: Earthshaker talent level 25 left -50% Echo Slam Cooldown 65 60 55 seconds right -1.5s Enchant Totem Cooldown 3.5 seconds teamfight frequency wave clear sustained damage lockdown Aftershock

display_text: Juggernaut is a Melee agility hero. Starting attributes are 20 Strength, 32 Agility, and 14 Intelligence. He has a base attack time of 1.4. His high base agility grants him high starting armor and good physical damage, but his movement speed is average. He relies on agility gain for scaling.
search_text: Juggernaut base stats Melee Attack Type BAT 1.4 Strength 20 Agility 32 Intelligence 14 starting armor

display_text: The Bladestorm Facet allows Blade Fury to critically strike based on the level of Blade Dance. This significantly increases magical and physical damage output during Blade Fury, making it a powerful offensive choice for burst damage builds, especially with attack speed items. The +Agility Facet is not defined in this data.
search_text: Juggernaut Facet Bladestorm Blade Fury crit critical strike Blade Dance facet choice

display_text: In the laning phase, use Blade Fury level 1 for trading or securing the first wave. Max Blade Fury first for kill potential and wave clear. Utilize Blade Dance to win right-click trades. Healing Ward provides crucial lane sustain; use it behind you to recover from harassment. Juggernaut has strong kill potential at level 6 with Omnislash.
search_text: Juggernaut laning guide Blade Fury level 1 Blade Dance trade Healing Ward sustain Omnislash level 6 kill

display_text: Power spikes: Level 6 with Omnislash for solo kills. Level 12 for maxed Blade Fury and Blade Dance. Level 18 for max Omnislash. Talent levels 10, 15, 20, and 25. Item timings: Battle Fury for farm acceleration, Manta Style for dispel and illusions, and Aghanim's Scepter/Shard for extra Swiftslash.
search_text: Juggernaut power spike level 6 12 18 talents Battle Fury Manta Style Aghanim Scepter Shard timing

display_text: Juggernaut is countered by instant disables that prevent him from using Blade Fury or Omnislash (e.g., Hex, Fiend's Grip). Ghost Scepter and Ethereal Blade negate his Omnislash damage. Heroes with high physical damage reduction or evasion (e.g., Solar Crest, Halberd) reduce his right-click impact. Break disables his Blade Dance passive.
search_text: counter Juggernaut Hex instant disable Ghost Scepter Ethereal Blade Omnislash evasion Break Blade Dance passive

display_text: Core Build: 1. Battle Fury accelerates farm and provides damage/mana regen. 2. Manta Style dispels silences, provides stats, and synergizes with his illusions during Omnislash. 3. Aghanim's Scepter/Shard for Swiftslash, providing a low-cooldown mini-Omnislash for pickoffs and extra damage in fights.
search_text: Juggernaut core items essential Battle Fury Manta Style Aghanim Scepter Shard Swiftslash

display_text: In teamfights, Juggernaut's role is to initiate with Blade Fury (magic immune) or be the follow-up damage dealer. Use Omnislash on key backline targets. Use Healing Ward to sustain your team during and after the engagement. With items like Skadi or Basher, he can also become a manfighter against enemy cores.
search_text: Juggernaut teamfight role Blade Fury magic immune initiate Omnislash backline Healing Ward sustain

display_text: Blade Fury: Juggernaut spins for 5 seconds, becoming debuff immune, gaining 80% magic resistance, and dealing 80/110/140/170 damage every 0.2 seconds to enemies within a 260 radius. The ability applies a strong dispel at the end. Does NOT pierce BKB. Cooldown: 36/30/24/18. You can use items and attack, but attacks only hit units unaffected by Blade Fury.

display_text: Healing Ward: Summons a ward that heals all nearby allied units for 2%/3%/4%/5% of their max health per second. The ward lasts 18/20/22/24 seconds, moves at 325 speed, and has 1 hit to kill. It has a 400 radius aura. Cooldown: 60 seconds.

display_text: Blade Dance: Juggernaut's passive grants a 35% chance to critically strike for 130%/150%/170%/190% damage. The talent can grant lifesteal.

display_text: Swiftslash: This is the Aghanim's Scepter/Shard upgrade. It performs a short Omnislash for 1 second.

display_text: Duelist: Juggernaut deals 12% more damage to targets facing him. This damage bonus always applies during Omnislash. The front angle is 55 degrees.

display_text: Omnislash: Juggernaut leaps 450 units towards a target, then slashes the target and nearby enemies within 425 radius for 3/3.25/3.5 seconds at 1.4x attack rate, gaining 40 bonus attack speed and 25/30/35 bonus damage per slash. He is invulnerable for the duration. Applies a basic dispel on cast. Cooldown: 60 seconds.

display_text: Scenario: Vs High Armor/High Physical EHP. In this case, Blade Fury is your primary damage source. Prioritize magic damage items like Mjollnir or items that reduce armor (Desolator, Assault Cuirass). The Bladestorm Facet is even more valuable here.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Focus on farming with Battle Fury and split-pushing with Healing Ward for sustain. Avoid fights until key items like Manta Style are completed. Use Omnislash defensively to kill diving heroes or to escape.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Heavy Disables (Bane, Batrider). Itemize for survivability and self-dispel. Buy Manta Style to dispel silences, Linken's Sphere to block single-target disables, or Black King Bar to ensure you can cast Blade Fury or Omnislash.

display_text: Scenario: Need to End Game Early. Skip Battle Fury. Build early fighting items like Diffusal Blade, Sange and Yasha, or Aghanim's Shard. Max Blade Fury and Healing Ward for teamfight presence. Group with your team and take objectives with Healing Ward.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Illusion Heroes (Phantom Lancer, Chaos Knight). Build Battle Fury for cleave and consider Mjollnir for static charge procs. Your Omnislash will bounce between illusions, reducing its effectiveness on the real hero.

Choose the 40% lifesteal on crits to become an unkillable manfighter, pairing extremely well with high attack speed and damage items.

display_text: Mirana is a Ranged Agility hero with 20 base Strength, 26 base Agility, and 22 base Intelligence. This gives her decent starting health and very good starting armor, but she has a relatively slow base attack time of 1.7 seconds. Her attack type is Ranged.
search_text: Mirana stats Ranged Agility hero base Strength 20 base Agility 26 base Intelligence 22 armor attack time 1.7

display_text: Facet Choice: Moonlight Shadow enhances Mirana's existing ultimate, making her team better at initiating and escaping with a lower fade delay (1.5s at max level). Solar Flare replaces the invisibility with a powerful damage and attack speed buff for her team over time, turning Moonlight Shadow into an aggressive teamfight aura. Starstruck makes Starstorm's second meteor significantly more impactful by blinding enemies, which is excellent against right-click carries. Leaps and Bounds adds a root on landing, giving Leap utility for lockdown and chasing. Pick Moonlight Shadow for a classic, utility-based playstyle. Pick Solar Flare if your team wants to stand and fight. Pick Starstruck to counter physical damage heroes. Pick Leaps and Bounds if your team needs more control.
search_text: Mirana Facet Moonlight Shadow Solar Flare Starstruck Leaps and Bounds root blind teamfight initiation utility damage

display_text: Laning Phase: Prioritize Sacred Arrow to secure last hits on ranged creeps and potentially harass or kill the enemy. Your high base Agility gives good damage and armor for trading. Use Leap primarily as an escape from ganks or to reposition. Coordinate with supports for long-range Arrow setups from the fog. Avoid using Starstorm early unless you can guarantee the double hit for maximum damage, as its mana cost is high relative to your early mana pool. Focus on securing farm and maintaining lane equilibrium.
search_text: Mirana laning Sacred Arrow last hit harass Leap escape Starstorm mana cost early game

display_text: Power Spikes: Level 6 provides team-wide invisibility for ganks and saves. Level 12 with a key item like Dragon Lance or Maelstrom significantly increases your farming and fighting potential. Level 18 gives a stronger ultimate. The Level 20 talent (+35 Base Damage) is a massive damage spike. Level 25 talents are game-changing: Multishot Arrows for area control or a drastically reduced Moonlight Shadow cooldown for constant map pressure. Aghanim's Scepter is a major timing, allowing Sacred Arrow to proc Starstorm.
search_text: Mirana power spikes level 6 ultimate level 12 item Dragon Lance Maelstrom level 20 talent base damage level 25 talent multishot arrows cooldown Aghanim's Scepter

display_text: Hard Counters: Heroes with instant, point-and-click disables (e.g., Lion, Shadow Shaman) can lock you down before you can Leap away. High burst magic damage can kill you through Leap's speed buff. True Sight and reveal abilities (e.g., Slardar's Amplify Damage, Bloodseeker's Thirst) completely negate the defensive value of Moonlight Shadow. Silence prevents the use of Leap and Moonlight Shadow for escape. Break disables Selemene's Faithful, reducing your Lotus healing.
search_text: Mirana counters instant disable point and click stun hex silence burst magic damage true sight reveal break Selemene's Faithful

display_text: Core Build: Dragon Lance is essential for increasing Sacred Arrow and Starstorm range, and providing good stats. Maelstrom/Mjollnir is the premier farming and wave-clear item, synergizing with your high attack speed from Leap. Boots of Travel are crucial in the mid to late game to split-push and join fights with Moonlight Shadow.
search_text: Mirana core items Dragon Lance range stats Maelstrom Mjollnir farming wave clear attack speed Leap Boots of Travel split push

display_text: Teamfight Role: Initiator and Backline Damage Dealer. Start fights by landing a long-range Sacred Arrow on a key target. Use Moonlight Shadow to position your team. In the fight, stay at the edge, using Leap to reposition and maintain your attack speed buff. Cast Starstorm on clumped enemies. Your job is to provide long-range disables with Arrow, area magic damage with Starstorm, and sustained physical damage with your right-clicks.
search_text: Mirana teamfight initiator backline damage dealer Sacred Arrow stun Moonlight Shadow positioning Leap reposition Starstorm area damage right-click

display_text: Ability: Starstorm (Q). Calls down a wave of meteors to damage nearby enemy units in a 675 radius for 75/150/225/300 magic damage. The closest enemy unit to Mirana within that radius will be struck a second time for 70% of the damage (52.5/105/157.5/210). Cooldown is 12 seconds at all levels. Does NOT pierce BKB. Notes: Starstorm is not blocked by Linken's Sphere. It cannot hit invisible units. The second meteor will choose a new target if the first one dies before impact.

display_text: Morphling is a ranged agility hero with 550 base attack range, 1.5 base attack time, and average movement speed (285). He has high starting agility (24) and poor starting intelligence (19). His primary attribute is Agility, which provides armor and attack speed.

display_text: Morphling has two Facets: 'Ebb' (Agility Primary) and a Strength-focused Facet (unnamed in data). 'Ebb' makes Waveform also attack enemies in its path. Choose 'Ebb' for a carry/agility damage-dealing playstyle, maximizing Waveform's farming and damage. The Strength Facet is for a tankier, initiator/utility build focused on Adaptive Strike stuns and survivability.
search_text: Morphling Facets Ebb Agility Primary Waveform attacks path Strength tank utility build

display_text: In lane, use Agility Shift to gain last-hit damage and armor. Use Waveform (1250 speed, 200 width) at level 1 or 2 for secure ranged last hits and light harassment. Manage mana carefully due to low intelligence. Against physical harass, shift to Strength. Against magical harass, consider more agility for health regen. Use Adaptive Strike (range 700-1000, scaling damage/stun) situationally after level 2-3.
search_text: Morphling laning last hit agility shift armor mana management Waveform 1250 speed 200 width harass adaptive strike 700-1000 range

display_text: Power spikes: Level 4 (max Attribute Shift for fast stats). Level 6 (Morph, 24s duration with Scepter) for replicate ganks or teamfight initiation. Level 12 (Level 2 Morph) for longer uptime. Level 18 (Level 3 Morph). Level 20 talent choice (+15 Agility vs +35 Strength) dictates carry or tank spike. Level 25 talents (+35 Strength vs -40% Waveform Cooldown). Item spikes: Manta Style, Ethereal Blade, Eye of Skadi, Scythe of Vyse, Aghanim's Scepter for stats and utility.
search_text: Morphling power spike level 4 attribute shift level 6 Morph 24 seconds level 12 level 18 level 20 talent 25 talent Manta Ethereal Blade Skadi Scythe Aghanim's Scepter

display_text: Hard counters include heroes with Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Hoodwink) which disables Attribute Shift, preventing Morphling from adjusting his stats. Instant disables and silences (e.g., Skywrath Mage, Death Prophet) prevent Waveform escape and Morph. High burst damage (e.g., Lina, Lion) can kill him during Morph animation or before he can shift to Strength. Percentage-based damage (e.g., Necrophos Reaper's Scythe) punishes high HP from Strength shift.
search_text: Morphling counters Break Silver Edge Hoodwink disables Attribute Shift silence Waveform escape burst damage Lina Lion percentage damage Necrophos

display_text: Core build is stat-focused. 1) Dragon Lance/Hurricane Pike for safe attack range and stats. 2) Manta Style (agility, illusions, dispel). 3) Eye of Skadi (all stats, tankiness, and slow). Essential for both agility and strength builds to amplify Attribute Shift scaling.
search_text: Morphling core items Dragon Lance Hurricane Pike Manta Style agility illusions dispel Eye of Skadi stats slow

display_text: In teamfights, Morphling's role is flexible. As agility carry, use Waveform to position, then right-click with high agility. Use Replicate to create confusion or escape. As strength initiator, use Waveform to engage, then Adaptive Strike for a long stun (up to 2.4s). Morph into key enemy heroes to steal crucial abilities (e.g., Black Hole, Ravage). He is a high-survivability split-pusher and backline threat.
search_text: Morphling teamfight role agility carry right click Waveform position replicate escape strength initiator adaptive strike stun 2.4s Morph steal abilities split push

display_text: Ability: Waveform (Q). Morphling surges forward 700/800/900/1000 units, damaging enemies in a 200 width path. Deals damage based on his agility plus a base. He is invulnerable during the dash. Cooldown: 21/18/15/12 seconds. Travel speed: 1250. Does NOT pierce BKB (process_procs: 0).
search_text: Waveform Q dash 700 800 900 1000 range 200 width invulnerable cooldown 21 18 15 12 speed 1250 agility damage

display_text: Ability: Adaptive Strike (W). Launches a projectile (1150 speed) that deals 25/50/75/100 base damage plus scaling damage based on Morphling's agility (0.5x to 2.5x multiplier). If agility is 50% higher than strength, uses max multiplier. It also knocks back and stuns the target for 0.5s to 2.4s based on Morphling's strength. If strength is 50% higher than agility, uses max 2.4s stun and 350 knockback distance. Cooldown: 17/14/11/8 seconds. Minimum knockback: 50.
search_text: Adaptive Strike W projectile 1150 speed damage 25 50 75 100 agility multiplier 0.5 2.5 stun 0.5 2.4 seconds knockback 50 350 cooldown 17 14 11 8

display_text: Ability: Attribute Shift (Agility Gain) (E). Toggle ability. Morphling converts 1 Strength point into 1 Agility point every 0.25/0.12/0.08/0.06 seconds (rate of 4/8/12/16 points per second tooltip). Costs 10 mana per point shifted. Can be cast while stunned (castable_while_stunned: 1). Does not convert stats from items.

display_text: Ability: Attribute Shift (Strength Gain) (R). Toggle ability. Morphling converts 1 Agility point into 1 Strength point every 0.25/0.12/0.08/0.06 seconds (rate of 4/8/12/16 points per second tooltip). Costs 10 mana per point shifted. Can be cast while stunned (castable_while_stunned: 1). Does not convert stats from items.
search_text: Attribute Shift Strength Gain R toggle convert Agility to Strength rate 4 8 12 16 per second mana cost 10 can cast while stunned no item stats

display_text: Ability: Morph (Ultimate) (R). Target an enemy hero to gain their basic abilities for 24 seconds. Can be toggled between Morphling's form and the replicated enemy's form for the duration. With Aghanim's Scepter, duration increases and stats are stolen. Does not copy Shard/Scepter abilities unless Morphling owns the item. Does not trigger spell reflect.
search_text: Morph Ultimate R copy enemy hero abilities 24 seconds duration toggle form Aghanim's Scepter steal stats no shard scepter copy unless owned no spell reflect

display_text: Innate Ability: Accumulation. Morphling receives 50% of his stat growth bonuses every half level instead of all at once at level up. Talent Tree bonuses that give All Attributes provide an additional +3 to his primary attribute (Agility with Ebb Facet).
search_text: Accumulation innate stat growth 50 percent half level talents all attributes bonus +3 primary attribute

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Shift heavily into Strength for survivability. Build utility items like Scythe of Vyse for control. Use Replicate to farm dangerously with an illusion. Avoid fights, focus on split-pushing with Waveform escape. Max Attribute Shift first to adapt quickly.
search_text: Morphling behind losing shift Strength tank utility Scythe of Vyse replicate illusion farm split push Waveform escape max attribute shift

display_text: Scenario: Vs High Magic Burst. Shift to Strength before engagement to increase HP pool. Consider buying a Linken's Sphere or Black King Bar to survive initial spells. Use Waveform invulnerability to dodge key projectiles. Morph into a tanky enemy hero for extra survivability.
search_text: Morphling vs magic burst nuke shift Strength HP Linken's Sphere BKB Waveform dodge invulnerable Morph tanky hero

display_text: Scenario: Vs Illusion/Single-Target Heroes. Build Mjollnir and/or Manta Style. Use Agility Shift for high attack speed to clear illusions. Your own Manta illusions benefit from high stats. Use Adaptive Strike (agility build) for high damage against the real hero.
search_text: Morphling vs illusions Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight Mjollnir Manta Style agility shift attack speed clear illusions adaptive strike damage

display_text: Scenario: Need to Initiate. Use Strength Facet. Build Blink Dagger. Shift to high Strength for a long 2.4s Adaptive Strike stun. Blink in, stun key target, then use Waveform to reposition or deal damage. Morph into an initiator enemy hero (e.g., Tidehunter) for a second round of initiation.
search_text: Morphling initiator Strength Facet Blink Dagger high Strength adaptive strike 2.4s stun Waveform reposition Morph copy Tidehunter

display_text: Scenario: Late Game Carry. Use Ebb Facet. Max Agility Shift. Core items: Eye of Skadi, Satanic, Butterfly, Divine Rapier. Use Replicate on a mobile hero for global mobility and escape. Stay at high agility, using Waveform for positioning and invulnerability frames in fights.
search_text: Morphling late game carry Ebb Facet agility shift Eye of Skadi Satanic Butterfly Divine Rapier replicate mobility escape high agility Waveform positioning

display_text: Talent Level 10: Left choice is +15% Magic Resistance. Right choice is +12s Morph Duration. Pick Magic Resistance if facing heavy magic damage lineup. Pick longer Morph duration if you are using Morph aggressively to copy key spells often, or need the replicate for extended split-pushing.
search_text: Morphling talent level 10 left +15% magic resistance right +12 seconds Morph duration magic damage lineup aggressive copy spells split push

display_text: Talent Level 15: Left choice is +250 Waveform Range (making it 1250 max range). Right choice is +75 Attack Range (making it 625). Pick Waveform range for better initiation, escape, and farming speed. Pick Attack Range for safer right-click damage in fights, especially as an agility carry.
search_text: Morphling talent level 15 left +250 Waveform range 1250 right +75 attack range 625 initiation escape farming right click damage

display_text: Talent Level 20: Left choice is -3s Adaptive Strike Cooldown (down to 5s at max level). Right choice is +15 Agility. Pick the cooldown reduction if you are playing a utility/strength build focused on spamming the stun. Pick the agility for pure right-click damage and armor if you are the primary carry.
search_text: Morphling talent level 20 left -3s Adaptive Strike cooldown 5s right +15 Agility utility stun spam strength build carry damage armor

display_text: Talent Level 25: Left choice is -40% Waveform Cooldown (down to 7.2s at max level). Right choice is +35 Strength. Pick Waveform cooldown for insane mobility, farming, and damage output, making you nearly unkillable with invulnerability frames. Pick Strength for massive HP pool (over 600 HP) making you extremely tanky and amplifying your Strength-based Adaptive Strike stun duration.
search_text: Morphling talent level 25 left -40% Waveform cooldown 7.2s right +35 Strength 600 HP mobility farm invulnerability tanky stun duration

display_text: Shadow Fiend is a ranged agility hero with 19 base strength, 25 base agility, and 18 base intelligence. He has an attack rate of 1.6, which is average. His high base agility for a ranged hero gives him decent starting armor and damage. He relies on farming and stacking souls to scale his damage output, making him a classic mid-lane core.
search_text: Shadow Fiend stats 19 strength 25 agility 18 intelligence attack rate 1.6 ranged attack

display_text: Shadow Fiend has two Facets. 'Lasting Presence' enhances his late-game scaling: each hero kill near him increases Presence of the Dark Lord's armor reduction by -1 and permanently increases Necromastery's maximum soul capacity by 1 until he dies. Choose this for a hard carry build focused on right-click damage. 'Shadowmire' adds a 3-second slow to Shadowraze (25% movement slow at all levels), improving his early-game kill threat and lane control. Choose this for a burst magic damage or ganking playstyle.
search_text: Shadow Fiend facets Lasting Presence Shadowmire Shadowraze slow armor reduction Necromastery souls

display_text: In the laning phase, Shadow Fiend aims to secure last hits and denies with his high base damage. Prioritize leveling Shadowraze (Q/W/E) for last-hitting ranged creeps and harassing. His low base health makes him vulnerable to ganks. Use Shadowraze to stack damage amplifiers on the enemy hero. Your goal is to reach level 5-7 with a fast bottle or Wraith Band to maximize farming speed and prepare for the mid-game.
search_text: Shadow Fiend laning last hits Shadowraze harass low health bottle

display_text: Power Spike 1: Level 3-5 with maxed Shadowraze (300 damage per cast) and a value point in Necromastery for damage. Power Spike 2: Level 8 with maxed Necromastery (up to +104 damage from 26 souls). Power Spike 3: Level 12 with talent (likely +30 Shadowraze stack damage). Power Spike 4: Level 18 with level 3 Requiem of Souls. Item spikes are Eul's Scepter for solo kills, Black King Bar for teamfight survivability, and Daedalus for critical damage.
search_text: Shadow Fiend power spikes level 3 level 8 level 12 level 18 Eul's Scepter Black King Bar Daedalus

display_text: Shadow Fiend is hard countered by heroes with Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Hoodwink) which prevents him from gaining new Necromastery souls, though existing souls remain. Instant disables like Hex prevent him from casting Requiem of Souls. High burst physical damage (e.g., Templar Assassin) can kill him through his low health pool. Anti-mage heroes with high magic resistance reduce his Shadowraze and Requiem damage.
search_text: Shadow Fiend counters Break Silver Edge Necromastery Hex instant disable burst physical damage

display_text: Core build essentials: 1) Black King Bar is non-negotiable for surviving teamfights and allowing him to right-click. 2) Satanic provides lifesteal and survivability to man-fight. 3) Eul's Scepter of Divinity is critical for setting up guaranteed Shadowraze combos and providing mana regeneration and dispel.
search_text: Shadow Fiend core items Black King Bar Satanic Eul's Scepter

display_text: In teamfights, Shadow Fiend's role is to deal massive AoE magic damage with Requiem of Souls at the fight's initiation, fearing and reducing the magic resistance of enemies hit. After using Requiem, he transitions to a right-click carry, using his high Necromastery damage and Feast of Souls attack speed bonus to kill priority targets from the edge of the fight. He must be protected as he is squishy.
search_text: Shadow Fiend teamfight role Requiem of Souls AoE magic damage fear right-click carry Necromastery

display_text: Ability: Shadowraze (Close). Shadow Fiend razes the ground directly in front of him (200 range), dealing 90/160/230/300 damage in a 250 radius. Adds a stacking damage amplifier that lasts 7 seconds, causing the target to take 35/50/65/80 bonus damage from subsequent Shadowraze casts per stack. The ability has a 9 second cooldown, but individual casts have a 3 second cooldown between uses. This ability does NOT pierce BKB.

Adds a stacking damage amplifier that lasts 7 seconds, causing the target to take 35/50/65/80 bonus damage from subsequent Shadowraze casts per stack. The ability has a 9 second cooldown, but individual casts have a 3 second cooldown between uses. This ability does NOT pierce BKB.

Adds a stacking damage amplifier that lasts 7 seconds, causing the target to take 35/50/65/80 bonus damage from subsequent Shadowraze casts per stack. The ability has a 9 second cooldown, but individual casts have a 3 second cooldown between uses. This ability does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Ability: Feast of Souls. Shadow Fiend gains 40/55/70/85 bonus attack speed and 5/7/9/11 bonus movement speed for 8 seconds. For the duration, Necromastery's max soul count is increased by 5. After the effect ends, he loses 5 souls. Costs 5 souls to cast. This ability has a 100% cast speed.

display_text: Ability: Presence of the Dark Lord. Passive aura in a 1200 radius. Reduces the armor of nearby enemies by 3/4/5/6. This armor reduction stacks fully with other sources.
search_text: Presence of the Dark Lord passive aura armor reduction -3 -4 -5 -6 radius 1200

display_text: Ability: Requiem of Souls. Shadow Fiend releases captured souls as lines of demonic energy in a 1000 radius. Each soul creates one line. Units hit take damage per line. Any unit damaged is feared and has its movement speed reduced by 20/25/30% and magic resistance reduced by 10% for 0.6 seconds per line hit, up to a maximum of 2.15 seconds. Cooldown is 120/110/100 seconds. Automatically casts on death. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Ability: Necromastery. Passive. Shadow Fiend gains 1/2/3/4 bonus damage per soul from units he kills. Max souls are 20/22/24/26. Hero kills grant 4 souls. On death, he releases 30% of his souls. Denies and neutral creeps also provide souls. Break disables gaining new souls but does not remove existing bonus damage.

display_text: Scenario: Facing multiple high-armor, high-physical damage cores (e.g., Terrorblade, Dragon Knight). Strategy: Prioritize maximizing Presence of the Dark Lord and the Lasting Presence Facet. Build items like Assault Cuirass and Desolator to compound armor reduction. Your physical damage output will be extremely high against these targets.
search_text: Shadow Fiend vs high armor Terrorblade Dragon Knight Presence of the Dark Lord armor reduction Assault Cuirass Desolator

display_text: Scenario: Playing from behind with a poor laning stage. Strategy: Focus on farming jungle camps with Shadowraze to recover. Use the Shadowmire Facet to help secure kills with your team if a gank opportunity arises. Itemize for survivability first (e.g., BKB, Hurricane Pike) before committing to pure damage items. Avoid risky fights until you have key items.
search_text: Shadow Fiend playing from behind farming jungle Shadowraze Shadowmire slow survivability BKB Hurricane Pike

display_text: Scenario: Enemy team has heavy magical burst (e.g., Zeus, Lina). Strategy: The magic resistance reduction from Requiem of Souls is less valuable. Prioritize the magic damage talent (+120 Shadowraze Damage) at level 15. Itemize for magic resistance (e.g., Black King Bar, Mage Slayer, Satanic) and consider a Linken's Sphere to block key initiations.
search_text: Shadow Fiend vs magic burst Zeus Lina Requiem magic resist reduction Shadowraze damage talent BKB Mage Slayer

display_text: Scenario: Your team lacks initiation and you need to start fights. Strategy: Use Blink Dagger to get into the enemy team and cast Requiem of Souls for a massive AoE fear and damage opener. Follow up with your team's spells. The level 25 talent '+0.2s Requiem Fear per line' becomes very powerful for this playstyle.
search_text: Shadow Fiend initiator Blink Dagger Requiem of Souls opener AoE fear talent fear duration

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has multiple heroes with spell immunity (e.g., Juggernaut, Lifestealer). Strategy: Shadowraze and Requiem do not pierce BKB. You must rely on physical damage. Max Necromastery and Presence of the Dark Lord early. Build right-click items like Monkey King Bar, Daedalus, and Satanic. Use Feast of Souls for attack speed during their BKB duration.

Right choice is +30 Feast of Souls Attack Speed, increasing the bonus from 40/55/70/85 to 70/85/100/115. Choose the Shadowraze talent if you are focusing on magic burst. Choose the Feast of Souls talent if you are building for right-click damage or need faster farming.

display_text: Level 15 Talents: Left choice is +2 Presence Armor Reduction, increasing the aura from -3/-4/-5/-6 to -5/-6/-7/-8. Right choice is +120 Shadowraze Damage, increasing the base damage from 90/160/230/300 to 210/280/350/420. Choose the armor reduction talent against high-armor lineups or if your team has strong physical damage. Choose the Shadowraze damage talent for maximum magic burst, especially with Eul's combo.

display_text: Level 20 Talents: Left choice is +2 Damage Per Soul, increasing Necromastery's bonus from 1/2/3/4 to 3/4/5/6 per soul. Right choice is +0.2s Requiem Fear per line, increasing the maximum fear duration from 2.15 seconds to 4.15 seconds (assuming 10 lines). Choose the Damage Per Soul talent for a massive late-game right-click damage increase. Choose the Fear duration talent for incredible teamfight control, especially if you are initiating with Blink+Requiem.

display_text: Level 25 Talents: Left choice is Feast of Souls Grants +30% Cast Speed. Right choice is Shadowraze Applies Attack Damage. The Cast Speed talent allows you to cast Shadowraze and items like Eul's and BKB much faster, crucial for getting your combo off. The Attack Damage talent makes each Shadowraze hit also apply your current attack damage, including crits and modifiers, turning it into a massive physical nuke. Choose Cast Speed for reliability and magic build. Choose Attack Damage for an extreme hybrid damage output, especially with high physical damage items.

display_text: Phantom Lancer is a melee agility hero with a base attack time of 1.7. He has balanced base attributes of 21 Strength, 26 Agility, and 21 Intelligence, giving him solid starting damage and armor from his high agility. As a melee hero, he must close the gap to farm and fight.
search_text: Phantom Lancer stats melee agility hero base attack time 1.7 strength 21 agility 26 intelligence 21

display_text: Phantom Lancer has four Facets. Convergence (Doppelganger spawns more illusions based on missing health) emphasizes survivability and creating chaos in the middle of a fight. Divergence (Juxtapose Illusions spawn randomly and deal more damage but are uncontrollable) is for high-risk, high-reward area damage and split-pushing pressure. Lancelot (Illusions' Spirit Lances deal extra damage) enhances single-target burst. Fractured is a non-functional Facet.
search_text: Phantom Lancer Facets Convergence Divergence Lancelot Fractured Doppelganger missing health Juxtapose uncontrollable illusions Spirit Lance illusion damage

display_text: In the laning phase, use Spirit Lance (10/9/8/7 sec cooldown, 100/160/220/280 magic damage, 3.75s slow) for safe harassment and last hitting. Its 675 range and 1000 speed make it reliable. Early points in Phantom Rush (15/11/7/3 sec cooldown, 20/30/40/50% evasion) provide trading power and chase potential. Use Doppelganger (22/18/14/10 sec cooldown) to disjoint enemy projectiles and dispel debuffs. Focus on farming your core items with your good base damage.
search_text: Phantom Lancer laning phase Spirit Lance harass slow 100 160 220 280 damage Phantom Rush evasion 20 30 40 50 Doppelganger dispel disjoint farm

display_text: Key power spikes: Level 6 unlocks Juxtapose, enabling jungle farming and skirmishes. Level 12 with maxed Phantom Rush and a Diffusal Blade is a major fighting spike. Level 18 with max Juxtapose and talents like +100 Spirit Lance Damage significantly increases burst. Level 20 talent -4s Doppelganger Cooldown drastically improves survivability and mobility. Key item timings are Diffusal Blade (early-mid game), Manta Style (illusion generation), and Heart of Tarrasque (tankiness).
search_text: Phantom Lancer power spikes level 6 Juxtapose level 12 max Phantom Rush Diffusal Blade level 18 level 20 talent Doppelganger cooldown Manta Style Heart

display_text: Phantom Lancer is hard countered by heroes with strong AoE and clear. Earthshaker, Legion Commander, and Sven destroy illusions with their abilities. Heroes with Break (e.g., Shadow Demon, Viper) disable his Juxtapose and Phantom Rush passives, removing his evasion and illusion generation. Instant disables like Hex prevent him from using Doppelganger to escape.
search_text: Phantom Lancer counters Earthshaker Legion Commander Sven AoE clear Break Shadow Demon Viper disable Juxtapose Phantom Rush hex Doppelganger

display_text: The core build for Phantom Lancer is absolutely Diffusal Blade and Manta Style. Diffusal Blade provides agility, mana burn that works on illusions, and a slow. Manta Style creates two strong, controllable illusions that benefit from all his passives, and dispels debuffs. A third essential late-game item is Heart of Tarrasque, granting immense health and regeneration to keep his illusion army alive.
search_text: Phantom Lancer core build Diffusal Blade Manta Style Heart of Tarrasque mana burn illusions dispel health regeneration

display_text: In teamfights, Phantom Lancer's role is to create chaos and overwhelm the enemy with illusions. He uses Doppelganger to reposition and dodge key spells. He focuses a key target with Phantom Rush and Spirit Lance, while his Juxtapose illusions automatically spawn and attack nearby enemies. His goal is to drain enemy mana with Diffusal, soak spells with his illusions, and clean up fights once major AoE cooldowns are used.
search_text: Phantom Lancer teamfight role chaos illusions Doppelganger reposition dodge Phantom Rush Spirit Lance focus target Juxtapose spawn illusions Diffusal Blade mana drain soak spells

display_text: Spirit Lance (Q) sends a lance to an enemy, dealing 100/160/220/280 magic damage and slowing their movement speed by 14/21/28/35% for 3.75 seconds. It also summons an illusion that lasts 3.5/5.0/6.5/8.0 seconds to attack the target. The illusion deals 300% incoming damage and takes 85% reduced outgoing damage. Cooldown is 10/9/8/7 seconds. Illusions within 675 range will mimic the cast animation.
search_text: Spirit Lance Q damage 100 160 220 280 slow 14 21 28 35 duration 3.75 illusion summon 3.5 5.0 6.5 8.0 seconds cooldown 10 9 8 7

display_text: Doppelganger (W) makes Phantom Lancer vanish for 1 second, then reappear at a targeted location within 575 range. Upon reappearing, he and any nearby illusions are moved, and two extra doppelgangers are created. All illusions have their duration extended by 2 seconds. One doppelganger takes normal damage and deals none. The other takes 500% bonus damage and deals 80 less damage. The ability dispels debuffs and has a 22/18/14/10 second cooldown.
search_text: Doppelganger W vanish 1 second reposition 575 range dispel basic dispel two extra illusions extended duration 2 seconds one takes normal damage one takes 500 damage cooldown 22 18 14 10

display_text: Phantom Rush (E) is a passive. When targeting an enemy to attack, Phantom Lancer charges toward them from a distance between 275 and 600/675/750/825 units away, gaining 800 movement speed and 20/30/40/50% evasion. The evasion lasts for the duration of the rush. Cooldown is 15/11/7/3 seconds. His illusions also have this ability.
