display_text: Illusory Armaments (Passive) is an innate ability. Whenever an illusion of Phantom Lancer is created, its outgoing damage cannot go below a fixed value of 18 for 3 seconds. This ensures new illusions always deal a minimum amount of damage.
search_text: Illusory Armaments passive illusion created minimum outgoing damage 18 duration 3 seconds

display_text: Juxtapose (Ultimate, R) is a passive. Phantom Lancer has a 40/45/50% chance on attack to create an illusion. Illusions created from Phantom Lancer last 8 seconds, while illusions created from other illusions last 4 seconds. Illusions from Juxtapose deal 500% incoming damage and take 85% reduced outgoing damage. The maximum number of illusions is 6/8/10. Illusions also have a 9% chance to create further illusions.

display_text: Scenario: Versus High Armor and Evasion. Heroes like Phantom Assassin or Troll Warlord can be problematic. Build a Monkey King Bar to deal with evasion and provide reliable physical damage. Consider a Silver Edge to break passives like Blur or Berserker's Rage and to gank. Your illusions benefit from the MKB's magic damage procs.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When your team is losing, focus on split-pushing with your illusions. Use the Divergence Facet to create uncontrollable illusions that pressure lanes automatically. Itemize for survivability like a Skadi or Butterfly to survive ganks. Avoid 5v5 fights until you have key defensive items like Heart or BKB.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Heavy Magic Burst. Heroes like Zeus or Lina can clear illusions and kill you quickly. Build a Mage Slayer or Pipe of Insight to reduce incoming magic damage. display_text: Pudge is a melee strength hero with a below-average base attack time of 1.7. He has high starting strength at 25, but very low starting agility and armor (11 agility). display_text: Pudge has three Facets. Fresh Meat enhances his Dismember ultimate, making it increase his Strength when damaging heroes, scaling his damage and health. Flayer's Hook changes his Meat Hook to deal bonus damage based on pull distance (while reducing base damage), favoring max-range picks. Rotten Core gives him bonus attack damage per second while Rot is active, enabling a more right-click oriented build with items like Blade Mail or Skadi.

display_text: In lane, Pudge struggles due to low armor and base damage. The primary goal is to secure last hits with Meat Hook (level 1 has 1300 range) on creeps or to threaten pulls on the enemy hero. Avoid trading hits. Use Rot conservatively for securing kills or zoning, as it damages you for 30/60/90/120 DPS. A point in Meat Shield at level 2 or 3 is crucial to mitigate harass. Focus on pulling creeps and stacking for recovery if lane is lost.

display_text: Pudge's power spikes are: Level 6 with Dismember for a reliable kill setup. Level 7 with maxed Meat Hook (360 damage, 12s cooldown). Level 15 talent (+8% Spell Lifesteal) combined with Rot and Dismember for sustain. Level 20 talent (-4s Meat Hook Cooldown) for frequent picks. Level 25 talent (x1.5x Flesh Heap and Meat Shield bonuses) makes him incredibly tanky. Key item spikes are Blink Dagger for initiation, Aghanim's Shard for a 2.75s channeled Dismember, and Heart of Tarrasque for unkillable status.

display_text: Razor has two facets. The Thunderhead facet causes Storm Surge to strike more often and hit all units within Eye of the Storm's area of effect when it is active. This greatly enhances his area damage and control during his ultimate. The Spell Amplification facet simply provides a flat bonus to spell damage.

display_text: In the laning phase, use Plasma Field (Q) to secure ranged creep last hits and harass enemies simultaneously. The ability deals damage twice and the slow/damage increases with distance. Use Static Link (W) at level 2 or 3 to drain enemy core damage for up to 18 seconds, securing last hits and denying theirs. His passive movement speed increase helps with positioning. Prioritize levels in Plasma Field for wave clear and harassment.

display_text: Razor's first power spike is at level 6 with Eye of the Storm, allowing him to pressure towers and win early fights. Level 12 is significant for maxing Static Link. The level 15 talent (+5 Static Link Damage Steal) is a major damage spike. Level 18 is for max ultimate. The level 20 talent (+25% Storm Surge Slow and Damage) and level 25 talents (Second Plasma Field or Static Link Steals Attack Speed) are massive late-game spikes. Key item spikes are Phase Boots for chase, Dragon Lance for range, and BKB for uninterrupted channeling.

Pure burst damage can kill him before he drains significant damage.

Black King Bar is essential to channel Static Link and stand in fights for his ultimate without being disabled.

He should initiate or counter-initiate by using Static Link on the enemy's highest physical damage dealer, significantly reducing their threat. Activate Eye of the Storm to shred armor and deal damage to low-health targets. Use Plasma Field to slow multiple enemies. His job is to absorb spells (proccing Storm Surge), drain damage, and create chaos with his persistent AoE damage.

The reduced strike interval (to 0.6/0.5/0.4) increases ultimate DPS and armor reduction frequency. display_text: Sandshroud: Increases Sand Storm radius and grants permanent invisibility while inside the storm. This facet is better for safer, sustained damage and escape. Sandblast: Applying Sand Storm blinds enemies you attack from within it, reducing their attack accuracy. This is strong against physical damage cores. Pinpoint: Creates a Caustic Finale explosion around every hero hit by Stinger's inner radius (125). This significantly amplifies burst damage in teamfights.

display_text: Sven has two Facets: 'Heavy Plate' and 'Str Scaling'. The Heavy Plate Facet causes his Warcry ability to provide a physical damage barrier to allies. This is a defensive choice, excellent against physical damage-heavy lineups. The Str Scaling Facet replaces his base barrier and armor/movespeed bonus from Warcry with a scaling effect based on his Strength, turning him into a more selfish, hard-carry version. Choose Heavy Plate for a more supportive, teamfight-focused role. Choose Str Scaling if you plan to be the primary damage source and need to scale harder with items.

search_text: Passive Vanquisher innate passive 20% bonus damage vs stunned enemies synergy Storm Hammer burst

display_text: Scenario: Against High Armor/Agi Carries. Enemy heroes like Phantom Assassin or Terrorblade have high armor. In this scenario, Sven must prioritize armor reduction items like Assault Cuirass and consider a Silver Edge for Break to disable their passives. His physical damage can be mitigated, so timing God's Strength with these items is critical.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. If Sven has a bad lane and is underfarmed, he should avoid fights and use Great Cleave to farm jungle stacks efficiently. Items like Mask of Madness or Echo Sabre can accelerate farm. Focus on getting core Blink and BKB before rejoining fights, using Storm Hammer defensively to save allies.

display_text: Scenario: Versus Illusion/Summon Heroes. Against Chaos Knight, Phantom Lancer, or Broodmother, Sven struggles to identify the real hero. In this scenario, Battle Fury becomes a valuable item to quickly clear illusions with cleave. Silver Edge can also be useful to Break key passives like Phantom Lancer's Juxtapose.

display_text: Scenario: As a Position 3 Offlaner. When played as an offlaner, Sven's build shifts to utility. Max Warcry early for lane sustain and team armor. Build items like Vladmir's Offering, Pipe of Insight, or Solar Crest to support the team. Use Storm Hammer for setup and God's Strength to create space as a frontliner, not necessarily as the primary damage dealer.

display_text: Talent Level 10: Choose between +5s Warcry Duration or +10% Vanquisher Bonus Damage. The Warcry talent extends its powerful armor and movespeed buff to 13 seconds, excellent for prolonged fights and team sustain. The Vanquisher talent increases bonus damage vs stunned targets from 20% to 30%, significantly boosting burst potential. Pick Warcry for team utility; pick Vanquisher for personal damage if you can reliably stun targets.

display_text: Talent Level 15: Choose between -12s God's Strength Cooldown or +25% Great Cleave Damage. The cooldown reduction talent lowers God's Strength CD to 98/93/88s, allowing it to be used in almost every fight and for farming. The cleave damage talent increases Great Cleave from 90% to 115%, massively improving farming speed and teamfight AOE damage. Pick CDR for more frequent fight presence; pick Cleave if you need to farm faster or face clustered enemies.

display_text: Talent Level 20: Choose between +20% God's Strength Slow Resistance or +8 Warcry Armor. The slow resistance talent stacks with the innate 30%, giving Sven 50% slow resistance during God's Strength, making him extremely hard to kite. The Warcry armor talent increases its bonus from 14 to 22 armor, a huge defensive boost for your entire team. Pick Slow Resist if you are being kited; pick Armor if the enemy has heavy physical damage.

display_text: Talent Level 25: Choose between +50% God's Strength Damage or +1s Storm Hammer Stun Duration. The damage talent increases God's Strength bonus from 190% to 240%, making Sven's attacks devastating. The stun talent increases Storm Hammer stun from 1.6s to 2.6s, providing incredible lockdown. Pick Damage to delete heroes instantly; pick Stun for crucial control against elusive enemies like Storm Spirit or Anti-Mage.

display_text: Tiny is a Melee Strength hero with exceptionally high base Strength of 30, but 0 base Agility and 17 base Intelligence. His base attack time is slow at 1.7. This makes him very tanky from level 1 with high HP and damage, but very slow and with low armor due to no Agility.
search_text: Tiny stats melee strength hero attack type range armor movement speed HP BAT base stats 30 strength 0 agility 17 intelligence 1.7 attack time

display_text: Tiny has two Facets. 'Crash Landing': Tossed units slow and deal bonus damage to other targets at the Toss destination. This amplifies Tiny's combo damage in teamfights, making him a better initiator/burster. 'Insurmountable': No specific description, but likely provides defensive stats or status resistance, making him a more durable frontliner. Choose Crash Landing for burst damage and combo play. Choose Insurmountable for survivability and sustained right-click.
search_text: Tiny facets Crash Landing Insurmountable toss slow bonus damage teamfight initiator durable frontliner burst survivability

display_text: In the laning phase, use your high base damage and HP to secure last hits and trade. Max Avalanche first for burst damage. Coordinate with a support who has a stun to guarantee Avalanche hits, followed by Toss for a full combo. Use Toss on enemy creeps to harass. Be mindful of your low armor and slow attack speed, so avoid taking repeated right-click harass. Mana management is crucial due to high combo cost.
search_text: Tiny laning phase last hits trade Avalanche burst combo Toss harass mana low armor attack speed

display_text: Key Power Spikes: Level 2 (Avalanche + Toss combo), Level 6 (Grow provides significant damage and armor, enabling kill threat). Level 12 (Maxed Grow, major physical damage increase). Level 15 (+8 Strength talent). Level 20 (Toss Requires No Target talent for huge initiation range). Level 25 (2 Toss Charges talent for massive burst). Item spikes: Blink Dagger (initiation), Echo Sabre (mana and burst), Daedalus (critical damage).
search_text: Tiny power spikes level 2 6 12 15 20 25 talents blink dagger echo sabre daedalus Grow damage

display_text: Tiny is hard countered by Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Doom), which disables the application of new Craggy Exterior stacks, reducing his survivability. Heroes with high mobility can dodge his Avalanche+Toss combo. High armor heroes mitigate his physical damage from Grow and Tree Grab. Instant disables like Hex prevent him from initiating with Blink or using his combo.
search_text: Tiny counters break Silver Edge Doom Craggy Exterior disable mobility dodge Avalanche Toss combo high armor hex

display_text: Core Build: 1. Blink Dagger is essential for initiating the Avalanche + Toss combo. 2. Echo Sabre solves mana issues and provides a double hit, synergizing with his high base damage. 3. Assault Cuirass addresses his low armor and amplifies his team's physical damage.
search_text: Tiny core items Blink Dagger Echo Sabre Assault Cuirass initiation combo mana armor

display_text: In teamfights, Tiny's role is to initiate and burst key targets. Blink in, use Avalanche on multiple enemies, then Toss a key hero back into your team or into the Avalanche area. Use Tree Grab to deal splash damage in melee. Your high HP and Grow armor make you durable, but avoid being kited. Focus on disabling and eliminating the enemy's core damage dealer.

display_text: Vengeful Spirit is a ranged agility hero with a base attack range of 650. She has average base attributes: 20 Strength, 23 Agility, and 19 Intelligence. Her base attack time of 1.7 is standard. As an Agility hero, her armor scales well with Agility gain. Her starting stats are balanced, with slightly higher Agility for better early right-click damage and armor.

display_text: Vengeful Spirit's Avenging Missile facet causes Magic Missile to deal additional damage per creep the target enemy has killed, rewarding aggression against farming cores. The Melee facet fundamentally changes her attack type and range, altering her itemization and positioning in fights. Choose Avenging Missile to amplify pick-off potential against a high-CS core. Choose the Melee facet for a different, more brawler-style playstyle (exact mechanics require in-game verification).
search_text: Vengeful Spirit facets Avenging Missile Magic Missile bonus damage per creep kill Melee facet attack type change

display_text: In the laning phase, Vengeful Spirit uses her high base damage from Agility and Vengeance Aura to secure last hits and harass. Magic Missile (85/170/255/340 damage, 1.2/1.4/1.6/1.8s stun) is a powerful, reliable stun for securing kills or saving allies. At level 2 or 3, a point in Wave of Terror provides armor reduction and vision. Prioritize Magic Missile for kill threat and lane control. Use Wave of Terror to secure ranged creeps from a distance and reduce enemy armor for right-clicks.

display_text: Vengeful Spirit's key power spikes are: Level 6 with Nether Swap (150 damage, position swap) for instant pick-offs. Level 10 with the +150 Nether Swap Damage talent or +100 Magic Missile Cast Range. Level 15 with -3 Wave of Terror Armor or +0.3s Magic Missile Stun. Level 20 with -2s Magic Missile Cooldown or Wave of Terror Steal. Level 25 with +16% Vengeance Aura damage or -18s Nether Swap Cooldown. Item spikes include early Mobility items (Force Staff) and Aghanim's Scepter for an illusion.

display_text: Vengeful Spirit is hard countered by Break mechanics (from Silver Edge, Doom) which disable her Vengeance Aura and Retribution passive. Instant disables (Hex, Batrider's Lasso) prevent her from casting Nether Swap. High burst magic damage can kill her before she can react. Heroes with built-in Spell Immunity (BKB, Lifestealer Rage) are difficult to lock down with Magic Missile. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Core items for Vengeful Spirit are Mobility and Utility. Force Staff or Blink Dagger is essential for positioning Nether Swap. Aghanim's Scepter creates a strong illusion upon death, allowing you to continue fighting. Solar Crest synergizes with Wave of Terror's armor reduction and provides stats. Magic Wand and Boots of Speed are essential early game.

display_text: In teamfights, Vengeful Spirit's role is to initiate or counter-initiate with Nether Swap to catch a key enemy or save an ally. She provides vision and armor reduction with Wave of Terror. She buffs her team's right-click damage with Vengeance Aura. She lands reliable Magic Missile stuns on priority targets. She is often a sacrificial initiator, using her death to apply the Retribution debuff and, with Aghanim's Scepter, create a fighting illusion.

display_text: Windranger is a Ranged agility hero with a base attack time of 1.5. Her starting stats are 18 Strength, 17 Agility, and 18 Intelligence. These balanced initial stats provide decent health and damage for a ranged laner. Her projectile speed and attack animation are critical for last hitting and harassment.
search_text: Windranger base stats attack type ranged 1.5 BAT agility 17 strength 18 int 18

display_text: Windranger has five Facets. Tailwind: Windranger's movement speed cannot be lower than 240 and can reach 600. Focus Fire Facet: Focus Fire rapidly attacks a single target. Whirlwind: Focus Fire randomly attacks enemies within range. Tangled: Shackleshot affects more enemies and grants damage per unit shackled. Killshot: Powershot executes low health enemy heroes. Choose Focus Fire Facet for sustained single-target damage and building destruction. Choose Whirlwind for chaotic teamfight AOE. Choose Tangled for more reliable stuns and AOE burst. Choose Killshot for hero pickoffs and securing kills. The Tailwind mobility is a passive default facet.
search_text: Windranger Facet choice Tailwind movespeed limit 240 min 600 max Focus Fire Facet single target Whirlwind random AOE Tangled Shackleshot more enemies bonus damage Killshot Powershot execute

display_text: Windranger's laning phase is focused on securing last hits with her good attack animation. Use Powershot at level 1 or 2 to secure ranged creeps and harass the enemy from a long distance. At level 2, Shackleshot can be used for kill attempts if an enemy is near a tree or creep. Use Windrun at level 3 or 4 to trade hits favorably and escape ganks due to its 100% evasion and 60% movement speed bonus. Focus on building items like Null Talismans, Boots, and a Magic Wand.
search_text: Windranger laning Powershot harass last hit Shackleshot stun tree creep Windrun evasion 100% movespeed 60% dodge gank

display_text: Windranger's key power spikes are at level 6 with Focus Fire, allowing her to take towers and kill isolated heroes with support. Level 12 (Focus Fire level 2) and level 18 (Focus Fire level 3) are massive damage spikes. Getting a Javelin/Maelstrom early accelerates farm and kill potential. Getting an Aghanim's Scepter or Mjollnir is a major mid-game spike. Level 20 talent (-10% Focus Fire Damage Reduction) dramatically increases single-target DPS. Level 25 talents are game-changing, especially Focus Fire Kills Advance Cooldown by 18s.
search_text: Windranger power spike level 6 Focus Fire 70 cooldown tower damage level 12 level 18 Aghanims Scepter Mjollnir talent 20 -10 damage reduction talent 25 cooldown advance 18s

display_text: Windranger is hard countered by heroes with Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Viper) which disables Windrun's evasion, leaving her vulnerable. Instant disables like Hex prevent her from activating Windrun or Shackleshot. Heavy magic burst damage bypasses Windrun's evasion. High armor heroes can significantly reduce the impact of her physical DPS from Focus Fire. Heroes with mobility spells can dodge her skillshots (Shackleshot, Powershot).

display_text: Windranger's core build is highly flexible but two essential items are Javelin (into Maelstrom/Mjollnir or Gleipnir) for farming and procs during Focus Fire, and Aghanim's Scepter which allows her to move while using Focus Fire and reduces its cooldown to 15 seconds. Boots of Travel are often prioritized for global map presence and split-pushing with Focus Fire.

display_text: In teamfights, Windranger's role is to initiate or follow-up with a multi-hero Shackleshot stun. She then uses Windrun to position aggressively and avoid physical damage. Her primary job is to use Focus Fire on the highest priority target (core hero or building) to burn them down quickly. She can use Powershot from the edges to damage and slow multiple enemies. With the Whirlwind facet, she can become an AOE damage threat instead.

display_text: Scenario: Against High Armor & Evasion Cores (e.g., Terrorblade, Phantom Assassin). In this situation, Windranger's physical damage from Focus Fire is heavily mitigated. The correct adaptation is to build items that provide magical or pure damage procs unaffected by armor, like Mjollnir and MKB. Prioritize using Shackleshot to lock down the evasive target and rely on teammates' magic damage. The Killshot facet can be valuable for execution.
search_text: Windranger vs high armor evasion build Mjollnir MKB magic pure damage proc Shackleshot lock down Killshot execute facet

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind / Split Pushing. When your team is losing teamfights, Windranger's strengths shift to split-pushing with Focus Fire on towers. Build Boots of Travel and Aghanim's Scepter early to maximize mobility and uptime on Focus Fire. Use Powershot to clear waves from a safe distance. Constantly pressure side lanes, forcing the enemy to respond, and use Windrun to escape when they arrive.
search_text: Windranger from behind split push Focus Fire tower Boots Travel Aghanims Scepter Powershot clear wave Windrun escape

display_text: Scenario: Initiating Fights with Shackleshot. Your team needs reliable initiation. Position yourself to land a multi-hero Shackleshot on key targets standing near each other or near trees/creeps. Use Blink Dagger or Force Staff to get the perfect angle. The Tangled facet is ideal here for increasing the shackle count and damage. Follow up immediately with Focus Fire on the most dangerous stunned target.
search_text: Windranger initiator Shackleshot multi-hero stun Blink Dagger Force Staff angle Tangled facet more shackles damage Focus Fire follow up

display_text: Level 10 Talents: Left: +1s Powershot Slow Duration. Right: +4 All Attributes. Choose +1s Powershot Slow Duration if you are playing a utility/poke role, as it increases the slow from 3s to 4s, enhancing chase and escape. Choose +4 All Attributes for a more well-rounded boost to health, damage, armor, and mana, which is generally better for a core Windranger focusing on survivability and right-clicks.
search_text: Windranger talent level 10 left +1s Powershot Slow Duration right +4 All Attributes utility slow stats

display_text: Level 15 Talents: Left: -2s Windrun Cooldown. Right: -15% Powershot Reduction. Choose -2s Windrun Cooldown to increase your survivability and uptime on your escape/chase tool, reducing cooldown to 10/9/8/7 seconds. This is almost always the better choice for a core Windranger. Choose -15% Powershot Reduction if you are playing a heavily spell-based, utility role and need more reliable AOE damage, reducing the per-hit damage reduction from 15% to 0%.
search_text: Windranger talent level 15 left -2s Windrun Cooldown right -15% Powershot Reduction survival utility nuke damage

display_text: Level 20 Talents: Left: +0.75s Shackleshot Duration. Right: -10% Focus Fire Damage Reduction. This is a major choice between utility and damage. +0.75s Shackleshot Duration increases the max stun to a massive 4 seconds, which is exceptional for locking down a key target. -10% Focus Fire Damage Reduction reduces the penalty from -25% to -15%, a significant 40% relative increase in your main damage output. The damage talent is typically preferred for a core role.
search_text: Windranger talent level 20 left +0.75s Shackleshot Duration 4s stun right -10% Focus Fire Damage Reduction -15% penalty damage increase

display_text: Level 25 Talents: Left: Windrun Cannot Be Dispelled. Right: Focus Fire Kills Advance Cooldown by 18s. Windrun Cannot Be Dispelled makes your defensive tool immune to purges like Nullifier or Demonic Purge, a strong defensive option against specific counters. Focus Fire Kills Advance Cooldown by 18s is an extremely powerful offensive talent; if your Focus Fire target dies, the cooldown is reduced by 18 seconds, potentially allowing you to use it multiple times in a single fight. The cooldown reduction talent is usually the game-winning choice.
search_text: Windranger talent level 25 left Windrun Cannot Be Dispelled purge immune right Focus Fire Kills Advance Cooldown 18s reset multi kill

display_text: Zeus is a Ranged Intelligence hero with below average Strength (19) and Agility (11), but very high starting Intelligence (23). This gives him high base mana and damage, but low physical durability and a slow 1.7 attack speed. His starting health and armor are poor, making him vulnerable to physical harass and ganks early on.
search_text: Zeus stats Ranged Intelligence hero attack type base armor move speed turn rate low hp low armor high int mana damage

display_text: Zeus has two Facets. The Livewire Facet causes Static Field to deal more damage to nearby enemies. This synergizes with his Arc Lightning and Nimbus for mass AOE damage in close-range fights. The Divine Rampage Facet increases Thundergod's Wrath damage to other enemies when it kills a target. This excels for securing kills across the map and executing fleeing low-health heroes. Choose Livewire for teamfight-focused builds where you get close, and Divine Rampage for a more global, assassination-oriented playstyle.
search_text: Zeus Facet Livewire Static Field more damage nearby Divine Rampage Thundergod's Wrath kill more damage other enemies choice teamfight global

display_text: In lane, max Arc Lightning first for spammable harass (90/120/150/180 damage, 1.6s cooldown). Use it to secure ranged creeps and chip down enemy heroes. Get 1 value point in Lightning Bolt (140 damage, 0.35s ministun) for last-hitting under tower or interrupting channeling. Your low armor and hp make you susceptible to physical attacks; position behind creeps. Prioritize mana regen items like Null Talismans and a Bottle to spam Arc Lightning for lane control.
search_text: Zeus laning phase skill build Arc Lightning max spam harass damage 90 120 150 180 cooldown 1.6 Lightning Bolt ministun last hit low armor mana regen Bottle

display_text: Zeus's power spikes are tied to his ultimate levels and key items. Level 6 unlocks Thundergod's Wrath (300 global damage), a massive spike for securing kills globally. Level 12 with maxed Arc Lightning and Lightning Bolt provides huge burst (180+380 damage). Level 18 unlocks his third basic ability, Heavenly Jump (700+ range mobility). Talent spikes at 10, 15, 20, and 25 are critical. Item spikes include Aghanim's Scepter (Nimbus) for map control, and Kaya upgrades for increased spell damage output.
search_text: Zeus power spikes level 6 Thundergod's Wrath 300 damage level 12 max Arc Lightning Lightning Bolt Heavenly Jump talent 10 15 20 25 item Aghanim's Scepter Nimbus

display_text: Zeus is hard countered by heroes with high magic resistance (e.g., Visage, Pudge), gap-close and silence (e.g., Storm Spirit, Riki, Night Stalker), and items that provide spell immunity (Black King Bar) as most of his damage is magical and does not pierce BKB. Break (e.g., from Silver Edge) disables his Static Field and Lightning Hands passives. Instant disables and high burst physical damage can kill him before he casts due to his low health pool.
search_text: Zeus counters magic resistance Visage Pudge silence Storm Spirit Riki Night Stalker BKB Black King Bar spell immunity break Silver Edge Static Field low hp

display_text: The core build for Zeus focuses on mana sustain and spell amplification. Bottle and Arcane Boots are essential for early mana. Aghanim's Scepter (Nimbus) is his most important core item, providing massive global teamfight presence and control. Following this, Kaya and Yasha or Kaya and Sange provide survivability and spell amp. Force Staff or Blink Dagger can be situational for positioning or escaping.
search_text: Zeus core build items essential Bottle Arcane Boots mana Aghanim's Scepter Nimbus Kaya Yasha spell amp Force Staff Blink Dagger

display_text: In teamfights, Zeus's role is backline magical artillery. He should stay at maximum range, using Arc Lightning to damage multiple enemies, Lightning Bolt to burst key targets and interrupt channeling, and Heavenly Jump to reposition or escape. He activates Thundergod's Wrath at the start of a fight to reveal and damage all enemy heroes globally. He uses Nimbus to create zone control, punish clumped enemies, and provide vision. His passive Static Field adds percentage-based damage to all his spell casts.
search_text: Zeus teamfight role backline artillery Arc Lightning AOE Lightning Bolt burst interrupt Heavenly Jump reposition Thundergod's Wrath global Nimbus zone control

display_text: Arc Lightning hurls a bolt that leaps through up to 5/7/9/11 nearby enemy units, dealing 90/120/150/180 damage per jump with a 1.6 second cooldown. The jump radius is 450. It is Zeus's primary farming and harassing tool.

display_text: Lightning Bolt strikes a target, dealing 140/220/300/380 damage and applying a 0.35 second ministun. It provides 5 seconds of unobstructed vision and True Sight in a 500 radius around the target. It can be cast on the ground, affecting the closest enemy hero within 325 range. It can interrupt channeling spells.
search_text: Lightning Bolt W ability damage 140 220 300 380 ministun 0.35s True Sight vision 500 radius ground cast interrupt channeling

display_text: Heavenly Jump makes Zeus leap forward and shock the closest visible nearby enemy (prioritizing heroes), dealing 25/50/75/100 damage and reducing their movement and attack speed by 80% and 100% respectively for 1.4 seconds. The leap range is 700/800/900/1000, the hop distance is 375/450/525/600, and it grants Zeus 900 unobstructed vision around him for 3 seconds. Cooldown is 26/22/18/14 seconds. It's a mobility and slow tool.

display_text: Nimbus creates a storm cloud anywhere on the map for 30 seconds. It has a 450 radius and automatically casts Zeus's current level of Lightning Bolt on nearby enemies every 2.5 seconds. It provides vision and can be destroyed by enemy heroes for 125 gold. Towers and creeps do half damage to it.

display_text: Lightning Hands is a toggled passive. When active, Zeus gains 30 attack speed and his attacks create Arc Lightnings that deal 50% of his attack damage to the target and nearby enemies (20% for illusions). This can be toggled off.

display_text: Thundergod's Wrath strikes all enemy heroes globally, dealing 300/475/650 damage. It also provides True Sight around each hero struck for 3 seconds. If an invisible hero is struck, it creates True Sight but deals no damage. This is Zeus's global execution and vision tool.

display_text: Static Field shocks any enemy Zeus attacks or hits with his abilities, dealing damage equal to 2.5%/3.25%/4%/4.75% of their current health. This is a percentage-based damage passive that amplifies all his spell damage.

Consider an early Aether Lens to cast spells from further away. Hold Heavenly Jump as an escape tool. Use Thundergod's Wrath to reveal their location before they engage.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy cores have Black King Bar. Your spells do NOT pierce BKB. You must focus on controlling and killing supports before BKB duration, or using Nimbus to hit enemies outside of BKB. Itemize for utility like Octarine Core for lower cooldowns on Nimbus and ults, or Refresher Orb for double Thundergod's Wrath burst when BKB is down.

Use Thundergod's Wrath to assist in fights and secure assist gold globally. Build utility items like Force Staff to save allies and yourself instead of rushing damage.

display_text: At level 10, Zeus chooses between +1 Heavenly Jump Target or +200 Health. The extra jump target improves his teamfight mobility and slow potential. The +200 Health significantly improves his survivability against early burst. Choose the Health if you are being targeted and dying, otherwise the extra target provides more utility.

display_text: At level 15, Zeus chooses between +75 Thundergod's Wrath Damage or -0.4s Arc Lightning Cooldown. The +75 damage boosts his global execution potential, crucial for securing kills. The Arc Lightning cooldown reduction (from 1.6s to 1.2s) dramatically increases his farming speed and sustained damage in fights. Pick the damage if you need burst, pick the cooldown for farming and constant DPS.

display_text: At level 20, Zeus chooses between +60 Arc Lightning Damage or +0.5s Lightning Bolt Ministun. The +60 damage (making it 240 at max level) significantly boosts his primary damage spell. The increased ministun (to 0.85s) provides much better lockdown for interrupting channels like TP scrolls or Black Hole. Choose damage if you need raw output, choose stun for control against key enemy spells.

display_text: At level 25, Zeus chooses between 325 AoE Lightning Bolt or +2% Static Field Damage. The 325 AoE turns Lightning Bolt into a potent area-of-effect nuke and slow, amazing against clumped enemies. The +2% Static Field increase (to 6.75% max) amplifies all his damage output, especially against high-health heroes. The AoE is better for teamfight presence, while the Static Field is better for single-target and tank-busting.

display_text: Kunkka is a melee Strength hero with 24 base Strength, 14 Agility, and 18 Intelligence. His 1.7 base attack time is standard. As a melee hero, his starting stats are robust, focusing on health and damage from Strength, but his low Agility base results in low initial armor.

display_text: Kunkka has two Facets. High Tide: Each enemy hero hit by Tidebringer reduces its cooldown. This encourages aggressive, spell-casting builds focused on fighting heroes and weaving more Tidebringer cleaves. Grog Blossom: When X Marks the Spot returns its target, Kunkka and nearby allies gain the Ghostship's Admiral's Rum buff. This facilitates a more support-oriented playstyle, providing team-wide damage mitigation and speed during ganks or saves.

display_text: Laning focuses on last hitting with Tidebringer to secure creeps and harass the enemy lane. The cleave damages multiple targets. Use Torrent for kill setup or to secure ranged creeps. Kunkka's melee range requires good positioning to avoid harass. Admiral's Rum provides early survivability against burst. X Marks can be used defensively to pull yourself or an ally back to safety after trading.

Level 20 talent for +15% Rum Damage Delay significantly increases survivability. Level 25 +130% Tidebringer Cleave Damage turns him into a devastating area damage threat. Item spikes include Blink Dagger for initiation, Shadow Blade/Silver Edge, Daedalus for burst, and Aghanim's Scepter for Tidal Wave.

This Facet facilitates a sustained damage, magic damage over time (DoT) playstyle, adding consistent pressure with each spellcast, especially effective against tanky heroes or in prolonged fights.

Use Corrosive Haze (5s cooldown) to provide vision and armor reduction for your carry in fights. Stay on the edge of fights, using Slithereen Crush (7s cooldown) for its stun and slow utility rather than diving deep. Building a Basher or Abyssal Blade can help lock them down after their BKB. Corrosive Haze's vision helps track their movements.

display_text: Facet: Kraken Swell. This facet modifies Kraken Shell, granting permanent Damage Block whenever an enemy hero dies under the effects of Anchor Smash. It significantly enhances Tidehunter's survivability in prolonged engagements and against physical damage lineups, turning him into a frontline juggernaut in fights where he can consistently apply his Anchor Smash debuff on dying enemies. Facet: Krill Eater. This facet causes Tidehunter's model to grow larger and grants unspecified extra bonuses per level, presumably augmenting his stats or abilities. It represents a scaling and more generic power increase compared to the conditional, fight-focused Kraken Swell.

display_text: In the laning phase, prioritize Gush (Q) for its 100/160/220/280 magical damage and 40% slow, creating kill threat with a lane partner. His high base Strength (27) provides good HP for trading. Use Anchor Smash on the enemy hero and creeps to reduce their damage by -25/-30/-35/-40 for 6 seconds, securing last hits while harassing. Kraken Shell passively blocks 15/35/55/75 physical damage from heroes, mitigating harassment. As a melee hero, focus on pulling, denying, and using Anchor Smash to secure the ranged creep.

display_text: The absolute core build for Tidehunter is Blink Dagger. This item is non-negotiable as it allows him to instantly position himself for a multi-hero Ravage initiation. Aghanim's Scepter is the second core item, upgrading his ultimate to Dead in the Water, a powerful single-target disable that leashes an enemy and can be crucial for locking down key targets.

His job is to use Blink Dagger to get into the center of the enemy team and cast Ravage, stunning all nearby enemies for up to 2.4 seconds. After initiating, he should apply Anchor Smash to reduce enemy attack damage and Gush to slow and armor-reduce priority targets. He soaks damage on the frontline thanks to Kraken Shell.

display_text: Witch Doctor is a ranged intelligence support hero with 600 attack range and a 1.7 base attack time. He has average starting stats of 20 Strength, 13 Agility, and 22 Intelligence.

display_text: Facet 1 (Headhunter): Paralyzing Cask deals more damage each bounce. This rewards chaining the stun between multiple enemies, increasing its offensive impact and kill threat in teamfights. Facet 2 (Voodoo Festeration): Voodoo Restoration deals damage to enemies and only heals Witch Doctor. This transforms his sustain ability into a selfish damage aura, ideal for a more aggressive, core-like Witch Doctor who wants to stay in the thick of fights. Facet 3 (Cleft Death): Death Ward attacks multiple targets with reduced damage. This provides area-of-effect (AOE) damage, making the ultimate effective against grouped-up enemies or illusions. Facet 4 (Malpractice): Enemies dying while Maledicted explode. This enhances Maledict's power in securing kills and provides additional burst damage in teamfights, punishing clumped enemies.

display_text: In lane, use your 600 attack range to harass. Use Paralyzing Cask (20.0 sec cooldown, 55 damage) when two enemy heroes are close to stun and damage both, securing last hits or harass. Max Voodoo Restoration first (20/30/40/50 heal per 0.33s, 9/12/15/18 mana per sec) to provide sustain for your lane partner. Take a value point in Maledict at level 2 or 4 to secure kills if your lane has burst damage. Your low armor and HP make positioning crucial; stay behind your core.

display_text: Facet: Frostbound - Chain Frost lingers on its last target if it fails to bounce and can bounce again if new targets become available. This is excellent for catching isolated heroes or controlling small skirmishes where kills create new targets. Facet: Growing Cold - When an enemy is killed by Chain Frost or while under the effect of Frost Shield, the duration of these abilities is extended. This rewards aggressive, snowballing play and teamfight cleanup. Facet: Evil Eye - Sinister Gaze applies a lingering effect that causes the target to take extra magic damage from Lich and amplifies incoming movement slows. This enhances Lich's solo kill threat and synergy with other slows on your team.
search_text: Lich facets Frostbound Chain Frost lingers bounce again Growing Cold kill extends duration Frost Shield Evil Eye Sinister Gaze extra magic damage amplify slow

display_text: Lich dominates the lane with Frost Blast (Q), dealing 40/80/120/160 damage to a single target and 80/120/160/200 area damage, while applying a 4-second 25% movement slow and 30/40/50/60 attack speed slow. Use it to secure ranged creeps and harass. Frost Shield (W) reduces damage from attacks against the target by 45/50/55/60% and pulses 18/32/46/60 damage every 1.0 second to nearby enemies for 4/5/6/7 seconds, also slowing them. Use it on your core or yourself to trade. display_text: Enigma is a ranged intelligence hero with base stats of 21 Strength, 14 Agility, and 19 Intelligence. His base attack speed is 1.7 seconds per attack, which is fairly slow. He has a low base HP pool but a decent mana pool for spellcasting in lane.

display_text: Enigma has two Facets: Gravity and Fragment. Gravity provides a passive Damage Reduction aura to allies near Enigma, scaling from 9%/11%/13%/15% at 200 distance to 0% at 500 distance. This facilitates a more supportive, teamfight-oriented playstyle, enhancing his allies' survivability around his Black Hole. Fragment modifies his Demonic Summoning, granting Eidolons a chance to split into additional Eidolons on attack. This facilitates a more aggressive, lane-dominating, and pushing playstyle.

display_text: In the laning phase, prioritize Demonic Summoning (Eidolons) to secure last hits, harass the enemy, and deny creeps. Use Malefice at level 1 or 2 for a reliable stun and damage to set up kills or escape ganks. Your low armor and health make you vulnerable, so position your Eidolons between you and the enemy. Use Midnight Pulse at level 4 or 5 to farm stacked camps quickly and control the lane equilibrium by clearing waves.

display_text: Enigma's primary power spike is at level 6 with Black Hole. A successful Black Hole can win a teamfight. Talent levels 10, 15, 20, and 25 offer significant upgrades. Item spikes include Blink Dagger for initiation, Black King Bar to secure channeling, and Refresher Orb for double Black Hole.

Heroes with silence or root that can be applied from outside his vision range also counter him (e.g., Skywrath Mage, Silencer). Heroes with abilities that grant status resistance or reduce disable duration (e.g., Tiny with Grow, Slark with Dark Pact) can reduce Malefice's effectiveness. Spell immunity (BKB) blocks Malefice and Midnight Pulse damage.

display_text: Tinker is an Intelligence-based ranged hero with 19 starting Strength, 17 starting Agility, and 30 starting Intelligence. With a base attack rate of 1.7 seconds per attack, his early game right-click is average to slow. He has no innate health or armor advantages, making him vulnerable in lane. His primary strength is his high Intelligence pool for early spellcasting.

display_text: The 'Repair Bots' facet makes March of the Machines apply a heal over time to allies it passes through, enabling a more supportive, pushing, and team-fight oriented playstyle. The 'Translocator' facet gives Tinker a blink when his Self-Defense Matrix barrier is fully absorbed, providing a significant defensive and mobility tool for a solo, ganking, or high-risk core playstyle where repositioning is key. Choose Repair Bots for utility and team sustain; choose Translocator for survivability and mobility to enable aggressive spellcasting.

display_text: Tinker's laning phase is defined by his high starting Intelligence (30) for spamming Laser. Use Laser (Q) at level 1 to harass, as it deals 75 Pure damage and blinds for 3 seconds, causing enemy carries to miss attacks. Max Laser first for kill threat. Use March of the Machines (E) to secure ranged creeps and shove the wave. He is weak to physical damage and ganks due to low armor and HP, so positioning is critical. Prioritize getting Soul Ring and Boots of Travel components.

display_text: Tinker's power spikes are tied to Rearm and key items. Level 6 enables Rearm for constant wave clear with March and Laser. Level 12 improves all basic spells. The biggest spike is Boots of Travel, allowing global map pressure. Level 18 (max Rearm) and Aghanim's Shard (Warp Flare) provide immense control. Level 20 talents (Laser AoE or -0.5s Keen Conveyance channel) are significant. Level 25 with +40 Intelligence and item cooldown reduction from Eureka! makes him a relentless spellcaster.

display_text: Tinker is hard-countered by heroes with instant silences, hexes, or long-duration stuns that prevent Rearm channeling (e.g., Skywrath Mage, Storm Spirit, Lion). Break effects disable his innate ability Eureka!, removing his item cooldown reduction. High burst physical damage (e.g., Phantom Assassin) can kill him through Defense Matrix. Heroes with global presence or high mobility can match his BoT split-push (e.g., Nature's Prophet, Spectre).

display_text: Tinker's absolute core build is Soul Ring (for mana sustain with Rearm) and Boots of Travel (for global mobility and map pressure). A third essential item is almost always Aghanim's Shard, which grants Warp Flare, providing a crucial repositioning and lockdown tool (150 damage, 2s root, range reduction). These three items are non-negotiable for his core gameplay loop.

display_text: In teamfights, Tinker's role is to spam high-impact spells from the outskirts. He initiates or disrupts with Warp Flare (Shard) to teleport and root key targets. He uses Defense Matrix on the initiator or himself for survivability. He blinds high physical damage dealers with Laser and zones/chips with constant March of the Machines. His job is to create chaos, control space, and burn enemy resources through relentless spellcasting, not to stand and right-click.

It has a 20-second cooldown on game start. This is Tinker's innate Boots of Travel ability, enabling his global playstyle.

display_text: Eureka! (Innate Passive) grants Tinker 1% item cooldown reduction per 3 Intelligence, up to a maximum of 60%. This passive scales with his Intelligence, significantly reducing the cooldown of items like Shiva's Guard, Scythe of Vyse, and Dagon.

Follow up with Scythe of Vyse for a long-duration hex. Coordinate with your team to burst the target during the 2-second root. Talent choices like +50 Laser Damage at 15 can help secure the kill.

The Manacost Reduction talent is generally superior as it directly improves your sustainability for spamming spells with Rearm, reducing the burden of your high mana costs.

The Defense Matrix talent significantly improves your and your allies' survivability, making it the better teamfight and defensive choice. The Laser Damage talent increases your burst potential. Pick Defense Matrix if you are being focused or need to save cores; pick Laser Damage if you are snowballing and need more kill threat.

The Status Resistance talent makes the buffed target much more resistant to disables, great against heavy control lineups. The Keen Conveyance talent reduces channel time to 2.5s, making your global teleports faster and less risky. Pick the channel time if split-pushing is your win condition; pick Status Resistance for teamfight durability.

The AoE Laser talent transforms Laser into a powerful teamfight ability that can blind multiple enemies, devastating against physical lineups. The +40 Intelligence talent provides massive mana, damage, and, via Eureka!, up to ~13% additional item cooldown reduction. display_text: Sniper is a ranged Agility hero with a base Attack Range of 550. He has very low base Strength (19), making him fragile. His high base Agility (27) gives him good starting armor and attack speed. His Intelligence (15) is average. His base attack time of 1.7 is standard. Sniper's primary weakness is his lack of mobility and escape.

display_text: Sniper has two facets. Ghillie Suit: While Take Aim is active, Sniper's attacks do not reveal him from fog of war. This facilitates a high-ground defensive playstyle, allowing him to attack from safety without being seen. Scattershot: Shrapnel slow and damage are increased, but duration is decreased. This facilitates a more aggressive area-denial and chasing playstyle, increasing burst damage and control.

display_text: During the laning phase, Sniper's primary goal is to secure last hits from a distance using his long base attack range. Use Shrapnel sparingly for wave clear, harassment, and securing range creep kills. The Headshot proc chance (40% at all levels) helps with harass and zoning. Be extremely cautious of positioning due to low HP and no escape. Focus on gaining levels and farm safely.

display_text: Sniper's first power spike is at level 6 with Assassinate (300 damage), allowing for long-range kill threat. Level 12 (level 2 ultimate, 400 damage) and level 18 (level 3 ultimate, 500 damage) are major damage spikes. Talent spikes at level 10 (+55 Take Aim range) increases safety, and level 15 (+30 Headshot damage) significantly increases DPS. A key item spike is Dragon Lance, which further increases his attack range and survivability. Refreshed Assassinate on kill (from level 6) creates snowball potential.

display_text: Sniper is hard countered by heroes with high mobility, gap-close, and long-range initiation. Heroes like Spirit Breaker, Clockwerk, Tusk, and Spectre can jump on him directly, bypassing his range advantage. Break mechanics (Silver Edge, Hoodwink) disable his Headshot and Take Aim passives. Instant disables (Hex) prevent him from using any skills or items. High physical burst damage can kill him before he can react.

Mask of Madness or Maelstrom provide crucial farming and attack speed. Power Treads are the standard boot for the attack speed and attribute flexibility. These items form his fundamental fighting and farming toolkit.

He uses Shrapnel to zone areas, slow enemies, and provide vision. He uses Assassinate to finish off fleeing low-health targets or to initiate on a key, vulnerable backline hero. His primary job is to deal damage from a position of safety.

display_text: Necrophos is a ranged intelligence hero with average starting attributes (18 Strength, 12 Agility, 23 Intelligence). His primary strength lies in his sustained magical damage and healing capabilities. He is inherently durable with his base Strength, but his slow 1.7 second base attack time and low Agility growth make his right-click damage and armor scaling mediocre. He relies on spellcasting and sustaining through fights.
search_text: Necrophos stats ranged intelligence 1.7 attack time 18 12 23 base agility strength

display_text: Facet 1: Profane Potency. This facet makes Sadist grant 1 bonus radius to Necrophos' area of effect abilities and items for each stack of Sadist he possesses. This is a scaling teamfight and farming facet, making his Death Pulse and items like Shiva's Guard and Radiance significantly larger as he gets kills, facilitating a high-tempo, snowballing playstyle. Facet 2: Rapid Decay. This facet changes Ghost Shroud. While active, it steals 16/19/22/25 movement speed from enemies within 700 range and reduces their healing and regeneration by 55%/60%/65%/70%. This turns Ghost Shroud into a powerful debuff tool, excelling against sustain-heavy lineups (e.g., with Wraith King, Alchemist, or multiple healing supports) and helps kite melee cores.
search_text: Necrophos Facet Profane Potency Rapid Decay Sadist radius AoE Ghost Shroud steal movement speed slow heal reduction

display_text: Laning phase (levels 1-5): Prioritize Heartstopper Aura at level 1 for consistent, unavoidable harassment. Skill Death Pulse at level 2 for last hitting, trading, and healing. Ghost Shroud is typically taken at level 3 or 4 for survivability and to amplify your Death Pulse healing. Use Death Pulse to secure ranged creeps and harass. Your goal is to stay in lane, use Heartstopper to pressure the enemy out of XP range, and build up Sadist stacks by last hitting to sustain your mana. You are vulnerable to high burst damage and ganks before level 6.
search_text: Necrophos laning skill build Heartstopper Aura Death Pulse Ghost Shroud last hit harass sustain Sadist

display_text: Level 6: Reaper's Scythe provides a powerful kill threat on low-health targets. Item timings like a completed Veil of Discord or Phylactery significantly boost your kill potential. Level 12: Maxed Heartstopper Aura and Death Pulse make you a major teamfight presence. Timings like Kaya & Sange or Shiva's Guard are key. Level 18: Maxed Reaper's Scythe with its 0.9 damage multiplier is a reliable execute. Level 20: Talent choice (e.g., +60 Death Pulse Heal) is a massive spike. Level 25: Both talents are extremely powerful, with -2.5s Death Pulse Cooldown making you nearly unkillable or +0.5% Heartstopper Aura damage scaling with enemy max HP. A late-game Radiance or Shiva's Guard with the Profane Potency facet is a massive power spike.
search_text: Necrophos power spikes level 6 12 18 20 25 Reaper's Scythe Veil Shiva's Guard Radiance talents

display_text: Hard Counters: 1. Break (from Silver Edge, Hoodwink's Sharpshooter, Viper's Nethertoxin) disables the gaining of new Sadist stacks, crippling his sustain. 2. High Burst Magic Damage: While Ghost Shroud amplifies magic damage taken, burst from heroes like Lina or Zeus can kill him through it. 3. Instant Disables and Silences: Heroes like Skywrath Mage (Ancient Seal) or Doom (Doom) prevent him from casting Ghost Shroud or Death Pulse, making him easy to focus. 4. Pure Damage and HP Removal: Heartstopper Aura damage is HP removal and does not trigger spell effects, but pure damage from heroes like Timbersaw or Enigma bypasses his natural durability.
search_text: counter Necrophos break Silver Edge Sadist burst magic damage silence doom pure damage

display_text: Core Build: 1. Veil of Discord / Phylactery: The cheap magic amplification from Veil or the targeted slow and damage from Phylactery synergize perfectly with Death Pulse and Reaper's Scythe, securing early kills. 2. Kaya & Sange: Provides all essential stats - HP, mana, status resistance, and spell amplification. This item makes Necrophos significantly tankier and more potent. 3. Shiva's Guard: Grants massive armor, intelligence, and an AoE attack speed slow/DoT. Its active slow synergizes with Heartstopper Aura to cripple physical carries and its aura benefits from the Profane Potency facet.
search_text: Necrophos core items Veil of Discord Phylactery Kaya and Sange Shiva's Guard

display_text: Teamfight Role: Necrophos is a front-line, sustained damage and healing core. His job is to stay alive in the middle of the fight, constantly pumping out Death Pulses (70/90/110/130 heal and damage) to heal allies and damage enemies. Use Heartstopper Aura to passively whittle down the entire enemy team's health pools. Activate Ghost Shroud strategically to amplify your own healing, slow/sap enemies (with Rapid Decay), or to become invulnerable to right-clicks. Use Reaper's Scythe to execute key targets and permanently gain stats. He excels in prolonged engagements where his sustained output overwhelms burst-oriented lineups.

display_text: Ability: Sadist (Passive). Necrophos gains a stack for 8 seconds for each unit he kills, granting 3.5/5.0/6.5/8.0 health regen and 3.5/5/6.5/8 mana regen per stack. Hero kills multiply this effect by 6. Break disables the gaining of new stacks but does not remove existing stack bonuses.

display_text: Ability: Reaper's Scythe (R). Targets an enemy hero within 600 range, stunning them for 1.5 seconds. At the end of the stun, deals damage equal to 0.7/0.8/0.9 multiplied by the amount of health the target is missing. If this kills the enemy, Necrophos permanently gains 1/2/3 health regen and 1/2/3 mana regen. The kill credit is given to Necrophos.
search_text: Reaper's Scythe R ultimate 600 range stun 1.5 damage 0.7 0.8 0.9 missing health execute permanent hp regen 1 2 3 mana regen 1 2 3 kill credit

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When your team is losing, focus on survival and wave clear. Build items like Hood of Defiance (into Pipe) or Lotus Orb to survive magic burst. Use Ghost Shroud defensively to avoid physical damage while you clear waves with Death Pulse from a distance. Avoid fights and farm safely, using Heartstopper Aura to slowly pressure lanes. Your Reaper's Scythe remains a threat to anyone who dives you or your teammates at low health, potentially turning fights.
search_text: Necrophos behind losing game defensive build Hood Pipe Lotus Orb wave clear Ghost Shroud avoid fight farm Heartstopper Aura

display_text: Scenario: Vs High Magic Burst Lineups. Against heroes like Lina, Zeus, or Skywrath Mage, Ghost Shroud is dangerous to use as it increases magic damage taken by 20%. Prioritize magic resistance items like Hood of Defiance and Pipe of Insight. Use Ghost Shroud only when you need the physical immunity or the amplified healing to survive physical damage, or to apply the Rapid Decay slow/heal reduction. Consider the BKB-piercing Death Seeker for initiation instead of the vulnerable Ghost Shroud.
search_text: Necrophos vs magic burst Lina Zeus Skywrath Ghost Shroud dangerous 20% magic amp Hood Pipe magic resist BKB Death Seeker

display_text: Scenario: With the Profane Potency Facet (AoE Radius). This facet encourages a snowballing, farming, and teamfight build. Prioritize items with AoE effects like Radiance, Shiva's Guard, and Octarine Core. Your goal is to get as many kills and assists as possible to stack Sadist and increase the radius of all these items and your Death Pulse. This makes you a massive area-denial hero in late-game teamfights.
search_text: Necrophos Profane Potency facet AoE radius snowball farm Radiance Shiva's Guard Octarine Core Sadist stacks teamfight

display_text: Scenario: With the Rapid Decay Facet (Heal Reduction). Pick this facet against enemy teams with strong healing or regeneration, such as Huskar, Alchemist, Wraith King, or multiple Holy Locket/Mekansm carriers. Use Ghost Shroud aggressively in fights to walk into the enemy team, applying a 55%/60%/65%/70% heal reduction aura and slowing them by 16%/19%/22%/25%. This severely cripples their sustain and allows your team to focus targets down.
search_text: Necrophos Rapid Decay facet heal reduction Huskar Alchemist Wraith King Ghost Shroud aura 55 60 65 70 percent slow 16 19 22 25

display_text: Scenario: Vs Illusion Heroes (Phantom Lancer, Chaos Knight). Necrophos struggles against numerous targets as his single-target damage is low. Heartstopper Aura and Death Pulse are effective against illusions due to their low health pools. Core items like Shiva's Guard (active and aura) and Radiance are essential for clearing illusions. Avoid being caught alone as illusions can quickly overwhelm you if your Death Pulse is on cooldown.
search_text: Necrophos vs illusions Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight Heartstopper Aura Death Pulse Shiva's Guard Radiance clear

display_text: Talent Level 10: Left Talent: -2.5s Ghost Shroud Cooldown. This reduces the cooldown from 16 seconds to 13.5 seconds at max level, providing more frequent uptime for physical immunity, self-heal amp, and the Rapid Decay debuff. Right Talent: +2s Sadist Stack Duration. This increases Sadist stack duration from 8 seconds to 10 seconds, making it easier to maintain stacks while farming and between fights, improving overall sustainability. Choose the left talent if you are fighting frequently and need Ghost Shroud's utility more often. Choose the right talent for a more farm-oriented, sustained lane presence.
search_text: Necrophos talent level 10 left -2.5s Ghost Shroud Cooldown right +2s Sadist Stack Duration sustain fight farm

display_text: Talent Level 15: Left Talent: +15% Ghost Shroud Movement Slow. This increases the slow from the Ghost Shroud aura from 25% to 40% at max level, making it significantly harder for enemies to escape or chase you while it's active. Right Talent: +60 Death Pulse Heal. This adds a flat 60 heal and damage to Death Pulse, raising it from 130 to 190 at max level. This is a massive boost to your teamfight sustain and damage output. Choose the left talent if you need more control and kiting power, especially with the Rapid Decay facet. Choose the right talent for raw healing and damage in prolonged fights.
search_text: Necrophos talent level 15 left +15% Ghost Shroud Movement Slow right +60 Death Pulse Heal control kiting sustain damage

display_text: Talent Level 20: Left Talent: +15% Ghost Shroud Self Restoration Amp. This increases your healing and regen amplification while in Ghost Shroud from 70% to 85% at max level, making your Death Pulse and any other healing (e.g., from a teammate or items) significantly stronger. Right Talent: +20% Heartstopper Regen Reduction. This adds a new effect to Heartstopper Aura, reducing health regeneration and healing on enemies by 20%. Choose the left talent to become incredibly tanky and hard to kill through healing, especially against physical damage. Choose the right talent as a powerful counter to high-regeneration heroes like Alchemist, Huskar, or anyone with a Satanic.
search_text: Necrophos talent level 20 left +15% Ghost Shroud Self Restoration Amp right +20% Heartstopper Regen Reduction healing tanky counter regen Huskar Alchemist

display_text: Talent Level 25: Left Talent: +0.5% Heartstopper Aura Damage. This increases the aura's damage from 2.3% to 2.8% of max health per second. This is a massive scaling damage increase against high-health strength heroes and in long teamfights. Right Talent: -2.5s Death Pulse Cooldown. This reduces the cooldown of Death Pulse from 5 seconds to 2.5 seconds at max level, effectively doubling your healing and damage output in fights, making you nearly unkillable. Choose the left talent for maximum percentage-based damage against tanky lineups. Choose the right talent for unparalleled sustain and teamfight presence, essentially becoming an AoE healing/damage machine gun.
search_text: Necrophos talent level 25 left +0.5% Heartstopper Aura Damage 2.8% max HP right -2.5s Death Pulse Cooldown 2.5 seconds heal damage machine gun

display_text: Warlock is a ranged intelligence hero with low base strength (22) and agility (10), but a high starting intelligence of 25. He has a slow base attack time of 1.7 seconds, making his right-click damage unreliable. He is a classic backline support with poor physical survivability and mobility.
search_text: Warlock stats ranged intelligence hero attack rate 1.7 slow BAT strength 22 agility 10 intelligence 25 base armor movement speed

display_text: Warlock has two Facets. 'Champion of Gorroth' causes his Chaotic Offering Golems to deal more burn damage based on his health and mana regeneration. This makes him a stronger teamfight presence when he can sustain and build regeneration items like Guardian Greaves or Kaya. The second Facet is unnamed in the data, offering no description; this likely means its effect is currently inactive or not defined.
search_text: Warlock facets Champion of Gorroth golem burn damage health regeneration mana regeneration Facet choice passive teamfight scaling

display_text: In the laning phase, use Shadow Word (W) to harass enemy heroes, dealing 15/25/35/45 damage per second to them while healing your lane partner for the same amount. It lasts 10 seconds and ticks every 0.5 seconds, for a total potential of 300 damage or heal. Fatal Bonds (Q) is excellent for securing ranged creeps and damaging multiple heroes if they stand close. Focus on harassing with Shadow Word and pulling creeps to secure farm for your core.
search_text: Warlock laning phase Shadow Word W harass heal 15 25 35 45 damage per second Fatal Bonds Q secure ranged creep pull camp support position 5

display_text: Warlock's primary power spike is at level 6 with his ultimate, Chaotic Offering. The golem provides a strong teamfight presence and pushing threat for 60 seconds. Level 12 is another spike with the Aghanim's Shard upgrade for Shadow Word. Level 20 talents offer a major choice between 25% Damage Resistance for Golems or +150 Shadow Word AoE. Level 25 with the 'Summons a Golem on death' talent makes him a massive late-game threat. Key item timings are Glimmer Cape or Force Staff for survival, Aghanim's Scepter for two golems, and Refresher Orb for double ultimate.
search_text: Warlock power spikes level 6 Chaotic Offering golem level 12 Aghanim Shard level 20 talent level 25 talent Golem on death item timing Aghanim Scepter Refresher Orb Glimmer Cape

display_text: Warlock is hard-countered by heroes with high single-target burst damage that can quickly kill him before he gets his spells off, like Nyx Assassin or Clinkz. Heroes with strong dispels like Oracle, Legion Commander, or Abaddon can remove his long-duration Fatal Bonds and Shadow Word. Illusion-based heroes like Phantom Lancer or Chaos Knight can overwhelm his Golem and dilute Fatal Bonds damage. Heroes with BKB-piercing control like Beastmaster's Primal Roar can lock him down through his defensive items.
search_text: Warlock counters hard counter burst damage Nyx Assassin Clinkz dispel Oracle Legion Commander Abaddon illusions Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight BKB-piercing stun

display_text: Warlock's core build consists of survivability and mana items. Glimmer Cape is essential for saving yourself or allies and setting up for your channeled Upheaval. Aghanim's Scepter is his most critical damage item, summoning a second Golem, dramatically increasing teamfight damage and pushing power. Boots of Travel are often needed later to split-push with Golems and join fights globally.
search_text: Warlock core items build Glimmer Cape Aghanim Scepter Aghs Boots of Travel BoTs mana sustain survivability Golem

display_text: In teamfights, Warlock's role is to initiate or counter-initiate from the backline. Lead with Chaotic Offering (R) to stun multiple enemies and summon the Golem. Follow up immediately with Fatal Bonds (Q) on the key targets to amplify all damage dealt. Cast Shadow Word (W) on a diving ally or a low-health enemy. Then, find a safe spot to channel Upheaval (E) to create a massive area slow (up to 55/70/85/100%) that controls the entire fight. Your goal is to enable your team's damage with Bonds and control the battlefield with Upheaval and the Golem.
search_text: Warlock teamfight role backline support initiator Chaotic Offering R stun Golem Fatal Bonds Q damage amp Shadow Word W heal Upheaval E channel slow 100% control

display_text: ABILITY: Fatal Bonds (Q). Binds 6 visible enemy units together within a 700 search radius. For 18 seconds, a percentage (15%/18%/21%/24%) of any damage dealt to one of them is shared as the same damage type to the others. This ability does NOT pierce BKB (based on standard mechanics; pierce_immunity attribute not present in data).
search_text: Fatal Bonds Q Warlock ability binds 6 units 700 radius 18 seconds damage share 15 18 21 24 percent amplification AOE teamfight

display_text: ABILITY: Shadow Word (W). Warlock casts a spell on a target unit. For 10 seconds, the target deals 15/25/35/45 magical damage every 0.5 seconds to nearby enemy units in a 225 radius and heals nearby ally units for the same amount. The target unit itself is also damaged if it is an enemy or healed if it is an ally. Cooldown is 15/14/13/12 seconds. With Aghanim's Shard, it also grants movement speed.
search_text: Shadow Word W Warlock ability damage heal over time 15 25 35 45 0.5 second tick 225 AOE 10 seconds cooldown 15 14 13 12 Shard movement speed

display_text: ABILITY: Upheaval (E). CHANNELED. Warlock creates a powerful slowing zone in a target area with an initial radius of 575/600/625/650. The slow increases by 11%/14%/17%/20% per second channeled, up to a maximum of 55%/70%/85%/100%. It also deals 10 damage per second, with a maximum damage of 35/60/85/110 if fully channeled for 1.0 second. The spell can be channeled for up to 10/12/14/16 seconds. Enemies are slowed for 1.0 second after leaving the area or when the spell ends. The talent '+=45 Upheaval Damage' adds a flat 45 damage per second.
search_text: Upheaval E Warlock ability channeled slow zone radius 575 600 625 650 slow per second 11 14 17 20 max slow 55 70 85 100 damage 10 per second max 35 60 85 110 channel time 10 12 14 16 seconds talent +45 damage

display_text: ABILITY: Eldritch Summoning (Innate Passive). Whenever an enemy unit dies while affected by any of Warlock's abilities, a minor imp is summoned that lasts 15 seconds. Imps have 50/130/210/290/370 health and explode on death for 20/65/110/155/200 damage in a 400 radius. They automatically seek nearby units, prioritizing heroes affected by Fatal Bonds, and explode on contact. Imps do not explode if killed by enemy heroes.
search_text: Eldritch Summoning Warlock passive innate ability imp summoned on death 15 seconds duration health 50 130 210 290 370 explode damage 20 65 110 155 200 radius 400 seek Fatal Bonds

display_text: ABILITY: Chaotic Offering (R). Summons a Golem from the depths, stunning enemies in a 600 radius for 0.8 seconds after a 0.5 second delay. The Golem lasts 60 seconds. Golem has 1500/2250/3000 health, 8/12/14 armor, deals 100/150/200 damage, has 25/50/75 health regen, 320/340/360 movement speed, and 60% bonus slow resistance. It takes reduced spell damage and has permanent immolation and flaming fists. Golems grant 100/150/200 gold bounty. With Aghanim's Scepter, a second Golem is summoned.

display_text: SCENARIO: Against Illusion or Summon-based Heroes (e.g., Phantom Lancer, Chaos Knight). Your Fatal Bonds is extremely powerful as it can link the real hero with their illusions, causing your team's AOE damage to devastate the entire group. Prioritize maxing Fatal Bonds. Items like Shiva's Guard or Refresher Orb become high-value to increase your AOE control and damage to clear illusions and amplify damage across all bonded units.
search_text: Warlock situational versus illusions Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight summons Fatal Bonds Q max AOE damage amplification item Shiva Guard Refresher Orb

display_text: SCENARIO: Playing from Behind. When your team is losing, Warlock's value shifts to high-ground defense. Upheaval can block choke points for long durations. Use Chaotic Offering defensively to stop pushes and create space. Itemize for survivability (Force Staff, Glimmer Cape) and save your cores with Shadow Word. Your Golem can help clear waves from a safe distance. Avoid getting caught; your death means your team loses all fight-swinging abilities.
search_text: Warlock playing from behind high ground defense Upheaval E choke point Chaotic Offering R stop push save items Force Staff Glimmer Cape Shadow Word W heal

display_text: SCENARIO: Vs High Burst or Jump Heroes (e.g., Storm Spirit, Clockwerk). Positioning is critical. Stay far behind your team. Items like Glimmer Cape, Force Staff, and later Aeon Disk are mandatory to survive the initial engagement. Your goal is to survive long enough to get your ultimate and Upheaval off. Cast Fatal Bonds and Shadow Word from maximum range. Consider a Linken's Sphere against single-target initiations.
search_text: Warlock situational versus burst jump Storm Spirit Clockwerk positioning survival items Glimmer Cape Force Staff Aeon Disk Linken Sphere survive to cast ultimate

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 10: Left talent is '+4% Fatal Bonds Damage', increasing the damage share to 19%/22%/25%/28%. Right talent is '+50 Upheaval Radius', increasing the initial slow area to 625/650/675/700. Choose the Fatal Bonds talent if your team has high AOE damage and fights are decisive. Choose the Upheaval radius if you need better control and fight in more open areas, or if you are struggling to keep enemies inside the zone.

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 15: Left talent is 'Maximum of +70 Attack Speed' from '+8 Upheaval Attack Speed per second on Allies'. This provides a scaling attack speed buff to allies standing in Upheaval, up to +70. Right talent is '+150 Shadow Word AoE', increasing its damage/heal radius from 225 to 375. Choose the Upheaval attack speed if you have strong right-click cores who can stand and fight in the slow. Choose the Shadow Word AoE for significantly better team sustain and AOE harassment in fights.
search_text: Warlock talent level 15 left +8 Upheaval Attack Speed per second on Allies max +70 right +150 Shadow Word AoE radius 375 choice physical DPS versus team heal damage

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 20: Left talent is '25% Damage Resistance for Chaotic Offering Golems'. This significantly increases the survivability of your primary teamfight tool. Right talent is '+150 Shadow Word AoE', increasing its radius from 225 to 375. This is a repeat of the level 15 right talent. At this level, the choice is between making your Golems incredibly tanky (especially against physical damage) versus having a massive AOE heal/damage aura. The Golem talent is generally preferred for late-game siege and sustain in prolonged fights.
search_text: Warlock talent level 20 left 25% Damage Resistance for Golems right +150 Shadow Word AoE radius 375 choice Golem survivability versus AOE heal

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 25: Left talent is '+=45 Upheaval Damage', adding a flat 45 damage per second to the ability, bringing its total to 55 DPS and maximum damage to 155/180/205/230. Right talent is 'Summons a Golem on death', creating a Chaotic Offering Golem when Warlock dies. This is a game-changing talent. The damage talent is rarely taken. The Golem on death talent is almost always the correct choice, as it guarantees massive fight impact even if you are focused and killed first, often turning a lost fight into a win.
search_text: Warlock talent level 25 left +=45 Upheaval Damage 55 DPS right Summons a Golem on death Chaotic Offering post-death choice damage versus guaranteed teamfight impact

display_text: Beastmaster is a Melee strength hero with an attack rate of 1.7 seconds per hit. He has above-average starting Strength (25) and average Agility (19) and Intelligence (16). As a melee core, his base stats are focused on survivability.
search_text: Beastmaster stats melee strength agility intelligence attack rate

display_text: Facet: Wild Hunt - Beastmaster attacks faster when his units are nearby. This favors a zoo/summoner playstyle, emphasizing grouping his summons (Boar, Hawk) to maximize personal attack speed. Facet: Beast Mode - Beastmaster can get a self attack speed and damage amplification boost. This favors a more self-sufficient, right-click oriented build where his personal damage is the focus, especially when not surrounded by his own units.
search_text: Beastmaster facet Wild Hunt Beast Mode summons attack speed damage zoo right click

display_text: Maximize Wild Axes (Q) for wave clear and harassment, dealing 30/65/100/135 damage per axe. Use Call of the Wild Boar (W) at level 2 to provide a slow (10/18/26/34%) for kills and zoning. Use Inner Beast (E) passively to increase your and your units' attack speed (10/30/50/70). Prioritize last hitting with your high base damage and utilizing the Boar to harass the enemy carry.
search_text: Beastmaster laning Wild Axes Call of the Wild Boar Inner Beast slow harass last hit

display_text: Level 6: Primal Roar is a powerful single-target initiation and ganking tool with a 3/3.5/4 second stun. Level 12: Access to level 2 Primal Roar and the level 15 talent (+15 Inner Beast Attack Speed or -5s Call of the Wild Cooldown). Level 18: Max level Primal Roar. Level 20: Key talent choice between +30 Damage to summons/self or +250 Health to summons/self. Level 25: Ultimate cooldown reduction (-25s Primal Roar) or removing the Wild Axes cooldown.
search_text: Beastmaster power spike level 6 12 18 20 25 Primal Roar talents

display_text: Break disables his passive Inner Beast attack speed bonus. Heroes with instant disables (Lion Hex, Shadow Shaman Shackles) can lock him down before he can cast Primal Roar. High physical burst damage can kill his summons (Boar, Hawk) quickly, negating his zoo power and Wild Hunt facet. Silences prevent him from using Primal Roar and summoning units.

display_text: Helm of the Overlord is essential to dominate a creep, adding another powerful unit to his army. Blink Dagger is critical for initiating with Primal Roar. Assault Cuirass amplifies the attack speed of all his units and reduces enemy armor.
search_text: Beastmaster core build Helm of the Overlord Blink Dagger Assault Cuirass

display_text: In teamfights, Beastmaster's primary role is to initiate with Blink Dagger and Primal Roar on a key enemy core or support. After initiating, he uses Wild Axes for damage amplification (7/9/11/13% per axe hit) on multiple targets and commands his summons (Boar, Hawk, dominated creep) to focus fire. His Drums of Slom provides sustained AoE damage and healing for his army. He functions as a frontline disabler and zoo army commander.

display_text: Wild Axes (Q): Beastmaster throws two axes that deal 30/65/100/135 damage each on the way out and back. Each axe can hit an enemy once, applying a 7/9/11/13% damage amplification from Beastmaster and his units for 12 seconds. Cooldown is 8 seconds. Range is 1500. The axes spread 450 units apart and have a radius of 175. Destroy trees.

display_text: Call of the Wild Boar (W): Summons a Boar that lasts 60 seconds. The Boar has 300/450/600/750 health, 320/330/340/350 movespeed, and deals 25/40/55/70 poison damage per attack, slowing the target's attack and move speed by 10/18/26/34% for 3 seconds. Cooldown is 30 seconds.

display_text: Call of the Wild Hawk (E): Summons a Hawk that circles Beastmaster for 25 seconds. The Hawk has 200/325/450/625 health, 600/650/700/750 flying vision, and 30/40/50/60% magic resistance. It automatically attacks nearby enemy heroes every 4 seconds (attack rate scales with its attack speed), diving to deal 50/80/110/140 damage and rooting them for 0.25/0.5/0.75/1.0 seconds. Cooldown is 45/40/35/30 seconds.

display_text: Inner Beast (Passive): Provides 10/30/50/70 attack speed to Beastmaster and all units under his control within a 1200 radius. This passive is disabled by Break.

display_text: Drums of Slom (Ultimate Passive): Attacks from Beastmaster or his nearby controlled units bang his drum, dealing 80 damage and healing him and his units for 20% of the damage dealt (5% vs creeps) in a 600 radius. Each attack reduces the interval between drum hits, down to a minimum of 0.5s after 20 attacks. The intervals increase if no attacks are made. Using Primal Roar counts as 10 attacks.

display_text: Primal Roar (R): Beastmaster roars, stunning a single target for 3/3.5/4 seconds and pushing them back 450 units over 1 second. The roar deals 150/225/300 damage in a 300 radius path. Units shoved aside take 150/225/300 damage and are slowed by 60% for 3/3.5/4 seconds. Beastmaster and his units gain 40% movement speed for 2 seconds. Cooldown is 90/75/60 seconds.

display_text: Queen of Pain is a ranged Intelligence hero with average base stats (20 Strength, 22 Agility, 25 Intelligence). Her attack type is Ranged and she has an attack rate of 1.5. This standard baseline makes her versatile but not particularly durable or hard-hitting without items and levels.

display_text: Facet: Succubus provides scaling Spell Lifesteal based on distance to enemies (14% at 150 range, down to 2% at 800 range). Facet: Bondage returns spell damage back to enemies, with Succubus's Spell Lifesteal also applying to the reflected damage. The Succubus facet is standard and enhances sustain during fights by rewarding aggressive, close-range positioning. The Bondage facet is a niche, defensive counter-pick against heavy spell damage compositions, turning enemy nukes against them.
