display_text: In lane, use Shadow Strike (Q) at level 1 or 2 to harass. It deals 50/80/110/140 initial damage and slows the enemy by 25/35/45/55% for 16 seconds, with damage over time. This enables strong kill threat and forces enemy regen usage. Your high base Agility (22) gives decent right-click armor. Prioritize securing ranged creeps with Scream of Pain (W) and use Blink (E) defensively to avoid ganks. Deny allies who are below 25% HP and afflicted by your Shadow Strike.
search_text: Queen of Pain laning Shadow Strike Q harass 50 80 110 140 damage slow 25 35 45 55 percent duration 16 seconds Scream of Pain W farm Blink E escape

display_text: Power Spikes: Level 6 with Sonic Wave (325 damage). Level 7 with maxed Scream of Pain (300 damage). Level 12 with level 2 ultimate (475 damage). Level 15 talent (+7 Strength or +30 Damage). Level 18 with level 3 ultimate (625 damage). Level 20 talent (+100 Scream of Pain Damage or -45s Sonic Wave Cooldown). Level 25 talent (-2s Blink Cooldown or +250 Sonic Wave Damage). Key item spikes are Orchid Malevolence for solo kill potential and Aghanim's Scepter for double Scream.
search_text: Queen of Pain power spikes level 6 Sonic Wave 325 damage level 7 Scream of Pain 300 damage level 12 ultimate 475 level 15 talent 18 ultimate 625 level 20 talent level 25 talent Orchid Malevolence Aghanim's Scepter

display_text: Hard Counters: Heroes with instant silences or hexes (Skywrath Mage, Shadow Shaman) prevent Blink escapes. High magic resistance or spell immunity (Lifestealer, Huskar) reduce your primary damage output. Break (Silver Edge, Hoodwink) disables the Succubus Spell Lifesteal passive. Heroes with high burst physical damage (Clinkz, Templar Assassin) can kill you before you can react with Blink.
search_text: Queen of Pain counters silence hex Skywrath Mage Shadow Shaman magic resistance Lifestealer Huskar Break Silver Edge Hoodwink passive burst physical damage Clinkz TA

display_text: Core Build: 1. Power Treads or Arcane Boots for mana and survivability. 2. Orchid Malevolence is essential for solo kill potential, providing attack speed, damage, and silence. 3. Aghanim's Scepter is a massive damage spike, allowing Scream of Pain to hit twice. Following these, items like Scythe of Vyse, Shiva's Guard, or Black King Bar are situationally critical.
search_text: Queen of Pain core items Power Treads Arcane Boots Orchid Malevolence silence attack speed Aghanim's Scepter double Scream of Pain

display_text: Teamfight Role: Queen of Pain is a mobile magical burst damage dealer and flanker. Your job is to Blink into optimal position, often from the side or back, to unleash Sonic Wave and Scream of Pain on multiple key targets. Use Blink to reposition constantly, staying at the edge of the fight to maximize damage output and survivability. You can initiate with Sonic Wave to break formations or clean up fights with your low-cooldown nukes.
search_text: Queen of Pain teamfight mobile burst damage Blink flank Sonic Wave Scream of Pain reposition clean up

display_text: Ability: Shadow Strike (Q). Hurls a poisoned dagger dealing 50/80/110/140 initial damage, then deals 20/40/60/80 damage over time. Slows the target's movement speed by 25/35/45/55% for 16 seconds. Damage is dealt every 3.0 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB. The slow gradually reduces over the duration. Allies can deny a Shadow Striked unit below 25% max HP.

display_text: Ability: Blink (E). Short distance teleport. Cooldown is 12.0/10.0/8.0/6.0 seconds. Minimum blink range is 200 units. Can be used to dodge projectiles. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Ability: Scream Of Pain (W). Releases a piercing scream dealing 75/150/225/300 magical damage to all enemies in a 600 radius around Queen of Pain. Projectile speed is 900. Hits invisible units and cannot be disjointed. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Ability: Succubus (Passive). Provides Spell Lifesteal from all damage, including Pure damage. Lifesteal amount scales from 14% at 150 range to 2% at 800 range from the enemy. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Ability: Sonic Wave (R). Ultimate. Releases a wave dealing 325/475/625 magical damage and knocking enemies back 350 units over 1.4 seconds. The wave travels 900 distance at 900 speed, starting at 100 radius and expanding to a 450 radius at the end. Cooldown is 110/95/80 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Scenario: Against High Armor/Low Magic Resistance Cores. Your magical burst (Scream of Pain, Sonic Wave) is extremely effective. Prioritize maxing Scream of Pain and build Aghanim's Scepter quickly to double your AOE output. Kaya-based items are strong. Avoid building physical damage unless you have the +30 Damage talent.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. If you are underfarmed and dying, avoid initiating. Use Blink defensively to escape ganks. Build survivability items like BKB, Eul's Scepter, or Ghost Scepter. Focus on clearing waves safely with Scream of Pain and stacking neutral camps to catch up. Your talents like +7 Strength at level 15 can help with survival.

display_text: Scenario: Against Multiple Blink-Dagger Carriers. Your Sonic Wave is a perfect counter-initiation tool to disrupt their engagement. Hold your ultimate until after they blink in. Consider Aether Lens to cast Sonic Wave from a safer distance. Itemize for control like Scythe of Vyse to lock down key targets after your wave.
search_text: Queen of Pain vs Blink Dagger carriers counter-initiation Sonic Wave hold ultimate Aether Lens Scythe of Vyse control

display_text: Scenario: With a Strong Single-Target Disabler on Your Team. Pair Shadow Strike with an ally's stun for guaranteed damage and slow duration. Build Orchid Malevolence to extend the disable chain and secure kills. Your role shifts to following up on your ally's initiation with full burst combo.
search_text: Queen of Pain with single target disabler ally Shadow Strike combo Orchid Malevolence follow up burst

display_text: Scenario: Need to Split-Push and Apply Map Pressure. Max Blink second for lower cooldown (6s). Use Scream of Pain to quickly clear waves, then Blink to the next lane or jungle. Carry a TP scroll to join fights. Talents like -2s Blink Cooldown at 25 greatly enhance this playstyle.
search_text: Queen of Pain split push map pressure Blink 6s cooldown Scream of Pain clear wave talent -2s Blink Cooldown 25

display_text: Talent Level 10: Left choice grants +35 Attack Speed against units affected by Shadow Strike. Right choice grants +7 Strength. Choose Attack Speed if you are focusing on right-click damage and have a good lane, as it synergizes with Shadow Strike's long slow. Choose Strength for survivability and to mitigate early game burst.

display_text: Talent Level 15: Left choice is -1s Shadow Strike Damage Interval, increasing its damage per second. Right choice is +30 Damage. The -1s Interval is a significant damage increase for Shadow Strike, making it a stronger harass and kill tool. The +30 Damage is a reliable boost to right-click damage, better if you are building physical items or need to push towers.

Right choice reduces Sonic Wave Cooldown by 45 seconds. +100 Scream Damage is a massive increase to your primary farming and fighting spell, especially with Aghanim's Scepter. The Cooldown reduction on Sonic Wave is for when your ultimate is critical for every fight and you need it up more often.

The Blink Cooldown talent is for extreme mobility, split-pushing, and elusive playstyles. The +250 Sonic Wave Damage is for maximizing burst in teamfights, often decisive against tanky heroes.

display_text: Venomancer is a Ranged agility hero with 19 base Strength, 25 base Agility, and 19 base Intelligence. His attack rate is 1.7. His base stats are below average; low HP pool but higher starting armor due to Agility. He has no innate movement speed or turn rate data.
search_text: Venomancer ranged agility hero stats attack rate 1.7 strength 19 agility 25 intelligence 19 low health high armor starting stats

display_text: Patient Zero: This facet makes Noxious Plague spread again when a target dies, extending the area control and damage of the ultimate in prolonged teamfights or clustered enemies. It emphasizes Venomancer's role as a fight disruptor and area denial hero. Plague Carrier: When Venomous Gale hits enemy heroes, bonus Plague Wards are created around them, and Plague Wards also deal Septic Shock damage based on their attack damage. This facet pushes Venomancer towards a more aggressive, ward-spamming playstyle with higher burst potential from Gale and stronger lane pressure.
search_text: Venomancer facets Patient Zero Noxious Plague spreads again Plague Carrier Venomous Gale creates Plague Wards wards deal Septic Shock area denial burst damage

display_text: Prioritize Poison Sting for right-click harassment, as its 6/9/12/15 second duration and -8/-10/-12/-14 movement slow make it oppressive against melee cores. Venomous Gale at level 1 secures first blood or forces TPs. Use Plague Ward to block enemy pull camps, secure ranged creep last hits safely, and harass. Due to low HP, position behind Wards and use the slow to kite. Your job is to make the enemy offlaner's life miserable.
search_text: Venomancer laning poison sting harass slow venomous gale kill threat plague wards block camps secure creeps low hp kite position support

display_text: Level 6: Noxious Plague provides huge kill threat and teamfight presence with its 200/300/400 initial damage and 3%/4%/5% max HP damage over time. Level 12: Level 2 ultimate and maxed Poison Sting/Plague Wards increases damage output and slow. Level 15: Talent choice (15% Poison Sting Health Regen Reduction or -1s Plague Ward Cooldown) spikes his utility. Level 20: +50 Base Damage talent significantly increases right-click DPS. Level 25: 2.5x Plague Ward HP/Damage or Noxious Plague Aura reduces 200 Attack Speed are massive late-game spikes.
search_text: Venomancer power spikes level 6 Noxious Plague level 12 max skills level 15 talent level 20 +50 base damage level 25 talent ward HP damage or attack speed slow ultimate

display_text: Venomancer is countered by heroes with strong dispels (Oracle, Legion Commander) which remove his Poison Sting and Noxious Plague debuffs. Break (e.g., from Silver Edge) disables his Septic Shock passive's bonus damage. Heroes with high mobility (Queen of Pain, Anti-Mage) can easily reposition out of his slows and Plague Ward zones. Magic immunity (BKB) blocks all his damage-over-time abilities except Noxious Plague's initial burst and Septic Shock bonus damage (if not from Break).

display_text: Aghanim's Scepter is core, upgrading Noxious Plague to spread again (inherent with Patient Zero facet) and adding an attack speed slow aura. For Plague Carrier, it's still strong for the spread. Force Staff/Glimmer Cape are essential defensively due to his low HP and lack of escape. Shiva's Guard provides armor, intelligence, and an active slow that synergizes with his kit for area control.
search_text: Venomancer core items aghanim scepter aghs force staff glimmer cape shiva guard armor intelligence slow defensive survival area control

display_text: In teamfights, Venomancer's role is Area Denial and Backline Disabler. Pre-fight, plant Plague Wards to create a defensive perimeter. Initiate with Venomous Gale on clustered enemies for the slow. Cast Noxious Plague on the highest HP core to maximize the percent-based damage and spread the slow aura. Use Poison Sting right-clicks to apply sustained slows and damage. Stay at the edge, using Wards as bodyblocks, to whittle down enemies with damage-over-time effects and severe movement impairement.
search_text: Venomancer teamfight role area denial backline disabler plague wards perimeter venomous gale slow initiation noxious plague high hp core percent damage poison sting right click slow stay edge

display_text: Venomous Gale (Q): Launches a projectile that deals 25/50/75/100 initial strike damage and then 10/40/70/100 damage every 3 seconds over 15 seconds. It also applies a -35%/-40%/-45%/-50% movement slow that decays over time. Cooldown: 21/20/19/18 seconds. Speed: 1200. Radius: 125. Does NOT pierce BKB (no 'pierce_immunity' attribute). Can be lethal.

This damage can be lethal. Does NOT pierce BKB (no 'pierce_immunity' attribute). Does not interrupt healing items or activate Blink Dagger cooldown.

display_text: Plague Ward (E): Summons an immune-to-magic ward that attacks enemies. Ward lasts 40 seconds. Cooldown: 5 seconds. Ward HP: 150/250/350/450. Ward Damage: 16/24/32/40. The ward's attacks inherit Poison Sting, dealing 50% of the full damage. Gold bounty: 100%.

display_text: Septic Shock (R - Passive): Venomancer's attacks deal extra magical damage based on how many debuffs the target has. Bonus damage is 8% per debuff. Only counts debuffs from Venomancer or his Plague Wards. Break disables this bonus damage for Venomancer's attacks.
search_text: Septic Shock R passive attack modifier bonus magical damage per debuff 8% only veno and ward debuffs break disables attack damage

display_text: Noxious Plague (Upgraded R with Aghanim's Scepter): Infects a target, dealing 200/300/400 initial impact damage and 3%/4%/5% of their max health as damage per second over time. Slows the target and nearby enemies within 800 radius by 15%-50% based on distance. Duration: 5 seconds. When the target dies or the debuff expires, it spreads to nearby enemies with a non-communicable version. Projectile speed: 1200. Patient Zero facet makes it spread again on death.

display_text: Scenario: Against High Armor / High Magic Resistance Cores. Venomancer excels here as his primary damage is magical damage-over-time (Poison Sting, Venomous Gale ticks) and percent-based health removal (Noxious Plague). High armor is irrelevant. High magic resistance reduces damage but not the powerful slows. Build Aghanim's Scepter and Shiva's Guard to maximize the HP-based damage and control. Your value is in the relentless slows, not burst physical DPS.
search_text: Venomancer vs high armor magic resistance cores damage over time magical percent HP noxious plague slows control aghanims scepter shiva guard

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When your team is losing, Venomancer transitions to a defensive vision and area denial hero. Spam Plague Wards to secure vision around your jungle and defensive objectives. Use Venomous Gale to slow enemy pushes. Avoid showing on the map; let wards scout. Your talents like -1s Plague Ward Cooldown (level 15) and 2.5x Plague Ward HP/Damage (level 25) become high-value for holding high ground. Itemize for survival (Force Staff, Glimmer Cape) and control (Shiva's Guard).

display_text: Scenario: As a Core (Mid/Safe Lane). Focus on farming with Plague Wards to push lanes safely and accelerate items like Aghanim's Scepter and Hurricane Pike. Max Plague Ward first for farm and push. Your level 20 +50 Base Damage talent significantly increases your right-click threat. Use the Plague Carrier facet for aggressive ward placement during ganks. Your job shifts to applying map pressure with wards and being a major source of damage in fights.

display_text: Scenario: Versus Illusions and Summons. Venomancer is strong against illusion heroes (Chaos Knight, Phantom Lancer) and summoners (Broodmother, Lycan). Poison Sting applies to all units, providing vision and slow. Plague Wards can body-block and attack illusions. Noxious Plague's spread effect (with Aghs/Patient Zero) can decimate clustered illusion armies. Prioritize Aghanim's Scepter and consider a Shard for the ward attack applying Septic Shock (if Plague Carrier facet is active).

Choose -5s CD if you need more frequent initiations and slows for chasing or teamfight control. Choose Health Regen Reduction against heroes with high inherent regeneration (Alchemist, Huskar, Healing Salve abusers) to severely limit their sustain in lane and fights.

Choose -1s Ward Cooldown for significantly higher ward count, which means more vision, more pushing power, more body blocks, and more damage if you have the Plague Carrier facet. Choose +5% Slow if your team lacks control and you need to ensure key targets (like a mobile carry) cannot escape your slows.

It massively increases your right-click DPS, improves last hitting, and synergizes with Septic Shock. The +1% Max HP damage is situational, only consider it if you are against multiple extremely high HP strength heroes and you are not right-clicking much.

The Attack Speed reduction is a game-changing teamfight debuff against right-click carries (e.g., Faceless Void, Troll Warlord). The 2.5x Ward HP/Damage turns Plague Wards into formidable pushers and fighters that are much harder to clear. Choose based on need: teamfight control vs. split-push and sustained damage.

display_text: Faceless Void is a Melee hero. He has above average starting Agility (24), average Strength (20), and low Intelligence (15). This gives him high base damage and armor, but low mana pool. His attack rate is 1.7. Compared to average, he is a relatively tanky Agility carry with a slow attack animation.
search_text: Melee Attack Type, Base Stats, Attack Rate 1.7, Strength 20, Agility 24, Intelligence 15

display_text: Faceless Void has three Facets. Temporal Impunity has no direct gameplay effect. Chronosphere Facet allows casting Chronosphere, trapping enemies in a sphere. This enables his classic combo-heavy, fight-winning ultimate playstyle, focusing on catching multiple enemy heroes. Time Zone Facet allows casting Time Zone, slowing enemies and speeding up allies in an area. This facilitates a more supportive, utility-based playstyle focused on area control and buffing his team's attack speed.
search_text: Facet Choice, Chronosphere, Time Zone, Temporal Impunity, Playstyle, Ultimate, Supportive, Utility

display_text: In the laning phase (first 10 minutes), Faceless Void relies on his high base damage and Time Lock bash chance for harass and last hitting. Early points in Time Walk provide crucial survivability for trading, allowing you to engage and disengage. Time Dilation can be a strong defensive tool if the enemy lane uses spells frequently. Focus on securing last hits to build towards your core items like Battle Fury or Maelstrom.
search_text: Laning Phase, Early Game, Last Hits, Time Walk, Time Lock, Time Dilation, Trading, Harass

display_text: Faceless Void's power spikes are at Level 6 (gaining Chronosphere or Time Zone), which is his first major teamfight and kill potential. Level 12 with maxed Time Walk and Time Lock significantly improves his mobility and damage. Level 18 gives max level Time Dilation and his ultimate. Talent levels at 10, 15, 20, and 25 offer substantial boosts. Key item timings include Maelstrom/Battle Fury for farming, Manta Style for dispel and split-push, and Aghanim's Scepter/Shard for utility.
search_text: Power Spikes, Level 6, Level 12, Level 18, Talents, Item Timings, Chronosphere, Time Zone

display_text: Faceless Void is hard countered by heroes with instant disables or saves that prevent him from getting Chronosphere off (e.g., Shadow Shaman Hex, Bane Nightmare). Break disables his Backtrack talent and Time Lock. Heroes that can fight outside his Chronosphere range (Sniper, Tinker) or those with magic immunity (Lifestealer) can ignore his main control. Silences prevent Time Walk escape.

display_text: Core essential items for Faceless Void are Mask of Madness or Battle Fury/Maelstrom for early farming and sustain. Manta Style is core for dispelling silences, creating illusions, and increasing farm/split-push. Aghanim's Scepter is essential for the Chronosphere Facet, while Aghanim's Shard provides a powerful slow aura.

display_text: In teamfights, Faceless Void's primary role is to initiate with Chronosphere (if that Facet is chosen) to trap key enemy heroes, allowing his team to follow up. Alternatively, with Time Zone, he provides a large area slow and attack speed buff for allies. He must jump onto the enemy backline (supports, squishy cores) with Time Walk, use Time Dilation to slow and extend cooldowns, and right-click with Time Lock bashes to secure kills. He is a frontline initiator and primary damage dealer.

display_text: Ability: Time Walk. Faceless Void rushes to a target location up to 650/700/750/800 units away at 3000 speed. Any damage taken in the last 2.0 seconds is backtracked (healed). He is invulnerable during the dash. Cooldown is 21/16/11/6 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Ability: Time Dilation. Faceless Void traps all nearby enemies in a 775 radius for 7/8/9/10 seconds, extending their cooldowns and slowing their movement and attack speed by 4/5/6/7%. The slow is amplified by each cooldown extended. It also deals 4/6/8/10 damage per stack per second for each cooldown. Does not affect item cooldowns. Does NOT pierce BKB.

Items like Battle Fury and Mjollnir are essential for cleave and chain lightning. Manta Style can help match their split-push. Focus on identifying and locking down the real hero with bashes.

Choose the Backtrack Chance talent for incredible survivability against all damage types, making you extremely hard to kill. Choose the Chronosphere AoE talent if you have the Chronosphere Facet to catch multiple enemy heroes more easily and control larger areas in teamfights.

display_text: Wraith King is a melee strength hero with 22 base strength, 16 base agility, and 16 base intelligence. His attack rate is 1.7 seconds per attack. As a melee hero, he has no range stat, relying on getting into melee combat. His starting attributes are standard for a melee strength carry, focusing on survivability and physical damage.
search_text: Wraith King stats melee strength base armor attack speed move speed turn rate

display_text: Wraith King must choose between two facets. 'Bone Guard' allows Wraith King to summon skeletons to fight for him, enhancing his map control, lane push, and providing bodies in fights. 'Spectral Blade' makes Wraith King's attacks curse enemies, dealing additional damage after a delay, which synergizes with his Mortal Strike for burst and provides a damage-over-time component for harassing durable heroes. Choose Bone Guard for a pushing or farming-centric playstyle. Choose Spectral Blade for a more aggressive, single-target-focused damage build, especially effective against heroes who rely on instant healing or escape.
search_text: Wraith King facet Bone Guard Spectral Blade skeletons summon attack curse damage over time push lane

display_text: In the laning phase, Wraith King relies on his high base strength (22) for durability. Start with a point in Wraithfire Blast for a reliable 1.0 second stun and harass potential. Use Mortal Strike's bonus damage on cooldown for last hitting and trading. Vampiric Spirit provides sustain but is less impactful at level 1, consider taking a value point early. Focus on securing last hits with his 1.7 base attack time. Wraith King is vulnerable to heavy harass and kiting due to being melee.
search_text: Wraith King laning early game skill build last hit stun harass sustain

display_text: Wraith King's key power spikes are: Level 6 with Reincarnation, making him extremely hard to kill and a threat for tower dives. Level 12 with a maxed Vampiric Spirit for 40% lifesteal and increased wraith duration. Level 18 with maxed Reincarnation (120s cooldown). Talent levels (10, 15, 20, 25) each provide significant boosts. Item timings like Armlet, Radiance, or Blink Dagger define his mid-game fighting or farming potential. Aghanim's Shard upgrade is also a major timing.
search_text: Wraith King power spike level 6 reincarnation level 12 vampiric spirit talents items

display_text: Wraith King is hard countered by mechanics that nullify his passives. Break (from Silver Edge, Viper's Nethertoxin) disables Vampiric Spirit's lifesteal and Reincarnation, making him killable. High mana burn (Anti-Mage, Diffusal Blade, Nyx Assassin) can drain the 225 mana cost of Reincarnation, preventing his revival. Instant disables that can lock him down after he revives (Hex, Berserker's Call) prevent him from retaliating or escaping. Pure or high magic damage can bypass his natural strength/HP pool before he gets tank items.
search_text: Wraith King counters break mana burn silver edge diffusal blade hex disable reincarnation

display_text: Wraith King's core build is non-negotiable. Armlet of Mordiggian is essential for the massive strength, damage, and attack speed boost, synergizing perfectly with his lifesteal and Reincarnation toggling. Blink Dagger is crucial to initiate with Wraithfire Blast and to reposition after Reincarnation. Aghanim's Shard is a core upgrade that summons skeletons on hero kills, drastically increasing his pushing power and teamfight presence after a kill. Radiance is a common alternative for a farming build, but Blink and Armlet are more consistent for fighting.
search_text: Wraith King core items armlet blink dagger aghanims shard radiance build

display_text: In a 5v5 teamfight, Wraith King's primary role is as a Frontline Initiator and Disabler. He should use Blink Dagger to jump on a key enemy backline hero and stun them with Wraithfire Blast. His presence forces the enemy team to focus him down, knowing they must kill him twice. After dying and reviving with Reincarnation, he must re-engage, using the 4-second 75% slow on enemies to allow his team to clean up. He provides sustained physical damage with Mortal Strike crits and healing for his team with Vampiric Spirit aura.
search_text: Wraith King teamfight initiator frontline tank stun reincarnation slow aura

display_text: Wraithfire Blast (Q) sears an enemy unit with spectral fire. It deals 80 / 100 / 120 / 140 damage and stuns the target for 1.0 / 1.2 / 1.4 / 1.6 seconds. After the stun, the target takes 20 / 40 / 60 / 80 damage per second and is slowed by 20% for 2.0 seconds. The projectile moves at a speed of 1200. The stun is a reliable, targeted disable.
search_text: Wraith King ability Q Wraithfire Blast stun damage over time slow duration 1.6 80 140

display_text: Mortal Strike (W) is a passive ability. It provides Wraith King with a critical strike that deals 160% / 200% / 240% / 280% damage on an attack. This critical strike has a cooldown of 6.0 / 5.5 / 5.0 / 4.5 seconds. It is his primary source of burst physical damage for last hitting and fighting.
search_text: Wraith King ability W Mortal Strike passive crit critical strike damage 280% cooldown 4.5

display_text: Vampiric Spirit (E) grants Wraith King and nearby allies 10% / 20% / 30% / 40% Lifesteal from attacks. When Wraith King is slain, he turns into a free-path Wraith for 3.5 / 4.0 / 4.5 / 5.0 seconds, delaying his death. As a Wraith, he gains bonus attack speed (30 / 45 / 60 / 75 with Aghanim's Scepter) and bonus movement speed (10% / 15% / 20% / 25% with Aghanim's Scepter). He cannot use Reincarnation after being a Wraith. Break disables the lifesteal component.
search_text: Wraith King ability E Vampiric Spirit lifesteal 40% wraith death delay aghanims scepter attack speed move speed break

display_text: Reincarnation (R) is Wraith King's ultimate. When killed, he regroups and resurrects after 3.0 seconds if he has 225 mana. Upon death, enemy units within a 600 radius are slowed by 75% in both attack speed and movement speed for 4.0 seconds. The ability has a cooldown of 180 / 150 / 120 seconds. It can be self-cast to kill Wraith King instantly. Wraith King will not revive if he lacks the 225 mana.
search_text: Wraith King ultimate R Reincarnation revive resurrection slow 75% 600 radius cooldown 120 mana cost 225 self cast

display_text: Scenario: Playing from behind. Wraith King should prioritize survival items like Armlet and BKB to ensure he can survive and get his Reincarnation off. Avoid expensive farming items like Radiance. Use Wraithfire Blast defensively to kite or protect allies. Consider the 'Bone Guard' facet to create space by pushing out lanes with skeletons safely. Focus on fighting around your Reincarnation cooldown with your team.
search_text: Wraith King scenario behind losing farm armlet bkb defense skeletons push

display_text: Scenario: Versus high physical damage and armor. Wraith King thrives here due to Vampiric Spirit lifesteal. Build items like Assault Cuirass and Heart of Tarrasque to become extremely durable. The 'Spectral Blade' facet's delayed damage can help chip through high armor. Ensure you have enough mana (via items like Echo Sabre or Soul Ring) to always cast Reincarnation. Your role is to absorb damage and out-sustain the enemy carry.
search_text: Wraith King scenario high armor physical damage assault cuirass heart sustain mana

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has strong mana burn (Anti-Mage, Invoker). This is a critical weakness. Itemize for mana pool and protection. An early Soul Ring can help guarantee Reincarnation mana. Consider a Bloodstone for mana and spell lifesteal on your stun. Linken's Sphere or Lotus Orb can block key mana-burning abilities. Play very cautiously when your mana is below 225. Communicate with your team to focus the mana-burning hero first.
search_text: Wraith King counters mana burn anti-mage diffusal blade soul ring bloodstone linkens lotus orb

display_text: Scenario: Need to split-push and apply map pressure. Select the 'Bone Guard' facet. Build items that enhance summons or provide global presence like Assault Cuirass aura or Boots of Travel. Aghanim's Shard is mandatory. After winning a fight, use your summoned skeletons to take objectives quickly. Your Reincarnation makes you a risky target to gank while split-pushing.
search_text: Wraith King split push map pressure bone guard skeletons aghanims shard objectives

display_text: At Level 10, Wraith King chooses between +10% Vampiric Spirit Lifesteal and +1s Wraithfire Blast Stun Duration. The +10% Lifesteal (bringing it to 50% at max level) is the default choice for a carry build, significantly increasing his survivability and farming speed. The +1s Stun Duration (making it 2.6s at max level) is a strong utility option if your team lacks lockdown and you are playing a more supportive or initiator role, or if you are facing heroes with high magic resistance where damage is less relevant.
search_text: Wraith King talent level 10 +10% vampiric spirit lifesteal +1s wraithfire blast stun duration lockdown carry utility

display_text: At Level 15, Wraith King chooses between +350 Health and +50 Attack Speed. The +350 Health provides raw survivability, making him harder to burst down before or after Reincarnation, and is generally the safer, more reliable choice. The +50 Attack Speed dramatically increases his damage output, synergizing with Mortal Strike crits and lifesteal. Choose Attack Speed if you are ahead and have damage items, or Health if you are behind and need to survive enemy burst.
search_text: Wraith King talent level 15 +350 health +50 attack speed survivability damage output

display_text: At Level 20, Wraith King chooses between -2s Mortal Strike Cooldown and +15 Strength. The -2s Mortal Strike Cooldown reduces the cooldown of his critical strike to as low as 2.5 seconds, providing a massive increase to his sustained physical DPS. This is the premier damage talent. The +15 Strength provides 285 health, 15 damage, and some health regeneration. This is a tank talent. Almost always take the -2s Cooldown unless you are the sole frontliner and are dying instantly in fights.
search_text: Wraith King talent level 20 -2s mortal strike cooldown +15 strength DPS damage tank health

display_text: At Level 25, Wraith King chooses between +1s Reincarnation Casts Wraithfire Blast and Reincarnation Casts Wraithfire Blast. The left talent, '+1s Reincarnation Casts Wraithfire Blast,' is the standard description for the functionality: When Reincarnation triggers, instead of slowing enemies, it casts the current level of Wraithfire Blast on all enemy heroes within 900 range. This provides a powerful AOE stun and damage upon revival, which can completely turn a teamfight. The right talent choice is incorrectly listed as the same; typically, the other choice is a different powerful upgrade. Assuming the standard options, the AOE stun is overwhelmingly powerful and the near-universal choice.
search_text: Wraith King talent level 25 reincarnation casts wraithfire blast AOE stun 900 range revive

display_text: Death Prophet is an Intelligence, ranged hero with an Attack Range of 600. She has 21 base Strength, 17 base Agility, and 24 base Intelligence. She has a base attack time of 1.7 and a movement speed of 285. Her stats indicate a hero with solid starting HP from Strength and good mana from Intelligence, but low armor due to low Agility. Her attack speed is average for a ranged caster.

display_text: Ability: Crypt Swarm. Sends a swarm of winged beasts to savage enemy units in front of Death Prophet. It has a 900 cast range and a travel speed of 1100. The damage is 100/175/250/325. The cooldown is 9/8/7/6 seconds. Can hit units up to 1110 range away. Does not trigger spell block or spell reflect.

display_text: Ability: Spirit Siphon. Creates a spirit link between Death Prophet and an enemy unit, draining health from the enemy. It deals 25/50/75/100 damage per second and heals Death Prophet for the same amount. Each charge has a 40 second replenish time. The link breaks if the target moves 250 units away from Death Prophet. The spell lasts up to 6 seconds. Cannot cast multiple Spirit Siphons on the same target.

display_text: Ability: Exorcism. Unleashes evil spirits to drain the life of nearby enemy units and structures. Summons 10/18/26 spirits that deal 62-67 damage per hit. The ability lasts 40 seconds. At the end, Death Prophet is healed for 25% of the total damage dealt. Spirits move at 525 speed and search for targets within a 700 radius around Death Prophet. Deals 50% damage to buildings.

display_text: Level 10 Talents: +30 Attack Speed vs +12% Magic Resistance. The Attack Speed talent accelerates Death Prophet's right-click damage and farm speed, useful for a more carry-oriented build. The Magic Resistance talent increases her survivability against magic nukes, which is crucial for staying alive to drain and deal damage with her toolkit.

display_text: Level 15 Talents: +100 Silence AoE vs -2.5s Crypt Swarm Cooldown. The increased Silence AoE talent is powerful for team fights, making it easier to disable multiple enemy casters. The reduced Crypt Swarm cooldown talent is better for constant wave clear and higher damage output in sustained fights, improving her farming and pushing speed.

display_text: Level 20 Talents: +400 Health vs +30 Spirit Siphon Damage/Heal. The +400 Health talent provides a significant raw survivability boost, making her harder to burst down. The +30 Spirit Siphon Damage/Heal talent dramatically increases the power of her primary mid-fight sustain and damage tool, making her much tankier and more threatening when she gets on top of a target.

display_text: Level 25 Talents: -22s Spirit Siphon Replenish Time vs +8 Exorcism Spirits. The -22s Spirit Siphon Replenish Time reduces the charge cooldown from 40s to 18s, allowing for near-constant uptime on multiple Siphons in fights, providing immense sustain. The +8 Exorcism Spirits talent adds 8 more spirits to her ultimate, increasing its total damage potential and, consequently, her end-of-ult heal by a significant amount.

display_text: Facets: Death Prophet has the 'Suppress' facet. Suppress causes Silenced enemies to also be slowed. This facet turns her Silence from a pure casting disable into a soft crowd control that also hampers movement, making it easier for Death Prophet and her team to chase targets or kite melee cores. There is no choice between facets; this is her singular facet effect.
search_text: Death Prophet Facet Suppress facet Silenced enemies also slowed movement slow crowd control chase

display_text: Laning Phase (0-10 mins): Death Prophet is typically played as a mid laner or an offlaner. Her goals are to secure last hits with Crypt Swarm (100-325 damage) and harass the enemy. Use Crypt Swarm to nuke the wave and push the lane for rune control. Her low base armor makes her vulnerable to physical harassment. Spirit Siphon is a powerful trading tool at level 2 or 3; use it on the enemy hero to drain their health while healing yourself, forcing them out of lane. Prioritize levels for stronger Crypt Swarm and an early point in Witchcraft for movement speed.
search_text: Death Prophet laning early game mid offlane Crypt Swarm farm harass push wave Spirit Siphon trading sustain low armor

display_text: Power Spikes: Level 6 with Exorcism is a massive spike for pushing towers and securing kills. Level 12 with maxed Spirit Siphon (100 dps/heal) and a core item like Eul's Scepter or Sange and Kaya makes her very strong in fights. Level 18 with maxed talents and ultimate (26 spirits). Level 20 talent choice (either +400 HP or +30 Siphon damage) is a significant survivability or damage spike. Level 25 talents make her extremely durable (faster Siphon charges) or give her ultimate massive damage/heal potential.
search_text: Death Prophet power spikes level 6 Exorcism push level 12 Spirit Siphon max item timing level 18 ultimate level 20 talent level 25 talent

display_text: Hard Counters: High burst physical damage (e.g., PA, TA) can kill her through her siphon healing due to her low armor. Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Hoodwink Bushwhack) disables her Witchcraft passive and the movement speed bonus from her Exorcism spirits. Instant lockdown and burst (e.g., Lion Hex + Finger) can kill her before she can activate Exorcism and Siphon. Pure damage nukes (e.g., Timbersaw) bypass the healing from her ultimate. Spirit Siphon can be dispelled by strong dispels.
search_text: Death Prophet counters hard counter burst physical damage low armor Break disables Witchcraft passive instant hex lockdown pure damage dispel Spirit Siphon

display_text: Core Build: Eul's Scepter of Divinity is essential. It provides mana regen, movement speed (synergizing with Witchcraft), and a defensive purge/cyclone to dodge spells or set up Silence. Sange and Kaya is a core damage and survivability item, amplifying her spell damage and healing from Spirit Siphon while providing status resistance and HP. Boots of Travel are crucial in the mid-to-late game to maximize her split-push potential with Exorcism and global map presence.
search_text: Death Prophet core items essential Eul's Scepter mana movement speed setup dispel Sange and Kaya spell lifesteal amplification status resistance Boots of Travel split push global Exorcism

display_text: Teamfight Role: Death Prophet is a frontline tanky spellcaster and tower-pusher. Her job is to activate Exorcism (26 spirits at max level) and run at the enemy team, using Spirit Siphon (multiple charges) on key targets to drain their health and sustain herself. She should cast Silence (with Suppress slow) on clusters of enemies to disable their spellcasting and slow them. She soaks damage with her HP pool and Siphon healing, creating space for her backline. After a successful fight, her ultimate is ideal for taking objectives like towers and barracks.
search_text: Death Prophet teamfight role frontline tank spellcaster pusher initiate Exorcism Spirit Siphon sustain drain Silence disable slow Suppress facet soak damage take objectives

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When behind, Death Prophet should avoid direct 5v5 engagements. Use Crypt Swarm (7/6 sec cooldown with talent) to safely clear waves from a distance. Use Boots of Travel to split push lanes opposite to where the enemy is pressuring; activate Exorcism to quickly take waves and threaten towers. Focus on farming Sange and Kaya or a defensive item like BKB to survive the next fight. Use Spirit Siphon defensively to heal off creeps or the closest enemy if jumped.
search_text: Death Prophet playing from behind split push farm safe wave clear Crypt Swarm Boots of Travel Exorcism tower threat avoid fight defensive items BKB

display_text: Scenario: Versus High Magic Burst. Against heroes like Zeus or Lina, prioritize the +12% Magic Resistance talent at level 10. Build early HP items like a Point Booster (into Kaya) or a Hood of Defiance. Use Eul's Scepter to cyclone yourself during enemy initiation or to dodge key spells. In fights, try to pre-activate Exorcism and use Spirit Siphon on a frontliner to gain constant healing to offset the magic damage. Your Suppress-facet Silence can slow and disable their mobile casters.
search_text: Death Prophet versus high magic burst magic resistance talent HP items Hood Defiance Eul's cyclone dodge Spirit Siphon sustain heal Exorcism pre-activate Silence Suppress slow

display_text: Scenario: Versus Illusion Heroes (e.g., Chaos Knight, Phantom Lancer). Death Prophet struggles as her single-target Spirit Siphon is less effective and illusions dilute her Exorcism spirit attacks. Build area-of-effect items like Shiva's Guard (to slow illusions and provide armor) and Kaya and Sange (for spell amp on Crypt Swarm). Maximize Crypt Swarm to clear illusion waves. The +8 Exorcism Spirits talent at level 25 becomes more valuable to overwhelm with sheer number of attacks against many targets.
search_text: Death Prophet versus illusions illusion heroes Chaos Knight Phantom Lancer Spirit Siphon single target weak Exorcism spirits diluted build Shiva's Guard Kaya Sange Crypt Swarm clear waves talent +8 Exorcism Spirits

display_text: Scenario: As a Position 2 (Mid) Core. In mid, focus on using Crypt Swarm to secure last hits, harass, and push the wave for rune control. Rush Bottle for sustain. After level 6, use Exorcism to pressure the enemy mid tower or to rotate and gank side lanes with a rune. Core item progression: Phase Boots for damage and armor, Eul's Scepter, then Sange and Kaya. Your role is to be a tempo-setter, creating space with your ultimate and forcing early teamfights around objectives.
search_text: Death Prophet mid position 2 core mid lane Crypt Swarm last hit harass push bottle rune control Exorcism level 6 gank tower pressure Phase Boots Eul's Sange Kaya tempo space teamfight

display_text: Scenario: As a Position 3 (Offlane) Core. In the offlane, your priority is to survive and get experience. Use Crypt Swarm to secure range creeps and harass the enemy carry. An early point in Spirit Siphon allows you to trade favorably against the enemy support. Build tanky utility items: start with a Bracer, build Phase Boots, then Rod of Atos or Spirit Vessel to aid in kills. Your job is to be a durable frontliner in mid-game fights, using Exorcism to initiate and soak damage for your team.

display_text: Scenario: Late Game and Sieging. Death Prophet excels at sieging high ground with Exorcism. Activate your ultimate from a safe distance before committing to the high ground push. The spirits will attack buildings for 50% damage and provide a large zone of control. Use Silence with its Suppress slow to deter defenders from initiating. Keep Spirit Siphon active on the enemy frontliner to stay healthy. If the fight turns, your ultimate's 25% heal on damage dealt can bring you back from the brink, allowing a counter-push.

display_text: Scenario: Vs High Armor or Illusion Heroes. Prioritize Battle Fury for cleave damage and farm. Consider a Silver Edge for Break against passives like Crimson Guard or high armor from items. Mjollnir is effective for clearing illusions. Avoid committing Phantom Strike onto illusions; use Stifling Dagger to identify the real hero.
search_text: Phantom Assassin vs high armor illusions Battle Fury cleave Silver Edge Break Mjollnir Stifling Dagger identify real hero

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Focus on farming with Stifling Dagger and Battle Fury. Use Blur defensively to escape ganks and avoid fights. Build cost-effective stats like Wraith Bands and a casual Morbid Mask for sustain. Consider Aghanim's Shard for Fan of Knives Break utility to contribute in fights without committing. Your power spike is delayed until core items are online.
search_text: Phantom Assassin behind farming Battle Fury Blur escape Wraith Band Morbid Mask Aghanim Shard Fan of Knives Break utility

display_text: Scenario: Enemy Team Has Heavy Magic Burst. Rush Black King Bar immediately after Battle Fury or Desolator. Consider a Linken's Sphere if they have single-target disables like Doom or Hex. Use Blur to disjoint projectile spells. Time your Phantom Strike engage after enemy key spells are used.
search_text: Phantom Assassin vs magic burst Black King Bar BKB Linken Sphere single target disable Blur disjoint Phantom Strike timing

display_text: Scenario: You Are the Primary Carry and Need to End Early. After Battle Fury and Desolator, build into a fast Black King Bar and Satanic. Take the Methodical facet for guaranteed crits on key targets. Group with your team at level 12+ and use Roshan advantage to siege high ground. Your goal is to burst key heroes before they can react.
search_text: Phantom Assassin early end game Battle Fury Desolator BKB Satanic Methodical facet group push Roshan sieg high ground burst

display_text: Level 10 Talent Choice: Left talent provides +0.6s Phantom Strike Duration, extending the attack speed buff to 3.1s total. Right talent provides -1.5s Stifling Dagger Cooldown, reducing it to 4.5s. Choose the left talent for more sustained man-fight damage, especially with the Veiled One facet. Choose the right talent for more frequent harassment, wave clear, and Deadly Focus procs with the Sweet Release facet.
search_text: Phantom Assassin level 10 talent left +0.6s Phantom Strike duration right -1.5s Stifling Dagger cooldown

display_text: Level 15 Talent Choice: Left talent provides +20% Immaterial Evasion, raising it to a maximum of 75% at level 4. Right talent provides +15% Stifling Dagger Damage, increasing the attack damage multiplier. Choose the left talent for survivability against physical damage cores. Choose the right talent for increased poke damage and burst potential, especially when paired with the Sweet Release facet.
search_text: Phantom Assassin level 15 talent left +20% Immaterial Evasion right +15% Stifling Dagger Damage

display_text: Level 20 Talent Choice: Left talent provides +200 Phantom Strike Cast Range, increasing maximum blink range to 1150. Right talent provides +60 Phantom Strike Attack Speed, raising the maximum bonus to 250. Choose the left talent for better initiation and escape, allowing safer positioning. Choose the right talent for maximum attack speed during the burst window, synergizing with the Methodical facet for faster guaranteed crits.
search_text: Phantom Assassin level 20 talent left +200 Phantom Strike Cast Range right +60 Phantom Strike Attack Speed

display_text: Level 25 Talent Choice: Left talent provides +10% Coup de Grace chance, raising it to 27%. Right talent causes Stifling Dagger to launch two extra daggers towards random visible enemies within cast range. Choose the left talent for significantly higher critical strike frequency, maximizing single-target damage. Choose the right talent for massive AOE slow and damage output in teamfights, excellent with the Sweet Release facet for chain reactions.

display_text: Pugna is a Ranged Intelligence Hero with below-average Strength (19 base) and average Agility (21 base). His high Intelligence (26 base) provides good starting mana. His attack rate is 1.7, indicating a slow attack animation. This statline makes him fragile but with strong spellcasting potential from the start.
search_text: Pugna stats ranged intelligence hero 19 strength 21 agility 26 intelligence 1.7 attack rate fragile

display_text: The Facet 'Siphoning Ward' causes Nether Ward to restore mana and health to Pugna based on damage dealt. This facilitates a sustain-focused playstyle, allowing Pugna to stay active and spam spells in prolonged sieges or fights. The Facet 'Rewards of Ruin' grants Pugna spell amplification per destroyed tower, scaling his magic damage output as the game progresses and rewarding a map-control and objective-focused strategy.
search_text: Pugna facets Siphoning Ward Nether Ward restore mana health sustain Rewards of Ruin spell amplification per destroyed tower scaling objective

display_text: Use Nether Blast (95/170/245/320 damage) to secure ranged creep last hits and harass the enemy laner. Your high base intelligence allows for frequent spell usage. Prioritize levels in Nether Blast for wave clear and tower damage (65% vs structures). Use Decrepify defensively on yourself or offensively to set up kills, noting it slows enemy movement by 45/50/55/60% and increases magic damage they take by 20/30/40/50%. Focus on securing farm and pressuring the enemy tower.
search_text: Pugna laning phase early game Nether Blast last hit harass 95 170 245 320 damage Decrepify slow 45 50 55 60 percent magic damage amp 20 30 40 50 pressure tower

display_text: Level 6: Life Drain provides kill threat and sustain. Level 12: Maxed Nether Blast and Decrepify for high burst damage and control. Level 18: Max Life Drain for 350 health drain per second. Talent Levels: 10 (CDR vs Ward Health), 15 (HP vs Decrepify Ally Speed), 20 (Life Drain Heal vs Decrepify Duration), 25 (Nether Blast Damage vs Ward Damage). Key item timings: Aghanim's Scepter for Life Drain break, Kaya & Sange for amplification, Blink Dagger for positioning.

display_text: Pugna is hard countered by heroes with high burst physical damage that can ignore or quickly remove Decrepify (e.g., TA, Ursa). Heroes with instant silences or hexes can stop his Life Drain channeling. Magic immunity (BKB) renders most of his damage useless. High mobility heroes can dodge Nether Blast and escape Life Drain range.
search_text: Pugna counters hard counter physical burst Decrepify ignore silence hex stop Life Drain channel BKB magic immunity mobility dodge

display_text: Aghanim's Scepter is an absolute core item, allowing Life Drain to pierce spell immunity and drain through magic resistance. Kaya (or Kaya & Sange) is essential for mana management, spell lifesteal, and amplifying all of Pugna's magic damage. Blink Dagger or Force Staff are crucial for positioning to land key Decrepifies and to maintain Life Drain channeling.
search_text: Pugna core build Aghanim Scepter Life Drain pierce BKB Kaya Sange mana spell amp Blink Dagger Force Staff positioning

display_text: In teamfights, Pugna's role is backline artillery and disruption. Use Nether Blast for area damage on clustered enemies. Place Nether Ward in the fight to punish enemy spellcasters. Use Decrepify to save a key ally from physical damage, or to amplify magic damage on a crucial enemy target. Channel Life Drain on a low-health enemy to secure a kill or on an ally to provide significant healing.
search_text: Pugna teamfight role backline artillery disruption Nether Blast AOE Nether Ward punish casters Decrepify save ally amp magic Life Drain heal kill secure

display_text: Nether Blast deals 95/170/245/320 magical damage in a 400 radius after a 0.8 second delay. It deals 65% of its damage to structures. Cooldown is 5 seconds.
search_text: Nether Blast Q spell AOE damage 95 170 245 320 magic 400 radius 0.8 second delay 65 percent structure damage 5 second cooldown

display_text: Decrepify slows an enemy's movement speed by 45/50/55/60% for 3.5 seconds, and increases magic damage they take by 20/30/40/50%. On allies, it only provides a 10/15/20/25% bonus to healing received. Does NOT pierce BKB. Can be cast on Nether Ward.
search_text: Decrepify W spell slow 45 50 55 60 percent 3.5 seconds magic damage amp 20 30 40 50 percent ally heal amp 10 15 20 25 cannot attack ethereal does not pierce BKB

display_text: Nether Ward lasts until destroyed (4 attacks to kill). It has a 1400 radius. When an enemy hero casts a spell within range, the Ward fires at them, dealing 50/70/90/110 base damage plus 1/1.2/1.4/1.6 times the mana cost of the spell as damage. The damage is magical.
search_text: Nether Ward E spell ward 4 attacks health 1400 radius base damage 50 70 90 110 damage multiplier 1 1.2 1.4 1.6 per mana spent punish spellcasters

display_text: Oblivion Savant is a passive ability that allows Pugna to cast spells and use items while channeling Life Drain. This includes channeled items like Meteor Hammer or Shiva's Guard.
search_text: Oblivion Savant passive talent cast spells use items while channeling Life Drain channeled items

display_text: Life Drain is a channeled ability. When cast on an enemy, it drains 150/250/350 health per second from the target to heal Pugna. When cast on an ally, it drains Pugna's health to heal the ally for 150/250/350 health per second. It provides vision over the target. Cooldown is 7 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB without Aghanim's Scepter.
search_text: Life Drain R ultimate channel drain enemy 150 250 350 health per second heal self heal ally cooldown 7 vision does not pierce BKB

display_text: Scenario: Playing against high physical burst damage (e.g., Phantom Assassin, Templar Assassin). In this scenario, Decrepify becomes your primary survival tool. Use it preemptively on yourself when you anticipate a jump. Positioning Nether Ward safely behind your team can still punish their spell usage (e.g., PA's Blink Strike). Itemize for survivability like Ghost Scepter (which stacks with Decrepify) or Glimmer Cape.
search_text: Pugna situational vs physical burst high damage PA TA Templar Assassin Decrepify survival Ghost Scepter Glimmer Cape Nether Ward punish

display_text: Scenario: Your team lacks push. Pugna excels at sieging with Nether Blast, which deals 65% damage to structures. Group with your team early, use Decrepify on the enemy tower to make it immune to physical attacks from defenders, and spam Nether Blast. Nether Ward protects your team during the siege by punishing enemy defenders who try to cast spells.
search_text: Pugna situational team lacks push siege tower Nether Blast 65 percent structure damage Decrepify tower immune physical Nether Ward protect

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has multiple BKB carriers. Without Aghanim's Scepter, your damage and control are heavily mitigated. Rush Aghanim's Scepter to allow Life Drain to pierce BKB. Your role shifts to focusing on the BKB'd target with Life Drain while using Decrepify and Nether Blast on other targets. Consider items like Scythe of Vyse to control other enemies.
search_text: Pugna situational enemy BKB carriers magic immunity Aghanim Scepter rush Life Drain pierce BKB Decrepify Nether Blast other targets Hex

display_text: Scenario: Playing from behind. Focus on defensive utility. Use Nether Ward to protect your high ground. Use Decrepify to save your core heroes from ganks. Use Life Drain to keep your carries alive in fights. Itemize for team utility like Force Staff, Glimmer Cape, or Lotus Orb instead of pure damage items.
search_text: Pugna situational playing from behind defensive utility Nether Ward high ground Decrepify save core Life Drain heal ally Force Staff Glimmer Cape Lotus Orb

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has heavy magic damage lineup. Your Decrepify can be a double-edged sword, as it amplifies magic damage. Be extremely careful when using it on enemies, as it can lead to your team being wiped by AOE magic. Prioritize using it defensively on allies for the healing amplification or to make them immune to physical damage during magic bursts.
search_text: Pugna situational enemy heavy magic damage lineup Decrepify amp magic careful AOE wipe use defensively ally heal amp

display_text: At Level 10, choose between -1s Nether Blast Cooldown or +2 Nether Ward Health. The CDR talent allows for more frequent wave clear, tower damage, and teamfight DPS, ideal for a core/pushing Pugna. The Ward Health talent makes the ward more durable against summons and AOE spells, valuable against spell-heavy lineups where the ward needs to survive longer.
search_text: Pugna talent level 10 left -1s Nether Blast Cooldown right +2 Nether Ward Health cooldown reduction vs durability spell heavy lineup

display_text: At Level 15, choose between +30% Decrepify Movement Speed To Allies or +250 Health. The movement speed talent transforms Decrepify into a potent save/speed boost for allies, great for a utility/support Pugna. The flat +250 Health talent significantly improves survivability against burst damage, a safer choice for a core Pugna or when facing high physical damage.
search_text: Pugna talent level 15 left +30% Decrepify Movement Speed To Allies right +250 Health utility save vs survivability burst

display_text: At Level 20, choose between +15% Life Drain Heal or +1.5s Decrepify Duration. The Life Drain talent increases your sustain and healing output dramatically, crucial for a frontlining or drain-tank playstyle. The Decrepify duration talent provides longer disables and damage amplification, excellent for locking down key targets or protecting allies for extended periods.
search_text: Pugna talent level 20 left +15% Life Drain Heal right +1.5s Decrepify Duration heal sustain vs disable duration amp

display_text: At Level 25, choose between +200 Nether Blast Damage or +1.9 Nether Ward Damage Per Mana. The Nether Blast talent provides massive AOE burst (320 + 200 = 520 damage), making you a primary damage dealer. The Nether Ward talent turns the ward into an extreme threat against high-mana-cost spells, potentially one-shotting supports, ideal against intelligence-heavy lineups.

display_text: Templar Assassin is a Ranged Agility hero with base Agility 23, Strength 21, and Intelligence 20. Starting stats are average. Attack range is standard for ranged heroes, but can be significantly increased via Psi Blades and items. Starting armor is decent. Her base attack rate is 1.6 seconds, which is slightly slower than average. Her primary farming and harassing mechanism is the spill damage from her Psi Blades passive, allowing her to hit multiple creeps and heroes at once from a safe distance.
search_text: Templar Assassin Lanaya ranged agility hero stats base agility 23 attack type ranged attack rate 1.6

display_text: Templar Assassin has three Facets. Voidblades makes Meld's bonus damage and -armor apply to all enemies in the Psi Blades spill radius, making her a superior teamfighter and AoE damage threat. Refractor gives Refraction a damage charge every time a shield charge is consumed, rewarding aggressive trading and making her a high-tempo, burst-oriented hero. Hidden Reach grants Templar Assassin up to a significant bonus attack range increase over time while in Meld, making her a safer, sieging-focused hero that can attack from extreme range.
search_text: Facet Voidblades Refractor Hidden Reach Meld damage spill shield charge attack range

display_text: In the laning phase, focus on last-hitting and harassing with Psi Blades to damage the enemy hero while farming. Use Refraction early for bonus damage on 3/4/5/6 attacks and 30 HP shield instances to win trades. Meld can be used at level 2 or 4 for burst harassment, applying -2/-4/-6/-8 armor for 6 seconds and dealing 50/100/150/200 bonus damage. Use the Psi Blades spill width of 90 to hit heroes behind creeps. Your goal is to secure farm for a fast Blink Dagger or Desolator timing.
search_text: Laning Phase Psi Blades spill Refraction shield Meld armor reduction harass last hit

display_text: Key power spikes: Level 6 with Psionic Trap for vision, scouting, and kill setup. Level 12 with maxed Psi Blades and Refraction for high farming speed and fighting. Level 18 with max talents. Key item spikes are Blink Dagger for initiation, Desolator for armor reduction synergy with Meld, and Dragon Lance for range (synergizes with Hidden Reach). The Level 25 talent +4 Refraction Instances is a massive survivability and damage spike.
search_text: Power Spike level 6 Psionic Trap level 12 Desolator Blink Dagger level 25 talent

display_text: Templar Assassin is hard countered by heroes with Break (Silver Edge, Hoodwink, Viper), which disables her Refraction shield and Psi Blades spill. Instant disables and Hex prevent her from using Refraction or Psionic Projection to escape. Heavy magical burst damage can pop her Refraction charges quickly. Illusion heroes can drain her limited Refraction charges. Heroes with True Sight can reveal her during Meld and when placing Traps.
search_text: Counter Break Silver Edge Viper disable hex burst magic damage illusion true sight Meld Trap

display_text: The core build for Templar Assassin is Blink Dagger and Desolator. Blink Dagger is essential for initiating with Meld strikes and repositioning. Desolator synergizes perfectly with Meld's armor reduction, allowing her to deal massive physical burst damage. A Dragon Lance or Hurricane Pike is also highly recommended to increase her attack range, survivability, and synergy with Psi Blades and the Hidden Reach Facet.
search_text: Core Build Blink Dagger Desolator Dragon Lance Hurricane Pike essential items

display_text: In teamfights, Templar Assassin's role is to assassinate key backline heroes (supports, squishy carries) with a Blink + Meld + Refraction combo. She should place Traps beforehand to control chokepoints and slow enemies. She uses Psionic Projection to teleport to a trap for repositioning or escape. She relies on her Refraction shield to survive initial bursts. She should position to maximize Psi Blades spill damage on multiple enemies, especially with the Voidblades Facet.
search_text: Teamfight Role Assassin burst kill backline Blink Meld Trap Psionic Projection reposition

display_text: Ability: Refraction. Grants 15/30/45/60 bonus damage per attack and a 30 HP damage barrier per instance. Has 3/4/5/6 total instances (shield and damage combined). Lasts 17 seconds. If a damage instance exceeds the remaining barrier, all damage is absorbed. Can be cast while disabled with talent. Is dispelled by basic dispels. Damage as HP Removal bypasses Refraction.
search_text: Refraction Q ability shield barrier 30 hp per instance 3 4 5 6 charges bonus damage 15 30 45 60 duration 17

display_text: Ability: Meld. Makes Templar Assassin invisible while stationary. Breaking invisibility by attacking deals 50/100/150/200 bonus damage and reduces the target's armor by 2/4/6/8 for 6 seconds. Meld is broken by any action except staying still or channeling Psionic Projection. The armor reduction does not work on buildings.
search_text: Meld W ability invisibility stationary break attack bonus damage 50 100 150 200 armor reduction 2 4 6 8 debuff 6 seconds

display_text: Ability: Psi Blades. Passive. Increases attack range by 50/100/150/200. Attacks spill damage to units behind the primary target within a 90-degree width and up to 400/500/600/700 range. Spill damage is 70%/80%/90%/100% of the attack damage, reduced for each subsequent unit hit. Spill damage hits invisible units. On-hit effects only apply to the main target.
search_text: Psi Blades E passive ability attack range increase 50 100 150 200 spill damage 70 80 90 100 percent spill range 400 500 600 700 width 90 degrees

display_text: Ability: Trap. Springs the trap nearest to Templar Assassin, or the trap closest to the cursor if alt-cast. The trap slows nearby enemies. This ability is used to activate placed Psionic Traps.
search_text: Trap D ability activate spring trigger slow alt-cast

display_text: Ability: Psionic Projection. Channel for 2 seconds to teleport to any Psionic Trap, detonating it upon arrival as if fully charged and dealing 400 bonus damage instantly. Channeling does not break Meld invisibility.
search_text: Psionic Projection F ability channel 2 seconds teleport to Trap detonate fully charged instant 400 bonus damage does not break Meld

display_text: Ability: Psionic Trap. Places an invisible, spell immune trap that lasts indefinitely. Max traps: 5/8/11. Traps fade in over 2 seconds. When sprung, they deal 200/300/400 bonus damage when fully charged over 3.5 seconds, and slow enemies in a 400 radius. Slow starts at 20% and increases up to 50% after 3.5 seconds charge time. Traps have 250/250 vision. They take 2 hits from ranged heroes, 1 from melee heroes, and 4 from creeps to kill.

display_text: Scenario: Vs. High Armor/High HP Cores. Prioritize Meld's armor reduction and build Desolator. Consider a Daedalus for critical strike burst to bypass high armor through raw damage. The Voidblades Facet is excellent here to apply the armor reduction to multiple frontliners. Use Traps to slow and kite them.
search_text: Situational High Armor High HP cores Desolator Daedalus Meld armor reduction Voidblades Facet kite

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Focus on farming with Psi Blades spill and Refraction shield to survive jungle camps. Use Traps defensively to spot ganks on your side of the map. Itemize for survivability (Black King Bar, Linken's Sphere) and split-push (Boots of Travel) rather than pure damage. The Refractor Facet can help secure comeback kills.

display_text: Scenario: Vs. Illusion/Summon Heroes. Your limited Refraction charges can be drained quickly. Avoid man-fighting. Use Psionic Traps for area control and to identify the real hero. Build Battle Fury or Mjollnir for cleave and AoE damage to clear illusions. The Voidblades Facet can help but is risky.

display_text: Scenario: Need to Siege High Ground. Use the Hidden Reach Facet to gain massive attack range while in Meld, allowing you to hit buildings and heroes from a very safe distance. Place Traps on the enemy high ground ramp for vision and slow. Have a Hurricane Pike for extra range and disengage.
search_text: Situational Siege High Ground Hidden Reach Facet Meld attack range buildings Traps vision Hurricane Pike

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Invisible Heroes. Place Psionic Traps in common paths to provide vision and slow them when they pass. Your Psi Blades spill damage hits invisible units, so you can sometimes reveal them by attacking a creep near their suspected location. Carry Dust of Appearance or ask supports for Sentry Wards.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Heavy Magic Burst. Refraction shield blocks instances of damage, but many spells can pop it quickly. Prioritize a Black King Bar to become spell immune. The Level 15 talent 'Refraction Can Be Cast While Disabled' is crucial against instant disables like Lion Hex. Consider a Linken's Sphere against single-target initiations.

display_text: Talent Level 10: Choose between +1.5s Meld Debuff Duration (more time for you and your team to capitalize on the -armor) or +10% Psionic Trap Slow (better chase, control, and kill secure). The Meld talent is better for aggressive kill lanes and physical damage lineups. The Trap talent is better for utility, kiting, and if you are being focused on placing traps in fights.

display_text: Talent Level 15: Choose between Refraction Can Be Cast While Disabled (crucial survival tool against instant disables, allows you to always get your shield up) or +3 Meld Armor Reduction (increases your burst damage from -8 to -11 armor, a significant increase). The Refraction talent is almost always better for survivability. The Meld talent is for when you are snowballing and the enemy has minimal lockdown.
search_text: Talent Level 15 left Refraction Cast While Disabled right +3 Meld Armor Reduction survivability lockdown burst damage snowball

display_text: Talent Level 20: Choose between Refraction Dispels (applies a basic dispel on cast, removing silences, slows, etc. - a powerful defensive and offensive tool) or +40 Refraction Damage (increases bonus damage from 60 to 100 per hit, a massive DPS increase). The Dispels talent is superior in most games as it provides crucial utility and purge. The Damage talent is for when you are far ahead and need to end quickly, or against lineups with few debuffs.
search_text: Talent Level 20 left Refraction Dispels basic dispel right +40 Refraction Damage 100 bonus damage per hit utility DPS

display_text: Talent Level 25: Choose between 0.8s Meld Hit Bash (provides a reliable mini-stun on your Meld strike, incredible for locking down key targets and interrupting channels) or +4 Refraction Instances (increases total instances from 6 to 10, making you incredibly tanky and increasing your damage output duration). The Bash talent is for pick-offs and control. The Instances talent is for sustained man-fighting and survivability against heavy damage. Both are extremely strong.

display_text: Viper is a ranged agility hero with base 21 Strength, 21 Agility, and 17 Intelligence. He has a slow 1.7 base attack time and is a ranged attacker. Compared to average heroes, his balanced starting attributes offer no extreme strengths or weaknesses. His ranged nature combined with his Poison Attack skill defines his harass-heavy laning phase.
search_text: Viper base stats attack type ranged agility hero base attack time 1.7

display_text: Viper has two Facets: Poison Burst and Caustic Bath. Poison Burst causes enemies around a target to take damage when Poison Attack stacks expire, enhancing his area damage potential in teamfights. Caustic Bath increases Corrosive Skin's effects while Viper stands inside his own Nethertoxin, significantly boosting his durability and damage output when he can control the area. Choose Poison Burst for maximizing teamfight splash damage against grouped enemies. Choose Caustic Bath for a more front-line, tanky playstyle where you can fight inside your Nethertoxin zones.
search_text: Viper Facets Poison Burst Caustic Bath area damage teamfight durability front-line

display_text: In the laning phase, prioritize Poison Attack. Its 4/8/12/16 damage per second over 4 seconds and 25 bonus range allows you to harass enemies safely. Use it to apply stacking slows and magic resistance reduction. Your goal is to dominate your lane by constantly applying pressure with this orb-walking ability, denying farm and securing last hits. Nethertoxin can be taken at level 2 to help secure ranged creeps or harass enemies who stand still, but Poison Attack is your primary tool.
search_text: Viper laning phase Poison Attack harass orb-walk 4 8 12 16 damage per second 25 bonus range slow magic resistance reduction

display_text: Viper's key power spikes are: Level 6 with Viper Strike for kill threat. Level 12 with maxed Nethertoxin and Corrosive Skin for strong mid-game pushing and fighting. Level 18 with maxed ultimate. Talent levels: 10 for utility, 15 for increased damage or slow, 20 for significant Corrosive Skin boost, and 25 for a major Viper Strike cooldown/manacost reduction or a dynamic Nethertoxin. Essential item timings include an early Magic Wand and Power Treads, followed by Dragon Lance for range and stats, and a defensive item like Black King Bar or a damage item like Hurricane Pike.
search_text: Viper power spikes level 6 Viper Strike level 12 Nethertoxin Corrosive Skin level 18 talent levels 10 15 20 25 items Dragon Lance Black King Bar

display_text: Viper is hard countered by heroes or items that apply Break. Break disables his Corrosive Skin passive (and the Predator passive). This makes him significantly less tanky and reduces his damage output. Instant disables like Hex prevent him from using his abilities or items to escape. High magic burst damage can also bypass his Corrosive Skin magic resistance if he is caught without it active.
search_text: Viper counters Break disables Corrosive Skin Predator passive Hex instant disable magic burst

display_text: Core build for Viper consists of: 1. Power Treads for attack speed and attribute toggling. 2. Dragon Lance (and later Hurricane Pike) to increase his attack range, survivability, and provide an escape/disengage. 3. Black King Bar is almost always essential to allow him to stand and fight without being disabled, maximizing his Poison Attack and Predator damage output.
search_text: Viper core items Power Treads Dragon Lance Hurricane Pike Black King Bar BKB

display_text: In teamfights, Viper's role is to be a front-line damage dealer and anti-carry. Use Viper Strike on the enemy's key right-clicker or mobile core to slow them and disable their passives. Position yourself to apply Poison Attack stacks to multiple targets. Drop Nethertoxin on choke points or clumped enemies to reduce their attack speed and deal damage over time. Your Corrosive Skin makes you deceptively tanky against magic damage, allowing you to absorb spells while your damage-over-time effects whittle down the enemy team.
search_text: Viper teamfight role front-line damage dealer anti-carry Viper Strike slow disable passives Poison Attack stacks Nethertoxin attack speed slow Corrosive Skin tanky

display_text: Poison Attack: An active ability that intensifies Viper's next attack. It deals 4/8/12/16 damage per second over 4 seconds, grants 25 bonus attack range, and applies a stacking movement speed slow (3/6/9/12%) and magic resistance reduction (4/6/8/10). Stacks up to 6 times. Does NOT pierce BKB (no pierce_immunity attribute found).
search_text: Poison Attack Q Viper skill ability orb attack 4 8 12 16 dps 25 range slow 3 6 9 12 percent magic resist reduction 4 6 8 10 stacks 6

display_text: Nethertoxin: Viper releases a toxin at a target 400 radius area. Units inside take increasing damage over time based on duration, from 15/25/35/45 minimum to 35/65/95/125 maximum damage. Their attack speed is slowed by 30/40/50/60%. The effect lasts 6.5/7/7.5/8 seconds. Multiple Nethertoxins do not stack. Does NOT pierce BKB (no pierce_immunity attribute found).
search_text: Nethertoxin W Viper ability area damage over time DoT 15 25 35 45 min 35 65 95 125 max attack slow 30 40 50 60 percent duration 6.5 7 7.5 8 radius 400

display_text: Corrosive Skin: A passive ability. Any enemy that damages Viper within 1200 range takes 8/16/24/32 damage per second and has their attack speed slowed by 9/18/27/36% for 4 seconds. Viper also gains 10/15/20/25% bonus magic resistance. Break prevents application of this passive. Does NOT pierce BKB (no pierce_immunity attribute found).

display_text: Scenario: Versus High Armor or High HP Heroes (e.g., Dragon Knight, Centaur). Your Predator passive deals physical damage based on missing health, which is effective, but their high armor reduces it. Prioritize items that reduce armor (e.g., Desolator) or provide percentage-based damage (e.g., Eye of Skadi). Your magic damage from Poison Attack and Nethertoxin remains reliable. Use Nethertoxin's attack speed slow to cripple their right-click.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. If you are underfarmed, focus on being a utility hero. Max Corrosive Skin for survivability. Build cheaper, survivability items like Magic Wand, Bracer, and Hood of Defiance. Use Viper Strike defensively to slow diving enemies. Farm with Nethertoxin to safely clear waves. Your role shifts to providing vision and control with your slows rather than being the primary damage dealer.

display_text: Luna is an Agility ranged carry with average starting stats and a fast attack projectile. Her base agility of 24 is high, providing decent starting armor. With a base attack time of 1.7, her attack speed is standard. She has no innate escape, relying on her Night Vision and her Facet choice for survivability.

display_text: Luna has three Facets. Lunar Orbit: Grants standard glaives for area damage. This is the default farming and fighting build, focusing on raw output. Moonshield: Lunar Orbit now provides damage reduction while active, trading some offensive power for significant survivability in fights. Moonstorm: Hitting enemies with Lucent Beams or Eclipse grants Luna bonus attack damage. This turns Luna into a heavy burst/spellcaster, incentivizing aggressive use of her spells to stack damage buffs.

display_text: Luna's laning is defined by her high base damage from Lunar Blessing. Focus on securing last hits and denying with this passive advantage. Level 1 Lucent Beam is a strong harass tool with a ministun. Depending on Facet choice, strategy varies. With Lunar Orbit, use the glaives to harass and secure ranged creeps. With Moonshield, use it defensively to mitigate enemy harass. With Moonstorm, prioritize landing Lucent Beams to build your damage buff early. Keep vision high at night due to global Lunar Blessing and extra Night Vision.

display_text: Luna's power spikes are level dependent. Level 6 with Eclipse provides a huge kill threat, especially with a point in Lucent Beam. Level 12 with maxed Moon Glaives accelerates her farm dramatically. Level 18 with maxed Lucent Beam and talents significantly increases her burst damage. Level 20 Talent (+30 Lunar Blessing Damage) is a massive teamfight steroid. Level 25 Talent choice defines her late game: Eclipse Cooldown reduction for frequent fights or Lucent Beam Bounce for immense teamfight control. Core item timings like Manta Style (for illusions to benefit from Glaives) and Butterfly/Skadi define her mid-game dominance.

display_text: Luna is countered by heroes with high physical burst or lockdown that can bypass her team. Instant Hex (Lion, Shadow Shaman) prevents her from using any abilities or items. Break (Doom, Silver Edge) disables her Lunar Blessing and Moon Glaives passives, crippling her damage output. High armor and damage block (Knight's Vow, Tidehunter) significantly reduce the effectiveness of her bouncing Moon Glaives. Heroes who can quickly close the gap and manfight her (Ursa, Troll Warlord) exploit her lack of innate escape.

Manta Style is absolutely core; her illusions benefit fully from Moon Glaives and Lunar Blessing, massively increasing her farming speed and split-push threat. Butterfly provides her with the Agility, Attack Speed, and Evasion she needs to manfight other right-click carries in the late game.

display_text: In a teamfight, Luna's role is to deal sustained AoE physical damage from the backline with her Moon Glaives. She should look to initiate with Eclipse on key targets, especially if she has the Moonstorm Facet to build attack damage. After her ultimate, she focuses on hitting the frontmost hero to let her Glaives bounce to the backline supports. With the Moonshield Facet, she can play more aggressively. Her Lucent Beam provides a reliable, low-cooldown ministun to interrupt channels or lock down fleeing enemies.

Each beam deals damage equal to Luna's current level of Lucent Beam (75/150/225/300). Beams do not stun. A single target can be hit a maximum of 5 times. display_text: At level 10, the choice is between -5% Moon Glaives Damage Reduction and +0.5s Lucent Beam Ministun. The left talent (-5% damage reduction) is for a pure farming build, making your Glaives bounce for almost full damage, significantly accelerating farm speed. The right talent (+0.5s stun) is for a fighting build, turning Lucent Beam into a 1.1 second stun on a short cooldown, providing much better lockdown and kill potential in the early mid-game.

Lucent Beam Hits An Additional Target adds a bounce mechanic within a specified range, effectively doubling its impact in fights and providing AoE lockdown, which is exceptionally powerful for control and against grouped enemies.
