display_text: Facet choice defines Dazzle's playstyle. Nothl Boon (Facet 1) empowers all his basic abilities globally: Poison Touch hexes enemies (1.4/1.6/1.8s), Shallow Grave heals on expiration (225/300/375 HP), and Shadow Wave cooldown is reduced by 30%/40%/50%. This makes him a much more aggressive, high-uptime healer and disabler from a safe distance. Poison Bloom (Facet 2) makes Poison Touch spread on attack to nearby enemies and strengthens Weave's armor reduction on enemies. This turns him into a core-focused right-click amplifier who excels at shredding armor for physical damage teams.

display_text: In the laning phase, Dazzle's primary tool is Poison Touch (Q). It deals 16/28/40/52 damage per second over 3.5/5/6.5/8 seconds and applies a 13%/16%/19%/22% slow that increases by an additional 2/2.5/3/3.5% each time Dazzle attacks the target, refreshing the full duration. Max this first. Use it aggressively to secure denies and harass. Shadow Wave (W) can be used at level 1 or 2 to heal your carry and deal 85/105/125/145 damage to nearby enemies if they are clustered. Your low armor and HP make positioning crucial.

Level 12 gives access to his Shard ability (Nothl Projection), radically changing his playstyle and safety. Level 18 is his maximum Weave armor swing. Talent levels 10, 15, 20, and 25 each offer significant, build-defining choices. Core items like Arcane Boots (into Guardian Greaves) and Solar Crest are essential spikes. Aghanim's Scepter is a huge spike, making Shadow Wave heal for 100% of its damage value.

Anti-heal effects (like Spirit Vessel) reduce the impact of his heals.

display_text: Ability: Nothl Projection (Shard Ability). Dazzle becomes an invulnerable, spell-casting spirit tethered to his body for 5-12 seconds. He can cast spells, attack, and use items in this form. While active, his basic abilities are empowered: Poison Touch hexes enemies for 1.4/1.6/1.8s, Shallow Grave heals the target for 225/300/375 HP upon expiration, and Shadow Wave cooldown is reduced by 30%/40%/50%. If the spirit goes beyond 1600 units from the body, a leash pulls it back with increasing strength. The spirit moves at 1200 speed initially and returns to the body in 0.75s when the spell ends. Cannot attack buildings.

display_text: Clockwerk's Facet choice depends on his role. 'Hookup' (Rattletrap Hookup) provides bonus armor and cooldown/manacost refund for Hookshot if an ally is near the target, enhancing teamfight initiation and survivability. 'Expanded Armature' (Rattletrap Expanded Armature) makes Power Cogs form in a larger radius, making it more reliable for trapping and zoning enemies. 'Chainmeal' (Rattletrap Junk Mail) allows Clockwerk to permanently gain armor by consuming Chainmails, scaling his passive damage and tankiness in a core/greedy role.

display_text: In lane, use Battery Assault to harass and secure last hits, as it deals 20/45/70/95 damage per tick with a 0.7-second interval and a 275 radius. It's your primary trading tool. Skill Rocket Flare at level 2 or 4 for vision and global harass or last hits (80/120/160/200 damage). Use Power Cogs carefully to trap enemies for kills or to block their path. Your low armor makes you vulnerable to physical harass, so prioritize stats and regen items.

display_text: Chronoptic Nourishment: Leshrac's attacks restore mana. This facilitates a sustained ability-spam playstyle in lane and later fights, rewarding you for weaving in auto-attacks between spells, especially valuable with his high mana costs. Misanthropy: Diabolic Edict explosion rate increased but it no longer damages buildings. This transforms Leshrac from a pushing hero into a pure hero-fight specialist, significantly boosting his single-target burst damage potential against enemy heroes at the cost of all building damage.

Prioritize Split Earth (Q) for its 1.7 second stun or Lightning Storm (E) for its long-range 75% slow to secure kills with a support. His low attack speed makes last-hitting difficult; rely on spells. Diabolic Edict (W) can be used at level 1 or 2 to threaten kills or push the wave. Manage mana carefully. Leshrac is vulnerable to ganks due to low mobility and armor.

display_text: Nature's Prophet has three Facets. Soothing Saplings (ID 105) makes Sprout heal allies near the created trees, supporting teamfight sustain. Ironwood Treant (ID 106) creates a single, powerful Reinforced Treant from Nature's Call, focused on pushing and fighting. Nature's Profit (ID 325) generates gold from hero kills nearby, accelerating his economy as a semicarry. Choose Soothing Saplings for team-oriented setups, Ironwood Treant for aggressive split-pushing, and Nature's Profit for a greedier core role.

display_text: In lane, Nature's Prophet uses Sprout (70/130/190/250 damage) to harass and secure range creep last hits. His low base damage and armor make him vulnerable to aggression. Use Treants from Nature's Call to harass, block camps, and secure farm. Prioritize Teleportation for escapes or to join early fights. Focus on acquiring core items like Power Treads and Orchid Malevolence to enable ganks and secure solo kills with Sprout's isolation.

display_text: Facet 1: Suppressive Fire. Strafe attacks cause enemies to miss more often. This adds a blind effect to the attack speed steroid, significantly improving Clinkz's man-fight potential and survivability against right-click heavy lineups. It makes him more of a direct, sustained DPS threat. Facet 2: Engulfing Step. Death Pact teleports Clinkz towards his victim and reduces its cooldown. This vastly improves Clinkz's farming speed, mobility, and pick-off potential. It facilitates a hit-and-run or ganking playstyle, allowing him to close gaps and clear camps more efficiently. Facet 3: Trial By Pyre. This facet is noted as having no effect.
search_text: clinkz facets suppressive fire strafe blind miss engulfing step death pact teleport cooldown reduction trial by pyre does nothing

display_text: In lane, focus on securing last hits with your high base damage. Use Searing Arrows (hotkey: W) for harass and secure ranged creeps; it deals bonus 20/35/50/65 physical damage. Skeleton Walk (hotkey: E) is a defensive escape tool with a 35/40/45 second duration and a long 24/21/18 second cooldown; use it judiciously to avoid ganks. Your goal is to reach level 6 for Death Pact to gain health (175/250/325/400 HP) from a neutral creep, massively improving your survivability and farming sustain.
search_text: clinkz laning phase last hit searing arrows harass skeleton walk escape death pact level 6 health bonus farming sustain early game

display_text: Level 6 is your first major spike, unlocking Death Pact for 400 bonus HP and improved jungle farming. Level 12 with maxed Searing Arrows (+65 damage) and a Dragon Lance or Orchid Malevolence makes you a strong pick-off hero. Level 18 with maxed talents and Burning Barrage allows for high teamfight AOE damage. Talent level 20 offers +350 Death Pact Bonus Health for tankiness or +40 Strafe Attack Speed for DPS. Level 25 talent enables Searing Arrows Multishot, which is a massive damage multiplier in fights.
search_text: Clinkz power spikes level 6 death pact level 12 searing arrows orchid level 18 burning barrage talent 20 25 multishot attack speed health

display_text: Clinkz is hard countered by heroes with instant disables or silences that prevent him from using Skeleton Walk (e.g., Storm Bolt, Hex, Silence). He is also countered by high burst damage due to his low health pool, even after Death Pact. Break effects (e.g., Silver Edge) will disable his Infernal Shred passive and his Facets. Heroes with AoE damage and wave clear can kill his fragile Skeleton Archers, reducing his damage output in fights.
search_text: counter Clinkz silence disable burst damage break silver edge infernal shred dead stop skeleton walk kill skeletons

display_text: 1. Dragon Lance (upgraded to Hurricane Pike): Essential for increasing your attack range and survivability. 2. Orchid Malevolence (or Bloodthorn): Solves mana issues, provides silence for pick-offs, and amplifies your single-target burst. 3. Black King Bar (BKB): Critical for ensuring you can channel Burning Barrage and deal sustained damage in teamfights without being locked down.
search_text: clinkz core build items dragon lance hurricane pike orchid malevolence bloodthorn black king bar bkb

display_text: In teamfights, Clinkz's role is to position safely and focus a high-priority target. Use Strafe to melt a key hero from a distance. If the enemy is clumped, channel Burning Barrage through them for massive area damage. Use Skeleton Walk and your high movement speed to reposition or escape. Your Skeleton Archers provide additional single-target DPS, and your Infernal Shred passive helps reduce the effectiveness of enemy armor.
search_text: clinkz teamfight role position focus target strafe attack speed burning barrage channel aoe damage skeleton walk reposition archers dps

display_text: Ability: Strafe (Q). Clinkz gains 120/160/200/240 attack speed and 200 bonus attack range for 3.5 seconds. Cooldown: 30/25/20/15 seconds. Any current and future Skeleton Archers within 1200 radius also gain the bonus attack range and 50% of the attack speed bonus. Casting Strafe does not break Skeleton Walk invisibility.
search_text: clinkz ability Q strafe attack speed 120 160 200 240 range bonus 200 duration 3.5 cooldown 30 25 20 15 buffs skeletons

display_text: Ability: Searing Arrows (W). A toggleable attack modifier. Imbues Clinkz's arrows with fire for 20/35/50/65 bonus physical damage per attack. Cast Range: 600. Skeleton Archers will fire Searing Arrows at targets Clinkz attacks for 50% damage. Deals damage to structures.
search_text: clinkz ability W searing arrows toggle attack modifier bonus damage 20 35 50 65 physical cast range 600 skeletons 50% damage buildings

display_text: Ability: Death Pact (R). Target Enemy Creep or Friendly Skeleton Archer. Consumes the target, healing Clinkz and granting him 175/250/325/400 bonus max health for 45 seconds. Cooldown per Charge: 40 seconds. Starts with 1/1/2/2 charges. Does not take Clinkz out of Skeleton Walk. Gain experience and gold if the target is an enemy creep.
search_text: clinkz ability R death pact consume creep skeleton heal bonus health 175 250 325 400 duration 45 charges 1 1 2 2 restore 40s

display_text: Ability: Burning Barrage (R - New). Channeled for up to 3 seconds. Clinkz shoots 6 waves of piercing arrows in a target direction up to 850 range (increased by Attack Range bonuses). Arrows hit all enemy units, dealing 75% of Clinkz's attack damage and applying attack modifiers. Projectile width is 200.
search_text: clinkz ability R burning barrage channeled pierce arrows 6 waves 75% attack damage applies modifiers range 850 width 200

display_text: Ability: Burning Army (Ultimate). Summons a row of 6 Burning Skeleton Archers in the target location, 900 range. They spawn over a 0.6-second interval. The Skeleton Archers are permanent until killed.
search_text: clinkz ability ultimate burning army summon 6 skeleton archers row location range 900 spawn interval 0.1

display_text: Ability: Skeleton Walk (E). Clinkz becomes invisible and gains 25%/35%/45% movement speed for up to 35/40/45 seconds. Fade time is 0.6 seconds. Cooldown: 24/21/18 seconds. Leaving invisibility creates 2/3/4 Skeleton Archers that last 20/25/30 seconds. Skeletons are immobile, die in 2 hits from heroes or towers, deal 20% of Clinkz's damage, and deal 25% less damage to buildings. Their attack range equals Clinkz's.
search_text: clinkz ability E skeleton walk invisibility move speed bonus 25 35 45 duration 35 40 45 fade 0.6s cooldown 24 21 18 spawn skeletons 2 3 4

display_text: Ability: Infernal Shred (Passive). Clinkz and his skeletons apply a stacking debuff on attack that pierces up to 20 of the target's total physical armor. Clinkz applies 3% per attack, skeletons apply 1%. Stacks last 5 seconds. This does not reduce the target's armor, but ignores it for damage calculation.
search_text: clinkz passive infernal shred armor pierce up to 20 stacks clinkz 3% skeleton 1% per attack duration 5 seconds

display_text: Scenario: Against High Armor / Strength Cores. Itemize for armor reduction and pure/magic damage. Your Infernal Shred passive helps, but you may need a Daedalus for critical strike burst or a Bloodthorn for true strike and magic damage amp. Burning Barrage's damage is physical and reduced by armor, so prioritize single-target focus with Strafe and Searing Arrows.
search_text: clinkz vs high armor strength daedalus bloodthorn infernal shred burning barrage physical damage single target focus

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. If you are underleveled and underfarmed, avoid man-fights. Use Skeleton Walk to split push lanes safely. Farm jungle with Death Pact on large creeps for sustain. Build cost-effective stats like Wraith Bands and a Falcon Blade. Your Burning Barrage can help defend high ground by clearing waves from a safe distance.
search_text: clinkz playing from behind split push skeleton walk farm jungle death pact wraith band falcon blade defend high ground

display_text: Scenario: Vs Illusion Heroes (e.g., Chaos Knight, Phantom Lancer). Your Burning Barrage is excellent for clearing illusions due to its piercing nature. Build a Battle Fury or Maelstrom/Mjollnir for additional cleave/proc damage. Avoid getting surrounded; use Strafe and Searing Arrows to focus the real hero once identified.
search_text: clinkz vs illusions chaos knight phantom lancer burning barrage clears maelstrom mjollnir battle fury focus real hero

display_text: Scenario: Need to End Game Early. If you have a strong early lead, pressure towers aggressively with your Skeleton Archers and Searing Arrows. Build Desolator to melt buildings and heroes. Group with your team and use Strafe to take Roshan quickly. Your physical damage peaks earlier than many carries.
search_text: clinkz end game early pressure towers desolator skeleton archers searing arrows group roshan strafe attack speed

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Invisible Heroes. Carry Dust of Appearance and Sentry Wards. Consider building a Silver Edge for its own break and invisibility, or a Bloodthorn for true strike on revealed targets. Your Skeleton Archers will not reveal invis units but can attack them if they are already revealed.
search_text: clinkz vs invisible heroes dust sentry wards silver edge bloodthorn true strike skeleton archers attack

display_text: Scenario: Need to Initiate or Chase. With the Engulfing Step Facet, Death Pact becomes a targeted teleport. Use it on a lane creep or neutral to close distance on an enemy, then use Orchid/Scythe to lock them down. This facet turns Death Pact into a powerful mobility and initiation tool.
search_text: clinkz initiate chase engulfing step facet death pact teleport creep close gap orchid scythe hex lockdown

display_text: Talent Level 10. Left: +0.75s Strafe Duration. Right: -4s Skeleton Walk Cooldown. Choose the Strafe duration if you are focusing on fights and man-fighting, as it gives you more time to deal damage. Choose the Skeleton Walk cooldown if you need more frequent escapes, mobility for farming, or are against aggressive gankers.
search_text: clinkz talent level 10 left +0.75 strafe duration right -4s skeleton walk cooldown fight vs mobility

display_text: Talent Level 15. Left: +60 Attack Range. Right: -10s Death Pact Charge Restore Time. The attack range talent synergizes with Strafe and Dragon Lance, making you much safer in fights. The Death Pact cooldown talent is for high-tempo play where you are constantly fighting and need the heal and HP buff more often, especially with the Engulfing Step facet.
search_text: clinkz talent level 15 left +60 attack range right -10s death pact charge restore time safe dps vs sustain uptime

display_text: Talent Level 20. Left: +350 Death Pact Bonus Health. Right: +40 Strafe Attack Speed. The health talent makes you significantly tankier, crucial if you are being focused. The attack speed talent is a massive DPS increase, ideal if you have survivability from items (BKB, Satanic) and can freely attack.
search_text: clinkz talent level 20 left +350 death pact health right +40 strafe attack speed survivability vs pure damage

display_text: Talent Level 25. Left: -9s Strafe Cooldown. Right: Searing Arrows Multishot (attacks fire an extra arrow at a random enemy in range). The cooldown reduction makes Strafe almost always available. The Multishot talent is one of the highest DPS talents in the game, effectively doubling your output in fights against multiple heroes. Almost always take Multishot.
search_text: clinkz talent level 25 left -9s strafe cooldown right searing arrows multishot extra arrow random enemy high dps

display_text: Omniknight is a Melee Strength hero with a base Strength of 22, Agility of 15, and Intelligence of 16. His attack rate is 1.7. This gives him a fairly standard Strength hero base damage and survivability, relying on his abilities for impact due to his lack of inherent mobility.
search_text: Omniknight stats melee strength 22 agility 15 intelligence 16 attack rate 1.7 base damage armor health

display_text: Omniknight has a choice of two Facets. Omnipresent causes Omniknight to deal more damage to heroes inside his Degen Aura. This facet encourages a more aggressive, core-style play, maximizing damage in fights. The Dmg/Heals facet focuses on enhancing the impact of his core healing and purification spells.
search_text: Omniknight facets Omnipresent degen aura hero damage Dmg/Heals purification heal facet strategy playstyle

display_text: In lane, use Purification (Q) to secure ranged creeps with its damage and simultaneously heal your laning partner or yourself from harass. Level 1 Repel (W) provides a strong trading tool with its debuff immunity and magic resistance, allowing your core to be aggressive. Hammer of Purity (E) can be used for last hitting under pressure or to secure kills with its slow. Focus on mana management to sustain your spells. As a melee hero, positioning is key to avoid being kited.
search_text: Omniknight laning phase level 1 10 minutes Purification heal nuke creep secure Repel magic immunity trade Hammer of Purity slow last hit mana

display_text: Key power spikes are Level 6 (Guardian Angel), providing 5 seconds of physical immunity in a teamfight. Level 12 with a maxed Purification (level 4) provides strong burst heal and damage. Level 18 for maxed ultimate duration (7 seconds). Level 20 talent for +2s Guardian Angel Duration or -3s Purification Cooldown significantly impacts fight tempo. Level 25 for +160 Purification Damage/Heal or +75% Hammer of Purity Bonus Base Damage defines late-game role. Item spikes like Holy Locket, Force Staff, or Aghanim's Scepter enhance his utility and survivability.
search_text: Omniknight power spikes level 6 Guardian Angel 5 seconds physical immunity level 12 Purification max level 18 ultimate 7s duration talent 20 25 timing items

display_text: Omniknight is hard countered by heroes with Break (Silver Edge, Hoodwink, Viper) which disables his Degen Aura and the Facet bonus from Omnipresent. High magical burst damage that can bypass or overwhelm Repel's 60% magic resist, like Zeus or Lina. Pure damage abilities that ignore Guardian Angel's physical immunity, such as Timbersaw's spells or Enigma's Midnight Pulse. Diffusal Blade and mana burn effects (Anti-Mage, Nyx Assassin) cripple his mana pool, preventing spell casts.
search_text: Omniknight counters break Silver Edge Viper disables Degen Aura Omnipresent magic burst damage Repel 60% resist Guardian Angel physical immunity pure damage Timbersaw Enigma mana burn Diffusal Blade

display_text: Core items for Omniknight are Soul Ring to solve his mana issues, enabling consistent Purification and Repel casts. Holy Locket amplifies all his healing, including Purification and the HP regen from Repel. Force Staff or Glimmer Cape provides crucial positioning tools for saving allies or yourself, as Omniknight lacks mobility.
search_text: Omniknight core build items essential Soul Ring mana Holy Locket heal amplification Purification Repel Force Staff Glimmer Cape save positioning

display_text: In teamfights, Omniknight's primary role is as a defensive support and counter-initiator. Use Guardian Angel (R) to negate all physical damage for your team for 5/6/7 seconds. Prioritize Repel (W) on your core carry to prevent disables and reduce magic damage. Use Purification (Q) to burst heal a key ally while damaging nearby enemies. Position yourself to apply Degen Aura's 10/20/30/40% slow to enemy melee cores. Use Hammer of Purity (E) to slow and burst priority targets. Your job is to extend the fight duration, making your team unkillable.
search_text: Omniknight teamfight role 5v5 Guardian Angel physical immunity Repel debuff immunity magic resist Purification heal nuke Degen Aura slow defensive support

display_text: Ability: Purification (Q). Instantly heals a friendly unit for 90/160/230/300 health and damages all nearby enemy units in a 260 radius for the same amount. The cooldown is 15/14/13/12 seconds. This is a targeted ability. It does not pierce BKB. The heal and damage are instant and simultaneous.
search_text: Purification Q heal 90 160 230 300 damage nuke radius 260 cooldown 15 14 13 12 ally enemy targeted ability

display_text: Ability: Repel (W). Grants Debuff Immunity with 60% magic resistance to the target for 5 seconds. Provides bonus Strength and HP Regen based on the amount of Debuffs on the target. Grants 6/12/18/24 base Strength and 8/12/16/20 base HP regen, plus an additional 3/4/5/6 Strength per debuff on the target. This is a targeted buff. It pierces BKB as it is a buff applied to an ally.
search_text: Repel W debuff immunity magic resistance 60% duration 5s strength bonus 6 12 18 24 hp regen 8 12 16 20 per debuff 3 4 5 6 target ally buff

display_text: Ability: Hammer of Purity (E). Omniknight's next attack has +75 range, deals 55/70/85/100 base pure damage, plus an additional 25/50/75/100 bonus pure damage (total 80/120/160/200), and slows the target's movement by 75% for 0.2 seconds. The cooldown is 20/15/10/5 seconds and it reduces attack cooldown by 1. This is an autocast ability. It does not pierce BKB (the slow does not affect BKB'd targets).
search_text: Hammer of Purity E attack modifier range +75 pure damage base 55 70 85 100 bonus 25 50 75 100 total 80 120 160 200 slow 75% 0.2s cooldown 20 15 10 5 autocast

display_text: Ability: Degen Aura (Passive). Passively slows the movement speed of enemy units within a 325 radius of Omniknight by 10/20/30/40%. The slow lingers for 1 second after leaving the aura. Does not affect debuff immune units (BKB). This is an aura slow.
search_text: Degen Aura passive slow movement 10% 20% 30% 40% radius 325 linger 1s aura does not pierce BKB debuff immune

display_text: Ability: Guardian Angel (R). Grants immunity from physical damage to all allied units in a 400 radius for 5/6/7 seconds. Cooldown is 100/90/80 seconds. Cast range is 600. Does not affect buildings. Does not pierce BKB (it is a buff applied to allies, unaffected by enemy BKB).
search_text: Guardian Angel R ultimate physical damage immunity allies radius 400 duration 5 6 7 cooldown 100 90 80 cast range 600

display_text: Scenario: Vs. Heavy Magic Damage Lineup. Prioritize leveling Repel early. Use it preemptively on your team's initiator or core before they take magic burst. Consider items like Pipe of Insight and Glimmer Cape. Your Guardian Angel does not protect against magic, so positioning and Repel timing are critical. The Omnipresent facet is less valuable here.
search_text: Omniknight scenario heavy magic damage lineup Repel priority Pipe of Insight Glimmer Cape positioning Guardian Angel physical only

display_text: Scenario: Vs. High Physical Damage / Right-Click Cores. This is Omniknight's ideal scenario. Max Guardian Angel at 6/12/18. Coordinate teamfights around its 5/6/7 second duration. Items like Solar Crest or Vladmir's Offering amplify your team's physical presence during the immunity. The Degen Aura slow is very effective against melee carries. Both facets can work, but Omnipresent adds damage against heroes you are already slowing.
search_text: Omniknight scenario high physical damage right-click cores Guardian Angel timing Solar Crest Vladmir Offering Degen Aura slow

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Focus on defensive itemization: Force Staff to save allies, Holy Locket to amplify your heals. Use Guardian Angel defensively to protect your high ground or disengage from lost fights. Do not overextend to apply Degen Aura. Use Purification to farm dangerous waves safely by healing a creep. The Dmg/Heals facet can help sustain your team longer in defensive sieges.
search_text: Omniknight scenario playing from behind defensive items Force Staff Holy Locket Guardian Angel disengage high ground Purification farm

display_text: Scenario: With a Mobile, Dive-heavy Team (e.g., Storm Spirit, Axe). Your job is to enable them. Use Repel on the diving core before they initiate to prevent disables. Be ready to Purification them from a distance. Consider Aether Lens for cast range. Guardian Angel can be used after the initiation to protect the rest of your team following up. A Blink Dagger can help you land a crucial multi-hero Purification or get in range for Repel.
search_text: Omniknight scenario mobile dive team Storm Axe Repel initiator Purification follow Aether Lens Blink Dagger Guardian Angel teamfight

display_text: Level 10 Talents: Left choice is +1s Repel Duration (increasing from 5s to 6s). Right choice is +35 Base Damage. Choose +1s Repel Duration if your team needs longer debuff immunity for a key hero against heavy disable lineups. Choose +35 Base Damage if you are playing a more core-oriented role, need better last hitting, or the extra disable duration is less critical.
search_text: Omniknight talent level 10 left +1s Repel Duration 6s right +35 Base Damage choice debuff immunity vs damage core support

display_text: Level 15 Talents: Left choice is +5 Repel Strength/HP Regen per Debuff (increasing from 3/4/5/6 to 8/9/10/11). Right choice is -20s Guardian Angel Cooldown (reducing from 100/90/80 to 80/70/60). Choose the Repel talent when facing heroes with many debuffs (e.g., Venomancer, Batrider) or if you are constantly using Repel on a debuffed ally. Choose the Guardian Angel cooldown talent to have your ultimate available for nearly every fight, crucial for high-tempo games.
search_text: Omniknight talent level 15 left +5 Repel Strength HP Regen per Debuff right -20s Guardian Angel Cooldown choice debuffs vs ultimate uptime

display_text: Level 20 Talents: Left choice is +2s Guardian Angel Duration (increasing from 5/6/7 to 7/8/9). Right choice is -3s Purification Cooldown (reducing from 15/14/13/12 to 12/11/10/9). Choose the Guardian Angel duration talent to massively extend your team's physical immunity window, often the game-winning choice. Choose the Purification cooldown talent for significantly higher sustained healing and damage output in longer fights, especially if you have mana sustain.
search_text: Omniknight talent level 20 left +2s Guardian Angel Duration 7 8 9s right -3s Purification Cooldown 12 11 10 9s choice long immunity vs spam heal

display_text: Level 25 Talents: Left choice is +160 Purification Damage/Heal (adding 160 to the 90/160/230/300 values). Right choice is +75% Hammer of Purity Bonus Base Damage (increasing the 25/50/75/100 bonus by 75%, making it ~44/88/131/175, for a total damage of ~99/158/216/275). Choose the Purification talent for an enormous 460 heal/damage nuke, transforming you into a potent burst healer/nuker. Choose the Hammer of Purity talent if you are playing as a right-click core, as it provides very high single-target pure damage on a low 5-second cooldown.

display_text: Enchantress is a ranged intelligence hero with very high base movement speed (340), slow base attack time (1.7), and good starting attack range (575). Her base stats (17 Strength, 19 Agility, 22 Intelligence) are decent for a ranged core. She has a high turn rate, which aids her mobility.

display_text: Enchantress has two Facets: Overprotective Wisps and Spellbound. Overprotective Wisps creates Healing Wisps when Enchantress takes damage, providing defensive sustain for front-line or baiting playstyles. Spellbound increases the cast range of Enchant and the attack range of Impetus against Enchanted targets, rewarding a more aggressive, long-range kiting and control strategy.

display_text: In the laning phase, use Enchant to dominate a powerful neutral creep (e.g., Hellbear Smasher) to harass and zone enemies. Max Untouchable first to heavily punish enemy right-clicks. Use Nature's Attendants to sustain yourself and your lane partner. Leverage her high base movement speed to position aggressively.

display_text: Huskar is a ranged strength hero. His base attack time is 1.6 seconds, which is standard for ranged heroes. He has a high base strength of 23, but very low base agility of 10. This gives him good starting HP but very low armor. His intelligence is average at 18, but irrelevant due to his passive removing mana entirely.
search_text: Huskar base stats attack type ranged attack rate 1.6 strength 23 agility 10 intelligence 18

display_text: Huskar has four Facets. Bloodbath: Life Break also slows enemies in a 500 radius. Nothl Transfusion: Life Break also heals friendly units around the target. Cauterize: Berserker's Blood can be activated, dispelling Huskar, costing health. After a delay, it heals for the health spent plus bonus per debuff dispelled. Incendiary: Burning Spears also burns for a percentage of the target's max health, costing max health instead of current health.
search_text: Huskar facets Bloodbath Life Break slow radius Nothl Transfusion Life Break heal allies Cauterize Berserker's Blood active dispel heal debuff Incendiary Burning Spears max health burn cost max hp

display_text: Huskar is a powerful laner due to Burning Spears. Spam Burning Spears on the enemy to stack damage over time, forcing them to use regen or leave lane. Manage his health cost (4 HP per attack). Inner Fire at level 1 can be used defensively if the enemy initiates, but maxing Burning Spears is priority. Aim to secure last hits and deny aggressively due to his high base damage. He is vulnerable to magic nukes and physical harass due to low armor.
search_text: Huskar laning phase early game Burning Spears spam stack DoT health cost 4 hp Inner Fire knockback defensive

display_text: Level 6 is a massive spike with Life Break, allowing solo kills. Level 12, with maxed Burning Spears and Berserker's Blood, he becomes very durable in fights. Level 18 with max ultimate. Level 20 talent: +30% Berserker's Blood Regen significantly increases his sustain. Level 25: +6s Burning Spear Duration turns him into a siege machine. Key item spikes are Armlet of Mordiggian (synergy with Berserker's Blood) and Black King Bar (allows him to fight through disables while low HP).
search_text: Huskar power spikes level 6 Life Break kill level 12 max abilities level 18 talent 20 30 Berserker's Blood regen talent 25 6s Burning Spear duration item Armlet BKB

display_text: Huskar is countered by Break mechanics (Silver Edge, Hoodwink) which disable Berserker's Blood. High physical burst damage (Ursa, Troll Warlord) can kill him before his regen kicks in. Percentage-based damage (Timbersaw) ignores his high health pool. Instant disables and hex (Lion, Shadow Shaman) prevent him from using Life Break or attacking. Pure damage and Skadi's heal reduction are also very effective.
search_text: Huskar counters hard counter Break Silver Edge Hoodwink disable Berserker's Blood physical burst Ursa Troll percentage damage Timbersaw instant hex Lion Shadow Shaman

display_text: Armlet of Mordiggian is absolutely core. The toggle synergizes perfectly with Berserker's Blood, allowing him to stay at low health for maximum bonuses while sustaining through the drain. Black King Bar is essential to prevent being kited or burst by magic damage during his low HP engagements. Satanic is a late-game core for the massive lifesteal and active to instantly recover health.
search_text: Huskar core build essential items Armlet of Mordiggian toggle synergy BKB Black King Bar magic immunity Satanic lifesteal

display_text: In teamfights, Huskar's role is to jump the highest priority backline hero with Life Break, silencing and slowing them. He then uses his high attack speed from Berserker's Blood and Burning Spear stacks to burn down targets. He acts as a high-damage, high-sustain frontliner who draws focus and forces enemies to deal with him while his team follows up. Use Inner Fire to peel enemies off himself or allies.
search_text: Huskar teamfight role initiate Life Break jump backline silence slow frontliner sustain damage Burning Spear stacks Inner Fire peel

display_text: Inner Fire: In a fiery rage, knocks all nearby enemies away from you to a fixed distance of 400, dealing 105/170/235/300 damage and silencing them. Silences for 1.5/2/2.5/3 seconds. Casts instantly in a 500 radius. Cooldown is 17/15/13/11 seconds. Costs 75/100/125/150 health.
search_text: Inner Fire Q ability Huskar knockback 400 distance damage 105 170 235 300 silence 1.5 2 2.5 3 seconds radius 500 cooldown 17 15 13 11 health cost 75 100 125 150

display_text: Burning Spear: Huskar sets his spears aflame, dealing 5/10/15/20 damage over time per stack with his regular attack. Multiple attacks stack additively. Each attack drains 4 of Huskar's health. Debuff lasts 9 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB. Huskar cannot kill himself with this skill.
search_text: Burning Spear W ability Huskar attack modifier DoT damage per second 5 10 15 20 stackable duration 9 seconds health cost 4 per attack no self kill

display_text: Berserker's Blood: Huskar's injuries feed his power. Provides increased attack speed, magic resistance and health regen based on missing health. At full HP: no bonus. At less than 10% HP: maximum bonuses. Maximum attack speed bonus: 170/220/270/320. Maximum magic resistance bonus: 15%/20%/25%/30%. Maximum health regen bonus: 25/40/55/70 (percentage of your strength).
search_text: Berserker's Blood E passive ability Huskar missing health low hp attack speed bonus max 170 220 270 320 magic resist max 15 20 25 30 health regen max 25 40 55 70 strength based

display_text: Life Break: Huskar draws upon his health to break an enemy's life. He leaps at a target, becoming debuff immune and having 60% increased magic resistance during the leap. Upon impact, he shatters 32%/38%/44% of that hero's current health as damage to the target and slows both their movement and attack speed by 60% for 3/4/5 seconds. Leap cannot be disjointed. Costs 32%/38%/44% of Huskar's current health. Cooldown is 16/14/12 seconds. Slow works on Spell Immune units.
search_text: Life Break R ultimate ability Huskar leap debuff immune 60 magic resist percent current health damage 32 38 44 slow 60 movement attack speed duration 3 4 5 seconds health cost 32 38 44 cooldown 16 14 12 pierces BKB slow

display_text: When facing heroes with high armor (e.g., Dragon Knight, Terrorblade), Huskar's physical damage falls off. Build items that provide percentage-based damage or armor reduction like Assault Cuirass or Desolator. Focus on Burning Spear magic damage stacks instead. The Incendiary Facet (Burning Spear Max Health Burn) is particularly effective here.
search_text: Huskar situational vs high armor Dragon Knight Terrorblade build Assault Cuirass Desolator armor reduction Burning Spear magic damage Incendiary facet max health burn

display_text: When playing from behind and unable to manfight, Huskar should avoid direct engagement. Focus on split-pushing with Burning Spears on towers (they take full damage). Build survivability items like Heart of Tarrasque or Satanic. Use Life Break defensively to jump away or initiate on a lone support with your team. The Nothl Transfusion Facet (Life Break Heal) can help sustain your team in defensive fights.
search_text: Huskar situational playing from behind split push towers Burning Spear build Heart Satanic defensive Life Break Nothl Transfusion facet heal allies

display_text: When facing heavy magic damage burst (e.g., Zeus, Lina), prioritize magic resistance. Berserker's Blood provides up to 30% magic resist at low HP. Build a quick Hood of Defiance or Pipe of Insight. Consider the Cauterize Facet for its dispel and heal to survive magic debuffs and nukes. Always have Black King Bar ready for fights.
search_text: Huskar situational vs magic burst Zeus Lina magic resistance Berserker's Blood Hood of Defiance Pipe of Insight Cauterize facet dispel heal BKB

display_text: When you need to be the primary initiator and your team lacks control, choose the Bloodbath Facet. The AoE slow from Life Break helps your team catch up and lock down the enemy team after you jump in.
search_text: Huskar situational primary initiator team lacks control Bloodbath facet Life Break AoE slow

display_text: Level 10 Talents: +0.75s Inner Fire Duration vs +2s Life Break Slow Duration. Choose Inner Fire for more defensive control. Choose Life Break Slow for better ganking and kill secure.
search_text: Huskar talent level 10 left 0.75 Inner Fire Duration right 2 Life Break Slow Duration

display_text: At level 15, choose between +5 Burning Spear Burn Damage and +12% Lifesteal. The Burning Spear damage increases your DPS and magic damage output significantly. The Lifesteal is essential for sustain in prolonged fights, especially when paired with Satanic. Choose based on whether you need more damage or more survivability.
search_text: Huskar talent level 15 left 5 Burning Spear Burn Damage right 12 Lifesteal

display_text: At level 20, choose between +30% Berserker's Blood Regen and -5s Life Break Cooldown. The +30% Berserker's Blood Regen is almost always the superior choice, massively increasing your health regeneration at low HP, making you incredibly hard to kill. The cooldown reduction is good if you need more frequent initiations and your survivability is already secured.
search_text: Huskar talent level 20 left 30 Berserker's Blood Regen right 5 Life Break Cooldown reduction

display_text: At level 25, choose between +22% Life Break Damage and +6s Burning Spear Duration. The +22% Life Break Damage increases the percentage health damage to 54%/60%/66%, allowing you to instantly cripple even the tankiest heroes. The +6s Burning Spear Duration (total 15s) is exceptional for siege, prolonged teamfights, and applying massive stacks of damage. Choose based on needing burst vs sustained damage.
search_text: Huskar talent level 25 left 22 Life Break Damage right 6 Burning Spear Duration

display_text: Night Stalker is a Melee Strength hero with 23 base Strength, 18 base Agility, and 15 base Intelligence. His attack speed is a relatively slow 1.7 attacks per second. This setup gives him a solid health pool and base damage for a melee core.
search_text: Night Stalker stats melee strength base 23 18 15 agility intelligence attack rate 1.7 BAT

display_text: Blinding Void enhances Void. Enemies under its effect do not share vision with allies and take damage over time if they are not visible to their own team. Voidbringer increases Void's maximum level. Level 4 Void at night becomes a ground-targeted area effect. Dayswap is a placeholder facet with no defined effect. Blinding Void is a powerful tool for initiating ganks in the fog and punishing lone heroes. Voidbringer is a lategame choice that increases your AoE disable and damage.
search_text: Night Stalker Facets Blinding Void vision damage not visible to allies Voidbringer ground target area effect Dayswap no effect

display_text: Focus on securing last hits with your base damage in the first few minutes. Use Void to harass and slow enemy laners. Since Crippling Fear has a high mana cost and long cooldown early, use it sparingly, primarily to secure kills or escape. Your passive, Hunter in the Night, provides minimal benefit during the day. Your goal is to survive the lane, get a few levels, and prepare for a major power spike at level 6 when night falls.
search_text: Night Stalker laning phase early game first 10 minutes last hit Void harass slow Crippling Fear mana cooldown kill escape Hunter in the Night day weak

display_text: Level 6 is your first major spike as Dark Ascension creates a long night. Level 7 with maxed Void deals high damage and long slow. Level 12 talent choice enhances either your core ability or ultimate. Aghanim's Shard upgrade is key. Level 18 with maxed Hunter in the Night gives massive night-time speed. Level 20 talent for +40 Crippling Fear DPS or +15 Strength. Level 25 talent for +100 Attack Speed or -40s ultimate cooldown defines your lategame role.
search_text: Night Stalker power spikes level 6 Dark Ascension night level 12 talent shard level 18 Hunter in the Night level 20 25 talent

display_text: Break mechanics disable Hunter in the Night, removing your night-time mobility and attack speed. Heroes with instant disables like Hex prevent your initiation and escape. High mobility heroes can kite you during the day. Heroes that can fight effectively at night or have superior night vision can challenge your dominance.
search_text: Night Stalker counters break disable Hunter in the Night passive hex instant disable kite mobility day time night vision

display_text: Phase Boots or Power Treads for mobility and stats. Echo Sabre is essential for mana regen, extra burst, and attack slow to keep targets in Void. Black King Bar is mandatory to avoid being disabled during fights so you can apply your damage and silence.
search_text: Night Stalker core build items Phase Boots Power Treads Echo Sabre mana regen slow Black King Bar BKB

display_text: Night Stalker's role in teamfights is to initiate or counter-initiate on key backline targets. Use Dark Ascension for flight and vision to find the ideal target. Use Void to slow and damage a core, then use Crippling Fear to silence them and nearby supports. Your high night-time movement speed and attack speed allow you to chase down and eliminate heroes after your initial disruption.

display_text: Void: Deals 80/160/240/320/400 magic damage and slows the target's movement and attack speed by 50% for 1.25 seconds. At night, the duration increases to 1.6/2.2/2.8/3.4/4.0 seconds and applies a mini-stun, interrupting channeling abilities. Cooldown is 11/10/9/8 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Against high armor or evasion heroes, prioritize itemization that negates their defenses. Items like Assault Cuirass for armor reduction and Monkey King Bar for true strike are critical. Your physical damage output with Hunter in the Night relies on hitting your targets.

The status resistance talent makes you significantly harder to control during night-time engagements, a key defensive option.

The attack speed talent turns you into an ultra-fast right-click monster during any night. The cooldown reduction brings your ultimate down to a ~90-second cooldown, allowing you to have near-constant night-time dominance.

display_text: Broodmother is a Melee hero with a base attack rate of 1.7 seconds. She has balanced starting attributes of 18 Strength, 18 Agility, and 18 Intelligence, making her a versatile but not exceptional laner in terms of raw stats. Her melee nature requires careful positioning in lane.
search_text: Broodmother Melee stats attack rate 1.7 base strength 18 agility 18 intelligence 18

display_text: Necrotic Webs: Reduces health restoration of enemies on webs. This facet is a powerful counter-pick against heroes reliant on healing, regeneration, or lifesteal, such as Huskar, Alchemist, or heroes with Heart of Tarrasque. It turns the web area into a zone of denial. Feeding Frenzy: Insatiable Hunger also applies to nearby Spiderlings. This facet amplifies Broodmother's core identity as a swarm pusher. It dramatically increases the physical damage output of the spider army during teamfights and tower pushes, making it the default choice for maximizing map pressure and objective taking.
search_text: Broodmother Facets Necrotic Webs reduces enemy healing regeneration on webs counter pick. Feeding Frenzy Insatiable Hunger buffs nearby Spiderlings swarm push damage army

display_text: In the laning phase, Broodmother's goal is to secure the enemy safe lane T1 tower. Use Spin Web (W) to create a network for free movement, high turn rate, and up to 46 bonus movement speed. This allows you to harass and last-hit with impunity. Max Incapacitating Bite (E) first to apply a 2-second slow and miss chance on each attack, making trades heavily favorable. Use Spawn Spiderlings (R) on the enemy hero or a creep to generate your army. Use Spider's Milk (passive) to sustain yourself and your spiderlings by last-hitting. Dominate the lane, then push the tower once you have a few levels and spiderlings.
search_text: Broodmother laning early game first 10 minutes push tower Spin Web free pathing Incapacitating Bite slow miss chance Spawn Spiderlings army sustain Spider's Milk

display_text: Level 6: Unlocking Spinner's Snare (ultimate) provides strong area control and pick-off potential within your web network. Level 12: With maxed Incapacitating Bite and Spawn Spiderlings, your solo kill potential and push speed peak. Key item timings like Orchid Malevolence or Diffusal Blade around this time are massive spikes. Level 18: Max level Spawn Spiderlings with level 2 ultimate. Level 20: Talent choice (e.g., +20% Incapacitating Bite Slow/Miss Chance) significantly increases lockdown. Level 25: The -0.2s BAT during Insatiable Hunger talent is a transformative DPS spike, especially with Feeding Frenzy facet.
search_text: Broodmother power spikes level 6 Spinner's Snare level 12 max skills item timing Orchid Diffusal level 18 level 20 talent level 25 talent BAT reduction

display_text: Hard Counters: 1. Area-of-Effect (AoE) Clear: Heroes like Legion Commander (Overwhelming Odds), Earthshaker (Echo Slam), or Kunkka (Tidebringer) can instantly wipe your spiderling army. 2. Break: Heroes like Shadow Demon (Demonic Purge) or items like Silver Edge disable your Incapacitating Bite and Spider's Milk passives, crippling your damage and sustain. 3. Instant Disables and Hex: Heroes with reliable, long-duration disables like Lion, Shadow Shaman, or an instant Hex from Scythe of Vyse can lock you down before you can use your web mobility.
search_text: Broodmother counters hard counters AoE clear Legion Commander Earthshaker Kunkka spiderlings Break Shadow Demon Silver Edge passives disable instant hex Lion Shadow Shaman Scythe of Vyse

display_text: Core Build: 1. Soul Ring is absolutely essential for unlimited mana to spam Spin Web and Spawn Spiderlings. 2. Orb of Corrosion synergizes perfectly with Incapacitating Bite, applying further slow and armor reduction for you and your spiderlings. 3. Diffusal Blade is a core damage item; the mana burn is applied by all spiderlings, and the active purge helps secure kills. Alternatively, Orchid Malevolence provides mana, damage, and a crucial silence for pick-offs.
search_text: Broodmother core items essential Soul Ring mana Orb of Corrosion slow armor reduction Diffusal Blade mana burn spiderlings Orchid Malevolence silence

display_text: In teamfights, Broodmother's role is to be a disruptive backline diver and area controller. Use your web network to flank or initiate from unexpected angles. Your primary target is the enemy support or core carry. Use Spinner's Snare to cut off escape routes or trap multiple enemies. Activate Insatiable Hunger to burst down a key target with high physical damage and lifesteal for sustain. Your spiderlings should be micro-managed to focus fire on the same target or to body block enemies. Your goal is to create chaos, split focus, and eliminate squishy heroes quickly.
search_text: Broodmother teamfight role 5v5 backline diver disrupt area control Spinner's Snare trap Insatiable Hunger burst lifesteal spiderlings focus fire micro

display_text: Spin Web (W): Broodmother spins a large web that grants her and her Spiderlings a passive movement speed increase of 10/22/34/46 and a 0.2 bonus turn rate while in its 1200 radius, as well as free pathing. It has 3/5/7/9 maximum charges with a 27-second charge restore time. Webs never expire and can be cast from anywhere as long as the new web touches an existing one. When the maximum limit is exceeded, the oldest web disappears.
search_text: Spin Web W ability free pathing movement speed bonus 10 22 34 46 turn rate 0.2 radius 1200 charges 3 5 7 9 restore time 27 seconds spiderlings

display_text: Incapacitating Bite (E): Broodmother's attacks apply a venom that slows the target's movement speed by 15/20/25/30% and gives them a 30/40/50/60% chance to miss attacks for 2.0 seconds. Her attacks also deal an additional 3/6/9/12 damage to the affected target. The miss chance stacks with evasion.

display_text: Insatiable Hunger (Q): Broodmother gains 40/50/60/70 bonus attack damage and 40/60/80/100% lifesteal for 8/10/12/14 seconds. With the Feeding Frenzy facet, this buff also applies to nearby Spiderlings. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Insatiable Hunger Q active buff bonus damage 40 50 60 70 lifesteal 40 60 80 100 percent duration 8 10 12 14 seconds Feeding Frenzy spiderlings not pierce BKB

display_text: Spawn Spiderlings (R): Broodmother injects an enemy, dealing 220/320/420 damage and slowing them for 4 seconds. If the target is killed within 20 seconds, 4/5/6 Spiderlings hatch. Spiderlings have 300 HP, last 40 seconds, deal bonus damage, and have 25/35/45 movement speed. Units killed by Spiderlings give birth to additional Spiderlings.

display_text: Spinner's Snare (Ultimate): Broodmother creates up to 5 invisible web lines touching any existing web. Enemy Heroes that cross a line are rooted for 3.0 seconds, revealed, and take 100 damage per second. The line is destroyed after being initially triggered, but multiple heroes can trip on it until then. The team is alerted via minimap when triggered.

display_text: Spider's Milk (Passive): Whenever Broodmother kills a unit, she and all units under her control in a 600 radius receive a buff that heals them for a percentage of the victim's max health every 0.5 seconds for 3 seconds. Healing from creep kills is reduced by 40%. The heal amount is 2% per tick for hero kills.

display_text: Scenario: Vs. High Armor / Damage Block. Heroes like Tidehunter, Timbersaw, or anyone with Crimson Guard can significantly reduce your physical spiderling damage. Solution: Build items that reduce armor, like Desolator or Assault Cuirass. Consider skipping heavy spiderling focus and building for Broodmother's own right-click with items like Monkey King Bar and Daedalus.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. If the enemy has strong counters and you've died a few times, your spiderlings will be underleveled and die instantly. Solution: Stop investing in Spawn Spiderlings. Max Spin Web and Incapacitating Bite for survivability and utility. Build survivability items like Black King Bar, Linken's Sphere, or Sange and Yasha. Play as a split-pusher using webs to escape, avoiding 5v5 fights.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Gem of True Sight or Sentry Wards. Your web mobility and Spinner's Snare traps are neutralized. Solution: Do not fight in areas without vision control. Carry your own Dust of Appearance or Sentry Wards to de-ward. Consider buying a Smoke of Deceit to move through enemy vision. Itemize for direct combat (BKB, Satanic) rather than pick-offs.

display_text: Scenario: Need to Break High Regeneration. Against heroes like Alchemist, Huskar, or Morphling with high regen, the Necrotic Webs facet is crucial. It severely reduces their healing from all sources while they are on your webs, making them much easier to kill. Combine this with items like Skadi or Spirit Vessel for maximum healing reduction.

display_text: Level 10 Talents: +80 Spawn Spiderlings Damage vs. +20% Insatiable Hunger Lifesteal. Choose +80 Damage if you are focusing on farming and pushing with your spider army, as it increases your clear speed and burst damage on heroes. Choose +20% Lifesteal if you are facing a lane or matchup where you need more sustain in man-fights, or if you are building Broodmother as a more traditional right-click carry.
search_text: Broodmother talent level 10 left +80 Spawn Spiderlings Damage right +20% Insatiable Hunger Lifesteal push farm vs sustain manfight

display_text: Level 15 Talents: +8 Incapacitating Bite Attack Bonus vs. +150 Spiderlings Health. Choose +8 Attack Bonus if you are the primary damage source and focusing on killing heroes, as it significantly increases your right-click damage per hit. Choose +150 Spiderling Health if the enemy has widespread but weak AoE damage (e.g., Radiance, Shard effects) that your spiderlings are barely dying to; the extra health lets them survive and continue dealing damage.
search_text: Broodmother talent level 15 left +8 Incapacitating Bite Attack Bonus damage right +150 Spiderlings Health survivability vs AoE

display_text: Level 20 Talents: -6s Spin Web Charge Restore Time vs. +20% Incapacitating Bite Slow/Miss Chance. Choose -6s Charge Restore if you need extreme map mobility, are split-pushing aggressively across multiple lanes, or are constantly re-positioning in fights. This provides an instant slow and terrain-passing chase after teleporting to a Haunt illusion, improving teamfight initiation and lockdown. FACET: Ephemeral - This facet does nothing and is a placeholder.

display_text: Break mechanics (e.g., Silver Edge, Viper Nethertoxin) disable the passive percent chance of Greater Bash, removing his core source of damage and lockdown from attacks. His charges are vulnerable to instant disables (Hex, Eul's Scepter) during the wind-up and travel time. Heroes with high mobility (Blink, Manta Style) can dodge Charge of Darkness impact. Planar Pocket can be exploited by enemies casting weak, single-target spells to waste its effect.
search_text: Spirit Breaker counters hard counters Break Silver Edge Viper Nethertoxin disables Greater Bash passive instant hex Eul's Scepter interrupt charge dodge mobility Planar Pocket exploit

display_text: Phase Boots are essential for the armor, damage, and active movement speed to stick to targets after a charge. Echo Sabre provides mana regeneration, strength, and the double attack which synergizes with Greater Bash for guaranteed lockdown. Black King Bar is non-negotiable for enabling you to charge into the enemy team and use Nether Strike without being chain-disabled.
search_text: Spirit Breaker core build essential items Phase Boots Echo Sabre Black King Bar BKB mana strength lockdown movement speed

display_text: In teamfights, Spirit Breaker's job is initiation and disruption. Use Charge of Darkness or Nether Strike on a key backline hero (support or core) to start the fight. Activate Bulldoze for status resistance and speed to run through the enemy team, applying Greater Bash procs. Use Planar Pocket on a key ally being focused, or on an enemy core to redirect spells to yourself. Your goal is to create chaos, lock down a primary target, and absorb spells with your tankiness and Planar Pocket.
search_text: Spirit Breaker teamfight role initiator disruptor Charge of Darkness Nether Strike backline Bulldoze status resistance Greater Bash Planar Pocket redirect spells chaos lockdown

display_text: Ability: Charge of Darkness (Q). Spirit Breaker charges at a target enemy unit from anywhere on the map, starting at 275 speed and reaching 425 max speed after 1.5 seconds. All enemies passed through and the target are hit by a Greater Bash, stunning for 1.3/1.6/1.9/2.2 seconds. Cooldown is 22/19/16/13 seconds. Charge provides shared vision of the target. If the target dies, he charges the nearest enemy. Does NOT pierce BKB on the bash application.
search_text: Charge of Darkness Q ability global charge movement speed 275 to 425 windup 1.5s stun 1.3 1.6 1.9 2.2 cooldown 22 19 16 13 shared vision target dies redirect

display_text: Ability: Bulldoze (W). Spirit Breaker gains 8%/12%/16%/20% movement speed and 40%/50%/60%/70% status resistance for 8 seconds. Cooldown is 22/20/18/16 seconds. Can be activated during Charge of Darkness. This ability provides significant tankiness against disables and slows, and enhances chase speed.
search_text: Bulldoze W ability movement speed 8 12 16 20 percent status resistance 40 50 60 70 duration 8s cooldown 22 20 18 16 cast during charge

display_text: Ability: Greater Bash (Passive). Each attack has a 17% chance to Greater Bash, dealing 25/30/35/40 damage plus 17% of Spirit Breaker's current movement speed as bonus damage. It stuns for 0.9/1.1/1.3/1.5 seconds and knocks the target back. The bash from Charge of Darkness and Nether Strike applies this effect regardless of the chance. Break disables the passive chance, but not the guaranteed bashes from spells.
search_text: Greater Bash passive ability chance 17 percent damage 25 30 35 40 plus movement speed percent 17 stun duration 0.9 1.1 1.3 1.5 knockback Break disables passive Charge Nether Strike apply

display_text: Ability: Planar Pocket (Shard). Spirit Breaker gains 75% magic resistance for 6 seconds and distorts reality around a selected hero (ally or enemy). Any unit-targeted spells cast by enemy heroes within 900 range are redirected to Spirit Breaker instead. This can be used to protect an ally from targeted disables or to absorb spells intended for an enemy.
search_text: Planar Pocket Shard ability Aghanim magic resistance 75 percent duration 6s radius 900 redirect enemy unit-targeted spells to Spirit Breaker protect ally absorb

display_text: Ability: Herd Mentality (Innate). The hero on your team whose level is lower than anybody else's on your team gains 50% more experience from all sources. This helps your team's lowest-level hero catch up.
search_text: Herd Mentality innate ability passive lowest level hero on team gains 50 percent more XP experience

display_text: Ability: Nether Strike (Ultimate, R). Spirit Breaker becomes invisible for 1 second, then appears behind the target enemy, applying a Greater Bash of the current level for double the normal knockback distance and dealing 150/250/350 bonus damage. This ability pierces spell immunity. It is a reliable initiation and lockdown tool.
search_text: Nether Strike R ultimate ability invisibility fade time 1s appears behind target Greater Bash double knockback bonus damage 150 250 350 pierces BKB spell immunity lockdown

display_text: Scenario: Vs High Physical Damage/Armor Reduction. Spirit Breaker's low base armor makes him vulnerable. Build early armor items like Phase Boots and consider a Medallion of Courage/Solar Crest for yourself or an ally. Use Bulldoze's status resistance to reduce the duration of physical slows like Skadi. Prioritize locking down the physical damage dealer with Nether Strike.
search_text: Spirit Breaker vs high physical damage armor reduction low armor counter build Phase Boots Medallion Solar Crest Bulldoze status resistance slow Nether Strike lockdown

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When your team is losing, focus on pick-offs with Charge of Darkness and Nether Strike on isolated targets. Build utility items like Spirit Vessel or Heaven's Halberd instead of pure damage. Use Herd Mentality to help your under-leveled core catch up. Avoid initiating 5v5 fights; play on the edges and look for counter-initiation.
search_text: Spirit Breaker playing from behind losing game pickoff Charge Nether Strike isolated target utility items Spirit Vessel Heaven Halberd Herd Mentality catch up avoid 5v5

display_text: Scenario: Vs Illusion/Summons Heroes. Greater Bash deals bonus damage based on movement speed, which is effective against illusions. However, the low bash chance can be unreliable for clearing. Build Radiance or Mjollnir for AOE damage. Use Charge of Darkness to quickly identify and jump on the real hero among illusions.
search_text: Spirit Breaker vs illusions summons Chaos Knight Phantom Lancer Naga Siren Greater Bash movement speed damage Radiance Mjollnir AOE Charge find real hero

display_text: Scenario: With Heavy Displacement Allies. If your team has heroes like Pudge, Tiny, or Dark Willow with knockbacks, take the 'Imbalanced' Facet. This causes enemies hit by your Nether Strike to be moved significantly further by your allies' forced movement, enabling devastating combos like Hook into Nether Strike into Toss.
search_text: Spirit Breaker combo with displacement allies Pudge Hook Tiny Toss Dark Willow Imbalanced Facet Nether Strike increases forced movement combo setup

display_text: Scenario: As a Position 4 Support. Max Charge of Darkness first for lower cooldown and longer stun. Build Urn of Shadows/Spirit Vessel, Solar Crest, and Aghanim's Shard. Your role is to scout, provide vision with charge, and save allies with Planar Pocket. Use Nether Strike to lock down key targets for your core.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Linken's Sphere. Your Charge of Darkness and Nether Strike are both single-target spells blocked by Linken's. You must carry a basic dispel (like Lotus Orb) or a cheap pop item (Force Staff, Eul's) to break it before initiating. Alternatively, use Charge of Darkness on another unit to bash and pop the Linken's with the AOE bash, then cast Nether Strike.

display_text: Level 10 Talents. Left: +500 Night Vision. Drastically improves vision for scouting, initiating charges from fog at night, and avoiding ganks. Right: +4 Armor. Compensates for Spirit Breaker's low base armor, significantly increasing physical EHP in the early-mid game. Choose Night Vision for aggressive map play and pick-offs. Choose Armor if you are the frontline and facing heavy physical damage.

display_text: Level 15 Talents. Left: -3s Bulldoze Cooldown. Reduces cooldown from 16s to 13s at max level, giving near 60% uptime on the status resistance and movement speed buff. Essential for constant fighting and tanking disables. Right: +45 Damage. A significant raw damage increase that enhances last hitting, bash damage, and right-click DPS. Choose Bulldoze cooldown if you are the initiator and need to survive. Choose Damage if you are playing as a core and need to kill targets faster.

This significantly increases lockdown and damage in prolonged fights. Right: Bulldoze 375 All Damage Barrier. Adds a 375-damage barrier that blocks all damage types when Bulldoze is activated, providing a massive survivability boost. Choose Bash cooldown for maximum control and damage output. Choose Barrier if you are being focused and burst down quickly.

display_text: Level 25 Talents. Left: +20% Greater Bash Damage. Multiplies the total damage of Greater Bash (base + movement speed component) by 1.2. This is a massive late-game damage spike, especially with high movement speed items. Right: -4s Charge of Darkness Cooldown. Reduces cooldown from 13s to 9s at max level, allowing for near-constant global map pressure and initiation. Choose Bash Damage to delete supports and cores with your bashes. Choose Charge Cooldown for unparalleled map control, pick-offs, and teamfight re-engagement.

display_text: Gyrocopter is a Ranged Agility hero with an Attack Time of 1.7 seconds. He has a strong base Agility of 25, paired with 22 Strength and 21 Intelligence. His starting physical survivability is decent, and his good Agility provides a solid foundation for early right-click damage.
search_text: Gyrocopter stats Ranged Agility base attack time BAT 1.7 strength 22 agility 25 intelligence 21

display_text: Facet choice dictates Gyrocopter's mid-fight mobility or his area control. Secondary Strikes: Call Down creates delayed secondary explosions around heroes it hits, significantly increasing its area damage and slow potential, ideal for zoning and controlling teamfights. Afterburner: Every successful hit of a Rocket Barrage rocket on an enemy hero increases Gyrocopter's movement speed for 0 seconds (data shows duration 0, but the mechanic exists), encouraging an aggressive, in-your-face playstyle where you dive into the enemy team to maximize Rocket Barrage and gain a speed boost.
search_text: Gyrocopter Facets Secondary Strikes Afterburner Call Down delayed explosions Rocket Barrage movement speed boost teamfight zoning aggressive dive

display_text: Gyrocopter's laning is defined by his high Rocket Barrage damage. At level 1, Rocket Barrage deals 8 damage per rocket, firing 10 rockets per second for 3 seconds, potentially dealing 240 magical damage if the full duration connects. Use this to secure kills or zone enemies. His base damage is decent due to 25 Agility. Use Homing Missile (stun duration 1.3/1.7/2.1/2.5 seconds, 90/180/270/360 damage) from the fog to set up kills or disrupt the enemy core's last hitting. Focus on farming and pressuring the enemy carry.
search_text: Gyrocopter laning Rocket Barrage level 1 8 damage 10 rockets per second 3 seconds 240 magical damage Homing Missile stun 1.3 to 2.5 sec damage 90 to 360 zone kill setup

display_text: Level 6: Call Down provides a huge teamfight and ganking power spike with 200 AoE damage and a 50% slow. Level 12: Maxed Rocket Barrage (26 damage per rocket) and Flak Cannon (5 attacks) allow you to farm quickly and melt heroes in close range. Level 18: Maxed Homing Missile provides a 2.5 second stun. Level 20: Talent choice between +14 Rocket Barrage Damage (more burst) or -4s Flak Cannon Cooldown (more sustained AoE). Level 25: Choice between -30s Call Down Cooldown (frequent teamfight ult) or +3 Flak Cannon Attacks (more teamfight physical damage). Key item spike is Black King Bar to safely channel Rocket Barrage.
search_text: Gyrocopter power spikes level 6 Call Down 200 damage slow level 12 max Rocket Barrage 26 damage Flak Cannon 5 attacks level 18 Homing Missile 2.5s stun level 20 talent level 25 talent BKB item timing

display_text: Gyrocopter is hard countered by heroes with Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Hoodwink). Break explicitly disables his Side Gunner talent from Flak Cannon, removing a significant portion of his late-game teamfight damage. Instant disables like Hex prevent him from activating Black King Bar or using Rocket Barrage. High magic burst can kill him before he can deal damage due to his average Strength gain. Heroes that can quickly destroy his Homing Missile (which has 3 hits to kill for heroes, 6 for towers) also negate his initiation and stun.
search_text: Gyrocounter counters Break Silver Edge disables Side Gunner Flak Cannon Hex instant disable prevent BKB Rocket Barrage magic burst Homing Missile destroy 3 hits to kill

display_text: Black King Bar is absolutely essential. It allows Gyrocopter to dive into the enemy team and use Rocket Barrage without being interrupted or burst down by magic damage. Mjollnir/Maelstrom provides crucial wave clear and magical damage procs that synergize with his Flak Cannon, allowing him to farm and fight effectively. Dragon Lance/Hurricane Pike provides much-needed attack range and survivability, letting him use Flak Cannon safely from a distance.
search_text: Gyrocopter core build Black King Bar BKB essential Rocket Barrage channel Mjollnir Maelstrom wave clear Flak Cannon procs Dragon Lance Hurricane Pike attack range survivability

display_text: In teamfights, Gyrocopter's primary role is to deal massive AoE physical and magical damage. Initiate from fog with Homing Missile to stun a key target. Pop Black King Bar, fly into the enemy backline, and activate Rocket Barrage to melt supports and agility cores. Use Flak Cannon to hit all enemy heroes from a safe distance. Use Call Down to slow the enemy team at the fight's start or to cut off escape routes. Your job is to create chaos and output unsustainable damage in a large area.

display_text: ABILITY: Rocket Barrage (Q). Launches a salvo of rockets at nearby enemy units in a 400 radius around Gyrocopter for 3 seconds. It fires rockets every 0.1 seconds (10 rockets per second). Each rocket deals 8/14/20/26 magical damage. Cannot hit units Gyrocopter has no vision over.

display_text: ABILITY: Homing Missile (W). Fires a homing missile that seeks the targeted enemy unit. The missile travels up to 1500 distance at 500 speed, accelerating over 15 acceleration. It has a 2.5 second pre-flight time before launching. Upon impact, it deals 90/180/270/360 magical damage and stuns for 1.3/1.7/2.1/2.5 seconds. Enemy heroes can destroy it in 3 hits; towers take 6 hits. It will follow and hit invisible units.

Only the main target receives attack modifiers like Critical Strike. Has a cooldown of 26/24/22/20 seconds. Provides a sidegunner with a fire rate and radius if the talent is taken. Break disables the sidegunner.

Cooldown is 90/75/60 seconds.

display_text: Scenario: Versus High Armor/High HP Cores (e.g., Dragon Knight, Bristleback). Rocket Barrage's magical damage becomes your primary tool. Build to survive and cast it multiple times: Aghanim's Scepter for Call Down upgrade, Black King Bar, and Shiva's Guard for armor and intelligence. Avoid relying solely on Flak Cannon physical damage.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Your farm is slow and you are vulnerable. Focus on defending high ground with Homing Missile stuns and Call Down slows. Build cheap, effective items like Magic Wand, Power Treads, and Drums of Endurance to help in early skirmishes. Use Flak Cannon to safely clear creep waves hitting your towers without showing on the map.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Multiple Illusion/Spawn Heroes (e.g., Phantom Lancer, Broodmother). Your Flak Cannon is extremely effective at clearing illusions and spiderlings. Prioritize maxing Flak Cannon after Rocket Barrage. Build Mjollnir for its active and proc damage. Consider a Battle Fury if the game goes very late for even better cleave.

display_text: Scenario: Need to Burst a Key Hero. Combine Homing Missile (2.5 second stun at max level) with instant follow-up. Use Shadow Blade or Blink Dagger to get close, then Rocket Barrage during the stun. Follow up with a point-blank Call Down for maximum damage. Items like Daedalus or Monkey King Bar amplify your burst if you can right-click during the stun.

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Heavy Silence or Root (e.g., Skywrath, Treant). These prevent you from using Rocket Barrage and Flak Cannon. Itemize for dispels: Manta Style (dispels silence/root, provides stats) or Satanic (dispels silence, provides lifesteal and survivability). Black King Bar remains critical against magical silences.

Initiate with Blink -> Call Down on multiple heroes for a huge slow and damage, then follow up with Homing Missile on a key target. This requires a very tanky build (e.g., Heart of Tarrasque) to survive going in first.

display_text: Alchemist is a Melee Strength hero with 23 base Strength, 22 base Agility, and 25 base Intelligence. His base Attack Time of 1.7 seconds is standard for melee heroes. He has a standard turn rate and movement speed.
search_text: Alchemist stats base melee strength agility intelligence attack time

display_text: Seed Money: Alchemist starts with more gold. This Facet accelerates early item timings like Soul Ring or Phase Boots, favoring an aggressive, high-tempo playstyle where you look to dominate the lane with an early gold advantage. Mixologist: Unstable Concoction and Berserk Potion recharge faster when in Chemical Rage. This Facet enhances Alchemist's teamfight contribution by allowing more frequent stuns and dispels/saves on allies, supporting a more utility-focused or tempo-fighting build. Dividends: Alchemist gains bonus GPM for each Aghanim's Scepter he has granted to allies. This Facet is for a dedicated support Alchemist who focuses on farming and gifting Scepters to his team, sacrificing his own combat power for team-wide power spikes.
search_text: Alchemist facets Seed Money Mixologist Dividends starting gold faster cooldowns GPM Aghanim Scepter gift

display_text: Focus on last hits to maximize Greevil's Greed gold bonus. Use Acid Spray (level 1) to secure ranged creeps, harass the enemy, and reduce their armor for easier denies. Skill build priority is typically maxing Acid Spray first for farming and lane control, followed by Greevil's Greed. Use Unstable Concoction cautiously for kills or to deter enemy aggression, but be mindful of the self-damage risk after 5.5 seconds. Mana management is key; Soul Ring is a common early purchase.
search_text: Alchemist laning last hit Greevil's Greed Acid Spray farm harass armor reduction Unstable Concoction kill deny Soul Ring

display_text: Level 6 (Chemical Rage): Major farming and sustain power spike. Allows you to farm jungle camps efficiently and stay in lane indefinitely. Level 12 (Maxed Acid Spray and Greevil's Greed): Peak farming speed. Radiance or first core item timing (e.g., Armlet, Blink Dagger) should be achieved around now. Level 18 (Maxed Chemical Rage): Significant increase in attack speed (1.0 BAT) and regen (120 HP/sec). Becomes a formidable manfighter. Level 20 Talent: Choice between +2.5 Damage per Greevil's Greed stack (more late-game scaling) or -0.1s Chemical Rage Base Attack Time (faster attacks). Level 25 Talent: Choice between +400 Unstable Concoction Max Damage (burst) or +50 Chemical Rage Regeneration (sustain). Aghanim's Scepter/Shard timings also define power spikes.
search_text: Alchemist power spikes level 6 Chemical Rage farm level 12 Radiance level 18 BAT attack speed regen level 20 talent level 25 talent Aghanim Scepter

display_text: Break effects (e.g., from Silver Edge, Doom) disable Greevil's Greed and Corrosive Weaponry, crippling his damage and slow. Instant disables (Hex, Orchid Malevolence) prevent him from using Berserk Potion (dispels) or throwing Unstable Concoction. High physical burst damage can kill him before Chemical Rage regeneration matters. Heroes with mana burn (Anti-Mage, Invoker) can drain his small mana pool, limiting his ability usage. Kiting heroes who can stay out of his melee range while slowed by Corrosive Weaponry are effective.
search_text: Alchemist counters break Silver Edge Doom Greevil's Greed Corrosive Weaponry disable hex orchid physical burst mana burn Anti-Mage kiting

display_text: Soul Ring is essential for sustaining Acid Spray and Unstable Concoction spam in lane due to his low mana pool. Radiance accelerates farm and provides miss chance for survivability; it is the core farming item for a greedy Alchemist. Black King Bar is mandatory in most games to prevent being disabled during Chemical Rage, allowing him to deal continuous right-click damage.
search_text: Alchemist core items Soul Ring mana Radiance farm Black King Bar BKB spell immunity Chemical Rage

display_text: In teamfights, Alchemist's role is to initiate or counter-initiate with Unstable Concoction (a long-duration AoE stun). Pop Chemical Rage and Black King Bar early to become a durable, high-DPS frontliner. Use Acid Spray on the fight's focal point to reduce enemy armor for your team's physical damage. Use Berserk Potion on a key ally (carry or initiator) to dispel debuffs and grant them attack speed and movement speed. His goal is to absorb damage and spells while outputting significant right-click damage and controlling the area with his slows and stuns.
search_text: Alchemist teamfight initiator Unstable Concoction stun Chemical Rage BKB frontliner DPS Acid Spray armor reduction Berserk Potion dispel buff ally

display_text: Acid Spray: Sprays high-pressure acid across a target area for 15 seconds. Enemy units in the area take 25/30/35/40 damage per second and have their armor reduced by 3/4/5/6. Radius is 350/400/450/500. Does NOT pierce BKB. Allies can be granted armor by a talent.
search_text: Alchemist Acid Spray Q damage per second armor reduction radius 350 400 450 500 duration 15

display_text: Unstable Concoction: Alchemist brews a potion for up to 5 seconds. When thrown, it stuns and damages enemies in a 250 radius. Minimum stun is 0.25 seconds for 0 damage. Maximum stun after 5 seconds is 1.7/2.2/2.7/3.2 seconds for 150/220/290/360 damage. Alchemist moves 4/8/12/16% faster while brewing. If not thrown after 5.5 seconds, it explodes on Alchemist, dealing max damage and stunning him (self-deny possible). Once thrown, the projectile cannot be disjointed.
search_text: Alchemist Unstable Concoction W brew charge stun damage 150 220 290 360 radius 250 move speed bonus self damage deny

display_text: Corrosive Weaponry (Passive): Alchemist's attacks apply a stacking debuff that reduces enemy base attack damage by 2/2.5/3/3.5 per stack and slows them by 2/2.5/3/3.5% per stack. Attacks apply 2 stacks. Debuff lasts 4 seconds. Max stacks are 7/10/13/16. Unstable Concoction applies 1 stack per second of brew time.
search_text: Alchemist Corrosive Weaponry passive E attack damage reduction slow per stack max stacks 7 10 13 16 duration 4

display_text: Berserk Potion: Alchemist throws a potion at an ally, applying a basic dispel and granting them 50 attack speed, 30 movement speed, and 40 HP regen for 15 seconds. Projectile speed is 900. Does NOT pierce BKB. Cooldown reduction from the Mixologist Facet applies.
search_text: Alchemist Berserk Potion R basic dispel attack speed 50 move speed 30 HP regen 40 duration 15

display_text: Greevil's Greed (Passive): Alchemist earns 2 base bonus gold and 2 extra bonus gold per kill. Killing another unit within 40 seconds adds another instance of Extra Bonus Gold. Extra Bonus Gold caps at 18 per stack. Also causes bounty runes to yield double gold. Grants +25 damage if Alchemist has Aghanim's Scepter.
search_text: Alchemist Greevil's Greed passive gold bonus 2 2 stack cap 18 bounty rune double Aghanim Scepter damage 25

display_text: Chemical Rage (Ultimate): Alchemist transforms, gaining 20/30/40 movement speed, 60/90/120 HP regen per second, and reducing his base attack time to 1.2/1.1/1.0 for 30 seconds. Provides a basic dispel on cast. Transformation time is 0.35 seconds, which can dodge projectiles.
search_text: Alchemist Chemical Rage ultimate transformation move speed 20 30 40 HP regen 60 90 120 BAT 1.2 1.1 1.0 duration 30 dispel dodge

display_text: When playing against high physical armor teams, Alchemist's value increases because Acid Spray reduces armor by up to 6, significantly amplifying physical damage from him and his allies. Prioritize maxing Acid Spray and consider an early Assault Cuirass to further reduce enemy armor.
search_text: Alchemist scenario vs high armor Acid Spray armor reduction Assault Cuirass physical damage

display_text: When playing from behind, Alchemist should avoid risky fights and focus on farming with Acid Spray and Chemical Rage. Split-push with Radiance illusions (Manta Style) or use Berserk Potion to save allies. Consider utility items like Shiva's Guard or Heaven's Halberd instead of pure damage. The Dividends Facet can be valuable to gift Scepters to your carries.

display_text: When facing multiple spellcasters or heavy disables, itemization is key. After core items like Soul Ring and Radiance, rush Black King Bar. Follow up with a Satanic to sustain through magic damage during BKB duration. The Mixologist Facet is strong here for more frequent Berserk Potion dispels.
