display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When behind, Arc Warden should avoid direct fights. Use Tempest Double to safely split-push lanes and farm the enemy jungle. Stack Spark Wraiths defensively at choke points and near your towers to deter pushes. Prioritize survival items like Hurricane Pike or BKB to stay alive. Your goal is to create space and farm until you hit critical item timings like Mjollnir and Daedalus.
search_text: Arc Warden behind losing split push Tempest Double farm jungle defensive Spark Wraith stacks survival items Hurricane Pike BKB

display_text: Scenario: Versus High Armor / Illusion Heroes. Against high armor or illusion heroes (e.g., Terrorblade, Phantom Lancer), your physical damage from right-clicks is less effective. Focus on magical burst with Spark Wraiths (they deal pure magical damage) and itemize for magic damage (e.g., Mjollnir active, Ethereal Blade). Flux is weak against illusion heroes as the illusions will be near the real hero, canceling the damage. Use Magnetic Field primarily for the attack speed bonus to proc Mjollnir more often.
search_text: Arc Warden vs high armor illusions Terrorblade PL Spark Wraith magic damage Mjollnir Ethereal Blade Flux canceled by illusions Magnetic Field attack speed procs

display_text: Scenario: Sieging High Ground. Use Tempest Double to safely hit buildings from outside the enemy base's range. Place Magnetic Field on the clone or your siege creeps to protect them from tower and hero attacks. Pre-place Spark Wraiths on the enemy ramp to zone defenders. Use Flux on any defender who steps forward alone to slow and damage them. If the enemy has long-range initiation (e.g., Hook, Spear), keep your main hero far back.
search_text: Arc Warden siege high ground Tempest Double hit buildings Magnetic Field protect siege creeps Spark Wraiths zone ramp Flux slow isolated defender

display_text: Scenario: Countering Mobile Heroes. Against highly mobile heroes (e.g., Storm Spirit, Queen of Pain), Spark Wraith is your primary tool. Pre-cast Wraiths where you predict they will blink or zip to. The ministun and slow will interrupt and catch them. If you have Gleipnir, use the root from the clone to lock them down before they can escape. Flux is less reliable as they can easily stay near other units.
search_text: Arc Warden vs mobile Storm Spirit QoP Spark Wraith prediction ministun slow catch Gleipnir root Flux unreliable isolation

Right choice offers +200 Health, providing crucial survivability against early burst damage. Choose range if you are safely farming and want to pressure from further away. Choose health if you are being targeted and need the buffer to survive ganks.

Right choice offers +25 Magnetic Field Attack Speed, boosting the bonus from 30-120 to 55-145, significantly increasing the DPS of heroes inside the field. Choose Flux slow if you need more catch and control. Choose Magnetic Field attack speed if you or your carry rely heavily on right-clicks and are fighting inside the bubble often.

Right choice offers -5s Magnetic Field Cooldown, reducing it from 17 to 12 seconds at max level, allowing for much more frequent use in fights and sieges. Choose Spark Wraith damage for instant pick-off potential. Choose Magnetic Field cooldown for sustained teamfight presence and objective control.

Right choice makes Tempest Double Have No Penalties, removing the 35% movement slow and accuracy loss (blind) on the clone, turning it into a perfect, full-power fighter for its entire duration. Choose Spark Wraith delay for unrivaled area control and burst. Choose no penalties on the Double to maximize its fighting and pushing power, effectively having two full-strength Arc Wardens.

display_text: Void Spirit is a Melee hero with a base attack time of 1.7. Starting attributes: 22 Strength, 21 Agility, 24 Intelligence. This gives him balanced base damage and survivability, with higher Intelligence for mana pool and spellcasting.

display_text: Void Spirit has three Facets. Sanctuary changes Resonant Pulse to provide an all-damage barrier, ideal against magic-heavy lineups. Symmetry provides a physical damage barrier, effective against physical attackers. Call of the Void creates Aether Remnants from Dissimilate portals, enhancing area control and burst damage in teamfights.
search_text: Void Spirit facets Sanctuary Symmetry Call of the Void Resonant Pulse all damage barrier physical barrier Dissimilate Aether Remnants

display_text: In the laning phase, Void Spirit should use Aether Remnant to pull enemies and secure last hits, dealing 90/140/190/240 damage. Resonant Pulse provides a shield absorbing 25/50/75/100 physical damage plus 35/50/65/80 per hero hit, allowing favorable trades. Dissimilate offers escape with 1.1 second phase duration. Manage mana with base 24 Intelligence and Intrinsic Edge bonus.
search_text: Void Spirit laning Aether Remnant damage pull Resonant Pulse shield absorb Dissimilate escape mana management

display_text: Void Spirit's power spikes at level 6 with Astral Step, enabling gap-close and burst damage. Level 10 talents enhance mana regen or Resonant Pulse damage. Level 15 talents increase Aether Remnant damage or add Outer Dissimilate Ring. Level 20 reduces Astral Step charge restore time to 22/17/12 seconds. Level 25 offers 140% Astral Step critical or Dissimilate root for 2 seconds.
search_text: Void Spirit power spikes level 6 Astral Step level 10 talents level 15 talents level 20 charge restore level 25 critical root

display_text: Void Spirit is countered by silence and break effects that disable his Intrinsic Edge and prevent spellcasting. Instant hex or stuns can stop Dissimilate and Astral Step. High burst magic damage can bypass his physical barrier if not using Sanctuary facet.

display_text: Core items for Void Spirit are Bottle for sustain, Witch Blade for intelligence scaling and slow, and Scythe of Vyse for reliable disable. These items synergize with his spell-based playstyle and intelligence growth.

display_text: In teamfights, Void Spirit's role is to initiate with Astral Step to mark and slow enemies, follow up with Dissimilate for area damage, and use Aether Remnant to pull key targets. Resonant Pulse provides survivability, and he should focus on bursting down backline heroes.

display_text: Aether Remnant places a remnant at a target location with a 0.4 second activation delay. It lasts 17 seconds and has a 300 radius. When a unit crosses within 130 radius of its gaze over 450 distance, it pulls the unit towards the remnant by 44/50/56/62 units over 1.0/1.2/1.4/1.6 seconds, dealing 90/140/190/240 magic damage. Cooldown scales from 17 to 11 seconds. Does not pierce creeps (pierces_creeps: 0).

Call of the Void facet creates Aether Remnants at portals.

display_text: Snapfire has two Facet choices. 'Ricochet II' causes missed or glancing attacks to release two ricochet attacks at nearby enemies, synergizing with her passive and Lil' Shredder. 'Full Bore' makes Scatterblast narrower and longer and grants bonus damage and slow to enemies NOT at point-blank range, changing its use pattern from a close-range nuke to a long-range poke and wave clear tool.

display_text: In laning, use Scatterblast (Q) to secure ranged creeps and harass enemies. Firesnap Cookie (W) provides an escape or engage tool for yourself or your lane partner. The Ricochet II Facet can help you harass and last-hit more effectively when attacks miss. Focus on levels and gold to reach your power spikes.

display_text: Snapfire's first major power spike is at level 6 with Mortimer Kisses (R), giving her global teamfight presence and area denial. Level 12 (Ultimate level 2) increases the damage significantly. Levels 15, 20, and 25 provide major talent upgrades that boost her damage output (Scatterblast, Lil' Shredder), utility (Cookie cooldown), or ultimate power (more Mortimer Kisses). Key item timings like Aghanim's Scepter/Shard can also create spikes.

display_text: Hard counters to Snapfire include heroes with Break to disable her passive Buckshot, heroes with instant silences or hexes to prevent her from casting Mortimer Kisses or Firesnap Cookie, and heroes with high mobility to dodge her skillshots. display_text: Facet 1 (Solar Charged): Whenever Dawnbreaker hits a target with a powered up Luminosity, she decreases the cooldowns of all of her abilities. This facet encourages a brawling, spell-spamming playstyle, rewarding you for staying in prolonged fights and maximizing Luminosity hits. Facet 2 (Gleaming Hammer): Celestial Hammer heals allies and damages enemies when on the ground. This facet provides area control and sustain, making Celestial Hammer a zoning and healing tool, beneficial for holding ground and supporting allies in teamfights or sieges. Facet 3 (Starsurge): Dawnbreaker and nearby allies gain bonus attack speed at the end of Starbreaker for each swipe that hit enemy heroes. This turns Starbreaker into a teamfight steroid, favoring a core, right-click oriented build where you want to follow up your initiation with physical damage. Facet 4 (Trailblazer): Walking along Celestial Hammer's fire trail gives bonus movespeed. This facet enhances mobility and positioning, allowing for better chases, escapes, or repositioning during fights, and synergizes with the hammer recall mechanic.

display_text: Dawnbreaker is hard countered by Break effects (e.g., Silver Edge, Doom). Break disables Luminosity, preventing her from gaining stacks and critically striking for heals, removing her core sustain. Heroes with instant disables (Lion Hex, Shadow Shaman Shackles) can stop her during Starbreaker's channel or prevent Solar Guardian channel. High mobility heroes can easily kite her Starbreaker and avoid Celestial Hammer. Heavy magic burst can kill her before she can trigger Luminosity heals. She struggles against illusions and summons that can body block her hammer recall path.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Counters Hard Counter Break disables Luminosity Silver Edge Doom Instant disable stops Starbreaker Solar Guardian Kiting mobility High magic burst Illusions body block hammer

display_text: Core Build: Phase Boots are essential for addressing her mobility issues, granting speed and armor. Echo Sabre is a core damage item that synergizes perfectly with Luminosity, providing stats, mana regen, and a double hit that quickly charges the passive. Blink Dagger is critical for reliable initiation, allowing you to Blink-Starbreaker or Blink-Solar Guardian for unexpected engagements. BKB is almost always required to ensure you can complete your Starbreaker combo and cast Solar Guardian uninterrupted in teamfights.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Core Build Items Phase Boots Echo Sabre Luminosity synergy double hit Blink Dagger initiation Starbreaker Solar Guardian Black King Bar BKB spell immunity

display_text: In teamfights, Dawnbreaker's role is to initiate or counter-initiate. Use Blink Dagger into Starbreaker to stun and damage key targets. Follow up with Celestial Hammer to slow enemies and create a zoning fire trail. Your primary job is to be in the middle of the fight, triggering Luminosity heals to sustain your entire team. Save Solar Guardian to save a critical ally being focused or to turn a fight by arriving with a large AoE stun. Protect your backline by bodyblocking and using your disruptive abilities on divers.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Teamfight Role 5v5 Initiate Counter-initiate Blink Starbreaker Stun Celestial Hammer slow zone Luminosity heal team sustain Solar Guardian save ally global stun

display_text: ABILITY: Starbreaker. Dawnbreaker whirls her hammer around 3 times, damaging enemies with her attack plus bonus damage (25/40/55/70 per hit). On the final strike, she smashes her hammer down, stunning (0.6/0.8/1.0/1.2s) and damaging enemies in front of her. She travels at 215 movement speed for the duration and is briefly stunned for 0.2s after. The first two strikes slow enemy movement speed by 100% for 0.12s. Cooldown: 17/15/13/11. Does NOT pierce BKB. Cannot be used while Celestial Hammer is out.
search_text: Starbreaker E Dawnbreaker Whirl hammer 3 attacks 25 40 55 70 bonus damage Final strike smash stun 0.6 0.8 1.0 1.2 seconds 215 movement speed self stun 0.2s 100% slow 0.12s first two hits Cooldown 17 15 13 11

display_text: ABILITY: Celestial Hammer. Dawnbreaker hurls her weapon, damaging (50/80/110/140) enemies along its path (200 radius). The hammer pauses for 2 seconds at the destination (700/900/1100/1300 range) before returning, leaving a trail of fire that slows enemies by 24/28/32/36%. She can recall the hammer at any time, pulling her towards it. The hammer damages on both the throw and return. The trail's slow debuff lasts 2.5/3/3.5/4 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: ABILITY: Luminosity. Passive. After 3 attacks, Dawnbreaker powers up, charging her next attack with a critical hit that deals +125/150/175/200 bonus damage. This critical hit heals all allied heroes within 650 units for 35/40/45/50% of the attack damage dealt. Creeps and neutrals provide only 40 healing. Allies are healed for 50% of the amount. Break disables the passive. The heal is increased by Lifesteal Amplification.

display_text: ABILITY: Converge. Dawnbreaker recalls her hammer, pulling her and the hammer together so they meet in the middle. This is the active component of Celestial Hammer.

display_text: ABILITY: Break of Dawn. Passive. Whenever the sun comes out, Dawnbreaker gains bonus base damage and vision that decreases throughout the course of the day. Gains up to 25% bonus base damage and 20% bonus vision at dawn, decreasing to minimum at dusk.

display_text: ABILITY: Solar Guardian. Channeled. Dawnbreaker creates a pulsing effect at a location within 350 units of an allied hero anywhere on the map, damaging enemies (30/50/70) and healing allies (45/70/95) every 0.5 seconds. After channeling for 1.7 seconds, she flies to the target over 0.8 seconds, dealing 130/160/190 damage and stunning enemies for 1.4/1.6/1.8 seconds upon landing in a 500 radius. Cooldown: 120/105/90. Does NOT pierce BKB. Cannot be cast without the Hammer.

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When behind, focus on utility. Max Celestial Hammer for safe wave clear from distance. Use Solar Guardian defensively to save your strongest ally who is farming. Build cheaper survivability items like Magic Wand, Bracer, and Phase Boots. Avoid committing Starbreaker aggressively unless it guarantees a kill on a fed enemy. Your value comes from the global save and sustain for your team's real cores.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Situational Behind Losing Game Defensive play Max Celestial Hammer wave clear Solar Guardian save ally Utility items Phase Bracer Magic Wand Avoid aggressive Starbreaker

display_text: Scenario: Vs High Magic Burst Lineups. Prioritize early Magic Resistance and HP. An early Hood of Defiance or Pipe of Insight is crucial. Your goal is to survive the initial burst to get a Luminosity heal off. Use Solar Guardian reactively after enemy spells are used, not to initiate. Consider a BKB earlier than usual. Facet choice may lean towards Gleaming Hammer for the extra sustain on the ground.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Situational vs Magic Burst High Magic Damage Build Hood Pipe Magic Resist HP Survive burst Luminosity heal Solar Guardian reactive save Early BKB Gleaming Hammer facet

display_text: Scenario: Sieging High Ground. Use Celestial Hammer to throw through the enemy base, creating a fire trail on the high ground to slow defenders and zone them. Stand in the trail with the Trailblazer facet for bonus movespeed to dodge spells. Your Luminosity heals can sustain the siege on your creep wave and allies. Hold Solar Guardian to save a key ally if they get initiated on during the push.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Situational Siege High Ground Push Celestial Hammer fire trail zone slow high ground Trailblazer movespeed Luminosity heal sustain creep wave Solar Guardian save

display_text: Scenario: Vs Illusion Heroes (Phantom Lancer, Chaos Knight). These heroes are problematic as they can body block your hammer recall and drain your Luminosity attacks. Build early Battle Fury or Cleave items to handle the illusions. Maelstrom/Mjollnir is also effective. Focus your Starbreaker and Celestial Hammer on the real hero when identified. Solar Guardian's large AoE damage and stun can help clear illusions on landing.
search_text: Dawnbreaker Situational vs Illusions Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight Body block hammer recall Luminosity stacks wasted Build Battle Fury Cleave Maelstrom Mjollnir Starbreaker target real hero

display_text: Level 10 Talents: Left: +15% Break of Dawn Max Damage. Right: +15% Celestial Hammer Slow. Choose +15% Celestial Hammer Slow for more reliable chase, lockdown, and teamfight control, as the slow increase from 24/28/32/36% is significant. Choose +15% Break of Dawn Max Damage only if you are playing a pure right-click core and are confident you can manfight during the day; the damage boost is situational and less reliable.

display_text: Level 15 Talents: Left: +40% Luminosity Critical Strike Damage. Right: -15s Solar Guardian Cooldown. The +40% Luminosity Critical Strike Damage is almost always superior. It increases your burst heal and damage substantially, making you much tankier and more threatening in fights. The -15s Solar Guardian Cooldown is niche, only valuable if the game revolves around constant global saves and you are playing purely as a support.

display_text: Level 20 Talents: Left: +150 Solar Guardian Radius. Right: -1 Luminosity Attacks Required. The -1 Luminosity Attacks Required talent is a massive power spike, changing the requirement from 3 attacks to 2. This dramatically increases your healing output and damage frequency, making you incredibly durable. The +150 Solar Guardian Radius (increasing from 500 to 650) is strong for teamfight control, but is generally outclassed by the transformative effect of the Luminosity talent.

display_text: Level 25 Talents: Left: -4s Starbreaker Cooldown. Right: +80% Celestial Hammer Cast Range/Speed. Choose -4s Starbreaker Cooldown to become a relentless initiator and disabler, reducing its cooldown to as low as 7 seconds. This is ideal for core Dawnbreaker. Choose +80% Celestial Hammer Cast Range/Speed for unparalleled range and zoning; the range becomes 1260/1620/1980/2340, allowing you to scout, initiate, or escape from extreme distances, better for a utility or save-oriented playstyle.

display_text: Marci is a melee hero with 1.7 base attack time, 23 base Strength, 20 base Agility, and 19 base Intelligence. Her melee attack type means she must be in close range, but her solid base Strength provides good early survivability.
search_text: Marci stats attack type melee base attack time 1.7 strength 23 agility 20 intelligence 19

display_text: Facet 1: Sidekick enhances Marci's ability to protect an ally by granting them shared lifesteal and damage. The effect is stronger if she is close to the ally, rewarding positioning. Facet 2: Bodyguard Facet also protects an ally, but focuses on counter-attacking enemies that attack the protected hero, requiring Marci to be in attack range. Facet 3: Buddy System shares a portion of health healed by Bodyguard between Marci and her partner. Facet 4: Pick-me-up allows Rebound to be alt-cast to bring the allied hero with Marci after her jump, increasing its utility. Facet 5: Fleeting Fury grants Marci a Fury combo immediately upon landing with Rebound, providing burst damage.
search_text: Marci facets Sidekick Bodyguard Facet Buddy System Pick-me-up Fleeting Fury ally lifesteal damage counter-attack shared healing bring ally combo Rebound

display_text: In laning, use Marci's melee range to secure last hits near the creep wave. Dispose can be used to throw an enemy hero into your tower or a teammate for harassment and a 20-50% slow. Rebound at level 1 provides an escape or engage tool with a 450-range jump to an ally, damaging and stunning enemies where she lands (75 damage, 0.8-1.7s stun). Bodyguard passive grants 12-30 lifesteal and bonus damage, which is effective for trading. Use Rebound to close gaps and Dispose to control enemy positioning.

Level 12: With maxed Dispose and Rebound, Marci has strong catch and initiation. Level 18: Maxed Unleash provides 1150 bonus attack speed. Level 20: Talent choice between +0.75s Rebound Stun Duration or +10% Unleash Movement Speed. Level 25: Talent choice between Unleash Duration Extended By 6s On Kill or +{s:bonus_bonus_damage}% Bodyguard Damage. Key item timings include Phase Boots for mobility and an early Blink Dagger for initiation.

Heavy slows and roots prevent her from closing distance for melee attacks and Dispose throws. Instant disables like hex or stun can interrupt Rebound's jump. Heroes with high burst damage can kill her before she can use Unleash combos. Mana burn effects can deplete her mana pool, limiting ability usage.

Core Build 2: Black King Bar is critical to ensure she can channel Unleash without being disabled. Core Build 3: Assault Cuirass or Heaven's Halberd provide survivability and utility; Cuirass for armor and attack speed, Halberd for disarm and evasion.

display_text: In teamfights, Marci's role is to initiate and disrupt. Use Blink Dagger and Rebound to jump onto a key enemy hero, stunning them. Follow up with Dispose to throw another target into your team or away from the fight, damaging and slowing them. Activate Unleash to rapidly attack a stunned or slowed target, slowing their attack and movement speed by 60-100% and 30% respectively, and dealing AoE pulse damage. Use Bodyguard on a vulnerable core ally to protect them and counter-attack threats.

Marci grabs an allied or enemy target and throws it effortlessly behind her, damaging and slowing the unit if it's an enemy (60/150/240/330 damage, 20/30/40/50% slow for 3s). Marci bounds to a targeted allied unit (450/550/650/750 cast range), choosing a direction and distance she will spring away from it (max jump distance same as cast range). Upon reaching the unit, she lunges to her final destination, damaging (75/150/225/300) and stunning enemies (0.8/1.1/1.4/1.7s) in a 300 radius where she lands. The ally receives 15/20/25/30% bonus movement speed for 4.0s. Cooldown is 24/20/16/12s. The ability can be disjointed. Does NOT pierce BKB unless specified.

display_text: Ability: Unleash. Marci taps a hidden power, gaining Fury charges that allow her to deliver a rapid sequence of 5 strikes per flurry that slow movement (30%) and attack speed (60/80/100%) of the target for 2.0s. The last strike in every Fury combo creates a damaging pulse (50/100/150 damage) around the target. Between Fury combos, Marci is unable to attack for 1.5s. She gains 15% movement speed when cast. Duration is 16s. Cooldown is 80/70/60s. Mana cost is 100/125/150. Once a Fury combo is started, each attack must be delivered within 1s or the combo expires. Removes attack speed limit. Does NOT pierce BKB unless specified.

Unleash pulse damage is magical, so focusing on increasing its impact with magic amplification is beneficial.

display_text: Primal Beast is a Melee strength hero with a slow base attack rate of 1.8. He has high base Strength (26) but low base Agility (15) and Intelligence (16), meaning he has high HP but low armor and a small mana pool. His attack range is standard for melee heroes, and his turn rate (0.7 from the Onslaught ability data) is average. His primary weakness in the laning stage is low armor and susceptibility to mana burn due to his low Intelligence.
search_text: Primal Beast stats melee strength 26 15 16 1.8 attack rate low armor mana

display_text: Facet 1: Romp n' Stomp - Trample grants bonus movement speed to Primal Beast and his allies. This is a team-oriented facet, ideal for initiating fights with your team or helping allies disengage. Choose this when your team lacks mobility and needs to stick to targets or escape together. Facet 2: Provoke the Beast - Gain 3 Uproar stacks upon being disabled by an enemy hero. This is an aggressive, bait-oriented facet. Pick this when facing heroes with many single-target disables (like Lion, Shadow Shaman) to turn their attempts to control you into a significant damage and armor buff. Facet 3: Ferocity - Each Pulverize slam increases Primal Beast's AoE. This is a late-game scaling facet for teamfight dominance. Select this when you expect long, drawn-out fights and need to control a larger area with your ultimate, especially against illusion or summon-heavy lineups.
search_text: Primal Beast facets Romp n Stomp Provoke the Beast Ferocity Trample speed Uproar stacks disable Pulverize AoE

display_text: In the laning phase (first 10 minutes), leverage Primal Beast's high base damage and the threat of Trample. Start with a point in Uproar for trading hits, as each instance of 50+ hero damage grants a stack, increasing your damage and armor when activated. Use Trample level 1 carefully for last-hitting ranged creeps or harassing, but be mindful of the 5.5-second disarm. Your primary goal is to survive and get levels, as your kill potential is low before level 6. Focus on securing last hits with your high base damage. Be cautious of mana usage; your Intelligence is low.
search_text: Primal Beast laning phase early game Trample Uproar stacks trade damage disarm last hit

display_text: Power Spike 1: Level 6 with Pulverize. This gives you solo kill potential with a full channel, dealing up to 100/175/250 + 20/40/60 bonus damage per hit. Power Spike 2: Level 12 with maxed Trample (cooldown reduced to 21s) and level 2 Pulverize. Power Spike 3: Level 18 with maxed Rock Throw and level 3 Pulverize. Talent Spikes: Level 10 talents (+30 Damage or +25% Magic Resistance During Trample) enhance your right-click or survivability. Level 15 (-5s Trample Cooldown or +6 Uproar Armor Per Stack) improves mobility/durability or tankiness. Level 20 (Basic Self-Dispel on Uproar Cast or +20% Trample Attack Multiplier) offers utility or massive damage. Level 25 (Cannot be slowed or rooted during Trample or +67% Pulverize Duration) are both massive late-game upgrades. Item Spike: Blink Dagger allows reliable initiation. Black King Bar ensures you can channel Pulverize uninterrupted.
search_text: Primal Beast power spikes level 6 12 18 20 25 talents Blink Dagger BKB Pulverize Trample

display_text: Primal Beast is hard countered by: 1. Break (Silver Edge, Hoodwink's Bushwhack). Break disables the gaining of new Uproar stacks, removing his primary source of bonus damage and armor. 2. Kiting and mobility. Heroes with high movement speed or blinks (Weaver, Storm Spirit) can easily avoid Onslaught and Trample. 3. Instant Disables and Silences. Hex (Lion, Shadow Shaman) or global silence (Silencer) can prevent him from activating Uproar or cancel Trample/Onslaught. 4. Mana Burn (Anti-Mage, Nyx Assassin). His low Intelligence pool makes him extremely vulnerable to having his mana depleted, rendering him unable to cast spells.
search_text: Primal Beast counters break Silver Edge Uproar kiting mobility silence hex mana burn Anti-Mage

display_text: Core Build: 1. Blink Dagger is absolutely essential. It allows Primal Beast to instantly close the gap and land Pulverize or start Trample on key targets. 2. Black King Bar is non-negotiable. Pulverize is a channeled ability that lasts 2.3 seconds and does NOT pierce BKB. BKB ensures you cannot be interrupted during your ultimate. 3. Aghanim's Scepter is a core luxury item. It provides Rock Throw, a long-range initiation and crowd control tool that also generates Uproar stacks.

display_text: In a 5v5 teamfight, Primal Beast's primary role is that of a disruptive initiator and frontline tank. Your job is to start fights with Blink + Pulverize on a key hero, locking them down for your team. Use Onslaught to disrupt enemy formations, knocking heroes out of position. Activate Trample to deal significant AoE damage while moving through the fight. Use Uproar reactively when you take damage to gain massive bonus damage and armor, making you incredibly hard to kill. Your goal is to absorb spells and create chaos, allowing your carries to clean up.

Use Rock Throw (with Aghanim's) or Onslaught to disrupt enemy pushes from a distance. Build survivability items like Heaven's Halberd or Lotus Orb. Your role becomes a counter-initiator; wait for the enemy to jump your cores, then use Pulverize on their follow-up heroes.

display_text: SITUATION: With Aghanim's Scepter Timing. Once you acquire Aghanim's Scepter, your playstyle changes. You now have a long-range initiation tool. Use Rock Throw to start fights from over 2000 range, stunning and damaging key targets before blinking in with Pulverize. display_text: SITUATION: Versus Heavy Magic Damage Lineups. Your low magic resistance makes you vulnerable. Solution: Take the level 10 talent for +25% Magic Resistance During Trample. Build a Pipe of Insight for your team. Use Trample to move through fights while reducing magic damage taken. Activate Uproar for the armor, but remember it doesn't give magic resistance.

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 10. Left Choice: +30 Damage. This significantly boosts your right-click damage, improving farming speed and man-fighting potential. Right Choice: +25% Magic Resistance During Trample. This is a defensive option that makes you much tankier while using your primary damage/mobility spell, especially against magic-heavy lineups. Choose the damage if you are farming well and need to scale. Choose the magic resistance if you are facing nukers and need to survive initial bursts to get your spells off.

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 15. Left Choice: -5s Trample Cooldown. Reduces Trample's cooldown from 21s to 16s at max level. This greatly increases your mobility, damage uptime, and wave clear. Right Choice: +6 Uproar Armor Per Stack. Increases the armor per Uproar stack from 5 to 11 at max level. This makes you incredibly tanky against physical damage when you have 6 stacks (66 bonus armor). Choose the cooldown if you need to be more active in fights and skirmishes. Choose the armor if the enemy team has high physical damage (e.g., Drow Ranger, Troll Warlord) and you are the main frontliner.

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 20. Left Choice: Basic Self-Dispel on Uproar Cast. When activated, Uproar removes basic debuffs like silences, slows, and roots. This is a crucial utility talent that prevents you from being kited or locked down. Right Choice: +20% Trample Attack Multiplier. Increases the attack damage multiplier of Trample from 35% to 55%. This is a massive damage increase, especially if you have built attack damage items. Choose the dispel if the enemy has key slows or silences you need to purge (e.g., Silencer, Venomancer). Choose the damage if you are snowballing and can freely Trample through fights without fear of disable.

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 25. Left Choice: Cannot be slowed or rooted during Trample. This makes Trample an unstoppable movement and damage spell, allowing you to ignore crowd control like Crystal Nova or Rod of Atos. Right Choice: +67% Pulverize Duration. Increases Pulverize's channel time from 2.3 seconds to approximately 3.84 seconds. This deals significantly more damage and lockdown. Choose the Trample immunity if you are being kited and need to guarantee you reach your target. Choose the Pulverize duration if you can reliably get the cast off (e.g., with BKB) and want to delete a single target or control an area for longer.

display_text: Dance of the Dead: The Calling becomes stronger when heroes die inside it. This is excellent for teamfight-oriented or objective-focused lineups where you can force fights in chokepoints. It synergizes with other AoE control and damage, rewarding you for successful fights. Ofrenda: Muerta can place a beneficial Ofrenda on the map to respawn there instead of the fountain. This grants incredible map presence and split-push security. It's strong for map control, allowing you to be aggressive and defend objectives instantly after respawning. Quickdraw: Dead Shot fears increase Gunslinger chance. Gunslinger procs reduce Dead Shot cooldown. This facet synergizes with itself, rewarding aggressive play and ability usage. It's ideal for players focusing on casting Dead Shot and auto-attacking, creating a fluid 'spell-carry' playstyle. Choose Dance of the Dead for teamfight power, Ofrenda for map control, or Quickdraw for personal power and synergy.

display_text: In lane, prioritize Dead Shot (Q) for harass, securing ranged creeps, and controlling the lane equilibrium by using its ricochet off creeps or trees to fear the enemy hero. Max Dead Shot first for damage. Gunslinger (E) is often taken at level 2 or 4 to increase your harass potential and CS ability; the secondary shot can be used to harass enemies behind creeps. Use your high base Intelligence to spam Dead Shot. Look for kills once you have 2-3 points in Dead Shot and can secure the slow and fear off a ricochet. Your starting attributes are not tanky, so positioning is key.

display_text: Level 6 is a significant spike as Pierce the Veil (R) allows you to trade aggressively, dive towers, and become immune to physical damage for 8 seconds. Level 12 (Ultimate Level 2) increases the bonus damage to 100. Level 15 talents (+20 Attack Speed vs +3.0% Max HP Regen) are a core fighting spike. Level 18 (Ultimate Level 3) brings bonus damage to 130. Level 20 talent (+55 Gunslinger Damage) is a massive DPS increase. Level 25 talent (2 Dead Shot Charges) is a game-changing late-game spike for control and burst. Key item timings include early mana regen (Bottle, Falcon Blade), mobility (Blink Dagger, Force Staff), and magic damage amplification (Kaya, Witch Blade).

display_text: Muerta is countered by heroes and items that negate magic damage or provide high magic resistance (e.g., Anti-Mage, Visage, Pipe of Insight, Hood of Defiance). Since her ultimate makes her immune to physical but not magic damage, nukers with magical burst (Lina, Zeus) can kill her during Pierce the Veil. Instant silences or hexes prevent her from activating her ultimate. Break (Silver Edge, Doom) disables Gunslinger's passive chance. Heroes with high mobility can dodge Dead Shot ricochet angles.

display_text: In teamfights, Muerta's role is to initiate or counter-initiate with The Calling (W) to zone and silence key targets, then use Dead Shot (Q) to fear enemies into the revenants or away from your team. Activate Pierce the Veil (R) to become immune to physical damage and convert all your attacks to magical damage. Position on the edge of the fight, using Gunslinger (E) to deal massive AoE magical damage. Your job is to provide crowd control (slow, fear, silence) and deal sustained magical damage while being untouchable by right-clickers.

display_text: ABILITY: Dead Shot (Q). Muerta fires a ghostly trickshot at an enemy unit or tree from 1000 range. When the bullet strikes, it deals 100/175/250/325 damage and briefly slows by 100% for 1 second. The bullet then ricochets in the targeted direction, traveling up to 1.5x the cast range (1500). The ricochet deals the same damage to all units it passes through, stopping when it hits a hero. Heroes hit by the ricochet are feared away from the impact point for 0.8/0.95/1.1/1.25 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB.

display_text: Monkey King is a Melee hero with a base attack range of 150. He has a base attack time of 1.6, which is average. He starts with 24 base Agility (good), 19 base Strength (average), and 20 base Intelligence (average). His primary attribute is Agility. display_text: Monkey King has three Facets. Wukong's Faithful extends the duration his Wukong's Command soldiers last and prevents them from dispersing unless he spends more than a specified time (value not provided in raw data) outside the area. This enhances his teamfight zone control. Simian Stride increases the max level of Tree Dance, and at max level reduces its cooldown to zero, enabling near-permanent mobility and escape. Changing of the Guard allows him to transform into one of his soldiers during Wukong's Command, adding deception and survivability. Choose Wukong's Faithful for pure teamfight, Simian Stride for maximum mobility and split-push, or Changing of the Guard for deceptive plays and evasion within your ultimate.

display_text: Monkey King's laning phase focuses on trading with Jingu Mastery. His goal is to land four consecutive attacks on the same enemy hero to activate the bonus damage and lifesteal. Use Boundless Strike to secure last hits, harass, and add a stack to the Jingu counter. Use Tree Dance primarily as an escape tool to avoid ganks or to set up for a Primal Spring nuke. Harass aggressively when Jingu Mastery is charged, using the lifesteal to sustain. Be wary of opponents with strong dispels or who can break your passive. Ward enemy jungle camps to use trees for mobility.

display_text: Monkey King's power spikes are tied to levels and key items. Level 2 is a minor spike with a point in Jingu Mastery. Level 6 with Wukong's Command provides massive kill threat in fights and dives. Level 12 (maxed Jingu Mastery) significantly increases his sustained damage and lifesteal. Level 15 talent (+0.2s Boundless Strike stun) increases lockdown. Level 18 (maxed Primal Spring) provides high burst damage. The level 20 talent choice (0 Cooldown Primal Spring or +60% Boundless Strike crit) defines his late-game playstyle as a mobile nuker or a burst fighter. Level 25 talents provide massive upgrades to his core mechanics. Item spikes include Echo Sabre (for easy Jingu procs), Desolator (armor reduction), and Black King Bar (to enable his ultimate).

display_text: Monkey King is hard countered by several mechanics. Break effects (e.g., from Silver Edge, Viper's Nethertoxin) disable the gaining of new Jingu Mastery stacks, crippling his trading. Instant disables and hexes prevent him from using Tree Dance to escape. Area-of-effect damage and stuns can knock him out of trees, causing a 3-second stun. Heroes with high mobility can kite him outside his Wukong's Command circle. Illusion and summon-based heroes can body-block his movements and dilute his single-target focus. Dispels can remove his active Jingu Mastery buff.

display_text: Monkey King's core build is essential for enabling his playstyle. Echo Sabre is the first major item, providing mana regeneration, strength, and a double-hit that guarantees a quick Jingu Mastery proc. Black King Bar is almost always mandatory to allow him to channel Primal Spring and fight inside Wukong's Command without being disabled. Desolator is a classic damage item that synergizes with his high physical damage output from Jingu Mastery and Boundless Strike, helping him shred armor.

display_text: In teamfights, Monkey King's role is to initiate or counter-initiate with a well-placed Wukong's Command, creating a large zone of control. He should look to land a multi-hero Boundless Strike for the stun and crit damage. His job is to jump on priority targets using Tree Dance and Primal Spring, then use his Jingu Mastery lifesteal to manfight within his ultimate circle. He must stay within the area of his soldiers to maintain the bonus armor and zone, unless using the Changing of the Guard Facet. He is a front-line fighter and disruptor.

Deals 20/40/60/80 flat damage plus a critical strike based on his attack. The critical multiplier is 120%/140%/160%/180%. Has True Strike. Cast range is 1100. Radius is 150. Cooldown is 24/21/18/15 seconds. Does not affect spell-immune enemies.

display_text: Tree Dance (W): Monkey King jumps to a tree and perches atop it. Ground jump distance is 800. Cast range is 800. While perched, he gains Primal Spring. His perched vision is 700 day and 500 night. If his tree is destroyed, he falls and is stunned for 3.0 seconds. Taking damage while on the ground puts Tree Dance on a 3.0 second cooldown. Cooldown starts upon landing.

display_text: Primal Spring (E): This is a channeled leap attack available while perched on a tree. Monkey King springs from his perch, damaging and slowing enemies where he lands. Damage is up to 110/200/290/380. Slow is up to 35%/50%/65%/80% for 4.0 seconds. Damage and slow scale with channel duration. Impact radius is 375. Cooldown is 18/16/14/12 seconds.

display_text: Jingu Mastery (Passive): Monkey King's attacks awaken the Jingu Bang's power. Upon the fourth hit on the same enemy hero, he earns 4 charged attacks. These charged attacks have 30/75/120/165 bonus damage and 20%/40%/60%/80% lifesteal. The counter debuff on the enemy lasts 5.5/7.0/8.5/10.0 seconds. The buff on Monkey King lasts a maximum of 35 seconds. Hitting an enemy with Boundless Strike adds to the hit counter. Break disables gaining new stacks but does not disable bonuses from existing stacks. The Jingu Mastery buff on self can be dispelled.

display_text: Mischief (F): Monkey King changes shape to a nearby object, creating a disguise. Taking damage, attacking, or using any item or ability breaks the disguise. Grants damage immunity for 0.1 seconds upon transforming. Revert Form ability returns him to normal.

display_text: Wukong's Command (R): Monkey King creates a circular formation of soldiers. The first ring has 5 soldiers at a 300 radius. The second ring has 9 soldiers at a 750 radius. Soldiers have Monkey King's attack damage and only target heroes. Monkey King gains 15/20/25 bonus armor for the 14 second duration. If Monkey King leaves the area, his soldiers disperse (modified by Facets). Soldiers do not receive Jingu Mastery bonuses, Basher, or Abyssal Blade effects. Cast range is 625.

display_text: When playing against high armor and evasion heroes, Monkey King struggles as his damage is primarily physical. Prioritize items like Monkey King Bar to counter evasion and Assault Cuirass or Desolator to reduce armor. Consider a Nullifier against targets with Ghost Scepter or Ethereal Blade. Your Jingu Mastery lifesteal is less effective against high physical resistance.

display_text: When playing from behind, Monkey King should avoid direct 5v5 confrontations. Use Tree Dance and Primal Spring to safely wave clear and split push lanes. Place deep wards to scout and use Mischief for deception when farming risky areas. Build cost-effective survivability items like Black King Bar and Sange and Yasha. Look for pick-offs with teammates using your mobility and Boundless Strike stun.

display_text: When facing heroes with instant disables or silences (e.g., Skywrath Mage, Lion), Monkey King's Tree Dance escape is nullified. Itemize for early Black King Bar. Consider a Linken's Sphere against targeted disables. Position carefully and rely on teammates to initiate or absorb spells first. The Changing of the Guard Facet can provide a moment of unpredictability.

display_text: With a level 10 talent, choose between +85 Primal Spring Max Damage or +0.2s Boundless Strike Stun Duration. Take the Primal Spring damage if you are focusing on burst and wave clear, especially from a distance using trees. Take the extra stun duration if your team needs more lockdown or you are fighting more directly, as it increases the window for your team to follow up.

display_text: With a level 15 talent, choose between +450 Tree Dance Cast Range or +110 Jingu Mastery Damage. The increased Tree Dance range dramatically improves your mobility, initiation range, and escape options, making you much harder to catch. The Jingu Mastery damage is a substantial raw damage increase for manfighting. Choose Tree Dance range for utility and survivability, or Jingu damage if you are the primary right-click core and can stick to targets.

display_text: With a level 20 talent, choose between 0 Cooldown Primal Spring or +60% Boundless Strike Critical Damage. 0 Cooldown Primal Spring transforms Monkey King into an extremely mobile hero, allowing constant leaps for damage, slow, and repositioning. The +60% Boundless Strike crit turns it into a massive nuke, especially when combined with high attack damage items. Choose based on whether you need mobility and sustained harass (Primal Spring) or front-loaded burst damage (Boundless Strike).
search_text: talent level 20 left 0 cooldown Primal Spring right +60% Boundless Strike crit damage mobility burst

display_text: With a level 25 talent, choose between -1 Jingu Mastery Required Hits or Additional Wukong's Command Ring. -1 Jingu Mastery Required Hits means you only need 3 attacks to activate the bonus damage and lifesteal, making it much easier to proc in fights and dramatically increasing your sustained damage and survivability. The Additional Wukong's Command Ring creates an extra ring of soldiers at an unspecified range, greatly increasing the area coverage and damage potential of your ultimate in teamfights. Choose Jingu for a self-focused damage boost, or the extra ring for area control and teamfight presence.

display_text: In the laning phase, use Catchy Lick (700 cast range) to pull enemy heroes out of position or to save an ally by pulling them 375 units. The damage (85-340) and basic dispel make it a strong trading tool. Frogstomp provides area control with a 350 radius slow (12-30%) and ministuns, useful for securing range creeps or discouraging dives. Start with Catchy Lick for dispels and trading, then level Frogstomp for wave clear and kill potential. Largo's low armor from 14 base agility makes him vulnerable to physical harass.

display_text: Largo's key power spikes are at level 6 (Amphibian Rhapsody) and level 12 (maxed basic abilities). Level 6 unlocks his song kit, allowing him to provide team-wide utility (heal, spell amp, or movement speed) but disarms him. Maxing Frogstomp by level 7 increases its total damage to 455 (65 damage per stomp for 7 ticks) and slow to 30%. Croak of Genius at max level (30 second duration, 45% damage echo) is a significant buff for mana-hungry cores. Item timings like Arcane Boots and Mekansm are essential to sustain his song mana costs (20-44 per cast).

Seeing Red allows Warpath to be activated for a duration, granting its bonuses and bonus movement/attack speed, at the cost of limited personal vision and no allied vision.

display_text: Techies has three facets. The first facet grants extra attack range, which is useful for safely applying pressure and last hitting from a distance. The second facet, Spleen's Secret Sauce, increases both the damage dealt and the self-damage taken by Blast Off!, making it a higher-risk, higher-reward option for aggressive, suicidal plays. The third facet increases backpack slot capacity, which is a highly utility-focused choice for carrying more items like wards, gems, or situational consumables.
search_text: Techies facets first extra attack range facet second Spleen's Secret Sauce increase Blast Off damage self damage third facet backpack slot capacity utility

display_text: During the laning phase, Techies relies on Sticky Bomb for harassment and zoning. The ability slows by 25/35/45/55% for 1.2 seconds, making it easier for you or your lane partner to secure kills. Use your high starting Intelligence to spam it. Place Proximity Mines defensively near your tower or offensively in lane bushes to control the area, but remember they have a 1-second activation delay and become visible if an enemy is within 500 AoE. Avoid taking bad trades due to your low base armor and HP.
search_text: Techies laning phase early game skill build Sticky Bomb slow 25 35 45 55 harassment Proximity Mine zone control activation delay 1 second visible range 500

display_text: Techies' first major power spike is at level 6 with Blast Off!, a 400 AoE stun and nuke. A good Blast Off! can win a fight. Level 12 unlocks the level 15 talent tier. The 25 talent of +252 damage is a massive spike, turning Techies into a right-click threat. Key item spikes are Aghanim's Scepter for mobile mines and Kaya & Sange for spell damage amplification and survivability. The 20 talent for -0.8s Proximity Mines activation delay is also a huge offensive spike.
search_text: Techies power spikes level 6 Blast Off stun damage level 12 15 talents level 20 talent -0.8s mine delay level 25 talent +252 damage Aghanim Scepter Kaya Sange

display_text: Techies is hard countered by heroes with high mobility and vision. Heroes like Zeus (Nimbus, True Sight), Bounty Hunter (Track), Slardar (Amplify Damage), or any hero with Gem of True Sight can reveal and destroy Proximity Mines easily. Instant disables like Hex or long-range stuns prevent Techies from using Blast Off! or escaping. High magic resistance or pipe carriers greatly reduce Techies' spell damage output.
search_text: Techies counters hard counters True Sight Zeus Bounty Hunter Slardar Gem instant hex stun stop Blast Off high magic resistance Pipe

display_text: Aghanim's Scepter is an absolutely essential core item for Techies. It allows Proximity Mines to move, transforming them from static area denial into active, roaming threats. Kaya & Sange is another core item, providing spell damage amplification for all of Techies' magic damage, plus status resistance and health for survivability. Aghanim's Shard is also critical, granting the Minefield Sign which makes mines within 500 range invulnerable and deal 15% more damage.
search_text: Techies core build essential items Aghanim Scepter mobile Proximity Mines Kaya Sange spell amp status resistance Aghanim Shard Minefield Sign invulnerable damage 15%

display_text: In a teamfight, Techies' primary role is area control and initiation. Use Blast Off! to jump into the enemy team, dealing 200/300/400/500 damage and stunning for 0.8/1/1.2/1.4 seconds in a 400 radius. Pre-fight, litter the battlefield with Proximity Mines and use Minefield Sign to protect key clusters. Use Sticky Bomb to slow key targets. Reactive Tazer provides a disarm for divers and, when detonated, a 450 radius AoE disarm. Your job is to disrupt and zone, not to manfight.
search_text: Techies teamfight role area control initiation Blast Off jump stun damage Proximity Mine zone Minefield Sign protect Sticky Bomb slow Reactive Tazer disarm

display_text: Sticky Bomb (Q): Techies lobs a bomb that sticks to the first enemy hero it lands near, slowing their movement speed by 25/35/45/55% for 1.2 seconds. After a 2-second countdown, it explodes for 95/170/245/320 magic damage in a 350 radius and applies a secondary slow of 15/25/35/45% for 3 seconds. The bomb has a travel speed of 500 and chases a target for up to 4 seconds if none is initially stuck. It has a 300 placement radius.
search_text: Techies Sticky Bomb Q slow 25 35 45 55 duration 1.2 damage 95 170 245 320 radius 350 secondary slow 15 25 35 45 countdown 2 travel speed 500 chase time 4

display_text: Reactive Tazer (W): Activating this ability grants Techies 18/22/26/30 bonus movement speed for 6 seconds. During this time, any enemy that attacks Techies is disarmed for 2.4/2.7/3/3.3 seconds and receives a Basic Dispel. At the end of the 6-second duration, the charge explodes, disarming all enemies within a 450 radius for the same duration. The ability can be manually detonated early.
search_text: Techies Reactive Tazer W bonus movement speed 18 22 26 30 duration 6 disarm attacker 2.4 2.7 3 3.3 Basic Dispel final AoE disarm radius 450 manual detonate

display_text: Blast Off! (E): Techies hurtles forward and explodes upon landing, dealing 200/300/400/500 magic damage and stunning enemies in a 400 radius for 0.8/1/1.2/1.4 seconds. The ability costs 20% of Techies' current HP as non-lethal pure self-damage and has a cooldown of 40/35/30/25 seconds. This ability destroys trees in its path.

display_text: Minefield Sign (R): Techies plants a sign with a 500 radius aura that lasts 60 seconds. Proximity Mines and Sticky Bombs within this aura become invulnerable and deal 15% increased damage. Only one sign can exist at a time. The ability has a 60-second cooldown.
search_text: Techies Minefield Sign R ultimate aura radius 500 duration 60 makes Proximity Mines Sticky Bombs invulnerable damage increase 15% one sign cooldown 60

display_text: Proximity Mines (D): Techies plants an invisible mine that lasts until destroyed or triggered. It becomes visible if an enemy is within its 500 active AoE. If an enemy remains within that AoE for 1.0 second, it explodes, dealing 400/575/750 magic damage (reduced by distance, down to 60 on the edge) and reducing the enemy's magic resistance by 15/20/25% for 5 seconds. Deals 30% damage to buildings. Mines cannot be placed within 350 range of another mine. They have a 15-second charge restore time. Grants 30 gold to an enemy hero if destroyed.
search_text: Techies Proximity Mines D invisible damage 400 575 750 radius 500 activation delay 1.0 second magic resistance reduction 15 20 25 building damage 30% charge cooldown 15 gold bounty 30

display_text: Scenario: Playing Against True Sight. When facing heroes like Zeus, Slardar, or a team with Gem/Gem carriers, your static Proximity Mines become useless. In this scenario, prioritize Aghanim's Scepter to make your mines mobile and harder to track. Avoid static minefields. Focus on using Sticky Bomb and Blast Off! for direct combat impact. Consider the Backpack Facet to carry your own Gem or Dust to counter enemy invisibility.
search_text: Techies vs True Sight Zeus Slardar Gem counter build Aghanim Scepter mobile mines avoid static minefields focus Sticky Bomb Blast Off backpack facet carry gem

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. When your team is losing map control, you cannot safely place offensive mines. Use Proximity Mines defensively around your towers and jungle entrances to deter pushes and secure farm for your carries. Skill the Reactive Tazer facet for extra utility and survivability. Build utility items like Force Staff, Glimmer Cape, or Lotus Orb to save allies. Your role shifts to defensive area denial and saving teammates with disarms and stuns.
search_text: Techies playing from behind defensive mines tower protection jungle entrances use Reactive Tazer utility build Force Staff Glimmer Cape Lotus Orb save allies

display_text: Scenario: High-Tempo Pushing Lineups. Against heroes like Lycan, Chen, or Death Prophet that want to group and push early, your mine setup time is limited. Max Sticky Bomb first for reliable wave clear and teamfight damage. Use Blast Off! aggressively to stop channeled spells or break up the push. The +200 Blast Off! Damage talent at level 15 becomes very valuable. Place Minefield Sign directly on the creep wave or under your tower to amplify your mine damage during the defense.
search_text: Techies vs push lineups Lycan Chen Death Prophet max Sticky Bomb wave clear Blast Off stop push talent +200 Blast Off damage Minefield Sign on creep wave

display_text: At Level 10, Techies chooses between +20% Magic Resistance or -3s Proximity Mines Cooldown. +20% Magic Resistance is the defensive choice, greatly increasing survivability against magic-heavy lineups. -3s Proximity Mines Cooldown is the aggressive, farming, and area-control choice, allowing you to lay mines more frequently. Choose Magic Resistance if you are being focused by spells. Choose the cooldown reduction if you have space to mine and need faster farm or map control.
search_text: Techies level 10 talent left +20 magic resistance right -3s Proximity Mines cooldown defensive vs magic aggressive farming map control

display_text: At Level 15, Techies chooses between +200 Blast Off! Damage or +3 Mana Regen. +200 Blast Off! Damage turns your initiation into a much more lethal nuke, increasing its damage to 400/500/600/700. This is crucial for burst damage and teamfight impact. +3 Mana Regen is a sustain talent, allowing for more frequent spellcasting, especially Sticky Bomb spam and mine placement, without needing mana items. Pick the damage if you are landing good Blast Off! initiations. Pick mana regen if you are struggling with mana or focused on prolonged sieging/defending.
search_text: Techies level 15 talent left +200 Blast Off damage right +3 mana regen burst damage initiation vs spell sustain spam

display_text: At Level 20, Techies chooses between -15s Blast Off! Cooldown or +160 Sticky Bomb Damage. -15s Blast Off! Cooldown reduces the cooldown to 25/20/15/10 seconds, allowing for multiple initiations or escapes in a single fight. This is incredibly powerful for a playmaking Techies. +160 Sticky Bomb Damage increases its explosion damage to 255/330/405/480, making it a potent spammable nuke. Choose the cooldown reduction for high-frequency disruption. Choose the damage if you need safer, long-range poke and wave clear.
search_text: Techies level 20 talent left -15s Blast Off cooldown right +160 Sticky Bomb damage more initiations vs higher damage poke wave clear

display_text: At Level 25, Techies chooses between +252 Damage or -0.8s Proximity Mines Activation Delay. +252 Damage is a transformative talent, turning Techies into a formidable right-click physical damage dealer, especially potent with the Attack Range facet. -0.8s Proximity Mines Activation Delay reduces the trigger time from 1.0s to 0.2s, making mines almost instantaneous and far more reliable against mobile heroes. This is a massive offensive spike for mine-based play. Choose +252 Damage to become a carry. Choose the activation delay to become an unescapable area-denial monster.
search_text: Techies level 25 talent left +252 damage right -0.8s Proximity Mines activation delay physical carry vs instant mines area denial

display_text: Dark Willow is a ranged Intelligence hero with an attack range of 600. Her base stats are balanced: 20 Strength, 18 Agility, and 21 Intelligence, giving her decent starting damage. Her attack speed is average with a 1.5 attack rate, but she has a low base armor of 0.7, making her susceptible to physical harassment in lane.
search_text: Dark Willow base stats strength agility intelligence attack range 600 armor 0.7 attack rate 1.5

display_text: Facet 1: Throwing Shade causes Dark Willow to deal more damage to nearby heroes when in Shadow Realm. This encourages an aggressive, close-range burst playstyle. Facet 2: Thorny Thicket makes Bramble Maze create extra brambles, enhancing area control and defensive utility. Facet 3: Shattering Crown makes Cursed Crown accumulate damage dealt by Dark Willow and her allies; a percentage is dealt again as magic damage when the stun triggers, turning it into a powerful delayed AoE nuke. Choose Throwing Shade for burst, Thorny Thicket for control, and Shattering Crown for scaling teamfight damage.
search_text: Dark Willow facets Throwing Shade damage Shadow Realm nearby Thorny Thicket extra brambles Bramble Maze Shattering Crown Cursed Crown accumulate damage delayed AOE

display_text: In the laning phase, prioritize Shadow Realm (W) for safe, high-damage harassment due to its 600 bonus attack range and scaling magic damage up to 360. Use Cursed Crown (E) on the enemy support or carry to zone them, as the 1.5-2.4 second AoE stun after a 4-second delay creates kill threat with a partner. Bramble Maze (Q) is costly; use it sparingly to secure ranged creeps or trap enemies near trees. Her low base armor means positioning is key; avoid trading right-clicks. Focus on levels and securing farm with Shadow Realm.
search_text: Dark Willow laning phase skill build Shadow Realm W harass 600 range 360 damage Cursed Crown E stun Bramble Maze Q trap low armor

display_text: Level 6 is a major spike with Bedlam (R), providing 70 DPS per target for 5.5 seconds. Levels 12 and 18 unlock her level 15 and 25 talents, enhancing damage or utility. The level 20 talent choice between +1.5s Shadow Realm Duration or -7s Bramble Maze CD is critical. Level 25 with +2 Bedlam Attack Targets is a massive teamfight spike. Item spikes include Aghanim's Shard for Terrorize, Eul's Scepter for combo setup, and Blink Dagger for initiation.
search_text: Dark Willow power spikes level 6 Bedlam level 12 talent level 15 talent level 18 talent level 20 talent level 25 talent item timing Aghanim Shard Eul Blink Dagger

display_text: Dark Willow is hard countered by instant silences (e.g., Skywrath Mage, Death Prophet) which prevent her from casting Shadow Realm to disjoint or become untargetable. Break (e.g., Silver Edge) disables her Pixie Dust passive regen. High mobility heroes (e.g., Storm Spirit, Anti-Mage) can easily escape her Bramble Maze and Cursed Crown. Instant hex (Scythe of Vyse) or BKB-piercing stuns prevent her from using her defensive abilities.

display_text: Eul's Scepter of Divinity is absolutely essential. It provides mana regen, movement speed, and a setup for her Cursed Crown (E) stun, guaranteeing the delay hits. Blink Dagger is core for positioning to land multi-hero Bramble Mazes (Q) and Terrorize (R). Aghanim's Shard is a core upgrade, granting the Terrorize ability, a powerful BKB-piercing fear that also deals 500 damage with the level 25 talent.

display_text: In teamfights, Dark Willow's role is to initiate or counter-initiate with Blink + Bramble Maze (Q) or Terrorize (R). She then uses Cursed Crown (E) on clustered enemies and activates Shadow Realm (W) to become untargetable while dealing burst damage with her empowered attack. She stays on the fringe, using Bedlam (R) for sustained damage on key targets. Her job is to lock down areas, set up kills with her stuns and fears, and survive using her untargetable states.

Build items like Scythe of Vyse for instant hex or Rod of Atos for root setup. Your Terrorize (with Shard) is crucial as it pierces BKB. Use Cursed Crown before BKB activation, or use Bramble Maze to zone and control the fight area while their BKB is active.

Use Blink + Cursed Crown (E) on a key target, followed by Bramble Maze (Q) to trap multiple enemies. Follow up with Bedlam (R) on a high-priority target. This combo requires precise timing but can single-handedly win a fight. Ensure you have mana for the full combo (Eul's helps).

display_text: Pangolier is a Melee hero with a base attack time of 1.7 seconds, indicating a slower than average attack speed. His starting attributes are 19 Strength, 18 Agility, and 16 Intelligence, providing balanced but unexceptional base stats. His attack range and armor are not specified in this data block.

display_text: Pangolier has two functional Facets. The Double Jump Facet allows Shield Crash to be cast twice in quick succession, dealing more damage and granting a larger barrier per hero hit. This is excellent for high-mobility plays, burst damage, and survivability in chaotic fights. The Thunderbolt Facet increases Rolling Thunder's forward move speed and turn rate, but decreases its overall duration. This is better for initiating and controlling teamfights where you need to zip through enemies quickly and change direction. The third Facet, Dangerous Liaisons, has no gameplay effect.
search_text: Pangolier Facets Double Jump Shield Crash two jumps extra damage barrier Thunderbolt Rolling Thunder faster move turn speed shorter duration

display_text: In the laning phase, use Swashbuckle (20/17/14/11 sec CD) to harass from 850 range, dealing 35/65/95/125 damage per slash and applying on-hit effects. The dash also serves as an escape. Lucky Shot provides a 17% chance on any damage you deal to apply a 40/80/120/160 attack speed slow and 3/5/7/9 armor reduction for 3.5/4/4.5/5 seconds, making your trades potent. Shield Crash (15/12/9/6 sec CD) can be used defensively to dodge spells or secure last hits while gaining a barrier of 60/120/180/240 per hero hit.
search_text: Pangolier laning Swashbuckle harass dash escape Lucky Shot slow armor reduction Shield Crash barrier dodge last hit

display_text: Pangolier's first major power spike is at level 6 with Rolling Thunder, enabling ganks and teamfight initiation. Level 12 (Rolling Thunder level 2) and 18 (level 3) significantly increase the ultimate's duration and stun. Talent levels 10, 15, 20, and 25 provide substantial customization. Key item spikes include Diffusal Blade (for Swashbuckle mana burn), Maelstrom/Mjollnir (for Swashbuckle chain lightning), and Aghanim's Scepter (which empowers Shield Crash).

display_text: Pangolier is hard countered by heroes with Break (e.g., Silver Edge, Doom) which disables his Lucky Shot passive. Instant, reliable disables like Hex or Abyssal Blade can stop him during Swashbuckle or Rolling Thunder. Heavy magic damage burst can bypass his physical barrier from Shield Crash. Heroes with high mobility can evade his Rolling Thunder path.
search_text: Pangolier counters Break Lucky Shot disabled instant hex stun stop Swashbuckle Rolling Thunder magic burst damage

display_text: Core items for Pangolier are Diffusal Blade and Maelstrom. Diffusal Blade's mana burn and slow are applied by each slash of Swashbuckle, massively amplifying its damage and utility. Maelstrom (and later Mjollnir) causes chain lightning procs on each Swashbuckle slash, providing immense wave clear and magical damage in fights. These items synergize perfectly with his kit.
search_text: Pangolier core build Diffusal Blade Maelstrom Mjollnir Swashbuckle procs onhit effects mana burn chain lightning

display_text: In teamfights, Pangolier's primary role is as a mobile initiator and disruptor. Use Rolling Thunder to roll through the enemy team, knocking back and stunning multiple targets. Follow up with Swashbuckle to inflict heavy damage and apply Lucky Shot debuffs. Use Shield Crash to gain a large barrier and deal AoE damage. He excels at creating chaos, breaking formations, and focusing down key backline heroes with his mobility and damage.
search_text: Pangolier teamfight role initiator disruptor Rolling Thunder stun knockback Swashbuckle damage Lucky Shot debuff Shield Crash barrier

display_text: Swashbuckle: Pangolier dashes 850 units in a line, then slashes 3 times in the targeted direction. Each slash deals 35/65/95/125 damage to enemies in a 155 radius and slows them by 100% for 0.4 seconds. The dash itself travels 575/650/725/800 units at 2000 speed. The ability has a 20/17/14/11 second cooldown. Swashbuckle applies on-hit effects (like Diffusal Blade or Maelstrom) and can proc Lucky Shot. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Swashbuckle Q dash slash damage 35 65 95 125 slow 100% 0.4s onhit effects Lucky Shot cooldown 20 17 14 11

display_text: Shield Crash: Pangolier jumps in a short arc and slams down, dealing 50/100/150/200 damage in a 500 radius. For each hero hit, he gains a barrier that absorbs 60/120/180/240 damage from all sources, lasting 6 seconds. The cooldown is 15/12/9/6 seconds. If used during Rolling Thunder, he maintains forward momentum and can jump over terrain. With Aghanim's Scepter, the barrier strength is increased and the damage dealt to enemies is increased by 75%.

display_text: Lucky Shot: Passive. Any damage dealt by Pangolier's attacks or abilities has a 17% chance to reduce the target's armor by 3/5/7/9 and slow their attack speed by 40/80/120/160 for 3.5/4/4.5/5 seconds.

display_text: Roll Up: Pangolier curls into a ball, becoming debuff immune and gaining 80% magic resistance for 2.25 seconds. He can turn but not move. If attacked, he will roll away from the attacker, dealing Rolling Thunder damage and stun to enemies in his path. Can be cast during Rolling Thunder to stop temporarily. Has a 0.5 second cast time. Does NOT pierce BKB (he gains immunity, but the effect itself is not a piercing disable).
search_text: Roll Up curl ball debuff immune 80% magic resistance 2.25s if attacked roll away Rolling Thunder damage stun

display_text: Rolling Thunder: Ultimate. Pangolier curls into a ball, becoming debuff immune and gaining 80% magic resistance. He rolls forward at 550 speed for 10/11/12 seconds, with a turn rate of 120 (increased to 165 after ramping up). Enemies he collides with are knocked back and take 100% of his attack damage plus a bonus, and are stunned for 0.8/1.0/1.2 seconds upon landing. Colliding with terrain causes a bounce. Cooldown is 90/85/80 seconds. Dispels Pangolier on cast with a Basic Dispel. Does NOT pierce BKB (he is immune, but the stun does not affect BKB'd units).

display_text: Earth Spirit is a Melee hero with a base Strength of 22, Agility of 17, and Intelligence of 18. With a base attack rate of 1.7, he has a below-average attack speed. His primary attributes are fairly balanced for an initiator, but his melee status and slow attack animation require careful positioning in lane.

display_text: Earth Spirit has four Facets. 1. Resonance: Provides bonus Attack Damage when Magnetizing Stone Remnants, scaling the ultimate's damage. 2. Stepping Stone: Changes Stone Remnant to a flat 0 cooldown instead of Charges, and Remnants are not destroyed by Rolling Boulder. This enables high-mobility play. 3. Ready to Roll: Rolling Boulder can roll over allied heroes for additional bonuses, enhancing team-based initiation and rescue. 4. Reformation: This Facet currently has no functional effect. Choose Resonance for more damage and scaling, Stepping Stone for maximum mobility and combo potential, or Ready to Roll for team-save utility.

display_text: In lane, use Boulder Smash to secure ranged creep kills and harass with the 110/180/250/320 damage from enemy heroes or Remnants. Rolling Boulder at level 1 provides a 1000 speed escape or chase with a 60 damage nuke and 0.3s stun. Prioritize levels in Rolling Boulder for mobility and Geomagnetic Grip for the silence. Use Stone Remnant charges sparingly to harass with Boulder Smash or secure kills. Your low armor and melee range make you vulnerable to ranged harass; use pulls and camps to maintain farm.
