display_text: Ability: Invading Force. Passive. After an allied hero completes a teleport (Town Portal Scroll or Boots of Travel), they gain 11/14/17/20 bonus movement speed and 4/6/8/10% damage reduction for 5 seconds. If they teleported via Underlord's Fiend's Gate, these bonuses are doubled. Does NOT pierce BKB unless specified otherwise (pierce_immunity data not present).
search_text: Invading Force passive teleport movement speed 11 14 17 20 damage reduction 4 6 8 10 percent duration 5 seconds fiend's gate double

display_text: Ability: Fiend's Gate. Ultimate. Opens two portals for 20 seconds, one at Underlord's location and one at a target location at least 1500 units away. Allies can channel for 3.5 seconds to teleport between them. Double-cast to target a portal in front of your fountain. Cooldown is 110/105/100 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB unless specified otherwise (pierce_immunity data not present).
search_text: Fiend's Gate R ultimate dark portal channel 3.5 seconds duration 20 cooldown 110 105 100 double click fountain global

display_text: Scenario: Playing Against High Magic Damage. Prioritize building Pipe of Insight early. Use your high base strength and Atrophy Aura to mitigate physical damage, but Firestorm's long cast time and channeling requirement make you vulnerable to magic burst. Positioning is key; use Pit of Malice defensively to root divers.
search_text: Underlord vs high magic damage pipe of insight early build strength aura mitigate physical positioning pit of malice defensive root

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Focus on defensive itemization like Guardian Greaves and Crimson Guard. Use Firestorm to safely clear waves under tower. Fiend's Gate becomes a crucial escape and repositioning tool for your team. Avoid fights and use your global presence to split push and create space.
search_text: Underlord playing from behind defensive items guardian greaves crimson guard firestorm wave clear fiend's gate escape split push

display_text: Scenario: Pushing High Ground. Use Pit of Malice to control the enemy team's choke points on the ramp. Firestorm can clear enemy creep waves and damage buildings from a safe distance. The Invading Force passive helps allies who teleport in to join the siege. Consider the Abyssal Horde facet for extra minion summons to pressure buildings.
search_text: Underlord high ground push pit of malice choke point firestorm clear creep wave building damage invading force teleport bonus abyssal horde minions

display_text: Scenario: Versus Illusion Heroes. Firestorm is effective at clearing illusions due to its area damage over time. Atrophy Aura's damage reduction applies to all enemy units, including illusions, reducing their effectiveness. Pit of Malice can root the real hero if identified among the illusions.
search_text: Underlord vs illusions firestorm clear area damage atrophy aura reduces illusion damage pit of malice root real hero

display_text: Level 10 Talents. Left Talent: +5% to both the Movement Speed and Damage Reduction bonuses from Invading Force. This enhances your team's survivability and mobility after teleports. Right Talent: +15 seconds to the duration of Atrophy Aura's temporary bonus damage buff. This allows you to maintain your damage stacks for much longer, increasing your right-click threat. Choose the left talent for more team utility and defense. Choose the right talent for more personal damage and farming speed.
search_text: Underlord talent level 10 left invading force movement speed damage reduction +5% right atrophy aura duration +15 seconds bonus damage

display_text: Level 15 Talents. Left Talent: Pit of Malice applies a 20% slow to affected enemies. This adds a slow to the root, making it harder for enemies to escape after the root ends. Right Talent: Reduces Firestorm's cooldown by 4 seconds (from 16/15/14/13 to 12/11/10/9). This increases your wave clear and teamfight damage frequency. Choose the left talent for stronger single-target control and catch. Choose the right talent for more frequent area damage and farming.
search_text: Underlord talent level 15 left pit of malice slow 20% right firestorm cooldown -4 seconds

display_text: Level 20 Talents. Left Talent: Increases Firestorm's burn damage by 0.6% of the target's max health per second. This significantly boosts the spell's damage against high-health heroes. Right Talent: Increases Atrophy Aura's Attack Damage Reduction and Bonus Damage gain by 10%. This makes the aura reduce enemy damage by 16/24/32/40% and increases the bonus damage you gain per hero kill. Choose the left talent for more magic damage output against tanks. Choose the right talent for stronger damage reduction and physical damage potential.
search_text: Underlord talent level 20 left firestorm burn damage +0.6% max health right atrophy aura +10% damage reduction gain

display_text: Level 25 Talents. Left Talent: Increases Pit of Malice root duration by 0.4 seconds (from 1.5/1.6/1.7/1.8 to 1.9/2.0/2.1/2.2). This provides extremely strong crowd control, potentially locking down key targets. Right Talent: Fiend's Gate deals 125 magical damage per second to enemies within a 700 radius. This turns your portals into dangerous area denial zones and adds massive teamfight damage. Choose the left talent for unparalleled control in fights. Choose the right talent for overwhelming area damage and zoning.
search_text: Underlord talent level 25 left pit of malice root duration +0.4 seconds right fiend's gate 125 dps 700 radius

display_text: Grimstroke is a ranged intelligence hero with 21 strength, 18 agility, and 25 intelligence. He has a 1.7 base attack time and is classified as a ranged attacker. This gives him average-to-low base HP for a support but decent intelligence for spellcasting in the early game.
search_text: Grimstroke stats ranged intelligence hero strength agility base attack time BAT 1.7

display_text: Facet Choice: Inkstigate allows Ink Swell to be manually detonated early. This provides tactical control, allowing you to pop the stun and damage immediately for a gank or to interrupt a channeling spell. Facet Choice: Fine Art makes Stroke of Fate also launch a reflected stroke from the right hand. This enhances his long-range poke, wave clear, and damage output in teamfights.
search_text: Grimstroke facets Inkstigate Fine Art Ink Swell detonate early Stroke of Fate reflected stroke double damage

display_text: In lane, prioritize Stroke of Fate (Q) for long-range harassment and securing ranged creeps. Its damage increases per unit hit, so angle it through the creep wave and enemy heroes. Phantom's Embrace (W) is a powerful trading tool; use it when the enemy cannot quickly kill the phantom (3 hero attacks). Coordinate with your core to secure kills when the enemy is silenced. Your right-clicks are weak; focus on spell usage.
search_text: Grimstroke laning phase Stroke of Fate harass Phantom's Embrace silence kill secure support

display_text: Power Spikes: Level 6 with Soulbind allows for deadly combos, especially when paired with a hero with a strong single-target spell. Level 10 talent for more Phantom's Embrace DPS or reduced Ink Swell cooldown. Level 15 talent choice enhances your role. Level 18 with level 3 ultimate. Level 20 talent for massive Stroke of Fate damage or more Phantom's Embrace durability. Level 25 talent for huge Ink Swell area or extreme Stroke of Fate range and speed. Key item timings include Aether Lens for cast range and Force Staff/Glimmer Cape for saves.
search_text: Grimstroke power spikes level 6 Soulbind level 10 15 20 25 talents Aether Lens Force Staff Glimmer Cape

display_text: Grimstroke is hard countered by heroes with fast attack speed or summons that can quickly kill the Phantom's Embrace spirit (3 hero attacks per hit). Instant silences or hexes prevent him from casting his disables. Spell immunity (Black King Bar) blocks Stroke of Fate, Ink Swell, Phantom's Embrace, and Soulbind. Does NOT pierce BKB. Heroes with strong dispels can remove the Ink Swell buff from allies or the Ink Trail/Soulbind debuffs.
search_text: counters Grimstroke Phantom's Embrace fast attacks summons BKB spell immunity dispel silence hex

display_text: Core Build: Aether Lens is essential to increase the cast range of all his spells, particularly Stroke of Fate and Soulbind. Force Staff provides crucial mobility for positioning and saving allies affected by Ink Swell. Glimmer Cape offers magic resistance and invisibility to save allies or yourself during channeling or after committing.
search_text: Grimstroke core items Aether Lens cast range Force Staff mobility Glimmer Cape save magic resist

display_text: In teamfights, Grimstroke's primary role is backline control and amplification. Use Soulbind on a key enemy to double the effect of your team's single-target spells (e.g., Laguna Blade, Finger of Death). Cast Ink Swell on an initiator or mobile ally to create a moving area stun. Use Stroke of Fate for long-range poke and slow. Phantom's Embrace silences and threatens a key target. Your illusion from Dark Portrait provides vision and significant right-click pressure.
search_text: Grimstroke teamfight role backline control Soulbind double spells Ink Swell stun Phantom's Embrace silence Dark Portrait illusion

display_text: Ability: Stroke of Fate. Grimstroke paints a curved path of ink, dealing 100/160/220/280 damage and slowing enemies by 50%/60%/70%/80% for 1.75 seconds. The damage is increased by 10/20/30/40 per creep and 20/40/60/80 per hero hit along the path. The path starts at 120 radius and ends at 160 radius, traveling 1400 units at 2000 speed. Applies Ink Trail on hit. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Stroke of Fate Q Grimstroke nuke slow 100 160 220 280 damage 50 60 70 80 slow 1.75 sec bonus damage per creep hero Ink Trail

display_text: Ability: Phantom's Embrace. Summons a phantom that moves at 1150 speed to a target, latching for 5 seconds. While latched, it deals 10/20/30/40 damage per second and silences. Hero attacks against the phantom count as 3 attacks each, and it takes 6/6/9/9 attacks to destroy. If it survives the full duration, it deals 120/200/280/360 damage and refreshes its own cooldown. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Phantom's Embrace W Grimstroke silence damage over time 10 20 30 40 DPS latch 5 sec 120 200 280 360 burst kill refreshes cooldown 3 hero attacks per hit

display_text: Ability: Ink Swell. Enshrouds an allied unit, granting it 20% movement speed for 3 seconds. Enemies within 375 radius are damaged by tendrils for 6/9/12/15 damage every 0.2 seconds (30/45/60/75 DPS). After 3 seconds, or when manually detonated with the Inkstigate facet, it explodes, dealing up to 90/180/270/360 damage and stunning for up to 1.6/2.0/2.4/2.8 seconds based on how long tendrils were near any hero. Cooldown is 27/24/21/18 seconds.
search_text: Ink Swell E Grimstroke buff ally movespeed 20% damage over time 6 9 12 15 per tick 30 45 60 75 DPS radius 375 explode stun max 90 180 270 360 damage 1.6 2.0 2.4 2.8 sec stun cooldown 27 24 21 18

display_text: Ability: Dark Portrait. Creates an ink illusion of a target enemy hero that lasts 25 seconds. The illusion is debuff immune, has 95% magic resistance, 30 bonus movement speed, and deals 125% damage. It takes 275% damage. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Dark Portrait D Grimstroke illusion 25 sec debuff immune 95 magic resist 30 movespeed 125 damage 275 damage taken

display_text: Ability: Soulbind. Binds a target enemy hero to its nearest allied hero within 600 radius for 6/7/8 seconds, leashing them together. Any unit-targeted spell cast on either bound hero is also cast on the other. If the leash breaks (beyond 700 distance), the target is rebound to its nearest ally in range. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: Soulbind R Grimstroke ultimate bind leash 6 7 8 sec radius 600 break 700 double spells unit-targeted

display_text: Scenario: Against High Magic Damage Teams. Your low strength gain makes you vulnerable. Prioritize Glimmer Cape and a BKB-piercing disable like Nullifier is less relevant. Focus on positioning and using Soulbind to double your team's burst. Ink Swell can be used defensively to speed an ally out of danger.
search_text: Grimstroke vs high magic damage team Glimmer Cape positioning Soulbind burst defensive Ink Swell

display_text: Scenario: Playing From Behind. When your team is losing, Grimstroke's wave clear with Stroke of Fate is critical for defending high ground. Use Dark Portrait to scout and create space. Soulbind can turn a fight by doubling a key disable like a stun from a teammate. Build utility items like Force Staff and Glimmer Cape to save your cores.
search_text: Grimstroke from behind losing game high ground defense Stroke of Fate wave clear Dark Portrait scout Soulbind turnaround utility items

display_text: Scenario: With a Global/Powerful Single-Target Ally. Pairing with Zeus (Thundergod's Wrath), Lion (Finger of Death), or Lina (Laguna Blade) makes Soulbind devastating. Coordinate to cast your ultimate just before they use their nuke to double its effect. Your role is to enable these combos with good vision and positioning.
search_text: Grimstroke combo Soulbind Zeus Thundergod's Wrath Lion Finger of Death Lina Laguna Blade global single-target nuke double damage

display_text: Level 10 Talents. Left: +65 Phantom's Embrace DPS. Significantly increases the kill threat of Phantom's Embrace, making it harder for enemies to survive the latch. Right: -4s Ink Swell Cooldown. Increases your crowd control uptime, allowing more frequent saves, initiations, and area damage. Choose DPS for more pick-off potential, or Cooldown for more teamfight control.
search_text: Grimstroke level 10 talent +65 Phantom Embrace DPS -4s Ink Swell cooldown DPS vs control

display_text: Level 15 Talents. Left: +25.0% Soulbind Spell Damage. Amplifies the damage of spells copied through Soulbind, a powerful choice if your team has heavy nukers. Right: +12% Ink Swell Movement Speed. Makes the buffed ally much faster, improving initiation, escape, and the area covered by the swelling ink. Choose based on whether your team needs more damage or more mobility/control.
search_text: Grimstroke level 15 talent +25 Soulbind spell damage +12 Ink Swell movement speed damage amp vs mobility

display_text: Level 20 Talents. Left: +80% Stroke of Fate Damage. A massive increase to your primary damaging and wave-clearing ability, scaling you into a significant damage threat. Right: +3 Hits to Kill Phantom. Makes Phantom's Embrace dramatically more durable, requiring 12/12/15/15 total hero attacks to kill (instead of 9/9/12/12). Choose based on whether you need more raw damage or more reliable silence and control.
search_text: Grimstroke level 20 talent +80 Stroke of Fate damage +3 Hits to Kill Phantom durability nuke vs control

display_text: Level 25 Talents. Left: +150 Ink Swell Radius. Increases the area of effect from 375 to 525, making it much easier to catch multiple enemies in the damage and stun. Right: +70% Stroke of Fate Speed and Travel Range. Increases projectile speed from 2000 to ~3400 and cast range from 1400 to ~2380, turning it into an almost instant, very long-range nuke and slow. Choose between massive teamfight area control or extreme, safe poke and wave clear.
search_text: Grimstroke level 25 talent +150 Ink Swell radius +70 Stroke of Fate speed travel range area control vs range poke

display_text: Terrorblade is a melee agility carry. His base stats are: 18 Strength, 23 Agility, 19 Intelligence. He has a 1.5 base attack time, which is slower than the standard 1.7, making his early attack speed sluggish. His starting armor from Agility is above average, but his movement speed is average. His low Strength and Strength gain make him vulnerable to burst damage. He is primarily a late-game scaling carry.
search_text: terrorblade stats melee agility carry base attack time 1.5 strength 18 agility 23 intelligence 19 armor attack speed

display_text: Terrorblade has two Facets: Condemned and Soul Fragment. Condemned makes Sunder have no minimum health threshold for Sundered enemies, allowing you to instantly kill low-health foes or self-heal to full from any amount. This is ideal for aggressive, clutch plays. Soul Fragment makes Conjure Image illusions always spawn at full health and cost health instead of mana, which improves illusion uptime and damage output in extended fights and farming, but makes you more vulnerable to health removal. Choose Condemned for burst and pick-off potential; choose Soul Fragment for consistent farming and siege.
search_text: terrorblade facets condemned soul fragment sunder no minimum health conjure image full health health cost

display_text: In the laning phase, Terrorblade is weak. Focus on last-hitting with your decent base damage. Skill Conjure Image at level 1 or 2 to help secure last hits and harass. Use Reflection to slow enemies for escapes or setups. You are very fragile; avoid trading hits. Use illusions to pull creep aggro and manipulate the wave. Your primary goal is to survive and get as much farm as possible, aiming for a fast Power Treads and possibly a Magic Wand or Wraith Band. Metamorphosis at level 6 gives you a brief window to pressure the tower or fight.
search_text: terrorblade laning phase early game farm last hit conjure image illusion reflection slow safe lane weak survival

display_text: Terrorblade's key power spikes are: Level 6 with Metamorphosis for pushing and fighting. Level 12 with level 2 Metamorphosis and maxed Conjure Image for strong illusions. Level 18 with max Sunder and high stats. Item spikes are: Power Treads and Dragon Lance for early stats and range, Manta Style for dispel and stronger illusions, Skadi or Butterfly for tank and damage. Level 20 talents significantly boost illusion power. Level 25 Metamorphosis duration talent (30s) makes him a constant high-damage threat.
search_text: terrorblade power spikes level 6 metamorphosis level 12 level 18 sunder manta style skadi butterfly level 20 talent level 25 talent

display_text: Terrorblade is hard-countered by: Heroes with high physical burst damage (e.g., PA, TA) who can quickly kill his low-health real hero. Heroes with AoE illusion clear (e.g., Earthshaker, Leshrac, Sven) that destroy his Conjure Images and Reflection illusions. Heroes with Break (e.g., Shadow Demon, Viper) which disable Metamorphosis and Demon Zeal. Instant disables and hexes (e.g., Lion, Shadow Shaman) prevent him from using Sunder. High mobility heroes can kite his Metamorphosis form.
search_text: terrorblade counters hard counter illusion clear aoe earthshaker sven break shadow demon viper hex lion physical burst

display_text: Terrorblade's core build is non-negotiable. Power Treads are essential for stats and attack speed. Dragon Lance provides crucial range and stats, later upgraded to Hurricane Pike for escape. Manta Style is the ultimate core item, providing stats, a dispel, and creating two powerful illusions that benefit from all his abilities and Facets. Without Manta, his teamfight and split-push potential are severely limited.
search_text: terrorblade core items build power treads dragon lance hurricane pike manta style essential stats illusions

display_text: In a teamfight, Terrorblade's role is to use Reflection to slow and create illusions of enemy carries, reducing their damage. He then activates Demon Zeal for bonus attack speed and movement speed. He positions with his illusions (from Conjure Image and Manta) to focus-fire a key target. He uses Metamorphosis to become a ranged high-damage dealer. He saves Sunder for a clutch self-heal or to finish a low-health enemy core. His illusions are also used to siege towers before or after fights.
search_text: terrorblade teamfight role reflection slow demon zeal metamorphosis ranged sunder heal illusions siege

display_text: Reflection creates invulnerable, untargetable dark reflections of all enemy heroes in a 400 range area. Reflections last 5 seconds and attack their source, dealing 30%/45%/60%/75% of the enemy's attack damage. They also apply a slow of 15%/20%/25%/30% to movement and attack speed. The slow debuff lasts 5 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB. Cooldown is 23/20/17/14 seconds. Mana cost is 60/65/70/75.
search_text: reflection Q ability terrorblade invulnerable illusion slow 15 20 25 30 attack speed slow duration 5 cooldown 23 20 17 14 mana 60 65 70 75

display_text: Conjure Image creates an illusion of Terrorblade that lasts 34 seconds. The illusion deals 20%/25%/30%/35% of Terrorblade's damage and takes 250% incoming damage. The illusion is visually distinct to enemies. Cooldown is 16 seconds at all levels. Mana cost is 55/65/75/85. Does NOT pierce BKB. With the Soul Fragment Facet, these illusions spawn at full health and cost health instead of mana.
search_text: conjure image W ability terrorblade illusion duration 34 damage 20 25 30 35 takes 250 damage cooldown 16 mana 55 65 75 85 soul fragment facet

display_text: Metamorphosis transforms Terrorblade into a powerful demon with a ranged attack for 35/40/45/50 seconds. Grants 20/40/60/80 bonus damage and 300/350/400/450 bonus attack range (total range 450/500/550/600). Base attack time becomes 1.5. Any Terrorblade illusions within 1200 range are also transformed. Cooldown is 145/140/135/130 seconds. Transformation time is 0.35 seconds. Demon Zeal cannot be cast during Metamorphosis.
search_text: metamorphosis E ability terrorblade transformation ranged demon bonus damage 20 40 60 80 attack range 450 500 550 600 duration 35 40 45 50 cooldown 145 140 135 130 illusions

display_text: Demon Zeal costs 20% of Terrorblade's current HP to cast. Provides Terrorblade and nearby illusions within 1200 range with 60 bonus movement speed, 100 bonus attack speed, and 20 HP regen for 30 seconds. Reflection illusions gain 50% of these bonuses. Cannot be cast while in Metamorphosis, and activating Metamorphosis will remove Demon Zeal.
search_text: demon zeal D ability terrorblade hp cost 20 percent movement speed 60 attack speed 100 hp regen 20 duration 30 radius 1200 illusions reflection

display_text: Sunder severs the life from Terrorblade and a target hero, exchanging a percentage of both units' current health. The target's health after the swap cannot fall below 35%/30%/25% (Condemned Facet removes this for enemies). Some health must remain for Terrorblade. Debuff Immune enemies don't get their HP modified. Cooldown is 120/80/40 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB.
search_text: sunder R ultimate terrorblade swap health percentage minimum 35 30 25 condemned facet no minimum cooldown 120 80 40

display_text: Dark Unity is a passive ability. Illusions created by Terrorblade (including Conjure Image and Reflection illusions) within 1200 range of him gain 60% bonus attack damage. Illusions outside this radius gain 0% bonus damage.
search_text: dark unity passive terrorblade illusions bonus attack damage 60 percent radius 1200 conjure image reflection

display_text: When your team is ahead, Terrorblade should group with his team under Metamorphosis and take all outer towers. Use Conjure Image illusions to farm dangerous lanes while your team groups. Build a Skadi after Manta to provide slow and tankiness for high-ground siege. Use Sunder aggressively to secure kills on key targets, not just as a save.
search_text: terrorblade scenario ahead winning game group metamorphosis push towers skadi siege sunder aggressive

display_text: When playing from behind, Terrorblade must split-push with illusions to create space and farm. Avoid fights without Metamorphosis. Buy a Hurricane Pike for escape. Consider a Black King Bar if locked down. Use Sunder defensively to survive ganks. Your goal is to drag the game late where your scaling can overcome the deficit.
search_text: terrorblade scenario behind losing game split push illusions farm avoid fight hurricane pike bkb sunder defensive late game

display_text: Against heroes with high armor or damage block (e.g., Timbersaw, Tidehunter), Terrorblade struggles. Prioritize items that reduce armor (e.g., Desolator, Assault Cuirass) or deal magical damage through illusions (e.g., Manta Style diffusal blade combo is less effective). Focus on controlling other heroes with Reflection and Sunder.
search_text: terrorblade scenario vs high armor timbersaw tidehunter desolator assault cuirass armor reduction reflection

display_text: When the enemy has strong AoE illusion clear (e.g., Earthshaker, Magnus), do not group all your illusions together. Send single illusions to different lanes to split push. In fights, wait for the big AoE spells to be used before committing your illusions. Consider a Linken's Sphere or status resistance items to survive initial lockdown.
search_text: terrorblade scenario vs aoe illusion clear earthshaker magnus split push single illusions wait cooldowns linkens sphere

display_text: At level 10, choose between +1s Reflection Duration or -2s Conjure Image Cooldown. The Reflection talent increases the slow and illusion uptime, which is better for fighting and pick-offs. The Conjure Image talent improves farming speed and illusion uptime for pushing. Generally, take the Conjure Image talent to accelerate farm unless you are fighting constantly.
search_text: terrorblade level 10 talent reflection duration 1 second conjure image cooldown -2 seconds farm push fight

display_text: At level 15, choose between -10s Metamorphosis Cooldown or +10% Reflection Slow/Damage. The Metamorphosis cooldown talent (145->135 secs at level 1) is core, as it allows you to have Metamorphosis available for almost every fight and significantly improves farming speed. The Reflection talent is situational, only valuable if the enemy has right-click cores you need to heavily counter. Almost always take the Metamorphosis cooldown.
search_text: terrorblade level 15 talent metamorphosis cooldown -10 seconds reflection slow damage 10 percent

display_text: At level 20, choose between +10 All Attributes or +10s Conjure Image Duration. The +10 All Attributes provides 10 damage, 10 attack speed, 190 HP, 120 mana, and 1.4 armor, making Terrorblade significantly tankier. The +10s Conjure Image Duration extends illusion lifetime from 34 to 44 seconds, improving split-push and vision. The attribute talent is almost always better for survivability and overall damage. Take the duration talent only if you are completely free-farming and need to rat.
search_text: terrorblade level 20 talent 10 all attributes stats conjure image duration 10 seconds split push

display_text: At level 25, choose between -30s Sunder Cooldown or +30s Metamorphosis Duration. The Sunder cooldown talent reduces it to as low as 10 seconds (with level 3 and talent), allowing for multiple uses in a fight and incredible survivability. The Metamorphosis duration talent increases it to up to 80 seconds (50+30), making you a permanent ranged siege monster. The Sunder talent is generally better for high-stakes, chaotic fights. The Metamorphosis talent is better for uninterrupted pushing and when you are not being focused.
search_text: terrorblade level 25 talent sunder cooldown -30 seconds metamorphosis duration 30 seconds

display_text: Skywrath Mage is a Ranged intelligence hero with a base attack time of 1.7 seconds. He has a very low Strength gain (base 21) and Agility gain (base 13), making him exceptionally fragile with low health, armor, and physical damage output. His starting Intelligence of 25 is high, emphasizing his role as a spellcaster. Compared to average heroes, his base stats highlight his extreme glass cannon nature, reliant on spells and positioning.
search_text: Skywrath Mage stats ranged intelligence attack rate 1.7 base strength 21 base agility 13 base intelligence 25 fragile low armor low health

display_text: Skywrath Mage has two facets. Shield of the Scion grants him a magic damage barrier when he deals damage to heroes, making him more resilient against retaliatory magic nukes after initiating. Staff of the Scion reduces his Cooldowns when he deals spell damage to enemy heroes, allowing for faster ability cycling and more frequent combos. Choose Shield for survivability in burst-heavy games. Choose Staff for maximum spell uptime and sustained damage output.
search_text: Skywrath Mage facets Shield of the Scion magic damage barrier on hero damage Staff of the Scion reduce cooldowns on spell damage facet choice

display_text: In the laning phase, Skywrath Mage dominates with his high base intelligence and low cooldown Arcane Bolt (Q). Use Arcane Bolt to harass the enemy laner, as it deals damage based on your intelligence and cannot be disjointed. Secure last hits with his attack animation and Arcane Bolt if needed. At level 2, Concussive Shot (W) provides additional burst and a slow. His fragility means you must position carefully behind creeps or your lane partner. Secure runes with Concussive Shot if needed. Primary goal is to win the lane through attrition and set up kills with your slow and high magic damage.
search_text: Skywrath Mage laning phase harass Arcane Bolt last hits Concussive Shot slow fragile positioning

display_text: Skywrath Mage's power spikes are tied to his ultimate and talent levels. Level 6 is a massive spike with Mystic Flare (R), allowing solo kills on low-health or silenced targets. Level 12 with maxed Arcane Bolt and Ancient Seal significantly increases burst. Level 15 talent (+20% Concussive Shot Slow) improves control. Level 20 talent (+10% Ancient Seal Increased Magic Damage) dramatically amplifies combo damage. Level 25 talent (+400 Mystic Flare Damage) is a huge finishing power boost. Essential item spikes are Rod of Atos (for setup) and Aghanim's Scepter (for teamfight area control).
search_text: Skywrath Mage power spikes level 6 Mystic Flare level 12 talents 15 20 25 Atos Aghanim Scepter

display_text: Skywrath Mage is hard-countered by heroes and items that counter fragile, spell-reliant heroes. Anti-Mage and Nyx Assassin can reflect or absorb his primary damage (Arcane Bolt). Heroes with high magic resistance (Huskar, Visage) or status resistance (Spirit Breaker) reduce his effectiveness. Instant silences, hexes, or blinks (Storm Spirit, Puck) can jump on him before he can cast. Items like BKB (which blocks all his spells except the non-damaging components), Pipe of Insight, and Glimmer Cape massively reduce his damage output. His low health pool makes him vulnerable to any physical burst.
search_text: Skywrath Mage counters Anti-Mage Nyx Assassin magic resistance BKB Pipe Glimmer Cape silence hex burst fragile

display_text: Skywrath Mage's core build consists of mana and setup items. Rod of Atos is absolutely essential to root enemies in place for his slow-moving Arcane Bolts and to guarantee the full duration of Mystic Flare. Aghanim's Scepter is a core extension, increasing Mystic Flare's radius and making it a massive area denial and teamfight tool. Boots of Travel are often prioritized for mobility and map presence to use his global or long-range abilities.
search_text: Skywrath Mage core items Rod of Atos root Aghanim Scepter Mystic Flare radius Boots of Travel mobility

display_text: In teamfights, Skywrath Mage's role is backline artillery and single-target deletion. He initiates or follows up by silencing a key enemy spellcaster or carry with Ancient Seal (E), amplifying all magic damage on them. He then unleashes his full combo: Mystic Flare (R) on the silenced target, followed by spamming Arcane Bolt (Q) and Concussive Shot (W). He must stay at maximum range due to his extreme fragility. His goal is to eliminate one hero instantly at the fight's start, creating a numbers advantage.
search_text: Skywrath Mage teamfight role backline artillery single target deletion Ancient Seal silence Mystic Flare combo fragile positioning

Upon impact, it deals 120/180/240/300 damage and applies a 40% movement slow in a 250 radius for 4 seconds. It provides 300 vision around the projectile and reveals the impact area for 3.34 seconds. If no enemy heroes are in range, it targets the nearest creep. Damages creeps around the impact area.

Prioritize items that reduce magic resistance, like Veil of Discord or a late-game Shiva's Guard. Coordinate with teammates who have natural magic resistance reduction (Shadow Demon, Pugna). Use Concussive Shot to safely scout and slow enemies. Use Ancient Seal to silence key initiators in fights. Build cheap survivability items like Force Staff, Glimmer Cape, or a Bracer. display_text: Hoodwink is an Agility Ranged hero with average stats. She has a high starting Agility of 25, giving her decent armor and attack speed early. Her Strength base of 17 is low, making her fragile against nukes and physical damage. display_text: Hoodwink has three Facets. The 'Go Nuts' facet makes Scurry provide bonus attack and cast range, ideal for a core build focusing on safe right-click damage and longer spell casts. The 'Treebounce Trickshot' facet allows Acorn Shot to bounce once on trees, significantly improving its farming and wave clear potential by interacting with the environment. The 'Hipshot' facet drastically changes her ultimate, Sharpshooter, making it a low-cooldown, quick-draw skill shot with reduced damage and debuff duration, trading burst for utility and spam.

display_text: In lane, use Acorn Shot (45/90/135/180 bonus damage, 80% attack damage) for harassment and last-hitting, especially near enemy creeps to bounce. Bushwhack (90/180/270/360 damage over 1.5/1.7/1.9/2.1 seconds, stun) is a powerful setup when enemies are near trees; use it to secure kills with a partner. Hoodwink's low Strength means positioning is key; use Scurry (15 flat movement speed, 20%/25%/30%/35% bonus, tree-walking) defensively to escape ganks. Her passive, Mistwoods Wayfarer, provides a 14%/21%/28%/35% chance to redirect attacks to a tree, mitigating harass but is unreliable.

display_text: Oracle has two Facets. Facet of Damage (oracle_facet_dmg) enhances his offensive capabilities, making his skills deal more damage or apply stronger offensive debuffs. Facet of Healing (oracle_facet_heal) amplifies his supportive role, increasing the potency of his healing spells and possibly reducing cooldowns on them. Choose Damage to play a more core, nuking role, especially if your team lacks magic burst. Choose Healing to be a dedicated, high-output save support.

display_text: In lane, Oracle uses Purifying Flames to harass enemy heroes, dealing 90/180/270/360 magic damage. Follow up with Fortune's End to root them and prevent retaliation, or use Fate's Edict to disarm them and prevent counter-harass. Use Purifying Flames on your lane partner to heal them for 150/250/350/450 total health over 10 seconds, but be mindful of its 2.5-second cooldown. His poor attack speed means last-hitting is difficult; focus on spell usage.

search_text: Winter Wyvern situation with physical carry Cold Embrace save Phantom Assassin Troll Warlord Winter's Curse coordination

display_text: Scenario: Enemy has Dispels (Eul's, Lotus, Manta). The slow and damage from Arctic Burn can be dispelled. In this scenario, use Arctic Burn primarily for the mobility and attack range to position for Splinter Blast or Winter's Curse. Do not rely on its damage against heroes who can easily dispel it. The slow from Splinter Blast is also dispellable.
search_text: Winter Wyvern situation vs dispels Arctic Burn dispellable Eul's Scepter Lotus Orb Manta Style use for mobility

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 10: Left choice is +20 HP/s Cold Embrace Heal. Right choice is +35 Damage. The heal talent significantly improves your save potential, adding 80 total healing over the 4-second duration. The damage talent helps with last hitting and harass. Choose the heal talent if you are the primary save support or against heavy physical damage. Choose the damage talent if you are having a good lane and want to transition into a more core/pushing role.
search_text: Winter Wyvern talent level 10 left +20 HP/s Cold Embrace Heal right +35 Damage save vs harass

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 15: Left choice is +3s Arctic Burn Debuff Duration. Right choice is +250 Splinter Blast Shatter Radius. The Arctic Burn talent increases the slow and damage duration from 5s to 8s, greatly enhancing your chase and poke. The Splinter Blast talent increases the shatter radius from 500 to 750, making it much easier to hit multiple heroes. Choose Arctic Burn talent for more single-target control and damage. Choose Splinter Blast talent for better teamfight impact and wave clear.
search_text: Winter Wyvern talent level 15 left +3s Arctic Burn duration right +250 Splinter Blast radius 750 control vs teamfight

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 20: Left choice is +17% Arctic Burn Slow. Right choice is +100 Splinter Blast Damage. The Arctic Burn talent increases the slow from 40% to 57%, making it extremely difficult for enemies to escape. The Splinter Blast talent increases its damage from 320 to 420, adding significant burst. Choose the slow talent if your team lacks control and you need to kite enemy cores. Choose the damage talent if you need burst to kill key targets like supports or to clear waves faster.
search_text: Winter Wyvern talent level 20 left +17% Arctic Burn slow 57% right +100 Splinter Blast damage 420 burst control vs damage

display_text: TALENT LEVEL 25: Left choice is +60 Winter's Curse Attack Speed. Right choice is Splinter Blast 1s Stun. The Winter's Curse talent increases the attack speed bonus on affected enemies from 65 to 125, dramatically increasing the speed at which the cursed target dies. The Splinter Blast talent adds a 1-second stun to the shatter effect, providing a reliable interrupt and setup. Choose the attack speed talent to guarantee kills with your ultimate, especially against tanky heroes. Choose the stun talent for extra teamfight control and to interrupt channeling spells like Black Hole or TP scrolls.

display_text: Abaddon is a Melee Strength hero with starting attributes of 21 Strength, 22 Agility, and 18 Intelligence. His base attack rate of 1.5 is standard for a melee core. With high base Agility, his starting armor is above average. His primary attribute being Strength provides a solid health pool. He has a standard turn rate.
search_text: Abaddon stats melee strength hero attack type 21 str 22 agi 18 int base attack time 1.5

display_text: Death Dude is a base facet. Mephitic Shroud changes Aphotic Shield: it absorbs more damage and reflects received damage instantly instead of exploding at the end, encouraging a tankier, counter-attack playstyle. Malignant Mist modifies Mist Coil to deal attack damage and apply on-hit effects, making it a tool for Abaddon to play as a more active right-click carry or ganker.
search_text: Abaddon facets Death Dude Mephitic Shroud Aphotic Shield reflect damage Malignant Mist Mist Coil attack damage on-hit effects

display_text: In lane, Abaddon's 1.5 base attack time and decent starting stats allow for solid harassment. Use Mist Coil (95/160/225/290 damage/heal, 40 self-damage) to secure ranged creeps, harass enemies, or save your lane partner. Prioritize Aphotic Shield to purge debuffs from yourself or your core and block 120/150/180/210 damage. Skill Curse of Avernus at level 2 or 4 to help with chasing and trading. Avoid using too many Mist Coils on enemies early due to the 40 self-damage cost.
search_text: Abaddon laning phase Mist Coil 95 160 225 290 damage heal 40 self damage Aphotic Shield purge 120 150 180 210 barrier Curse of Avernus slow attack speed

display_text: Level 6 is a major spike with Borrowed Time, making you nearly unkillable for 4/5/6 seconds and enabling aggressive dives. Level 12 with maxed Aphotic Shield (6s cooldown) allows for constant dispels and damage absorption. Level 15 talents (+8 Aphotic Shield Regen or +40 Curse DPS) enhance your chosen playstyle. Level 18 with max Borrowed Time. Level 20 talent (+35 Mist Coil Damage/Heal) significantly boosts your burst and sustain. Level 25 talents (+65 Curse Attack Speed or 350 AoE Mist Coil) are game-changing. Key item spikes are Solar Crest, Phase Boots, and Blademail for an aggressive core, or Holy Locket for a dedicated healer.
search_text: Abaddon power spikes level 6 Borrowed Time 4 5 6 seconds level 12 Aphotic Shield 6s cooldown level 15 talents regen dps level 20 talent 35 Mist Coil level 25 talents attack speed AoE

display_text: Break disables the passive component of Borrowed Time, preventing its auto-activation when health falls below 400. Heroes with break (e.g., Viper, Doom) are hard counters. High burst damage that can kill you from above the 400 HP threshold before Borrowed Time can activate. Anti-healing effects like Ancient Apparition's Ice Blast, as your ultimate converts damage to healing. Disarms and silences prevent you from using your abilities (though Borrowed Time can still be manually activated while disabled, not silenced).
search_text: Abaddon counters break disables Borrowed Time passive 400 HP threshold burst damage anti-heal Ancient Apparition Ice Blast disarm silence

display_text: Core item 1: Solar Crest. Provides stats, armor, and the active which synergizes perfectly with your role as a frontliner and buffer for your carry. Core item 2: Blade Mail. As a hero who wants to absorb damage, Blade Mail punishes enemies for attacking you, especially during Borrowed Time. Core item 3: Heart of Tarrasque. Maximizes your tankiness and HP regeneration, making you incredibly difficult to bring down.
search_text: Abaddon core build Solar Crest Blade Mail Heart of Tarrasque tank items

display_text: In teamfights, Abaddon is a disruptive frontliner and support. Your primary job is to absorb key spells and damage with your high HP and Borrowed Time. Use Aphotic Shield (12/10/8/6s cooldown) to dispel crucial debuffs (like stuns or silences) from your cores and provide a 120/150/180/210 damage barrier. Use Mist Coil to burst heal allies or finish off low enemies. Apply Curse of Avernus to key targets to slow them and boost your team's attack speed against them. Your presence forces the enemy to either ignore you or waste resources trying to kill you through your ultimate.
search_text: Abaddon teamfight role frontliner support dispel Aphotic Shield 120 150 180 210 barrier Mist Coil heal Borrowed Time tank Curse of Avernus slow attack speed

display_text: Mist Coil (Q): Abaddon releases a coil that can damage an enemy unit for 95/160/225/290 magic damage or heal a friendly unit for the same amount. Costs 40 of Abaddon's health when cast. Missile speed is 1300. Cannot be used to kill Abaddon himself. Does NOT pierce BKB unless specified elsewhere. The Malignant Mist facet changes this ability to also deal attack damage and apply on-hit effects.
search_text: Mist Coil Q ability damage 95 160 225 290 heal 95 160 225 290 self damage 40 health cost missile speed 1300 Malignant Mist facet attack damage on-hit

display_text: Aphotic Shield (W): Places a shield on a target ally, applying a Strong Dispel. The shield absorbs 120/150/180/210 damage for 12 seconds. When the shield expires or is broken, it explodes, dealing 120/150/180/210 magic damage in a 675 radius. Cooldown is 12/10/8/6 seconds. Does NOT pierce BKB unless specified elsewhere. The Mephitic Shroud facet changes this ability to absorb more damage and reflect damage received instead of exploding.
search_text: Aphotic Shield W ability dispel strong dispel absorbs 120 150 180 210 damage duration 12 seconds explosion 120 150 180 210 damage radius 675 cooldown 12 10 8 6 Mephitic Shroud facet reflect

display_text: Curse of Avernus (E): Passive. Abaddon's attacks apply a chilling curse for 2 seconds. Cursed enemies are slowed by 10/15/20/25% and take 15/25/35/45 damage per second. All attacks against the cursed target (from any source) gain 10/20/30/40 attack speed. Damage over time to buildings is reduced by 100%. The slow duration is 2 seconds.
search_text: Curse of Avernus E passive slow 10 15 20 25 percent dps 15 25 35 45 attack speed bonus 10 20 30 40 duration 2 seconds building damage reduced

display_text: Withering Mist (F): Passive. Damaging enemies applies the Withering Mist debuff for 5.0 seconds. Affected enemies have their Health Restoration reduced by 35% if they are below 40.0% HP. This is a built-in passive and not a facet.
search_text: Withering Mist F passive health restoration reduction 35 percent threshold 40 percent HP duration 5 seconds

display_text: Borrowed Time (R): Ultimate. When activated, applies a Strong Dispel on Abaddon. For 4/5/6 seconds, all damage dealt to Abaddon heals him instead of harming him. Cooldown is 90/85/80 seconds. If off cooldown, it automatically activates when Abaddon's health falls below 400. Can be manually activated while disabled (but not silenced). Passive activation is prevented by Break.
search_text: Borrowed Time R ultimate strong dispel duration 4 5 6 seconds damage to heal cooldown 90 85 80 auto activate 400 HP threshold break disables passive manual cast while disabled

display_text: Scenario: Against heavy magic burst (e.g., Zeus, Lina). Prioritize maxing Aphotic Shield early to absorb and reflect magic nukes. Build items like Pipe of Insight and Hood of Defiance. Your role is to soak key spells for your team and dispel silences.
search_text: Abaddon scenario vs magic burst nuke Aphotic Shield Pipe of Insight Hood of Defiance dispel silence

display_text: Scenario: Playing from behind as a support. Focus on saving your cores. Max Mist Coil and Aphotic Shield. Build cheap utility items like Glimmer Cape, Force Staff, and Holy Locket to amplify your healing and save potential. Use Borrowed Time to bait enemies into overcommitting on you.
search_text: Abaddon scenario playing from behind support save cores Mist Coil Aphotic Shield Glimmer Cape Force Staff Holy Locket bait Borrowed Time

display_text: Scenario: When your team lacks physical damage and you have a good start. Consider the Malignant Mist facet and build carry items like Armlet of Mordiggian, Desolator, and Assault Cuirass. Use Mist Coil to apply on-hit effects from range.
search_text: Abaddon scenario carry physical damage Malignant Mist facet Armlet Desolator Assault Cuirass Mist Coil on-hit

display_text: Scenario: Against heroes with Break (Viper, Doom). You must play much more cautiously as your Borrowed Time will not auto-activate. Consider buying a Linken's Sphere or Lotus Orb to block the Break application. Communicate with your team to focus the Break hero first.
search_text: Abaddon scenario vs break Viper Doom Borrowed Time no auto activate Linken's Sphere Lotus Orb

display_text: Talent Level 10: Choose between +10% Withering Mist Health Restoration Reduction (left) or Aphotic Shield provides +8 HP Regen (right). The left talent enhances your anti-sustain against low-HP enemies, useful against healers like Oracle or Huskar. The right talent provides significant personal sustain, making you tankier and less reliant on Mist Coil for self-heal. Choose left if the enemy has strong healing, otherwise right is generally more reliable.

display_text: Talent Level 15: Choose between +40 Curse of Avernus DPS (left) or +35 Mist Coil Heal/Damage (right). The left talent increases your damage output and farming speed, especially if you are building as a right-clicker or have the Malignant Mist facet. The right talent significantly boosts your burst heal and nuke, better for a support/healer build. Pick based on your role in the game.

display_text: Talent Level 20: Choose between +80 Aphotic Shield Barrier Amount (left) or +65 Curse of Avernus Attack Speed Bonus (right). The left talent makes your dispel and barrier much stronger, increasing survivability for you and your allies. The right talent dramatically boosts your and your team's right-click damage against cursed targets, a huge DPS increase for physical lineups. Choose left if you need more save, right if you need more damage.

display_text: Talent Level 25: Choose between 100 Borrowed Time Immolation DPS in a 500 AoE (left) or 350 AoE Mist Coil (right). The left talent turns Borrowed Time into a significant AoE damage aura, excellent for tanking in the middle of fights. The right talent allows Mist Coil to hit all enemies and allies in a huge 350 radius, providing massive teamfight heal and damage. Left is better for a pure tank/disruptor, right is incredible for a support/healer or to apply on-hit effects in an AoE with the Malignant Mist facet.

display_text: Elder Titan is a Melee Strength hero with a base Attack Rate of 1.7 seconds. His base stats are 26 Strength, 16 Agility, and 23 Intelligence. This gives him a good health pool for a Strength hero at level 1. As a Melee hero, he has a 150 attack range and a standard 315 movement speed.
search_text: Elder Titan stats melee attack range 150 movement speed 315 strength 26 agility 16 intelligence 23 attack rate 1.7

display_text: Elder Titan has two Facets. The Deconstruction Facet changes Natural Order to drain Magic Resistance and Armor over time, applying its full effect gradually rather than instantly. The other Facet, which currently has no name or description in the data, provides +100 Attack Speed. Deconstruction is better for drawn-out fights and comboing with his area-of-effect spells, while the Attack Speed Facet significantly boosts his right-click damage potential, enabling a more carry-oriented playstyle.
search_text: Elder Titan facets Deconstruction Natural Order drains armor magic resistance over time Attack Speed facet 100 attack speed right click carry

display_text: In the laning phase, focus on using Astral Spirit to secure last hits from a distance and harass enemies. At level 1, the spirit deals 50 pass-through damage and steals 1.5 movement speed from heroes and 1.5% from creeps per unit hit. Landing it on multiple units gives you significant bonus damage and armor for 10 seconds, allowing for favorable trades. Use Echo Stomp (1.7s channel, 0.2s initial stun) carefully for setup or disengage; it deals 60 physical damage around you and 60 magic damage around the Spirit. Prioritize levels in Astral Spirit for damage and Natural Order for armor reduction.
search_text: Elder Titan laning phase guide first 10 minutes Astral Spirit last hit harass 50 damage movement speed steal Echo Stomp 1.7 channel 0.2 stun 60 damage

display_text: Key power spikes are at level 6 with Earth Splitter for long-range initiation and percent-based damage. Level 12 with maxed Astral Spirit (80 hero damage, 6 armor, 7% move speed per hero) is a major timing for fights. Level 18 provides maxed ultimate. Talent spikes at 15 (+75 Echo Stomp Damage), 20 (+25 Astral Spirit Hero Attack), and 25 (+100% Cleave) are significant. A core item like Blink Dagger is a major spike for initiating with Echo Stomp into Earth Splitter. Aghanim's Scepter provides magic immunity per hero hit with Astral Spirit, creating a huge survivability spike in teamfights.
search_text: Elder Titan power spikes level 6 Earth Splitter level 12 max Astral Spirit talent 15 20 25 Blink Dagger Aghanim Scepter magic immunity

display_text: Elder Titan is countered by heroes with silence or interrupt abilities that can cancel his 1.7-second Echo Stomp channel, such as Silencer or Skywrath Mage. High mobility heroes can dodge his Astral Spirit and Earth Splitter. Illusion-based heroes dilute the effectiveness of his single-target percent-based damage from Earth Splitter. Heroes that can quickly burst him down before he can cast his spells or before his Astral Spirit returns are also strong counters.
search_text: Elder Titan counters hard counters Silencer Skywrath Mage interrupt Echo Stomp channel mobility dodge Astral Spirit Earth Splitter illusions Anti-Mage

display_text: Elder Titan's absolutely essential core items are Blink Dagger and Aghanim's Scepter. Blink Dagger is non-negotiable for initiating with Echo Stomp. Aghanim's Scepter is critical as it provides 1.2 seconds of magic immunity per hero hit by Astral Spirit, making him incredibly durable in teamfights and allowing him to follow up his initiation. A third core item is often Shiva's Guard or Assault Cuirass to further leverage his aura-based Natural Order and provide team utility.
search_text: Elder Titan core build essential items Blink Dagger Aghanim Scepter Shiva Guard Assault Cuirass magic immunity per hero Astral Spirit

display_text: In teamfights, Elder Titan's primary role is as an initiator and disabler. He should use Blink Dagger to position and channel Echo Stomp, putting multiple enemies to sleep for 2.0 to 4.4 seconds. He should use Astral Spirit before engaging to gain bonus stats and apply his Natural Order aura to reduce enemy base armor and magic resistance. After initiation, he follows up with Earth Splitter to deal massive percent-based damage (34%/42%/50% max HP) and slow enemies by 30%/40%/50%. He must stay alive to keep the Natural Order aura active on key targets.
search_text: Elder Titan teamfight role job initiator disabler Blink Echo Stomp sleep 4.4 seconds Astral Spirit Natural Order armor magic resistance reduction Earth Splitter percent damage slow

display_text: ECHO STOMPS: Elder Titan channels for 1.7 seconds, then he and his Astral Spirit both stomp, damaging and knocking unconscious nearby enemies for 2.0/2.8/3.6/4.4 seconds. The Titan stomp deals 60/100/140/180 Physical damage. The Spirit stomp deals 60/100/140/180 Magical damage. Units hit by both take both instances of damage. Waking a unit by dealing more than 100/150/200/250 damage to it ends the sleep early. Cooldown: 14/13/12/11 seconds.
search_text: Elder Titan Echo Stomp Q ability channel 1.7 seconds sleep 2.8 3.6 4.4 physical damage 60 100 140 180 magical damage wake damage limit 100 150 200 250 cooldown 14 13 12 11

display_text: ASTRAL SPIRIT: Sends a controllable spirit 1200 units away, dealing 50 damage to units it passes through. For each unit hit, Elder Titan gains bonus damage, armor, and movement speed for 10 seconds. Versus creeps: +3/7/11/15 damage, +0.5 armor, +1.5% move speed. Versus heroes: +17/38/59/80 damage, +1.5/3/4.5/6 armor, +4/5/6/7% move speed. The spirit lasts 10 seconds, moves at 900 speed, and can cast Echo Stomp and Natural Order. Aghanim's Scepter grants 1.2 seconds of magic immunity per hero hit. Return Spirit ability brings it back early.
search_text: Elder Titan Astral Spirit W ability spirit duration 10 seconds range 1200 speed 900 damage 50 pass through hero bonus 80 damage 6 armor 7% move speed creep bonus 15 damage 0.5 armor 1.5% move speed scepter magic immunity per hero

display_text: NATURAL ORDER: Reduces the base armor and base magic resistance of all enemy units within a 350 radius by 40%/60%/80%/100%. When the Astral Spirit is active, the magic resistance reduction is centered on the spirit, while armor reduction is centered on Elder Titan. When the spirit is not present, both reductions are centered on Elder Titan. The Deconstruction Facet changes this to drain resistance over time instead of instantly.
search_text: Elder Titan Natural Order E ability aura passive reduces base armor magic resistance 40 60 80 100 percent radius 350 spirit hero split Deconstruction drains over time

display_text: EARTH SPLITTER: After a 3-second delay, creates a fissure that damages enemies based on 34%/42%/50% of their maximum health. Half the damage is Magical, half is Physical. Also slows affected enemies by 30%/40%/50% for 3/4/5 seconds (4/5/6 seconds with Aghanim's Shard). The crack travels 2400 units at 1100 speed and provides vision along its path for 4 seconds. Cooldown is 100 seconds.
search_text: Elder Titan Earth Splitter R ultimate percent max HP damage 34 42 50 half magical half physical slow 30 40 50 percent duration 3 4 5 seconds cooldown 100 shard slow duration 4 5 6 seconds

display_text: Scenario: Playing Against High-Armor/High-Magic-Resistance Teams. Elder Titan excels here. His Natural Order aura completely negates 100% of base armor and magic resistance at max level, making enemies significantly more vulnerable to all forms of damage from your team. Prioritize staying alive in fights to keep the aura active. Coordinate with allies who deal physical or magical burst damage to capitalize on the exposed enemies.
search_text: Elder Titan situational high armor magic resistance teams Natural Order 100% reduction base armor base magic resistance aura stay alive coordinate burst damage

display_text: Scenario: Playing from Behind. Focus on defensive utility. Use Astral Spirit for safe scouting and vision. Your main contribution will be landing a multi-hero Echo Stomp initiation with Blink Dagger to create pick-off opportunities. Earth Splitter remains valuable for its percent-based damage even when you are under-leveled. Build survivability items like Shiva's Guard or Pipe of Insight to enable you to cast your spells in fights.
search_text: Elder Titan playing from behind defensive utility Astral Spirit scouting vision Blink Echo Stomp initiation percent damage Earth Splitter survivability items Shiva Pipe

display_text: Scenario: Initiating a Teamfight. The standard combo is: 1) Cast Astral Spirit through multiple heroes. 2) Blink into the enemy team. 3) Channel Echo Stomp. 4) As the spirit returns, it will also stomp, potentially hitting enemies twice. 5) Cast Earth Splitter on the sleeping or retreating enemies. With Aghanim's Scepter, the magic immunity from the spirit allows you to channel Echo Stomp safely even if Blink is disabled.
search_text: Elder Titan initiating teamfight combo Astral Spirit Blink Echo Stomp channel Earth Splitter Aghanim Scepter magic immunity combo sequence

display_text: Scenario: Versus Illusion or Summon Heroes. Your Astral Spirit damage bonus scales with the number of units hit, giving you massive temporary damage against large groups. However, Earth Splitter's percent damage is less effective as it's split among illusions. Focus on using your area-of-effect disables (Echo Stomp) to control the real hero and key illusions. Natural Order significantly reduces the armor of all units, making them easier for your team to clear.
search_text: Elder Titan versus illusions summons Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight Astral Spirit bonus damage many units Echo Stomp control Natural Order reduces armor clear

display_text: Scenario: Sieging or Defending High Ground. Elder Titan is strong in high-ground standoffs. Use Astral Spirit to scout and harass defenders. A well-placed Earth Splitter can zone enemies off the tower or deal massive damage if they commit. His passive, Tip the Scales, causes creeps affected by Glyph of Fortification or a Banner to deal 100% bonus damage, making pushes under Glyph or with a Banner extremely potent.
search_text: Elder Titan sieging defending high ground Earth Splitter zoning Astral Spirit scouting Tip the Scales passive glyph fortification banner creeps 100% bonus damage

Right talent gives +25 Attack Speed, improving right-click damage. Choose the move speed if you need to be more mobile for initiating or escaping. Choose the attack speed if you are focusing on a right-click build, especially with the Attack Speed Facet.

display_text: Level 15 Talents: Left talent gives +75 Echo Stomp Damage, increasing the burst damage of your initiation from 180 to 255. Right talent gives +25 Astral Spirit Hero Attack, increasing the permanent bonus damage per hero hit from 80 to 105. Choose Echo Stomp damage for more immediate burst in fights. Choose Astral Spirit damage for significantly higher sustained right-click damage after landing your spirit on heroes.

display_text: Level 20 Talents: Left talent gives +350 Echo Stomp Wake Damage, raising the threshold to break the sleep from 250 to 600, making it nearly impossible for enemies to break free early. Right talent gives +150 Natural Order Radius, increasing the aura from 350 to 500, making it much easier to apply the armor and magic resistance reduction. Choose the wake damage if enemies have high burst to break sleep. Choose the radius to provide more consistent debuff coverage in chaotic fights.

display_text: Level 25 Talents: Left talent gives -60s Earth Splitter Cooldown, reducing it from 100 to 40 seconds, allowing it to be used in almost every engagement. Right talent gives +100% Cleave, enabling powerful area-of-effect physical damage with right-clicks, especially potent with the Attack Speed Facet and other damage items. Choose the cooldown reduction for more frequent fight-winning ultimates and control. Choose Cleave to transition into a formidable late-game carry with massive area damage.

display_text: Legion Commander is a melee strength hero with a high strength base of 24 and an attack rate of 1.7. Her starting armor is 3.2, which is above average for a melee carry. Her attack range is 150, typical for melee. Her base movement speed is 325. Her starting attributes are well-rounded but skewed towards strength, making her durable from level 1 with decent base damage (60-64).
search_text: Legion Commander base stats melee strength 24 str 18 agi 20 int 150 attack range 3.2 armor 325 move speed 1.7 BAT

display_text: The Stonehall Plate facet provides a barrier based on Overwhelming Odds damage dealt to enemy heroes, making Legion Commander significantly tankier in fights where she can hit multiple heroes. This facet is best for a front-line, initiator, or offlane build. The Spoils of War facet grants all allies who damage a Dueled target permanent bonus damage if she wins, and auto-casts Press the Attack on Legion Commander and allies after a win. This facet is best for a more supportive or utility-based role, encouraging team coordination and rewarding assists, effectively turning your team into a snowballing force.
search_text: Legion Commander facet choice Stonehall Plate Overwhelming Odds damage barrier Spoils of War Duel reward assists bonus damage Press the Attack

display_text: In lane, prioritize Press the Attack for dispels, sustain (24-60 HP regen), and bonus movement speed (13-22%). Use it to remove disables from yourself or your lane partner. Overwhelming Odds at level 1 or 2 is strong for trading and securing ranged creeps, dealing 40 base damage plus 14 per unit in a 600 radius. Use Moment of Courage for survivability against harassment. Focus on last hitting and using Overwhelming Odds to harass and secure denies due to its high damage against creeps. Boots and Wand are essential early items.
search_text: Legion Commander laning phase early game Press the Attack dispel heal Overwhelming Odds harass creep secure Moment of Courage lifesteal

display_text: Level 6 is a major spike with Duel, allowing for kill attempts with a +10 damage reward. Level 12 (maxed skills) and level 18 (max Duel duration at 5.0s) are significant. Talents at 10, 15, 20, and 25 offer power boosts. Key item spikes are Blink Dagger for initiation, Blade Mail for Duel survivability/damage, and Black King Bar. Aghanim's Shard can be a spike, providing bonus effects. Winning Duels is the ultimate scaling mechanic, with permanent +10/20/30 damage per win.
search_text: Legion Commander power spikes level 6 Duel level 12 max skills level 18 Blink Dagger Blade Mail BKB Aghanim's Shard talent permanent damage

display_text: Break effects (Silver Edge, Hoodwink) disable Moment of Courage and Outfight Them!, removing her lifesteal and sustain. Heroes with instant disables or saves (Shadow Demon, Oracle, Dazzle) can prevent Duel kills or save the target. High burst magic damage (Lina, Lion) can kill Legion Commander during Duel. Illusion or summon heroes (Phantom Lancer, Chaos Knight) can make targeting difficult and reduce Overwhelming Odds effectiveness. Kiting heroes with high mobility (Windranger, Puck) can avoid Duel initiation.
search_text: Legion Commander counters break Silver Edge Moment of Courage Outfight Them! disable save Shadow Demon Oracle burst magic Lina Lion illusions Phantom Lancer kiting

display_text: Blink Dagger is absolutely essential for initiation and landing Duel on key targets. Blade Mail is core for surviving and reflecting damage during Duel, especially against physical damage dealers. Black King Bar is critical in most games to ensure you can cast Duel without being disabled by magic spells and to survive the duel duration.
search_text: Legion Commander core build Blink Dagger initiation Blade Mail damage reflect Duel Black King Bar BKB magic immunity

display_text: In teamfights, Legion Commander's primary role is to initiate with Blink Dagger and Duel a high-priority, squishy core or support to lock them down and secure a kill for permanent damage. Use Overwhelming Odds for AoE damage and attack speed. Use Press the Attack to dispel key disables (like stuns or silences) on yourself or crucial allies. After winning a Duel, she becomes a significant right-click threat that must be focused or kited.
search_text: Legion Commander teamfight role initiator Duel priority target lock down Overwhelming Odds AoE Press the Attack dispel save

display_text: Overwhelming Odds: Deals 40/70/100/130 base damage plus 14/16/18/20 damage per unit in a 600 radius. Deals 40/70/100/130 extra damage per enemy hero in the area. Grants Legion Commander 50/75/100/125 bonus attack speed for 6.0 seconds. Cooldown is 18 seconds. Can be cast during Duel. The Stonehall Plate facet grants a damage barrier based on damage dealt to heroes.
search_text: Overwhelming Odds Q ability AoE damage 40 70 100 130 per unit 14 16 18 20 per hero bonus attack speed 50 75 100 125 duration 6s cooldown 18 cast during Duel

display_text: Press the Attack: Strongly dispels debuffs and disables from a target ally. Grants them 24/36/48/60 health regeneration and 13/16/19/22% bonus movement speed for 5.0 seconds. Level 25 talent can grant 1.5 seconds of Debuff Immunity and 50% Magic Resistance. Level 20 talent can make it affect a 300 AoE.
search_text: Press the Attack W ability strong dispel remove stun heal 24 36 48 60 HP regen move speed 13 16 19 22% duration 5.0s

display_text: Moment of Courage: Passive ability. When attacked, Legion Commander has a 25% chance to perform an instant counter-attack with 55/65/75/85% lifesteal. The counter-attack buff lasts 1.0 second. Level 15 talent increases proc chance by +8%. Level 20 talent increases lifesteal by +75%.
search_text: Moment of Courage E passive counter attack 25% chance lifesteal 55 65 75 85% duration 1.0s proc chance lifesteal talent

display_text: Outfight Them!: Passive ability. Attacking an enemy hero that is equal or higher level increases your Health Restoration (from all sources) by 30/40/50/60% for 4.0 seconds. This amplifies lifesteal, Press the Attack regen, and healing items.
search_text: Outfight Them! passive level comparison heal amp 30 40 50 60% health restoration duration 4.0s

display_text: Duel: Targets an enemy hero within 200 range, forcing both to attack each other for 4.0/4.5/5.0 seconds. Neither can use items or abilities (except Legion's Overwhelming Odds). If either hero dies during Duel, the winner gains 10/20/30 permanent bonus damage. Cooldown is 60/55/50 seconds. The Spoils of War facet grants allies who damage the Dueled target permanent damage if she wins, and auto-casts Press the Attack.
search_text: Duel R ultimate forced attack 4.0 4.5 5.0 sec duration 10 20 30 permanent damage reward cooldown 60 55 50 Overwhelming Odds allowed

display_text: Scenario: Facing a heavy magic damage lineup (Zeus, Lina). In this case, prioritize an early Black King Bar after Blink Dagger to survive Duel initiation. Consider a Pipe of Insight if your team needs it. Talent choice at level 25 for Press the Attack granting Debuff Immunity and Magic Resistance becomes very high value.
search_text: Legion Commander vs magic damage lineup BKB Pipe of Insight talent 25 Press the Attack debuff immunity magic resist

display_text: Scenario: Playing from behind and losing Duels. Shift focus to utility. Build items like Blade Mail, Heaven's Halberd, or Assault Cuirass to help your team. Use Duel defensively to lock down the enemy's primary carry during their BKB timing, even if you don't win the duel. The Spoils of War facet can be better here to reward allies for helping you secure Duel wins.
search_text: Legion Commander playing from behind utility Blade Mail Halberd AC defensive Duel Spoils of War facet

display_text: Scenario: Enemy team has strong save heroes (Dazzle, Oracle). Wait for them to use their save abilities (Shallow Grave, False Promise) before committing Duel. Consider a Nullifier to dispel saves, or coordinate with an ally who has a silence or hex to lock the save hero down first.
search_text: Legion Commander vs save heroes Dazzle Oracle wait for cooldowns Nullifier dispel coordination silence hex

display_text: Scenario: Against illusion heroes (Phantom Lancer, Chaos Knight). Overwhelming Odds is excellent for clearing illusions and revealing the real hero. Consider a Battle Fury for cleave and farm acceleration. Shard can be valuable. Focus on Dueling the real hero after identifying them.
search_text: Legion Commander vs illusions Phantom Lancer Chaos Knight Overwhelming Odds clear Battle Fury cleave shard identify real hero

display_text: Scenario: Your team lacks initiation. In this case, Legion Commander with Blink Dagger is the primary initiator. Communicate with your team to follow up on your Duel. Consider a Faceless Void or Enigma on your team for wombo-combo potential. Your job is to start the fight cleanly.
search_text: Legion Commander primary initiator Blink Dagger team communication follow up Duel wombo combo

display_text: At Level 10, choose between -2s Overwhelming Odds Cooldown or +12% Press The Attack Movement Speed. The cooldown reduction on Overwhelming Odds is better for farming, wave clear, and frequent skirmishes. The movement speed bonus on Press The Attack is better for chasing, escaping, and supporting allies in fights, especially if you are behind and need utility.
search_text: Legion Commander Talent Level 10 -2s Overwhelming Odds Cooldown +12% Press The Attack Movement Speed farming skirmish chase utility

display_text: At Level 15, choose between +40 Press The Attack HP Regen or +35 Overwhelming Odds Damage per Hero. The +40 HP Regen massively increases the survivability granted by Press the Attack for you or an ally, making it a powerful save and sustain tool. The +35 Damage per Hero on Overwhelming Odds significantly increases its burst damage in teamfights, especially when paired with the Stonehall Plate facet for a bigger barrier. Choose based on needing more sustain vs more damage.
search_text: Legion Commander Talent Level 15 +40 Press The Attack HP Regen +35 Overwhelming Odds Damage per Hero sustain save burst damage barrier

display_text: At Level 20, choose between 300 AoE Press The Attack or +8% Moment of Courage Proc Chance. The 300 AoE Press The Attack is a game-changing teamfight talent, allowing you to dispel and heal multiple allies at once, countering large AoE disables. The +8% Proc Chance on Moment of Courage increases your lifesteal and survivability in man-fights, making you much harder to kill through right-clicks. Choose between team utility/playmaking or personal survivability/dueling power.
search_text: Legion Commander Talent Level 20 300 AoE Press The Attack +8% Moment of Courage Proc Chance team dispel heal AoE lifesteal survivability duel

display_text: At Level 25, choose between +75% Moment of Courage Lifesteal or Press the Attack grants 1.5s Debuff Immunity and 50% Magic Resistance. The +75% Lifesteal makes your Moment of Courage heal for a massive amount (up to 160% total), turning you into an unkillable sustain monster in physical fights. The Press the Attack upgrade provides a short but powerful defensive buff (immunity to new debuffs and magic resistance) for you or an ally, which can be critical against chain disables and magic burst. Choose based on needing to man-fight versus needing to survive initiation and magic damage.
search_text: Legion Commander Talent Level 25 +75% Moment of Courage Lifesteal Press the Attack 1.5s Debuff Immunity 50% Magic Resistance sustain tank dispel immunity magic resist
